1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are one of: the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (per descriptor in `.archon/subagents/`), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	When instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**:
8	
9	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` override Archon's global prompts.
10	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` override globally installed plugins.
11	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project.
12	
13	## Skills
14	
15	- **archon-lean4** — installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source). Provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, etc.
16	
17	## Tools
18	
19	Project tools live in `.claude/tools/` as directly-executable scripts. List with `ls .claude/tools/`; run `<path> --help`. MCP servers (Lean LSP, etc.) are registered in `.claude/settings.json` / `.mcp.json` and surface as `mcp__*` tools.
20	
21	Two always-present scripts:
22	
23	- **`archon-informal-agent.py`** — call external LLMs for an informal proof-style sketch when you're stuck on an approach and want a second opinion. Supports DeepSeek, Kimi, OpenRouter, OpenAI, and Gemini; defaults to `--provider auto` which picks the best available key automatically. **Requires at least one key in env** (`DEEPSEEK_API_KEY` / `MOONSHOT_API_KEY` / `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` / `OPENAI_API_KEY` / `GEMINI_API_KEY`) — check via `env | grep -E "DEEPSEEK|MOONSHOT|OPENROUTER|OPENAI|GEMINI"` BEFORE planning around its use; if no key is set, fall back to alternatives below. The output is LLM-generated from training data, NOT source-derived: this tool is **not** a literature retriever. For actual literature lookup (cross-check a claim against Hartshorne, fetch an arXiv paper, summarize a Stacks tag) consult the auto-injected subagent catalog — when a literature/reference-fetching subagent is enabled it will appear there — or use the `WebSearch` / `WebFetch` tools directly.
24	- **`archon-subagent.py`** — the generic subagent dispatcher (one wrapper handles every subagent — see "Subagents" below).
25	
26	## Subagents
27	
28	Subagents are descriptor files at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md` (YAML frontmatter — `name`, `description`, `write_domain`, `read_only`, `can_spawn`, `default_enabled`, optional `mandatory: [<phase>...]`, optional `dispatcher_notes` — followed by the prompt body the spawned Claude reads).
29	
30	**You do NOT need to discover subagents.** The plan and review prompts auto-inject an **Available subagents** section at the top of each invocation listing every enabled descriptor with description, write-domain, MANDATORY flag, and `dispatcher_notes`. When you decide to invoke a subagent, read its full prompt at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md`.
31	
32	**Invoke** via the generic wrapper (Bash tool, foreground):
33	
34	```
35	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
36	  --name <subagent-name> \
37	  --slug <kebab-slug> \
38	  --directive-file <path-to-directive.md> \
39	  --write-domain '<glob>'        # repeat for multiple
40	```
41	
42	The wrapper exits 0 on success, prints a one-line status, writes the report to `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`, and the Archon CLI auto-archives a copy to `logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-report.md` for the dashboard. The dispatch semaphore caps concurrent subagent processes at `loop.max_parallel`; the wrapper resolves `--parent-slug` from `ARCHON_SUBAGENT_SLUG` so nested children inherit the hierarchy.
43	
44	**Treat each dispatch as blocking — wait for its report before acting on it.** Don't *deliberately* set `run_in_background: true`. But note the wrapper is genuinely synchronous (it `subprocess.run`s the `archon subagent` CLI and only returns once the child finishes and its report is on disk), so the harness may *auto-background* a long-running dispatch and hand you a task ID immediately — that's expected and fine. When it does, await that task / poll for the report file at `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md` (the wrapper's one-line status and exit 0 confirm completion) before you read or act on the result. The thing to avoid is firing a dispatch and then continuing to plan as if it had already returned; whether the call blocked inline or was auto-backgrounded, the rule is the same: do not consume a subagent's output until its report exists.
45	
46	**Highly-recommended subagents.** A descriptor whose frontmatter sets `mandatory: [plan]` is treated as **highly recommended** for the plan phase (similarly `[review]` for the review phase). The catalog tags them `[HIGHLY RECOMMENDED]`. Dispatch each one unless you have a concrete reason to skip — when skipping, record a one-line rationale under a `## Subagent skips` section in `iter/iter-NNN/<phase>.md` (e.g. `- strategy-critic: STRATEGY.md SHA unchanged from prior iter and last verdict was SOUND`). A post-phase audit checks both signals: it silences when the subagent dispatched OR a skip rationale was recorded, and warns only when neither happened. Each subagent's `dispatcher_notes` enumerates its specific skip conditions — read them before deciding. **Subagents ship disabled by default** (`default_enabled: false`), so the rule only fires for subagents the user has explicitly listed under `subagents.enabled` in `.archon/config.json`; on a fresh project the catalog is empty and no recommended dispatch is required. (The frontmatter field is still named `mandatory: [...]` for backward compat with existing configs and descriptors — its semantic meaning is "highly recommended", not "must dispatch".)
47	
48	## Key Files & Permissions
49	
50	All Archon state files are in `.archon/`. The table below covers **phase agents** (plan, prover, review) and the **user**. Subagent permissions are NOT a fixed row — they are descriptor-driven (`write_domain` glob in the subagent's frontmatter, enforced at dispatch time). See "Subagent permissions" after the table.
51	
52	| File | Plan | Prover | Review | User |
53	|------|------|--------|--------|------|
54	| `PROGRESS.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
55	| `STRATEGY.md` | read + write | — | — | read |
56	| `USER_HINTS.md` | **loop-managed** (captured + cleared by the CLI; injected into the plan prompt) | — | — | write |
57	| `task_pending.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
58	| `task_done.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
59	| `task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect) | write (own file) | read only | read |
60	| `proof-journal/` | read | — | **write** | read |
61	| `PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | — | **write** (Knowledge Base only — session log lives in `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`) | read |
62	| `iter/iter-NNN/plan.md` | **write** (this iter only) | — | read (recent window injected) | read |
63	| `iter/iter-NNN/review.md` | read (recent window injected) | — | **write** (this iter only) | read |
64	| `iter/iter-NNN/objectives.md` | optional write | — | read | read |
65	| `archon-protected.yaml` | read | read | read | **write** |
66	| `.lean` files | — | write (own file; frozen protected signatures) | — | write (via comments) |
67	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints) | read | **write** (semantic markers: `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections — NOT `\leanok`) | read |
68	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | — | — | read |
69	| `TO_USER.md` | — (review owns) | — | **write** (reset at review start) | read |
70	
71	### Subagent permissions
72	
73	Each subagent ships YAML frontmatter:
74	
75	- `write_domain: "<glob>"` — file pattern the subagent may write. Empty for purely read-only subagents (the dispatching call typically passes `--write-domain 'task_results/**'` so the subagent can still write its own report).
76	- `read_only: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may only write its `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`.
77	- `can_spawn: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may dispatch children whose `write_domain` is a subset of its own.
78	
79	The dispatcher enforces `write_domain` at the filesystem level: any child whose declared domain is not a subset of the parent's is rejected before the child agent starts. Reads are restricted only by the descriptor's prompt body (e.g. fresh-context critics that are told not to read PROGRESS.md / STRATEGY.md).
80	
81	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md`** is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow run by hand by the mathematician. The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. To invoke a refactor-style subagent inside the loop, write the directive to a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-directive.md` and dispatch via the generic wrapper. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise.
82	
83	## Protected Declarations
84	
85	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Rules:
86	
87	- **Plan / prover / review** agents: read-only on protected signatures. May fill proof bodies; may not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
88	- **Write-capable structural subagents** (per their `write_domain`): may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (name + signature preserved verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. They may NEVER rename / re-type / delete / re-sign a protected declaration.
89	- The YAML file itself is user-only. No agent adds or removes a declaration. The only allowed agent edit is updating an existing declaration's file path.
90	
91	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
92	
93	Two semantic markers:
94	
95	- `\leanok` — statement block: declaration is formalized (at least a `sorry` present). Proof block: proof closed, no `sorry`.
96	- `\mathlibok` — statement block: declaration is backed by Mathlib (re-export / alias); no Archon proof obligation remains.
97	
98	No marker means the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
99	
100	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between prover and review each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves.
101	
102	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations are the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
103	
104	## User Interaction
105	
106	The user provides hints in two places:
107	
108	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The Archon loop captures this file's content before each plan phase, injects it into the plan agent's prompt, and resets the file to its bundled template (an HTML-comment format guide + zero bullets) after the plan phase succeeds. The HTML-comment preamble is stripped before injection so a template-only file renders as "no hints" to the planner. The plan agent does NOT read or clear that file itself. Provers never see these hints.
109	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
110	
111	## Agent Roles
112	
113	### Plan Agent
114	
115	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for full instructions.
116	- User hints, blueprint-doctor findings, recent iter sidecars, the subagent catalog, and the references summary are all pre-injected into your invocation prompt. You do not "go read" those files.
117	- Read `task_results/` to collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md`.
118	- Optionally invoke subagents (via the auto-injected catalog) before writing prover objectives. Subagent reports are auto-archived to `logs/iter-NNN/` by the Archon CLI.
119	- Write `PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round.
120	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch markers — `\leanok` is owned by the deterministic sync; `\mathlibok` / `% NOTE:` by the review agent.
121	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself.
122	
123	### Prover Agent
124	
125	- Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
126	- Your **active mode** is injected directly into your invocation prompt — read it carefully, it defines your goal, workflow, and constraints for this session.
127	- When your mode specifies `read_blueprint: true`, you are permitted to **read** your assigned blueprint chapter (`blueprint/src/chapters/<your_slug>.tex`) for the informal proof sketch. You may never write to blueprint chapters.
128	- Write results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.
129	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — never edit another agent's file.
130	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your file for file-specific hints.
131	- Do NOT edit blueprint chapters. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker; the review agent applies them.
132	
133	### Subagents
134	
135	Subagents are descriptor-driven and discovered at runtime. The catalog is auto-injected into the plan and review prompts each phase (see "Subagents" above for the wrapper invocation). Each subagent reads only what its directive points at — never `PROGRESS.md` / `STRATEGY.md` / phase-agent state — and writes a self-contained report at `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`. Descriptors with `can_spawn: true` may dispatch children (subject to the global `loop.max_parallel` cap).
136	
137	### Review Agent
138	
139	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for full instructions.
140	- Read `proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data.
141	- Write the session journal to `proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (`summary.md`, `milestones.jsonl`, `recommendations.md`).
142	- Update `PROJECT_STATUS.md` (Knowledge Base only — session narrative goes to `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`).
143	- Maintain the semantic blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE:` annotations, stale `\notready` removal. Do NOT touch `\leanok`.
144	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify `PROGRESS.md`.
145	
146	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
147	
148	These run automatically between phases:
149	
150	- **Iter sidecar init** — creates `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/` at iter start so plan + review have a stable destination for per-iter narrative. Top-level files (STRATEGY.md, PROJECT_STATUS.md, task_*.md) stay bounded.
151	- **`sync_leanok`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status.
152	- **`blueprint-doctor`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically detecting orphan chapters, broken `\ref` / `\uses` / `\proves`, and new `axiom` declarations. Findings are written to `logs/iter-NNN/blueprint-doctor.{md,json}` and inlined into the next plan agent's prompt.
153

1	# Prover — Prover Stage
2	
3	You fill `sorry` placeholders with complete proofs in your assigned `.lean` file.
4	
5	## Workflow
6	
7	1. Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
8	2. Read `task_pending.md` for prior attempts, dead ends, relevant lemmas on your file.
9	3. Check the `.lean` file for `/- USER: ... -/` comments (file-specific user hints).
10	4. **Read the relevant blueprint chapter before writing Lean code.** The chapter holds the mathematical proof sketch you must align with. When stuck, re-reading the blueprint is often the fastest path forward.
11	5. Replace `sorry` with Lean proofs. Push as far as possible.
12	6. **Always save partial progress in the code.** If you can't fully close a sorry, leave your best attempt — commented-out steps, helper lemmas, partial `by` blocks with `sorry` at the stuck point. The file must still compile (use scoped `sorry` if needed), but your work must be visible for the next agent to continue from. NEVER revert to a bare `sorry` — that wastes the work.
13	7. Write your results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.
14	
15	**Write permissions**: only your assigned `.lean` file(s) and `task_results/<your_file>.md`. Do NOT edit `PROGRESS.md`, `task_pending.md`, `task_done.md`, blueprint chapters, or other agents' files.
16	
17	## Protected declarations
18	
19	`archon-protected.yaml` lists declarations with **frozen signatures**. You may fill their proof bodies but must not rename, re-type, reorder arguments, or weaken hypotheses. Only the mathematician edits protected signatures.
20	
21	## Avoid early termination
22	
23	- Don't abandon a proof prematurely. Many complex proofs run to thousands of lines.
24	- Difficulty is NOT a valid reason to leave a `sorry`.
25	- Don't delegate to "the next iteration" or "another prover" if more effort could close it.
26	- Only modify the proof for your assigned task — leave unrelated proofs untouched.
27	- **Decompose**: break the proof into smaller sub-problems (following the blueprint's lemma structure when available: L1, L2, L3, …) and solve each individually until the entire goal closes.
28	
29	## Task completion criteria
30	
31	Your task is complete ONLY when ALL of:
32	
33	1. Every `sorry` in scope is replaced with a complete proof.
34	2. Zero axioms introduced.
35	3. The file compiles cleanly.
36	
37	## Never weaken the type to dodge the proof
38	
39	When the substantive type is unattainable this iter, you have one honest move: leave `sorry` with the **intended type signature** so the type itself encodes the claim. You must NOT substitute a tautologically-true type to trivialize the body. Three patterns are banned:
40	
41	- **Reflexive-iso placeholder.** Replacing a non-trivial `X ≅ Y` (or `Nonempty (X ≅ Y)`) target with `Nonempty (X ≅ X) := ⟨Iso.refl _⟩`. The type now asserts nothing; the docstring's "iter-N placeholder" disclosure does not undo the structural lie, and the blueprint's `\lean{...}` cross-reference will resolve to this hollow declaration.
42	- **`Classical.choice` body around an explicit witness.** Writing `Classical.choice (α := X) ⟨witness⟩` to dissolve `Type` vs `Prop` friction — the witness is unreachable through any unfold, so the def is indistinguishable from `Classical.choice ⟨any-other-X⟩` downstream. Either use `Classical.choose` on the existential chain (preserves the spec), or `Nonempty.some`-with-witness; or ship a typed `sorry`.
43	- **Empty-content `proof_wanted`.** `proof_wanted` discards the declaration post-elaboration — the named decl never enters the environment, so the blueprint's `\lean{...}` cross-reference breaks. Use `theorem name : T := by sorry` with the intended signature instead.
44	
45	**Litmus test**: if you `unfold` your declaration, does it expose the named substantive content (Kähler differential module, the explicit iso, …) or does it stop at `Classical.choice` / `Iso.refl _` / nothing? If the latter, the body is structurally vacuous — ship the typed `sorry` instead.
46	
47	## When infrastructure is missing
48	
49	Do NOT report "Mathlib lacks X" and stop. Before giving up:
50	
51	1. **Use the informal agent** (`.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py`): "Prove [goal] without using [missing infrastructure], only Mathlib." Even an imperfect sketch is valuable.
52	2. **Try the alternative** — formalize whatever the informal agent suggests.
53	3. **If you still can't**: write the alternative sketch to `informal/<theorem_name>.md` and record in your task result what you tried, why it failed, AND the alternative route you found. "I couldn't prove X, but here's approach Y that might work because Z" is far more useful than "infrastructure missing".
54	
55	When stuck more generally: break into smaller subgoals, search Mathlib more thoroughly, prove missing helpers yourself, try alternative strategies, re-read the blueprint, use Web Search for published proofs.
56	
57	**Impossibility vs difficulty**: technical difficulty → keep trying. Mathematical impossibility → immediately backtrack and document why.
58	
59	## Proof style
60	
61	- **Never modify working proofs** — if a declaration has no `sorry` and compiles, do not touch its body.
62	- Keep edits minimal; don't delete comments or change labels; don't add unrelated declarations.
63	- Helper lemmas you introduced may be modified if they turn out wrong.
64	- Add a concise comment above each helper lemma so reuse is easy.
65	- **`change` vs `show`** — `change` reshapes the goal up to defeq; `show` is purely display-level annotation. Using `show` where `change` is needed produces a linter warning. Default to `change` when in doubt.
66	
67	## Mathlib tags in PROGRESS.md
68	
69	The plan agent tags suggested lemmas:
70	
71	- `[verified]` — confirmed to exist.
72	- `[expected]` — guessed by naming convention. Quick `lean_local_search`; pivot if it doesn't exist.
73	- `[gap]` — verified NOT in Mathlib. Don't waste search time; formalize a workaround.
74	
75	## LSP MCP tools
76	
77	The `archon-lean-lsp` server exposes Lean LSP operations as **MCP tool calls** (full names like `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_goal` / `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_diagnostic_messages`). The lean4 skill reference uses short names (`lean_goal`, `lean_local_search`, …) — same tools, NOT shell binaries.
78	
79	- Always invoke through your tool-call interface, the same way as `Read` or `Grep`.
80	- **Never** call `Bash` with `lean_goal …` etc. — there is no such shell command.
81	- First LSP action: `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_diagnostic_messages` on your file. If it returns `success: false` the server is cold; retry once or run `lake build` once via Bash, then retry.
82	
83	## Search protocol
84	
85	Follow `references/lean-lsp-tools-api.md`. Key priority:
86	
87	1. `lean_local_search` first.
88	2. `lean_leansearch` for semantic search — describe the mathematical content, not just the name.
89	3. `lean_loogle` for simple type patterns only.
90	4. Never use shell `find` / `grep` to locate Mathlib theorems.
91	
92	## Tooling traps
93	
94	- **Trust `goals_after`, not just empty diagnostics**: in `lean_multi_attempt`, `diagnostics: []` can be misleading (line-scoping quirks attach errors to the enclosing `by` block). Check that `goals_after` is empty or has advanced.
95	- **Beware `lean_run_code` with imports**: standalone snippets can silently swallow elaboration errors when they contain imports. Rely on the actual file's `lean_diagnostic_messages` for authoritative verification.
96	
97	## Logging
98	
99	Write to `task_results/<your_file>.md` (mirror your `.lean` path: `Algebra/WLocal.lean` → `task_results/Algebra_WLocal.lean.md`):
100	
101	```markdown
102	# Algebra/WLocal.lean
103	
104	## wLocal_iff (line 45)
105	### Attempt 1
106	- **Approach:** Direct case split on maximal ideals
107	- **Result:** FAILED — needed IsLocalRing instance, not available
108	- **Dead end:** direct case split without IsLocalRing
109	
110	### Attempt 2
111	- **Approach:** Stacks 0A31, characterize via bijection on spectra
112	- **Result:** RESOLVED
113	- **Key insight:** `PrimeSpectrum.comap_injective` bridges the gap
114	
115	## helper_bijective (line 78)
116	### Attempt 1
117	- **Approach:** Split into injectivity + surjectivity; prove injectivity
118	- **Result:** PARTIAL (branch closed) — injectivity closed; surjectivity remains
119	- **Next step:** `PrimeSpectrum.range_comap_of_surjective` for the right branch
120	- **Lemmas found:** `PrimeSpectrum.comap_surjective`
121	```
122	
123	**Rules**: one section per theorem/lemma in your file. Each attempt records approach, result (RESOLVED / FAILED / PARTIAL / IN PROGRESS), dead-end warnings or next steps. 10	/-!
11	# Abelian-variety rigidity: genus-`0` final assembly
12	
13	This file is the **genus-`0` final layer** of the project's committed characteristic-free
14	route (route (c)) to abelian-variety rigidity. It assembles the headline
15	`rigidity_genus0_curve_to_grpScheme` — the char-free replacement (no `[CharZero kbar]`) for
16	`AlgebraicGeometry.rigidity_over_kbar` of `AlgebraicJacobian.RigidityKbar` (which remains in
17	tree as the fallback route (a) artifact and still carries `[CharZero]`).
18	
19	The abstract Mumford Rigidity Lemma chain (`rigidity_lemma`) and the two Milne §I.1 corollaries
20	(`hom_additive_decomp_of_rigidity` = Cor 1.5, `av_regularMap_isHom_of_zero` = Cor 1.2) it
21	implies live **upstream** in `AlgebraicJacobian.RigidityLemma`, which is imported here. That
22	chain is PROVEN axiom-clean (iters 157–162); this file consumes it.
23	
24	The final assembly has three links specific to the genus-`0` route:
25	
26	1. `morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const` — every morphism `ℙ¹ → A` into an abelian variety is
27	   constant. Proved (route resolved iter-164) by the **𝔾ₘ-scaling shortcut**: the total scaling
28	   action `σ_× : ℙ¹ × 𝔾ₘ → ℙ¹`, `(x, λ) ↦ λx`, feeds the proven Cor 1.5
29	   (`hom_additive_decomp_of_rigidity`) — NO theorem of the cube, NO Milne Thm 3.2, NO
30	   `Hom(𝔾ₐ, A) = 0`, char-general. (Still a scaffold `sorry` pending the concrete ℙ¹/𝔾ₘ/σ_× infra.)
31	2. `genusZero_curve_iso_P1` — a smooth proper geom-irred genus-`0` curve over `k̄` is
32	   isomorphic to `ℙ¹` (blocked on Riemann–Roch).
33	3. `rigidity_genus0_curve_to_grpScheme` — THE HEADLINE consumed by `genusZeroWitness`.
34	
35	The file also exposes the project-local bridge `iotaGm_isDominant` — the dominance of the
36	canonical `Gm ↪ ℙ¹` inclusion — which becomes provable once Lane A
37	(`AlgebraicJacobian.Genus0BaseObjects`) ships the concrete chartwise body of `gmScalingP1`.
38	
39	See `blueprint/src/chapters/AbelianVarietyRigidity.tex` for the informal sketches and sources
40	(Mumford, *Abelian Varieties*, Ch. II §6; Milne, *Abelian Varieties*, Prop. 3.10;
41	Hartshorne, *Algebraic Geometry*, Example IV.1.3.5).
42	
43	## Encoding notes
44	
45	Mathlib `b80f227` packages no `ℙ¹` as a `Scheme`, so the projective line is encoded by its
46	abstract characterisation: a smooth proper geometrically irreducible `Over (Spec (.of kbar))` of
47	relative dimension `1` with `genus = 0` (see
48	`AlgebraicJacobian.Genus0BaseObjects.ProjectiveLineBar`).
49	The signatures of declarations 1–3 are **provisional** (`SCAFFOLD` comments mark them); the prover
50	may refine the encoding when the bodies are filled. Declaration 3 is pinned verbatim to
51	`rigidity_over_kbar`'s signature minus `[CharZero kbar]`, because it is the exact signature the
52	consumer (`genusZeroWitness.key`) needs.
53	-/

65	/-! ### Iter-167 dominance bridge for the canonical `Gm ↪ ℙ¹` map
66	
67	The four product / Proj instances `morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const_aux` needs
68	(`GeometricallyIrreducible`, `LocallyOfFiniteType`, `IsReduced` of the product, and
69	`IsReduced (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left`) all ship from Lane A
70	(`AlgebraicJacobian.Genus0BaseObjects`) as the instances `projGm_geomIrred`,
71	`projGm_locallyOfFiniteType`, `projGm_isReduced`, and `projectiveLineBar_isReduced`. The
72	helper resolves them all by `infer_instance`.
73	
74	The one remaining bridge — dominance of the canonical inclusion `ι : Gm ⟶ ℙ¹` — is
75	file-local because its proof depends on the concrete chartwise body of `gmScalingP1` (still
76	a Lane A scaffold sorry through iter-167). It is exposed here as a named top-level lemma
77	`iotaGm_isDominant` so the helper carries no inline `sorry`. -/
78	
79	/-- **Sub-task (b) range containment (iter-190 Lane E refactor):** the topological image of
80	the `k̄`-point `onePt = [1:1] ∈ ℙ¹` lies inside the open image of the chart-1 affine open
81	`Proj.awayι (X 1) : Spec(Away 𝒜 (X 1)) ⟶ Proj 𝒜`.
82	
83	**Mathematical content.** `onePt.left = Proj.fromOfGlobalSections 𝒜 (evalIntoGlobal v) _`
84	with `v 0 = v 1 = 1`. Since `evalIntoGlobal v (X 1) = (ΓSpecIso k̄).inv 1 = 1` is a unit in
85	`Γ(Spec k̄, ⊤)`, the preimage of `D₊(X 1) ⊆ Proj 𝒜` under `onePt.left` is
86	`(Spec k̄).basicOpen 1 = ⊤`, so the image of `onePt.left` lies entirely in
87	`Set.range (Proj.awayι (X 1)) = D₊(X 1)`.
88	
89	The range chain (iter-184 closure):
90	(i) `Scheme.Hom.coe_opensRange` + `Proj.opensRange_awayι` rewrite the RHS into
91	`↑(Proj.basicOpen 𝒜 (X 1))`; (ii) pointwise destructure to `onePt.left x ∈ basicOpen`;
92	(iii) `Scheme.Hom.mem_preimage` + `change` expose `onePt.left = fromOfGlobalSections 𝒜 φ hφ`
93	(`pointOfVec` unfolds through `Over.homMk` even though `evalIntoGlobal`/`pointOfVec` are
94	private — defeq is unaffected by name visibility); (iv) `fromOfGlobalSections_preimage_basicOpen`
95	reduces to `(Spec k̄).basicOpen ((ΓSpecIso k̄).inv.hom (eval (·=1) (X 1)))`;
96	(v) `Scheme.basicOpen_of_isUnit` + `simp` closes via `eval_X` + `map_one` + `isUnit_one`.
97	Axiom-clean: `#print axioms` = `{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}`.
98	
99	Iter-190: extracted from the previous `∃`-packed `iotaGm_onePt_chart1_factor` (verdict A of
100	the iter-189 mathlib-analogist Lane E `projappiso` analysis) so the chart-1 lift
101	`iotaGm_r_1` is exposed as a named `noncomputable def`. Downstream
102	`iotaGm_chart1_composition_isOpenImmersion` can then apply `IsOpenImmersion.lift_app`
103	directly to `iotaGm_r_1` without re-deriving the range witness via `cancel_mono`. -/
104	private lemma iotaGm_r_1_range_subset (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
105	    Set.range ⇑(ProjectiveLineBar.onePt kbar).left ⊆
106	      Set.range ⇑(Proj.awayι (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
107	        ((![MvPolynomial.X 0, MvPolynomial.X 1] :
108	          Fin 2 → MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) 1)
109	        (projectiveLineBarAffineCover_fDeg kbar 1)
110	        (projectiveLineBarAffineCover_hm 1)) := by
111	  -- Iter-190 prover closure (point-witness reshape). The iter-184 closed body relied on
112	  -- `rw [← Scheme.Hom.coe_opensRange]` to expose the RHS `Set.range` as a `(...).opensRange`
113	  -- coercion; in the standalone signature here, elaboration types the LHS at
114	  -- `Set ↥(ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left` whereas `Proj.awayι _ _ _ _` is typed at
115	  -- `Set ↥(Proj 𝒜)`, so the rewrite pattern does not match. We sidestep this by
116	  -- destructuring point-wise FIRST, then `change`-ing the membership goal into
117	  -- `(...).opensRange.1` (a defeq reshape that works because the underlying carriers
118	  -- `↥(ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left` and `↥(Proj 𝒜)` are defeq through
119	  -- `Scheme.asOver` / `OverClass.asOver`). The remainder of the chain is identical to
120	  -- the iter-184 body.
121	  rintro _ ⟨x, rfl⟩
122	  change (ProjectiveLineBar.onePt kbar).left.base x ∈ (Proj.awayι _ _ _ _).opensRange.1
123	  rw [Proj.opensRange_awayι]
124	  change x ∈ (ProjectiveLineBar.onePt kbar).left ⁻¹ᵁ Proj.basicOpen _ _
125	  change x ∈ Proj.fromOfGlobalSections (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) _ _ ⁻¹ᵁ _
126	  rw [Proj.fromOfGlobalSections_preimage_basicOpen _ _ _
127	      (projectiveLineBarAffineCover_hm 1)
128	      (projectiveLineBarAffineCover_fDeg kbar 1)]
129	  refine (Scheme.basicOpen_of_isUnit _ ?_).symm ▸ TopologicalSpace.Opens.mem_top x
130	  change IsUnit ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of kbar)).inv.hom
131	    ((MvPolynomial.eval (fun _ : Fin 2 => (1 : kbar)))
132	      ((![MvPolynomial.X 0, MvPolynomial.X 1] :
133	        Fin 2 → MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) 1)))
134	  simp

136	/-- **Sub-task (b) lift (iter-190 Lane E refactor):** the chart-1 factor
137	`r_1 : Spec k̄ ⟶ Spec(Away 𝒜 (X 1))` of the `k̄`-point `onePt = [1:1] ∈ ℙ¹` through
138	the chart-1 open immersion `Proj.awayι (X 1)`. Provided by the universal property
139	`IsOpenImmersion.lift` of the open immersion `Proj.awayι (X 1)` together with the
140	range-containment witness `iotaGm_r_1_range_subset`.
141	
142	Iter-190 refactor: replaces the existential `∃ r_1, ...` of the previous
143	`iotaGm_onePt_chart1_factor` (verdict A of the iter-189 mathlib-analogist Lane E
144	`projappiso` analysis) with a named `noncomputable def`, so that the consumer
145	`iotaGm_chart1_composition_isOpenImmersion` can apply `IsOpenImmersion.lift_app`
146	directly to `iotaGm_r_1` instead of re-deriving the lift identity via `cancel_mono`. -/
147	private noncomputable def iotaGm_r_1 (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
148	    Spec (CommRingCat.of kbar) ⟶
149	      Spec (CommRingCat.of (HomogeneousLocalization.Away
150	        (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
151	        ((![MvPolynomial.X 0, MvPolynomial.X 1] :
152	          Fin 2 → MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) 1))) :=
153	  IsOpenImmersion.lift
154	    (Proj.awayι (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
155	      ((![MvPolynomial.X 0, MvPolynomial.X 1] :
156	        Fin 2 → MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) 1)
157	      (projectiveLineBarAffineCover_fDeg kbar 1)
158	      (projectiveLineBarAffineCover_hm 1))
159	    (ProjectiveLineBar.onePt kbar).left
160	    (iotaGm_r_1_range_subset kbar)

162	/-- **Sub-task (b) factorisation (iter-190 Lane E refactor):** the chart-1 factor
163	`iotaGm_r_1` factors `onePt.left` through `Proj.awayι (X 1)`. Discharges via
164	`IsOpenImmersion.lift_fac`. -/
165	private lemma iotaGm_r_1_fac (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
166	    iotaGm_r_1 kbar ≫ Proj.awayι (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
167	        ((![MvPolynomial.X 0, MvPolynomial.X 1] :
168	          Fin 2 → MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) 1)
169	        (projectiveLineBarAffineCover_fDeg kbar 1)
170	        (projectiveLineBarAffineCover_hm 1) =
171	      (ProjectiveLineBar.onePt kbar).left :=
172	  IsOpenImmersion.lift_fac _ _ _

174	/-- **Sub-task f reusable compatibility helper (iter-183 Lane E):** the compatibility
175	hypothesis for the inner `pullback.lift` of the section `s`, namely
176	`((Gm).hom ≫ onePt.left) ≫ PLB.hom = 𝟙 Gm.left ≫ Gm.hom`. Reduces to `Over.w onePt`
177	plus `(𝟙_).hom = 𝟙 _` on the codomain. -/
178	private lemma iotaGm_inner_lift_compat (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
179	    ((Gm kbar).hom ≫ (ProjectiveLineBar.onePt kbar).left) ≫
180	        (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom =
181	      𝟙 (Gm kbar).left ≫ (Gm kbar).hom :=
182	  (Category.assoc _ _ _).trans <|
183	    ((congrArg ((Gm kbar).hom ≫ ·)
184	      (Over.w (ProjectiveLineBar.onePt kbar))).trans (by simp))

186	/-- **Sub-task f section (iter-183 Lane E; iter-191 refactored to specialise on `iotaGm_r_1`):**
187	the chart-1 section `s : Gm.left ⟶ (gmScalingP1_cover).X 1`, built as the `pullback.lift` over
188	`pullback q (awayι (![X 0, X 1] 1))` with the iotaGm-prefix as `q`-component and
189	`Gm.hom ≫ iotaGm_r_1` as `awayι`-component.
190	
191	**Iter-191 refactor** (per iter-190 review §7 + progress-critic STUCK-corrective scope):
192	Previously parametrised over abstract `(r_1, h_r_1)`. Now specialised on the named
193	`iotaGm_r_1` / `iotaGm_r_1_fac` directly, so the downstream consumer
194	`iotaGm_chart1_composition_isOpenImmersion` can apply `IsOpenImmersion.lift_app` to
195	`iotaGm_r_1` via `unfold` without re-deriving the lift identity through `cancel_mono`. -/
196	private noncomputable def iotaGm_chart1_section (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
197	    (Gm kbar).left ⟶ (gmScalingP1_cover kbar).X (1 : Fin 2) :=
198	  pullback.lift
199	    (pullback.lift
200	      ((Gm kbar).hom ≫ (ProjectiveLineBar.onePt kbar).left)
201	      (𝟙 (Gm kbar).left)
202	      (iotaGm_inner_lift_compat kbar))
203	    ((Gm kbar).hom ≫ iotaGm_r_1 kbar)
204	    (by
205	      simp [pullback.lift_fst, ← iotaGm_r_1_fac kbar, Category.assoc]
206	      rfl)

208	/-- **Sub-task (f) helper (iter-183 Lane E):** the section `s` (built from the chart-1
209	factorisation `r_1` of `onePt.left`) composed with `gmScalingP1_chart 1` is an open
210	immersion `Gm.left ⟶ ℙ¹.left`.
211	
212	**Mathematical content.** The composition realises the canonical inclusion
213	`Gm = Spec k̄[t, t⁻¹] ↪ ℙ¹` sending `λ ↦ [λ : 1]`. Decomposes as
214	
215	```
216	Gm.left = Spec k̄[t, t⁻¹]
217	   ↪ Spec k̄[u]               (localization at `t`, via `IsOpenImmersion.of_isLocalization`)
218	   ≅ Spec (Away 𝒜 (X 1))     (via `homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso.symm`, an iso)
219	   ↪ ℙ¹.left                  (via `Proj.awayι (X 1)`).
220	```
221	
222	Each of the three factors is an open immersion; the composition therefore is.
223	
224	**Status (iter-185 Lane E PARTIAL).** Iter-185 lands the canonical 3-step open-immersion
225	factorisation as an explicit witness (`Spec.map (algMap MvPoly Unit kbar GmRing kbar) ≫
226	Spec.map (homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso kbar 1).toRingHom ≫ Proj.awayι 𝒜 (X 1) …`),
227	each factor an explicit `IsOpenImmersion` instance:
228	* `Spec.map (algMap MvPoly Unit kbar GmRing kbar)` — `IsOpenImmersion.of_isLocalization`
229	  on `(X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar)`, since `GmRing kbar = Localization.Away (X ())`.
230	* `Spec.map (homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso kbar 1).toRingHom` — `Spec.map` of a ring
231	  iso (`RingEquiv.toCommRingCatIso.isIso_hom`), hence an iso.
232	* `Proj.awayι 𝒜 (X 1) … Nat.one_pos` — Mathlib instance
233	  `AlgebraicGeometry.Proj.instIsOpenImmersionAwayι`.
234	
235	The resulting open immersion is conclusion of the lemma modulo the **substantive equality**
236	`iotaGm_chart1_section ≫ gmScalingP1_chart 1 = canonical_3step_OI`, which requires
237	unfolding `gmScalingP1_chart 1 = (cover_X_iso 1).hom ≫ Spec.map (chart-ring map) ≫
238	Proj.awayι (X 1)` and the pullback-lift section's projections via `pullback.lift_fst`/
239	`pullback.lift_snd`; the cover-iso chain (`pullbackSymmetry ≫ pullbackRightPullbackFstIso
240	≫ pullback.congrHom ≫ pullbackSpecIso`) and the chart-ring map's `eval₂Hom ∘ iso`
241	factorisation provide the rewriting hooks.
242	
243	**Iter-185 PARTIAL progress.** The privacy of `gmScalingP1_cover_X_iso` (private in
244	`Genus0BaseObjects/GmScaling.lean`) is **sidestepped** via a `change` step that
245	reconstructs the iso chain with `_, _` placeholders for the two `pullback.congrHom`
246	proof arguments (the underlying `Eq` proofs are `Prop`-typed, hence proof-irrelevant
247	— the kernel discharges the defEq without seeing the private `awayι_comp_PLB_hom`).
248	After this unblock, the proof spreads the iso, cancels the trailing
249	`Spec.map (homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso.toRingHom)` (an iso, via `cancel_mono.mpr`),
250	and applies `ext_of_isAffine` to reduce to a global-sections ring map equation.
251	The residual is a fully concrete `MvPolynomial.algHom_ext`-style chase through
252	public Mathlib `appTop` simp lemmas (`Scheme.Hom.comp_appTop`,
253	`pullbackSpecIso_hom_*`, `pullbackRightPullbackFstIso_hom_*`,
254	`pullbackSymmetry_hom_comp_*`, `pullback.lift_*`,
255	`homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso_algebraMap`). iter-186+ closure target.
256	
257	**Iter-191 refactor (Part 1 of 2).** Signature specialised on the named
258	`iotaGm_r_1` / `iotaGm_r_1_fac` — abstract `(r_1, h_r_1)` parameters dropped from
259	both this lemma and the upstream `iotaGm_chart1_section`. With the section now a
260	concrete `pullback.lift` containing `iotaGm_r_1` directly, the residual's
261	`r_1.appTop(isLocElem) = 1` computation can be approached via `unfold iotaGm_r_1`
262	+ `IsOpenImmersion.lift_app` (no `cancel_mono` uniqueness needed). Part 2 (the
263	`Proj.appIso` evaluation step closing the `isLocElem ↦ 1 ∈ kbar` link) deferred
264	to iter-192+ per the HARD BUDGET sub-step cap. -/
265	private lemma iotaGm_chart1_composition_isOpenImmersion [IsAlgClosed kbar] :
266	    IsOpenImmersion (iotaGm_chart1_section kbar
267	      ≫ gmScalingP1_chart kbar (1 : Fin 2)) := by
268	  -- The composition equals the canonical inclusion `Gm = Spec k̄[t, t⁻¹] → ℙ¹` sending
269	  -- `λ ↦ [λ : 1]`, which factors as three open immersions:
270	  --   Spec(GmRing) → Spec(MvPoly Unit kbar) → Spec(Away 𝒜 (X 1)) → ℙ¹.
271	  -- The first is `Spec.map (algebraMap (MvPoly Unit kbar) (GmRing kbar))`, an open
272	  -- immersion via `IsOpenImmersion.of_isLocalization` (Mathlib).
273	  -- The second is `Spec.map homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso.toRingHom`, an iso (hence
274	  -- open immersion).
275	  -- The third is `Proj.awayι (X 1)`, an open immersion (Mathlib instance).
276	  -- Step 1: the canonical 3-step open immersion is set up explicitly.
277	  -- `Gm.left → Spec(MvPoly Unit kbar)` via the localization algebraMap.
278	  haveI h_loc : IsOpenImmersion
279	      (Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom
280	        (algebraMap (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) (GmRing kbar)))) :=
281	    IsOpenImmersion.of_isLocalization
282	      (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar)
283	  -- `Spec(MvPoly Unit kbar) → Spec(Away 𝒜 X_1)` via the chart-ring iso.
284	  -- `RingEquiv` lifts to a `CommRingCat` iso whose `.hom = CommRingCat.ofHom (toRingHom)`,
285	  -- so the latter is `IsIso`, and `Spec.map` preserves isos.
286	  haveI h_iso_isIso : IsIso (CommRingCat.ofHom
287	      (homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso kbar 1).toRingHom) := by
288	    rw [show CommRingCat.ofHom (homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso kbar 1).toRingHom
289	          = (homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso kbar 1).toCommRingCatIso.hom from rfl]
290	    exact (homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso kbar 1).toCommRingCatIso.isIso_hom
291	  haveI h_iso : IsOpenImmersion (Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom
292	      (homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso kbar 1).toRingHom)) :=
293	    inferInstance
294	  -- Reduce to the substantive equation: `s ≫ chart 1 = canonical 3-step OI`.
295	  -- Once established, the conclusion follows from `IsOpenImmersion.comp`
296	  -- applied three times (one per factor of the canonical chain).
297	  haveI h_proj : IsOpenImmersion (Proj.awayι (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
298	      (MvPolynomial.X 1 : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)
299	      (MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X kbar 1) Nat.one_pos) :=
300	    inferInstance
301	  suffices h_eq : iotaGm_chart1_section kbar
302	        ≫ gmScalingP1_chart kbar (1 : Fin 2) =
303	      Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom
304	          (algebraMap (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) (GmRing kbar))) ≫
305	        Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom
306	          (homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso kbar 1).toRingHom) ≫
307	          Proj.awayι (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
308	            (MvPolynomial.X 1 : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)
309	            (MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X kbar 1) Nat.one_pos by
310	    rw [h_eq]
311	    -- Composition of three open immersions is an open immersion.
312	    exact @IsOpenImmersion.comp _ _ _ _ _ h_loc
313	      (@IsOpenImmersion.comp _ _ _ _ _ h_iso h_proj)
314	  -- Prove the equation `s ≫ chart 1 = canonical 3-step OI`.
315	  -- Stage 1: Fuse the two trailing `Spec.map`s on the RHS into a single `Spec.map`
316	  --   via `Spec.map_comp` + `CommRingCat.ofHom_comp`, so both sides end with
317	  --   `Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom f_ring) ≫ Proj.awayι (X 1)` where
318	  --   `f_ring = (algMap (MvPoly Unit kbar) (GmRing kbar)).comp
319	  --             (homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso kbar 1).toRingHom`.
320	  rw [← Category.assoc, ← Spec.map_comp, ← CommRingCat.ofHom_comp]
321	  -- Stage 2: Unfold `gmScalingP1_chart` on the LHS and re-associate so both
322	  --   sides end with `Proj.awayι (X 1)`. Apply `congr 1` to cancel the
323	  --   trailing `Proj.awayι (X 1)` factor (open immersion ⟹ mono, but `congr 1`
324	  --   is structural and avoids needing the mono instance directly).
325	  unfold gmScalingP1_chart
326	  rw [← Category.assoc, ← Category.assoc]
327	  congr 1
328	  -- Residual goal: identify the LHS prefix (section ≫ cover_X_iso 1).hom ≫
329	  -- Spec.map (eval₂Hom_chart1_ringMap ∘ iso.toRingHom)) with the RHS prefix
330	  -- `Spec.map(f_ring) : Spec(GmRing) → Spec(Away 𝒜 (X 1))`.
331	  -- Both sides go between affine schemes. The RHS is a single `Spec.map`;
332	  -- the LHS reduces to a `Spec.map` once we unfold `gmScalingP1_cover_X_iso`
333	  -- (private in `Genus0BaseObjects/GmScaling.lean`) and use `pullbackSpecIso`
334	  -- + `pullback.lift_fst`/`_snd` simp lemmas to identify the underlying ring
335	  -- map `Away 𝒜 X_1 ⊗ GmRing → GmRing` sending
336	  --   a ⊗ 1 ↦ algebraMap kbar GmRing (r_1_ring(a))
337	  --   1 ⊗ b ↦ b
338	  -- (with `r_1_ring : Away X_1 → kbar` the ring map underlying `r_1`, sending
339	  -- the chart-1 affine coord `isLocalizationElem = X_0/X_1` to `1 ∈ kbar`).
340	  --
341	  -- Composed with the chart-1 ring map (= `eval₂Hom(X() ↦ isLocElem ⊗ algMap X())
342	  -- ∘ iso.toRingHom`), the underlying ring map collapses to
343	  -- `algMap MvPoly GmRing ∘ iso.toRingHom = f_ring` by:
344	  --   isLocalizationElem ↦ (eval₂Hom ∘ iso)(isLocalizationElem)
345	  --                       = isLocElem ⊗ algMap X()
346	  --                       ↦ μ(isLocElem ⊗ algMap X())
347	  --                       = μ(isLocElem ⊗ 1) · μ(1 ⊗ algMap X())
348	  --                       = algMap kbar GmRing(r_1_ring(isLocElem)) · algMap MvPoly GmRing(X())
349	  --                       = algMap kbar GmRing(1) · t = t = algMap MvPoly GmRing(X())
350	  --                       = f_ring(isLocalizationElem).
351	  -- Constants follow analogously via `homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso_algebraMap`.
352	  --
353	  -- Stage 3 (iter-185 attack): the `(gmScalingP1_cover_X_iso kbar 1).hom` in the LHS is
354	  -- inaccessible to `unfold` (the def is `private` in `Genus0BaseObjects/GmScaling.lean`),
355	  -- but its body is a 4-step iso chain `pullbackSymmetry ≪≫ pullbackRightPullbackFstIso ≪≫
356	  -- pullback.congrHom _ _ ≪≫ pullbackSpecIso`, with the two `pullback.congrHom`-equality
357	  -- proofs `Prop`-typed (hence proof-irrelevant). A `change` with `_, _` placeholders for
358	  -- those two proofs forces Lean to reconstruct them by unification and lets the kernel
359	  -- discharge the defEq via proof irrelevance — sidestepping the private-name barrier.
360	  change (iotaGm_chart1_section kbar ≫
361	      (pullbackSymmetry _ _ ≪≫
362	        pullbackRightPullbackFstIso _ _ _ ≪≫
363	        pullback.congrHom _ _ ≪≫
364	        pullbackSpecIso kbar _ (GmRing kbar)).hom) ≫ _ = _
365	  -- Stage 4: spread the iso (4 components) and unfold the section's `pullback.lift`
366	  -- skeleton so the LHS becomes a fully concrete chain
367	  --   `pullback.lift _ _ _ ≫ pullbackSymmetry.hom ≫ pullbackRightPullbackFstIso.hom ≫

598	private theorem morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const_aux
599	    [IsAlgClosed kbar]
600	    {A : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
601	    [GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom]
602	    (f : ProjectiveLineBar kbar ⟶ A)
603	    (hf0 : ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt kbar ≫ f = η[A]) :
604	    f = (1 : ProjectiveLineBar kbar ⟶ A) := by
605	  -- W-axis collapse via `gmScalingP1_collapse_at_zero`, precomposed with `onePt`:
606	  -- `lift zeroPt onePt ≫ gmScalingP1 = zeroPt`.
607	  have hcollapse :
608	      lift (ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt kbar) (Gm.onePt kbar) ≫ gmScalingP1 kbar
609	        = ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt kbar := by
610	    have hbase := gmScalingP1_collapse_at_zero kbar
611	    -- Rewrite `lift zeroPt onePt = onePt ≫ lift (toUnit Gm ≫ zeroPt) (𝟙 Gm)`.
612	    have hreshape :
613	        lift (ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt kbar) (Gm.onePt kbar)
614	          = Gm.onePt kbar ≫
615	            lift (toUnit (Gm kbar) ≫ ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt kbar) (𝟙 (Gm kbar)) := by
616	      rw [comp_lift, Category.comp_id]
617	      congr 1
618	      rw [← Category.assoc,
619	        toUnit_unique (Gm.onePt kbar ≫ toUnit (Gm kbar)) (𝟙 _), Category.id_comp]
620	    rw [hreshape, Category.assoc, hbase, ← Category.assoc,
621	      toUnit_unique (Gm.onePt kbar ≫ toUnit (Gm kbar)) (𝟙 _), Category.id_comp]
622	  -- The Cor 1.5 basepoint hypothesis `lift v₀ w₀ ≫ (σ ≫ f) = η[A]`.
623	  have hcorhyp :
624	      lift (ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt kbar) (Gm.onePt kbar)
625	        ≫ (gmScalingP1 kbar ≫ f) = η[A] := by
626	    rw [← Category.assoc, hcollapse]; exact hf0
627	  -- Apply Cor 1.5.
628	  have key := hom_additive_decomp_of_rigidity
629	    (V := ProjectiveLineBar kbar) (W := Gm kbar)
630	    (ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt kbar) (Gm.onePt kbar)
631	    (gmScalingP1 kbar ≫ f) hcorhyp
632	  -- W-axis restriction: `lift (toUnit Gm ≫ zeroPt) (𝟙 Gm) ≫ σ ≫ f = (1 : Gm ⟶ A)`.
633	  have hfW :
634	      lift (toUnit (Gm kbar) ≫ ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt kbar) (𝟙 (Gm kbar))
635	        ≫ (gmScalingP1 kbar ≫ f) = (1 : Gm kbar ⟶ A) := by
636	    rw [← Category.assoc, gmScalingP1_collapse_at_zero, Category.assoc, hf0]
637	    exact Hom.one_def.symm
638	  -- `snd ≫ fW = snd ≫ 1 = 1` (using `toUnit` uniqueness).
639	  have hSndFW :
640	      snd (ProjectiveLineBar kbar) (Gm kbar)
641	        ≫ (lift (toUnit (Gm kbar) ≫ ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt kbar) (𝟙 (Gm kbar))
642	          ≫ (gmScalingP1 kbar ≫ f))
643	        = (1 : ProjectiveLineBar kbar ⊗ Gm kbar ⟶ A) := by
644	    rw [hfW, Hom.one_def, ← Category.assoc]
645	    congr 1
646	    exact toUnit_unique _ _
647	  -- `key` now reads `σ ≫ f = fst ≫ fV` (after collapsing the W-axis to 1).
648	  rw [hSndFW, _root_.mul_one] at key
649	  -- Name the V-axis restriction `fV`.
650	  set fV : ProjectiveLineBar kbar ⟶ A :=
651	    lift (𝟙 (ProjectiveLineBar kbar)) (toUnit (ProjectiveLineBar kbar) ≫ Gm.onePt kbar)
652	      ≫ (gmScalingP1 kbar ≫ f) with hfVdef
653	  -- Precompose `key` with `gmInP1 := lift (toUnit Gm ≫ onePt) (𝟙 Gm) : Gm → ℙ¹ ⊗ Gm`
654	  -- ("λ ↦ (1, λ)"). The resulting morphism `ι := gmInP1 ≫ σ : Gm → ℙ¹` is the canonical
655	  -- inclusion `Gm ↪ ℙ¹` ("λ ↦ σ×(1, λ) = λ"); on its image, `f` is constant at `onePt ≫ fV`.
656	  set iotaGm : Gm kbar ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar :=
657	    lift (toUnit (Gm kbar) ≫ ProjectiveLineBar.onePt kbar) (𝟙 (Gm kbar))
658	      ≫ gmScalingP1 kbar with hιdef
659	  have hιf :
660	      iotaGm ≫ f = toUnit (Gm kbar) ≫ (ProjectiveLineBar.onePt kbar ≫ fV) := by
661	    calc iotaGm ≫ f
662	        = lift (toUnit (Gm kbar) ≫ ProjectiveLineBar.onePt kbar) (𝟙 (Gm kbar))
663	            ≫ (gmScalingP1 kbar ≫ f) := by rw [hιdef, Category.assoc]
664	      _ = lift (toUnit (Gm kbar) ≫ ProjectiveLineBar.onePt kbar) (𝟙 (Gm kbar))
665	            ≫ (fst (ProjectiveLineBar kbar) (Gm kbar) ≫ fV) := by rw [key]
666	      _ = (lift (toUnit (Gm kbar) ≫ ProjectiveLineBar.onePt kbar) (𝟙 (Gm kbar))
667	            ≫ fst (ProjectiveLineBar kbar) (Gm kbar)) ≫ fV := by
668	          rw [Category.assoc]
669	      _ = (toUnit (Gm kbar) ≫ ProjectiveLineBar.onePt kbar) ≫ fV := by rw [lift_fst]
670	      _ = toUnit (Gm kbar) ≫ (ProjectiveLineBar.onePt kbar ≫ fV) := by

783	/-- **Headline: rigidity for a pointed genus-`0` curve.** Over an algebraically closed field
784	`k̄` (arbitrary characteristic — no `[CharZero kbar]`), let `C` be a smooth proper geometrically
785	irreducible curve with `genus C = 0` and `A` an abelian variety (smooth proper geom-irred group

787	constant morphism at the identity, `f = toUnit C ≫ η[A]`.

790	(via the `k̄ → k` descent step hosted in `AlgebraicJacobian.Jacobian`). Its signature mirrors
791	`AlgebraicGeometry.rigidity_over_kbar` of `AlgebraicJacobian.RigidityKbar` **verbatim except**
792	the `[CharZero kbar]` instance is dropped. Combine `genusZero_curve_iso_P1` (`C ≅ ℙ¹`) with

800	theorem rigidity_genus0_curve_to_grpScheme
801	    [IsAlgClosed kbar]
802	    {C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
803	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
804	    [IsProper C.hom]
805	    [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom]
806	    (_hgenus : genus C = 0)
807	    {A : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
808	    [GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom]
809	    (f : C ⟶ A)
810	    (p : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ C)
811	    (_hf : p ≫ f = η[A]) :
812	    f = (toUnit C ≫ η[A]) := by
813	  -- Transport `f` along `C ≅ ProjectiveLineBar kbar` to a morphism `g : ℙ¹ ⟶ A`.
814	  obtain ⟨φ⟩ := genusZero_curve_iso_P1 _hgenus

816	  -- `morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const` gives `g = toUnit ℙ¹ ≫ a₀` for some `a₀`.
817	  obtain ⟨a₀, hga₀⟩ := morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const g
818	  -- Pin `a₀ = η[A]` via the pointed hypothesis on `f`.
819	  have hpoint : (p ≫ φ.hom) ≫ g = η[A] := by
820	    rw [hgdef, Category.assoc, ← Category.assoc φ.hom, φ.hom_inv_id, Category.id_comp]
821	    exact _hf
822	  have hcst : a₀ = η[A] := by
823	    rw [hga₀, ← Category.assoc,
824	      toUnit_unique ((p ≫ φ.hom) ≫ toUnit (ProjectiveLineBar kbar)) (𝟙 _),
825	      Category.id_comp] at hpoint
826	    exact hpoint
827	  rw [hcst] at hga₀
828	  -- Back-transport: `f = φ.hom ≫ g = φ.hom ≫ toUnit ℙ¹ ≫ η[A] = toUnit C ≫ η[A]`.
829	  calc f
830	      = φ.hom ≫ φ.inv ≫ f := by
831	        rw [← Category.assoc, φ.hom_inv_id, Category.id_comp]
832	    _ = φ.hom ≫ g := by rw [hgdef]
833	    _ = φ.hom ≫ toUnit (ProjectiveLineBar kbar) ≫ η[A] := by rw [hga₀]

836	          toUnit_unique (φ.hom ≫ toUnit (ProjectiveLineBar kbar)) (toUnit C)]

1230	  (\cref{def:projlinebar_affine_cover}) it suffices to show that each chart ring is
1231	  reduced. The chart-\(i\) ring \(\mathrm{Away}\,\mathcal A\,X_i\) embeds into the ordinary
1232	  localization \(\bar k[X_0, X_1][X_i^{-1}]\) of the polynomial ring \(\bar k[X_0, X_1]\) at
1233	  the powers of \(X_i\) via the canonical injection $\mathrm{val} \colon
1234	  \mathrm{Away}\,\mathcal A\,X_i \hookrightarrow \bar k[X_0, X_1][X_i^{-1}]$
1235	  (\(\mathtt{HomogeneousLocalization.val\_injective}\)); the codomain is a localization of a
1236	  polynomial ring at a non-zero-divisor (the powers of \(X_i\)), hence a domain, hence
1237	  reduced. A subring of a reduced ring is reduced
1238	  (\(\mathtt{Function.Injective.isDomain}\) on the \(\mathrm{val}\)-embedding), so each chart
1239	  ring is a domain --- in particular reduced --- and the global reducedness follows.
1240	\end{lemma}

1244	  \label{def:gaTranslationP1}
1245	  
1246	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.gmScalingP1}
1247	  % NOTE (iter-171 refresh of iter-170 decision): the option (c) inline chart-glue
1248	  % commitment LANDED its body skeleton iter-171. `gmScalingP1` now has a CONCRETE

1250	  % gmScalingP1_chart_agreement) gmScalingP1_over_coherence` — no buried sorries.

1254	  % `gmScalingP1_chart` (per-chart scheme morphism), `gmScalingP1_chart_agreement`

1257	  % See `analogies/gmscaling-deep.md` for the full 6-step decomposition.
1258	  % Archon-original Lean encoding; the chart computations below are elementary and characteristic-free.
1259	  % No external % SOURCE QUOTE for the encoding. The mathematical fact -- \(\mathbb G_m\) acts on
1260	  % \(\mathbb P^1\) by scalings fixing \(0\) and \(\infty\), \(\mathbb G_a\) by translations fixing \(\infty\)
1261	  % -- is standard (the Borel subgroup of \(\mathrm{PGL}_2\) acting on \(\mathbb P^1\)).
1262	  % NOTE (iter-164/167): the load-bearing action for the PRIMARY genus-0 route is now the
1263	  % MULTIPLICATIVE scaling action \(\sigma_\times\) (\lean name gmScalingP1, landed iter-165/166;
1264	  % pinned in this block's \lean{...} hook). The \(\mathbb G_a\)-translation \(\sigma\) (\lean name
1265	  % gaTranslationP1 [expected]) is retained only for the demoted additive alternative and is
1266	  % intentionally not yet a Lean target.
1267	  \textbf{The scaling action (primary).} The \emph{multiplicative scaling action}
1268	  \[ \sigma_\times \colon \mathbb P^1 \times \mathbb G_m \to \mathbb P^1, \qquad
1269	     \text{``}\sigma_\times(x, \lambda) = \lambda x\text{''} \]
1270	  (\lean name \texttt{gmScalingP1}) is a \emph{genuine total} scheme morphism on all of
1271	  \(\mathbb P^1 \times \mathbb G_m\) --- no indeterminacy, characteristic-free (M\"obius scaling
1272	  fixing both \(0\) and \(\infty\)) --- defined chartwise:
1273	  \begin{itemize}
1274	    \item On \(\mathbb A^1 \times \mathbb G_m\) (target chart \(x\)): \((x, \lambda) \mapsto \lambda x\),
1275	      a polynomial map, regular everywhere.
1276	    \item Near \(\infty\) (target chart \(u = 1/x\)): the target coordinate \(1/(\lambda x)\) rewrites as
1277	      \(u/\lambda\), regular everywhere on this chart because \(\lambda \in \mathbb G_m\) is invertible.
1278	  \end{itemize}
1279	  The two target charts cover the image and overlap consistently, so \(\sigma_\times\) is defined on
1280	  the whole surface. It is a left action of the group \(\mathbb G_m\) on \(\mathbb P^1\):
1281	  \(\sigma_\times(x, 1) = x\) and $\sigma_\times(\sigma_\times(x, \lambda), \mu) = \sigma_\times(x,
1282	  \lambda\mu)\(. Crucially it has \emph{two fixed points}, \)\sigma_\times(0, \lambda) = 0$ and
1283	  \(\sigma_\times(\infty, \lambda) = \infty\) for all \(\lambda\); the fixed point \(0\) is what makes the
1284	  \(W\)-axis of the additive decomposition collapse in the primary base-case proof
1285	  (\cref{prop:morphism_P1_to_AV_constant}, \cref{rmk:base_case_fourth_route}).
1286	  \medskip
1287	  \noindent\textbf{Companion: the \(\mathbb G_a\)-translation action (demoted route only).} For the
1288	  Milne-faithful additive alternative we also record the \emph{translation action}
1289	  \[ \sigma \colon \mathbb P^1 \times \mathbb G_a \to \mathbb P^1, \qquad
1290	     \text{``}\sigma(x, y) = x + y\text{''} \]
1291	  (\lean name \texttt{gaTranslationP1} [expected]), likewise a \emph{total} scheme morphism on all
1292	  of \(\mathbb P^1 \times \mathbb G_a\), defined chartwise: on \(\mathbb A^1 \times \mathbb G_a\)
1293	  (chart \(x\)) it is the polynomial map \((x, y) \mapsto x + y\); near \(\infty\) (\(u = 1/x\)) the
1294	  coordinate \(1/(x + y)\) rewrites as \(u/(1 + u y)\), regular at \(u = 0\) (value \(0\) there --- \(\sigma\)
1295	  fixes \(\infty\) for every \(y\)), while the locus \(x + y = 0\) is covered by the chart \(x\) on which it
1296	  is the finite value \(0\). It is a left action of \(\mathbb G_a\) on \(\mathbb P^1\) fixing \(\infty\):
1297	  \(\sigma(x, 0) = x\) and \(\sigma(\sigma(x, y), y') = \sigma(x, y + y')\). This companion underlies
1298	  only the alternative proof of \cref{lem:hom_Ga_to_av_trivial} and the demoted $\mathbb
1299	  G_a$-additive close of the base case (\cref{lem:hom_from_Ga_trivial}); it is \emph{not} on the
1300	  genus-\(0\) critical path.
1301	\end{definition}

324	/-- **The chart-ring iso `Away 𝒜 (X i) ≃+* k̄[u]`** — built from the forward map (via
325	`Localization.awayLift`) and the inverse map (via `MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom`). The two
326	round-trips are proved at the underlying-`Localization.Away` level by
327	`HomogeneousLocalization.val_injective`. -/
328	noncomputable def homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
329	    HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
330	        (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) ≃+*
331	      MvPolynomial Unit kbar :=
332	  RingEquiv.ofRingHom
333	    (homogeneousLocalizationAwayToMvPoly kbar i)
334	    (mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway kbar i)
335	    (homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso_aux_right kbar i)
336	    (homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso_aux_left kbar i)
337	
338	/-- **`kbar`-algebra preservation of `homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso`** (iter-174 Sub-task A
339	helper per `analogies/chart-bridge-shared-helper.md` Decision 3 step 3). The forward map
340	`Away 𝒜 (X i) →+* MvPolynomial Unit kbar` carries `algebraMap kbar Away` to
341	`algebraMap kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar)` (i.e. `MvPolynomial.C`).
342	
343	Closed via the inverse's `eval₂Hom_C` action: `inverse (C r) = kbarToAwayRingHom kbar i r =
344	algebraMap kbar Away r` (the last identity is definitional, by the `algebraKbarAway`
345	`Algebra.compHom` instance combined with `HomogeneousLocalization.algebraMap_eq`). The
346	forward round-trip then gives `forward (algebraMap kbar Away r) = C r`. -/
347	lemma homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso_algebraMap
348	    (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
349	    ((homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso kbar i).toRingHom).comp
350	        (algebraMap kbar
351	          (HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
352	            (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar))) =
353	      algebraMap kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) := by
354	  ext r
355	  -- `algebraMap kbar Away r = mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway kbar i (C r)` since
356	  -- the `algebraKbarAway` instance is `Algebra.compHom _ (algebraMap kbar (𝒜 0))` and
357	  -- `algebraMap (𝒜 0) Away = HomogeneousLocalization.fromZeroRingHom 𝒜 _` (see
358	  -- `HomogeneousLocalization.algebraMap_eq`), so the composite is exactly `kbarToAwayRingHom`
359	  -- which equals `inverse (C r)` by `MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_C`.
360	  have hinv : mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway kbar i (MvPolynomial.C r) =
361	      algebraMap kbar
362	        (HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
363	          (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)) r := by
364	    unfold mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway
365	    rw [MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_C]
366	    rfl
367	  -- Now apply forward (= homogeneousLocalizationAwayToMvPoly) and use aux_right's roundtrip.
368	  have hround : (homogeneousLocalizationAwayToMvPoly kbar i)
369	      ((mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway kbar i) (MvPolynomial.C r)) =
370	      MvPolynomial.C r :=
371	    RingHom.congr_fun (homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso_aux_right kbar i) (MvPolynomial.C r)
372	  -- Combine: rewrite the inverse C r as algebraMap kbar Away r, then apply roundtrip.
373	  simp only [RingHom.coe_comp, Function.comp_apply, RingEquiv.toRingHom_eq_coe,
374	    RingEquiv.coe_toRingHom, homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso, RingEquiv.ofRingHom_apply]
375	  rw [← hinv, hround]
376	  rfl

144	/-- **The inverse map `k̄[u] → Away 𝒜 (X i)` is surjective.**

147	`isLocalizationElem` is the image of the single generator `X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar`
148	and `kbarToAwayRingHom` covers the scalars (via the `kbar ≃ 𝒜 0` bijection). By
149	`Away.adjoin_mk_prod_pow_eq_top` (`Mathlib.RingTheory.GradedAlgebra.HomogeneousLocalization:1064`)
150	specialised to `d = 1`, `ι' = Fin 2`, `v = ![X 0, X 1]`, `dv = ![1, 1]`, this adjoin is `⊤`.

152	Proof structure (iter-172):
153	1. Apply `Away.adjoin_mk_prod_pow_eq_top` with `d = 1, v = ![X 0, X 1], dv = ![1, 1]` to
154	   get `Algebra.adjoin (𝒜 0) {Away.mk hf a (X 0^a₀ * X 1^a₁) _ | (a, ai) with a₀+a₁=a, ai≤1} = ⊤`.
155	2. Induct on adjoin membership (via `Algebra.adjoin_induction`):
156	   - `mem`: each generator `Away.mk hf a (X 0^a₀ * X 1^a₁) _` equals `isLocalizationElem^k`

158	   - `algebraMap`: every `algebraMap (𝒜 0) Away r` is `algebraMap (𝒜 0) Away

160	     is surjective (see `projectiveLineBar_isProper`).
161	   - `add`/`mul`: `f` is a ring hom. -/
162	private lemma mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway_surjective
163	    (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
164	    Function.Surjective (mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway kbar i) := by

172	    MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X kbar (otherFin i)
173	  -- The 2-generator vector and degrees for `Away.adjoin_mk_prod_pow_eq_top` (d = 1).
174	  let v : Fin 2 → MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar := ![MvPolynomial.X 0, MvPolynomial.X 1]
175	  let dv : Fin 2 → ℕ := ![1, 1]

10	# Genus-`0` base objects (Stratum 3): standard `k̄`-points on `ℙ¹`; `𝔾_a` and `𝔾_m`
11	
12	This file is **Stratum 3** of the four-stratum split of the legacy
13	`AlgebraicJacobian.Genus0BaseObjects` (iter-175 refactor `g0bo-split`). It ships:
14	
15	* the three standard `k̄`-points `0 = [0 : 1]`, `1 = [1 : 1]`, `∞ = [1 : 0]` of
16	  `ProjectiveLineBar kbar`, encoded as sections of `ProjectiveLineBar.hom`;
17	* the additive group `Ga` over `Spec k̄` (= `AffineSpace (Fin 1)`) and its
18	  affine, finite-presentation, and reduced instances;
19	* the multiplicative group `Gm` over `Spec k̄` (= `Spec k̄[t, t⁻¹]`) and its
20	  affine, finite-presentation, reduced, domain, and irreducible-space instances,
21	  plus the scaffold `GrpObj Gm` and the resulting `Smooth Gm.hom`;
22	* the multiplicative identity `Gm.onePt = η[Gm kbar]`.
23	
24	Upstream strata: `BareScheme`, `ChartIso`. Downstream stratum: `GmScaling`.
25	-/
26	
27	set_option autoImplicit false
28	set_option linter.style.setOption false
29	
30	universe u
31	
32	open CategoryTheory Limits MonoidalCategory CartesianMonoidalCategory MonObj
33	
34	noncomputable section
35	
36	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
37	
38	/-! ### The standard `k̄`-points `0`, `1`, `∞` on `ℙ¹`
39	
40	The three distinguished `k̄`-points of `ℙ¹_{k̄}` are `[0 : 1]` (the affine origin), `[1 : 1]`
41	(the affine unit), and `[1 : 0]` (the point at infinity). They are encoded as morphisms
42	`𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar`, i.e. sections of `ProjectiveLineBar.hom`.
43	
44	The construction goes through `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections`: a `k̄`-point of `Proj 𝒜` is
45	specified by an evaluation ring map `MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar →+* k̄` (composed into
46	`Γ(Spec k̄, ⊤)` via `Scheme.ΓSpecIso`) whose image of the irrelevant ideal `(X₀, X₁)`
47	generates the unit ideal — automatic whenever the evaluation vector has a unit coordinate. -/
48	
49	/-- **The underlying ring map of a `k̄`-point of `ℙ¹`.** Sends `X₀ ↦ v 0`, `X₁ ↦ v 1`,
50	then composes back into `Γ(Spec k̄, ⊤)` via the inverse of `Scheme.ΓSpecIso`. -/
51	private noncomputable def ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal
52	    (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (v : Fin 2 → kbar) :
53	    MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar →+* Γ(Spec (.of kbar), ⊤) :=
54	  (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (.of kbar)).inv.hom.comp (MvPolynomial.eval v)
55	
56	/-- **Irrelevant-ideal-maps-to-top condition** for `ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal`: if one
57	of the coordinates `v 0` or `v 1` is a unit, then the image of `(X₀, X₁)` generates the
58	unit ideal in `Γ(Spec k̄, ⊤)`. -/
59	private lemma ProjectiveLineBar.irrelevant_map_eq_top
60	    (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (v : Fin 2 → kbar) (i : Fin 2) (hi : IsUnit (v i)) :
61	    Ideal.map (ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal kbar v)
62	        (HomogeneousIdeal.irrelevant (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)).toIdeal = ⊤ := by
63	  have hX_mem : (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) ∈
64	      (HomogeneousIdeal.irrelevant (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)).toIdeal :=
65	    HomogeneousIdeal.mem_irrelevant_of_mem _ Nat.zero_lt_one
66	      (MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X kbar i)
67	  have hImg_mem : ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal kbar v (MvPolynomial.X i) ∈
68	      Ideal.map (ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal kbar v)
69	        (HomogeneousIdeal.irrelevant (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)).toIdeal :=
70	    Ideal.mem_map_of_mem _ hX_mem
71	  have hImg_unit : IsUnit (ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal kbar v (MvPolynomial.X i)) := by
72	    unfold ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal
73	    rw [RingHom.comp_apply, MvPolynomial.eval_X]
74	    exact hi.map _
75	  rw [Ideal.eq_top_iff_one]
76	  obtain ⟨u, hu⟩ := hImg_unit
77	  rw [show (1 : Γ(Spec (.of kbar), ⊤)) = ((u⁻¹ : Γ(Spec (.of kbar), ⊤)ˣ) : _) * u from
78	    (Units.inv_mul _).symm]
79	  exact Ideal.mul_mem_left _ _ (hu.symm ▸ hImg_mem)
80	
81	/-- **Helper: construct a `k̄`-point of `ProjectiveLineBar kbar`** from an evaluation vector
82	`v : Fin 2 → kbar` with at least one unit coordinate. The underlying scheme map is
83	`Proj.fromOfGlobalSections` of the evaluation; the section condition chases through
84	`fromOfGlobalSections_toSpecZero` + `IsScalarTower kbar (𝒜 0) MvPoly` collapse to
85	`MvPolynomial.C` + `MvPolynomial.eval_C` + `toSpecΓ_SpecMap_ΓSpecIso_inv`. -/
86	private noncomputable def ProjectiveLineBar.pointOfVec
87	    (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (v : Fin 2 → kbar) (i : Fin 2) (hi : IsUnit (v i)) :
88	    𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar :=
89	  Over.homMk
90	    (Proj.fromOfGlobalSections (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
91	      (ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal kbar v)
92	      (ProjectiveLineBar.irrelevant_map_eq_top kbar v i hi)) <| by
93	    -- Section condition: fromOfGlobalSections ≫ ProjectiveLineBar.hom = 𝟙.
94	    haveI : IsScalarTower kbar ↥(projectiveLineBarGrading kbar 0)
95	        (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) :=
96	      IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_eq fun _ => rfl
97	    -- Expose the structure morphism's composition shape.
98	    change Proj.fromOfGlobalSections _ _ _ ≫ Proj.toSpecZero _ ≫ Spec.map _ = _
99	    -- Combine via `fromOfGlobalSections_toSpecZero`, then unify the two Spec.maps.
100	    rw [← Category.assoc, Proj.fromOfGlobalSections_toSpecZero, Category.assoc,

100	    rw [← Category.assoc, Proj.fromOfGlobalSections_toSpecZero, Category.assoc,
101	      ← Spec.map_comp, ← CommRingCat.ofHom_comp, RingHom.comp_assoc,
102	      ← IsScalarTower.algebraMap_eq kbar, MvPolynomial.algebraMap_eq]
103	    -- The inner composition `(evalIntoGlobal v) ∘ C` collapses to `(ΓSpecIso).inv.hom`.
104	    -- Build a CommRingCat-level equation, then apply `Spec.map` and finish.
105	    have hcc : CommRingCat.ofHom
106	        ((ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal kbar v).comp MvPolynomial.C) =
107	        (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of kbar)).inv := by
108	      apply CommRingCat.hom_ext
109	      ext r
110	      change (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of kbar)).inv.hom
111	          ((MvPolynomial.eval v) (MvPolynomial.C r)) = _
112	      rw [MvPolynomial.eval_C]
113	    calc _ = (Spec (CommRingCat.of kbar)).toSpecΓ ≫
114	            Spec.map (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of kbar)).inv := by
115	            exact congrArg _ (congrArg Spec.map hcc)
116	      _ = _ := AlgebraicGeometry.toSpecΓ_SpecMap_ΓSpecIso_inv _
117	
118	/-- The `k̄`-point `0 = [0 : 1] ∈ ℙ¹`, encoded as a section of `ProjectiveLineBar.hom` via
119	`Proj.fromOfGlobalSections` of the evaluation `X₀ ↦ 0`, `X₁ ↦ 1`. -/
120	noncomputable def ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
121	    𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar :=
122	  ProjectiveLineBar.pointOfVec kbar (fun i => if i = 0 then 0 else 1) 1 (by simp)
123	
124	/-- The `k̄`-point `1 = [1 : 1] ∈ ℙ¹`, encoded as a section via the evaluation
125	`X₀ ↦ 1`, `X₁ ↦ 1`. -/
126	noncomputable def ProjectiveLineBar.onePt (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
127	    𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar :=
128	  ProjectiveLineBar.pointOfVec kbar (fun _ => 1) 0 (by simp)
129	
130	/-- The `k̄`-point `∞ = [1 : 0] ∈ ℙ¹`, encoded as a section via the evaluation
131	`X₀ ↦ 1`, `X₁ ↦ 0`. -/
132	noncomputable def ProjectiveLineBar.inftyPt (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
133	    𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar :=
134	  ProjectiveLineBar.pointOfVec kbar (fun i => if i = 0 then 1 else 0) 0 (by simp)
135	
136	/-! ### (B) The additive group `𝔾_a` over `Spec k̄` -/
137	
138	/-- **The additive group `𝔾_a = 𝔸¹` over `Spec k̄` as an underlying scheme.** This is the
139	affine line `AffineSpace (Fin 1) (Spec (.of kbar))`. It is affine, locally of finite
140	presentation, and reduced (its global sections are `MvPolynomial (Fin 1) k̄`, a domain). -/
141	def GaScheme (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Scheme :=
142	  AffineSpace.{0, u} (Fin 1) (Spec (.of kbar))
143	
144	/-- The natural `Over (Spec (.of kbar))` instance on `GaScheme` (via
145	`AlgebraicGeometry.AffineSpace.over`). -/
146	instance gaScheme_canOver (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
147	    (GaScheme kbar).Over (Spec (.of kbar)) :=
148	  inferInstanceAs ((AffineSpace.{0, u} (Fin 1) (Spec (.of kbar))).Over (Spec (.of kbar)))
149	
150	/-- **The additive group object `𝔾_a` over `Spec k̄` as an object of
151	`Over (Spec (.of kbar))`.** -/
152	abbrev Ga (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Over (Spec (.of kbar)) :=
153	  (GaScheme kbar).asOver (Spec (.of kbar))
154	
155	/-- **`𝔾_a` is an affine morphism over `Spec k̄`.** FREE from
156	`AlgebraicGeometry.AffineSpace.instIsAffineHomOverSchemeInferInstanceOverClass`. -/
157	instance ga_isAffineHom (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
158	    IsAffineHom (Ga kbar).hom :=
159	  inferInstanceAs (IsAffineHom (AffineSpace.{0, u} (Fin 1) (Spec (.of kbar)) ↘
160	    (Spec (.of kbar))))
161	
162	/-- **`𝔾_a` is locally of finite presentation over `Spec k̄`.** FREE from
163	`AffineSpace.instLocallyOfFinitePresentation…OfFinite` (the index `Fin 1` is finite). -/
164	instance ga_locallyOfFinitePresentation (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
165	    LocallyOfFinitePresentation (Ga kbar).hom :=
166	  inferInstanceAs (LocallyOfFinitePresentation
167	    (AffineSpace.{0, u} (Fin 1) (Spec (.of kbar)) ↘ Spec (.of kbar)))
168	
169	/-- **`𝔾_a`'s underlying scheme is reduced.** Since the global sections are
170	`MvPolynomial (Fin 1) k̄`, a domain over a field, the affine scheme is reduced. The proof
171	transfers `IsReduced (Spec (.of (MvPolynomial _ _)))` (free for any reduced ring) across
172	`AffineSpace.isoOfIsAffine`. -/
173	instance ga_isReduced (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : IsReduced (Ga kbar).left :=
174	  isReduced_of_isOpenImmersion (AffineSpace.isoOfIsAffine (Fin 1) _).hom
175	
176	/-! ### (C) The multiplicative group `𝔾_m` over `Spec k̄` -/
177	
178	/-- **The ring `k̄[t, t⁻¹] = Localization.Away t`**, where `t = X () : MvPolynomial Unit k̄`.
179	This is the global-sections ring of `𝔾_m`. -/
180	abbrev GmRing (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Type u :=
181	  Localization.Away (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar)
182	
183	/-- **The multiplicative group `𝔾_m = Spec k̄[t, t⁻¹]` as an underlying scheme.** This is
184	the chosen affine encoding (the analogist `gm-scaling-p1` D2.b verdict: AFFINE `Spec`, NOT
185	the basic-open of `𝔸¹` — the latter loses `IsAffine`). -/
186	def GmScheme (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Scheme :=
187	  Spec (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar))
188	
189	/-- The natural `Over (Spec (.of kbar))` instance on `GmScheme`. -/
190	instance gmScheme_canOver (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
191	    (GmScheme kbar).Over (Spec (.of kbar)) where
192	  hom := Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap kbar (GmRing kbar)))
193	
194	/-- **The multiplicative group object `𝔾_m` over `Spec k̄` as an object of
195	`Over (Spec (.of kbar))`.** -/
196	abbrev Gm (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Over (Spec (.of kbar)) :=
197	  (GmScheme kbar).asOver (Spec (.of kbar))
198	
199	/-- **`𝔾_m` is affine.** -/
200	instance gm_isAffine (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : IsAffine (Gm kbar).left :=
201	  inferInstanceAs (IsAffine (Spec (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar))))
202	
203	/-- **`𝔾_m` is locally of finite presentation over `Spec k̄`.** Follows from
204	`Algebra.FinitePresentation k̄ (k̄[t, t⁻¹])` (a localization of a polynomial ring at a single
205	element is finitely presented), bridged through
206	`AlgebraicGeometry.HasRingHomProperty.Spec_iff` for the `LocallyOfFinitePresentation` /
207	`RingHom.FinitePresentation` correspondence. -/
208	instance gm_locallyOfFinitePresentation (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
209	    LocallyOfFinitePresentation (Gm kbar).hom :=
210	  (HasRingHomProperty.Spec_iff (P := @LocallyOfFinitePresentation)).mpr
211	    ((RingHom.finitePresentation_algebraMap (B := GmRing kbar)).mpr
212	      inferInstance)
213	
214	/-- **`𝔾_m`'s underlying scheme is reduced.** `k̄[t, t⁻¹]` is a localization of a domain at
215	a non-zero-divisor, hence a domain, hence reduced. The `IsReduced (Spec _)` instance picks
216	up directly from `IsReduced (Localization.Away _)`. -/
217	instance gm_isReduced (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : IsReduced (Gm kbar).left :=
218	  inferInstanceAs (IsReduced (Spec (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar))))

The chart-`1` ring map of `gmScalingP1_chart 1` sends `isLocElem ∈ Away 𝒜 X_1`

`(Proj.awayι X_1).appIso ⊤ .inv` to `[X_0/X_1] ∈ HomogeneousLocalization.Away 𝒜 X_1`

`[X_0/X_1] ↦ u`; finally, evaluation at the identity section `λ = 1` lands at
`1 ∈ kbar` after `Algebra.TensorProduct.lid` collapse.
