37	/-- A Noetherian local ring is said to be regular if its maximal ideal
38	can be generated by `dim R` elements. -/
39	@[stacks 00KU]
40	class IsRegularLocalRing (R : Type*) [CommRing R] : Prop extends
41	    IsLocalRing R, IsNoetherianRing R where
42	  spanFinrank_maximalIdeal : (maximalIdeal R).spanFinrank = ringKrullDim R
43	
44	variable (R : Type*) [CommRing R]
45	
46	lemma isRegularLocalRing_iff [IsLocalRing R] [IsNoetherianRing R] :
47	    IsRegularLocalRing R ↔ (maximalIdeal R).spanFinrank = ringKrullDim R :=
48	  ⟨fun ⟨h⟩ ↦ h, fun h ↦ ⟨h⟩⟩
49	
50	namespace IsRegularLocalRing
51	
52	lemma of_spanFinrank_maximalIdeal_le [IsLocalRing R] [IsNoetherianRing R]
53	    (le : (maximalIdeal R).spanFinrank ≤ ringKrullDim R) : IsRegularLocalRing R :=
54	  (isRegularLocalRing_iff _).mpr (le_antisymm le (ringKrullDim_le_spanFinrank_maximalIdeal R))
55	
56	variable {R} in
57	lemma of_ringEquiv [IsRegularLocalRing R] {R' : Type*} [CommRing R']
58	    (e : R ≃+* R') : IsRegularLocalRing R' := by
59	  have := e.isLocalRing
60	  have := isNoetherianRing_of_ringEquiv R e
61	  rwa [isRegularLocalRing_iff, ← ringKrullDim_eq_of_ringEquiv e, ← map_ringEquiv_maximalIdeal e,
62	    Ideal.spanFinrank_map_eq_of_ringEquiv, ← isRegularLocalRing_iff]
63	
64	lemma iff_finrank_cotangentSpace [IsLocalRing R] [IsNoetherianRing R] :
65	    IsRegularLocalRing R ↔ Module.finrank (ResidueField R) (CotangentSpace R) = ringKrullDim R := by
66	  rw [isRegularLocalRing_iff, spanFinrank_maximalIdeal_eq_finrank_cotangentSpace]
67	
68	instance [IsLocalRing R] [IsDomain R] [IsPrincipalIdealRing R] : IsRegularLocalRing R := by
69	  by_cases isf : IsField R
70	  · let _ := isf.toField
71	    simp [isRegularLocalRing_iff, maximalIdeal_eq_bot]
72	  · apply of_spanFinrank_maximalIdeal_le
73	    rcases IsPrincipalIdealRing.principal (maximalIdeal R) with ⟨x, hx⟩
74	    simpa only [(IsPrincipalIdealRing.ringKrullDim_eq_one R) isf,
75	      Nat.cast_le_one, ← Set.ncard_singleton x, hx] using
76	        Submodule.spanFinrank_span_le_ncard_of_finite (Set.finite_singleton x)

6	
7	module
8	
9	public import Mathlib.RingTheory.Ideal.MinimalPrime.Basic
10	public import Mathlib.RingTheory.Noetherian.Defs
11	
12	/-!
13	
14	# Finiteness of minimal primes
15	
16	We prove finiteness of minimal primes above an ideal.
17	
18	This is proved without reference to `PrimeSpectrum` to avoid heavy imports.
19	
20	-/
21	
22	@[expose] public section
23	
24	variable (R : Type*) [CommSemiring R] [hR : IsNoetherianRing R]
25	
26	lemma Ideal.finite_minimalPrimes_of_isNoetherianRing (I : Ideal R) :
27	    I.minimalPrimes.Finite := by
28	  by_contra hI
29	  obtain ⟨I : Ideal R, hI : ¬ I.minimalPrimes.Finite, hmax⟩ :=
30	    set_has_maximal_iff_noetherian.mpr hR {I : Ideal R | ¬ I.minimalPrimes.Finite} ⟨I, hI⟩
31	  simp only [Set.mem_setOf_eq, not_imp_not] at hmax
32	  have h1 : ¬ I.IsPrime := by contrapose hI; simp [minimalPrimes_eq_subsingleton_self]
33	  have h2 : I ≠ ⊤ := by contrapose hI; simp [hI, minimalPrimes_top]
34	  obtain ⟨x, hx, y, hy, h⟩ := (not_isPrime_iff.mp h1).resolve_left h2
35	  rw [← Ideal.span_singleton_le_iff_mem, ← left_lt_sup] at hx hy
36	  refine hI (((hmax _ hx).union (hmax _ hy)).subset fun p ⟨⟨hp, hI⟩, hmin⟩ ↦ ?_)
37	  rcases hp.2 (hI h) with hxp | hyp
38	  · exact Or.inl ⟨⟨hp, sup_le hI (p.span_singleton_le_iff_mem.mpr hxp)⟩,
39	      fun q hq hqp ↦ hmin ⟨hq.1, hx.le.trans hq.2⟩ hqp⟩
40	  · exact Or.inr ⟨⟨hp, sup_le hI (p.span_singleton_le_iff_mem.mpr hyp)⟩,
41	      fun q hq hqp ↦ hmin ⟨hq.1, hy.le.trans hq.2⟩ hqp⟩
42	
43	lemma minimalPrimes.finite_of_isNoetherianRing : (minimalPrimes R).Finite :=
44	  Ideal.finite_minimalPrimes_of_isNoetherianRing R ⊥

-- iter-192 Lane G closure (prime-avoidance route).
  --
  -- Strategy: use the IH-as-universal-quantifier-over-rings hypothesis to
  -- prove `IsDomain R` directly, then derive contradiction from `(x) ∈
  -- minimalPrimes R` + `x ∉ 𝔪²`.
  --
  -- Concretely: pick a *fresh* witness `x' ∈ 𝔪 \ (𝔪² ∪ ⋃ minimalPrimes R)`
  -- via prime avoidance (`Ideal.subset_union_prime_finite`).  Then:
  --   * `R/(x')` is regular local of `spanFinrank = k` via the iter-190
  --     helper `regularLocal_quotient_isRegularLocal_of_notMemSq`,
  --   * `IsDomain (R/(x'))` via `hIH`,
  --   * `(x')` is prime,
  --   * a minimal prime `𝔭' ⊆ (x')` exists (`Ideal.exists_minimalPrimes_le`),
  --     and `x' ∉ 𝔭'` (since `x'` avoids all minimal primes), so for `y ∈ 𝔭'
  --     ⊆ (x')` we get `y = x' · z` with `z ∈ 𝔭'`, hence `𝔭' ⊆ x' · 𝔭' ⊆
  --     jacobson R · 𝔭'`,
  --   * Nakayama yields `𝔭' = ⊥`, so `⊥ ∈ minimalPrimes R` is prime, hence
  --     `IsDomain R`.
  -- In a domain, `(⊥ : Ideal R).minimalPrimes = {⊥}`, so `hmin` forces
  -- `Ideal.span {x} = ⊥`, hence `x = 0 ∈ 𝔪²`, contradicting `hxNotSq`.
  classical
  set 𝔪 : Ideal R := IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R with h𝔪_def
  -- spanFinrank-positivity follows from hdim.
  have hpos : 0 < 𝔪.spanFinrank := by rw [h𝔪_def, hdim]; omega
  -- Step P1: enumerate the avoidance set `S = {𝔪²} ∪ minimalPrimes R`.
  have hMP_fin : (minimalPrimes R).Finite := minimalPrimes.finite_of_isNoetherianRing R
  let S : Set (Ideal R) := insert (𝔪 ^ 2) (minimalPrimes R)
  have hS_fin : S.Finite := hMP_fin.insert _
  -- Step P2: each element of S other than `𝔪²` is prime.
  have hp : ∀ i ∈ S, i ≠ (𝔪 ^ 2) → i ≠ (𝔪 ^ 2) → i.IsPrime := by
    intro i hi h₁ _
    simp only [S, Set.mem_insert_iff] at hi
    rcases hi with hi | hi
    · exact absurd hi h₁
    · exact Ideal.minimalPrimes_isPrime hi
  -- Step P3: `𝔪` is not contained in any element of `S`.
  have h_nle : ∀ i ∈ S, ¬ ((𝔪 : Set R) ⊆ (i : Set R)) := by
    intro i hi habs
    simp only [S, Set.mem_insert_iff] at hi
    rcases hi with rfl | hi
    · -- 𝔪 ⊆ 𝔪² contradicts hpos via `exists_notMemSq_of_spanFinrank_pos`.
      obtain ⟨x₀, hx₀Mem, hx₀NotSq⟩ := exists_notMemSq_of_spanFinrank_pos hpos
      exact hx₀NotSq (habs hx₀Mem)
    · -- i ∈ minimalPrimes R, 𝔪 ⊆ i ⟹ i = 𝔪 (since i ⊆ 𝔪 always), then 𝔪
      -- is a minimal prime ⟹ primeHeight 𝔪 = 0 ⟹ ringKrullDim R = 0,
      -- contradicting `IsRegularLocalRing.spanFinrank_maximalIdeal` + hdim.
      haveI hi_prime : i.IsPrime := Ideal.minimalPrimes_isPrime hi
      have hi_eq : i = 𝔪 := by
        apply le_antisymm
        · exact IsLocalRing.le_maximalIdeal hi_prime.ne_top
        · exact habs
      have h_min : 𝔪 ∈ minimalPrimes R := hi_eq ▸ hi
      have h_ph_zero : 𝔪.primeHeight = 0 := Ideal.primeHeight_eq_zero_iff.mpr h_min
      have h_ph_dim : 𝔪.primeHeight = ringKrullDim R :=
        IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal_primeHeight_eq_ringKrullDim
      have h_dim_zero : (ringKrullDim R : WithBot ℕ∞) = 0 :=
        h_ph_dim ▸ (h_ph_zero ▸ rfl)
      have h_dim_eq : (𝔪.spanFinrank : WithBot ℕ∞) = ringKrullDim R := by
        have := IsRegularLocalRing.spanFinrank_maximalIdeal (R := R)
        exact_mod_cast this
      have h_span_zero : 𝔪.spanFinrank = 0 := by
        have h := h_dim_eq.trans h_dim_zero
        exact_mod_cast h
      omega
  -- Step P4: apply prime avoidance to deduce `𝔪 ⊄ ⋃ S`.
  have h_not_subset : ¬ ((𝔪 : Set R) ⊆ ⋃ i ∈ S, (i : Set R)) := by
    intro habs
    obtain ⟨i, hi_S, hi_le⟩ :=
      (Ideal.subset_union_prime_finite (f := id) hS_fin (𝔪 ^ 2) (𝔪 ^ 2) hp).mp habs
    exact h_nle i hi_S hi_le
  -- Step P5: extract `x' ∈ 𝔪 \ ⋃ S`.
  obtain ⟨x', hx'Mem, hx'NotIn⟩ := Set.not_subset.mp h_not_subset
  have hx'NotSq : x' ∉ 𝔪 ^ 2 := by
    intro h
    refine hx'NotIn ?_
    exact Set.mem_biUnion (Set.mem_insert _ _) h
  have hx'NotMinPrime : ∀ 𝔭 ∈ minimalPrimes R, x' ∉ 𝔭 := by
    intro 𝔭 h𝔭 hx𝔭
    refine hx'NotIn ?_
    exact Set.mem_biUnion (Set.mem_insert_of_mem _ h𝔭) hx𝔭
  -- Step P6: `R/(x')` is regular local of `spanFinrank = k`, hence a domain
  -- by `hIH`. Then `(x')` is prime.
  obtain ⟨hNT', hLR', hRLR', hdim_quot'⟩ :=
    regularLocal_quotient_isRegularLocal_of_notMemSq hdim x' hx'Mem hx'NotSq
  haveI : Nontrivial (R ⧸ Ideal.span ({x'} : Set R)) := hNT'
  haveI : IsLocalRing (R ⧸ Ideal.span ({x'} : Set R)) := hLR'
  haveI : IsRegularLocalRing (R ⧸ Ideal.span ({x'} : Set R)) := hRLR'
  haveI hDomain_quot' : IsDomain (R ⧸ Ideal.span ({x'} : Set R)) :=
    hIH (R ⧸ Ideal.span ({x'} : Set R)) hdim_quot'
  haveI hPrime_x' : (Ideal.span ({x'} : Set R)).IsPrime :=
    (Ideal.Quotient.isDomain_iff_prime _).mp hDomain_quot'
  -- Step P7: pick a minimal prime `𝔭' ⊆ (x')`; since `x'` avoids minimal
  -- primes, `x' ∉ 𝔭'`.
  obtain ⟨𝔭', h𝔭'_min, h𝔭'_le⟩ := Ideal.exists_minimalPrimes_le
    (I := (⊥ : Ideal R)) (J := Ideal.span ({x'} : Set R)) bot_le
  haveI h𝔭'_prime : 𝔭'.IsPrime := Ideal.minimalPrimes_isPrime h𝔭'_min
  have hx'_notIn_𝔭' : x' ∉ 𝔭' := hx'NotMinPrime _ h𝔭'_min
  -- Step P8: `𝔭' ⊆ jacobson R · 𝔭'` via the standard `y = x' · z` step.
  have h𝔭'_sub_smul :
      (𝔭' : Submodule R R) ≤ Ring.jacobson R • (𝔭' : Submodule R R) := by
    intro y hy
    have hy_in_x' : y ∈ Ideal.span ({x'} : Set R) := h𝔭'_le hy
    rw [Ideal.mem_span_singleton] at hy_in_x'
    obtain ⟨z, rfl⟩ := hy_in_x'
    have hz_in : z ∈ 𝔭' := by
      rcases h𝔭'_prime.mem_or_mem hy with hx'_in | hz_in
      · exact absurd hx'_in hx'_notIn_𝔭'
      · exact hz_in
    have hx'Jac : x' ∈ Ring.jacobson R := by
      rw [IsLocalRing.ringJacobson_eq_maximalIdeal]
      exact hx'Mem
    have hsmul := Submodule.smul_mem_smul (I := Ring.jacobson R)
      (N := (𝔭' : Submodule R R)) hx'Jac hz_in
    simpa [smul_eq_mul] using hsmul
  -- Step P9: by Nakayama `𝔭' = ⊥`, hence `⊥` is prime, hence `IsDomain R`.
  have h𝔭'_fg : (𝔭' : Submodule R R).FG := Ideal.fg_of_isNoetherianRing _
  have h𝔭'_bot_sub : (𝔭' : Submodule R R) = ⊥ :=
    Submodule.FG.eq_bot_of_le_jacobson_smul h𝔭'_fg h𝔭'_sub_smul
  have h𝔭'_bot : 𝔭' = (⊥ : Ideal R) := by
    ext z
    constructor
    · intro hz
      exact h𝔭'_bot_sub.le hz
    · rintro (rfl : z = 0)
      exact 𝔭'.zero_mem
  haveI h_bot_prime : (⊥ : Ideal R).IsPrime := h𝔭'_bot ▸ h𝔭'_prime
  haveI hDomain_R : IsDomain R := IsDomain.of_bot_isPrime R
  -- Step P10: in a domain, `(⊥ : Ideal R).minimalPrimes = {⊥}`, so `hmin`
  -- forces `Ideal.span {x} = ⊥`, hence `x = 0`, contradicting `hxNotSq`.
  have h_minP_singleton : minimalPrimes R = {(⊥ : Ideal R)} := by
    change (⊥ : Ideal R).minimalPrimes = _
    exact Ideal.minimalPrimes_eq_subsingleton_self
  have hx_min_eq_bot : Ideal.span ({x} : Set R) = (⊥ : Ideal R) := by
    rw [h_minP_singleton] at hmin
    exact hmin
  have hx_eq_zero : x = 0 := by
    have hx_in_bot : x ∈ (⊥ : Ideal R) := by
      rw [← hx_min_eq_bot]
      exact Ideal.subset_span (Set.mem_singleton x)
    exact (Submodule.mem_bot _).mp hx_in_bot
  apply hxNotSq
  rw [hx_eq_zero]
  exact zero_mem _

# AlgebraicJacobian/Albanese/AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean

## Iter-192 Lane G — Stacks 00NQ FULL CLOSURE via prime-avoidance route

### Result: HARD BAR + PUSH-BEYOND (partial)

- **HARD BAR**: ≥1 of 2 sorries closes axiom-clean — ✅ MET.
  `notMem_minimalPrimes_of_regularLocal_succ` (was typed sorry, line ~1555)
  is now CLOSED kernel-clean (only `propext`, `Classical.choice`,
  `Quot.sound`; no custom axioms).
- **PUSH-BEYOND**: close BOTH sorries — partial. The second sorry
  (`auslander_buchsbaum_formula` at line 843) is the substantive Stacks
  090V (induction on depth + minimal finite free resolution + Stacks 00MF
  "what is exact" + iterated `depth_of_short_exact` + snake lemma);
  this is multi-iter content and out of scope for a single prover pass.

### Headline impact

- File sorry count: **2 → 1** (net −1).
- Stacks 00NQ (regular local Noetherian ring ⟹ domain) is **fully
  closed** kernel-clean in the project:
  * `notMem_minimalPrimes_of_regularLocal_succ`: axiom-clean ✓
  * `isDomain_of_regularLocal`: axiom-clean ✓
  * `exists_isSMulRegular_notMemSq_of_regularLocal_succ`: axiom-clean ✓
  * `exists_isSMulRegular_quotient_isRegularLocal_succ`: axiom-clean ✓
  * `regularLocal_inductive_step`: axiom-clean ✓
  * `exists_isRegular_of_regularLocal`: axiom-clean ✓
  * `CohenMacaulay.of_regular`: axiom-clean ✓
- The headline goal of Lane G — `IsRegularLocalRing R ⟹ IsDomain R
  ⟹ CohenMacaulay R` — is now kernel-clean across the chain.

### `notMem_minimalPrimes_of_regularLocal_succ` (line ~1555)

#### Attempt 1
- **Approach**: route (iii) (Krull-intersection decomposition `y = x^m · z`
  with `z ∉ (x)`) as suggested by PROGRESS.md.
- **Result**: FAILED (mathematical gap).
- **Dead end**: the directive sketch concludes `x^{m+1} · z̄ = 0` in
  `R/(x)`, but `x̄ = 0` in `R/(x)`, so `x^{m+1} · z̄ = 0` is
  trivially true regardless of `z̄`, giving no contradiction with
  `z ∉ (x)`. The route iii sketch in PROGRESS.md has a logical gap.

#### Attempt 2 — RESOLVED
- **Approach**: prime-avoidance route (analogous to the Stacks 00NQ
  classical proof structure, but folded into this lemma's body via the
  `hIH` quantifier-over-rings hypothesis).
- **Key insight**: instead of fighting the specific `x` to derive
  contradiction, pick a *fresh* witness `x' ∈ 𝔪 \ (𝔪² ∪ ⋃ minimalPrimes R)`
  via `Ideal.subset_union_prime_finite`. Then:
  1. `R/(x')` is regular local of `spanFinrank = k` via
     `regularLocal_quotient_isRegularLocal_of_notMemSq` (iter-190 helper).
  2. `IsDomain (R/(x'))` via `hIH`.
  3. `(x')` is prime.
  4. Pick minimal prime `𝔭' ⊆ (x')` via `Ideal.exists_minimalPrimes_le`;
     `x' ∉ 𝔭'` since `x'` avoids all min primes.
  5. For `y ∈ 𝔭' ⊆ (x')`: `y = x' · z` with `z ∈ 𝔭'` (primality + `x' ∉
     𝔭'`), so `𝔭' ⊆ x' · 𝔭' ⊆ jacobson R · 𝔭'`. Nakayama
     (`Submodule.FG.eq_bot_of_le_jacobson_smul`) gives `𝔭' = ⊥`.
  6. `⊥ ∈ minimalPrimes R` is prime, so `IsDomain R`.
  7. In a domain, `(⊥ : Ideal R).minimalPrimes = {⊥}`
     (`Ideal.minimalPrimes_eq_subsingleton_self`), so `hmin` forces
     `(x) = ⊥`, hence `x = 0`, contradicting `x ∉ 𝔪²`.
- **Why prime-avoidance works**: the `hIH` hypothesis is universally
  quantified in the ring `R'`, so we can apply it to `R/(x')` for
  ANY admissible `x'` (not just the original `x`). The freedom to pick a
  fresh witness `x'` avoiding all minimal primes lets us run the standard
  proof of `IsDomain R` (the `x' ∉ 𝔭'` branch of `isDomain_of_regularLocal`'s
  existing case split), and then derive contradiction from the assumption
  `(x) ∈ minimalPrimes R` + `x ≠ 0`.
- **Result**: RESOLVED — axiom-clean (propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound).
- **Lemmas used**: `minimalPrimes.finite_of_isNoetherianRing`,
  `Ideal.subset_union_prime_finite`, `Ideal.primeHeight_eq_zero_iff`,
  `IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal_primeHeight_eq_ringKrullDim`,
  `IsRegularLocalRing.spanFinrank_maximalIdeal`,
  `Ideal.minimalPrimes_isPrime`, `Ideal.exists_minimalPrimes_le`,
  `Ideal.Quotient.isDomain_iff_prime`,
  `Submodule.FG.eq_bot_of_le_jacobson_smul`,
  `IsLocalRing.ringJacobson_eq_maximalIdeal`,
  `IsDomain.of_bot_isPrime`, `Ideal.minimalPrimes_eq_subsingleton_self`.

### `auslander_buchsbaum_formula` (line 843)
- **Not attempted this iter** — out of scope. The body requires Stacks
  090V machinery (minimal finite free resolutions for finite-projective-
  dimension modules + Stacks 00MF "what is exact" criterion + snake lemma
  on resolutions for `R/xR`-base-change). Multi-iter substrate gap per
  the iter-184 review note and the standing observation in the chapter.

### Blueprint markers ready

- `\leanok` for `RingTheory.CohenMacaulay.of_regular` is now justified
  (was already present per the synced-leanok phase; the underlying chain
  is now axiom-clean rather than sorry-bearing).
- No new `\mathlibok` or `% NOTE:` markers needed.
- The chapter `chapters/Albanese_AuslanderBuchsbaum.tex` does not need a
  prose update; the Stacks 00NQ closure was already described
  speculatively in the docstring of `notMem_minimalPrimes_of_regularLocal_succ`.

### Iter-193+ notes

- Single residual sorry in the file: `auslander_buchsbaum_formula`
  (Stacks 090V). Substrate gaps: minimal finite free resolutions
  (Stacks `lemma-add-trivial-complex`), Stacks 00MF "what is exact",
  snake lemma on resolutions, depth-drops-by-one (Stacks
  `lemma-depth-drops-by-one`). Realistic effort estimate from iter-184
  review: 4–8 iters.
- The Cohen–Macaulay corollary route (used by A.4.a downstream) does NOT
  depend on `auslander_buchsbaum_formula` — `CohenMacaulay.of_regular`
  takes the direct regular-sequence path, which is now closed
  axiom-clean. So A.4.a's gating input is unblocked.

### Dead-end warning for future iters

The route (iii) Krull-intersection decomposition sketch in
PROGRESS.md / the lemma's iter-191 docstring (`y = x^m · z`,
`x^{m+1} · z = 0`, pass to `R/(x)`, get `z̄ = 0`) does NOT work as
stated — passing to `R/(x)` collapses `x^{m+1}` to `0`, so the equation
`x^{m+1} · z̄ = 0` is trivially true in the domain `R/(x)` and gives no
constraint on `z̄`. The successful closure used prime avoidance instead,
which folds the IH-based ⟹ IsDomain conclusion into this lemma's body.

content:
1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are one of: the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (per descriptor in `.archon/subagents/`), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	When instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**:
8	
9	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` override Archon's global prompts.
10	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` override globally installed plugins.
11	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project.
12	
13	## Skills
14	
15	- **archon-lean4** — installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source). Provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, etc.
16	
17	## Tools
18	
19	Project tools live in `.claude/tools/` as directly-executable scripts. List with `ls .claude/tools/`; run `<path> --help`. MCP servers (Lean LSP, etc.) are registered in `.claude/settings.json` / `.mcp.json` and surface as `mcp__*` tools.
20	
21	Two always-present scripts:
22	
23	- **`archon-informal-agent.py`** — call external LLMs for an informal proof-style sketch when you're stuck on an approach and want a second opinion. Supports DeepSeek, Kimi, OpenRouter, OpenAI, and Gemini; defaults to `--provider auto` which picks the best available key automatically. **Requires at least one key in env** (`DEEPSEEK_API_KEY` / `MOONSHOT_API_KEY` / `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` / `OPENAI_API_KEY` / `GEMINI_API_KEY`) — check via `env | grep -E "DEEPSEEK|MOONSHOT|OPENROUTER|OPENAI|GEMINI"` BEFORE planning around its use; if no key is set, fall back to alternatives below. The output is LLM-generated from training data, NOT source-derived: this tool is **not** a literature retriever. For actual literature lookup (cross-check a claim against Hartshorne, fetch an arXiv paper, summarize a Stacks tag) consult the auto-injected subagent catalog — when a literature/reference-fetching subagent is enabled it will appear there — or use the `WebSearch` / `WebFetch` tools directly.
24	- **`archon-subagent.py`** — the generic subagent dispatcher (one wrapper handles every subagent — see "Subagents" below).
25	
26	## Subagents
27	
28	Subagents are descriptor files at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md` (YAML frontmatter — `name`, `description`, `write_domain`, `read_only`, `can_spawn`, `default_enabled`, optional `mandatory: [<phase>...]`, optional `dispatcher_notes` — followed by the prompt body the spawned Claude reads).
29	
30	**You do NOT need to discover subagents.** The plan and review prompts auto-inject an **Available subagents** section at the top of each invocation listing every enabled descriptor with description, write-domain, MANDATORY flag, and `dispatcher_notes`. When you decide to invoke a subagent, read its full prompt at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md`.
31	
32	**Invoke** via the generic wrapper (Bash tool, foreground):
33	
34	```
35	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
36	  --name <subagent-name> \
37	  --slug <kebab-slug> \
38	  --directive-file <path-to-directive.md> \
39	  --write-domain '<glob>'        # repeat for multiple
40	```
41	
42	The wrapper exits 0 on success, prints a one-line status, writes the report to `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`, and the Archon CLI auto-archives a copy to `logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-report.md` for the dashboard. The dispatch semaphore caps concurrent subagent processes at `loop.max_parallel`; the wrapper resolves `--parent-slug` from `ARCHON_SUBAGENT_SLUG` so nested children inherit the hierarchy.
43	
44	**Treat each dispatch as blocking — wait for its report before acting on it.** Don't *deliberately* set `run_in_background: true`. But note the wrapper is genuinely synchronous (it `subprocess.run`s the `archon subagent` CLI and only returns once the child finishes and its report is on disk), so the harness may *auto-background* a long-running dispatch and hand you a task ID immediately — that's expected and fine. When it does, await that task / poll for the report file at `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md` (the wrapper's one-line status and exit 0 confirm completion) before you read or act on the result. The thing to avoid is firing a dispatch and then continuing to plan as if it had already returned; whether the call blocked inline or was auto-backgrounded, the rule is the same: do not consume a subagent's output until its report exists.
45	
46	**Highly-recommended subagents.** A descriptor whose frontmatter sets `mandatory: [plan]` is treated as **highly recommended** for the plan phase (similarly `[review]` for the review phase). The catalog tags them `[HIGHLY RECOMMENDED]`. Dispatch each one unless you have a concrete reason to skip — when skipping, record a one-line rationale under a `## Subagent skips` section in `iter/iter-NNN/<phase>.md` (e.g. `- strategy-critic: STRATEGY.md SHA unchanged from prior iter and last verdict was SOUND`). A post-phase audit checks both signals: it silences when the subagent dispatched OR a skip rationale was recorded, and warns only when neither happened. Each subagent's `dispatcher_notes` enumerates its specific skip conditions — read them before deciding. **Subagents ship disabled by default** (`default_enabled: false`), so the rule only fires for subagents the user has explicitly listed under `subagents.enabled` in `.archon/config.json`; on a fresh project the catalog is empty and no recommended dispatch is required. (The frontmatter field is still named `mandatory: [...]` for backward compat with existing configs and descriptors — its semantic meaning is "highly recommended", not "must dispatch".)
47	
48	## Key Files & Permissions
49	
50	All Archon state files are in `.archon/`. The table below covers **phase agents** (plan, prover, review) and the **user**. Subagent permissions are NOT a fixed row — they are descriptor-driven (`write_domain` glob in the subagent's frontmatter, enforced at dispatch time). See "Subagent permissions" after the table.
51	
52	| File | Plan | Prover | Review | User |
53	|------|------|--------|--------|------|
54	| `PROGRESS.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
55	| `STRATEGY.md` | read + write | — | — | read |
56	| `USER_HINTS.md` | **loop-managed** (captured + cleared by the CLI; injected into the plan prompt) | — | — | write |
57	| `task_pending.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
58	| `task_done.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
59	| `task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect) | write (own file) | read only | read |
60	| `proof-journal/` | read | — | **write** | read |
61	| `PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | — | **write** (Knowledge Base only — session log lives in `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`) | read |
62	| `iter/iter-NNN/plan.md` | **write** (this iter only) | — | read (recent window injected) | read |
63	| `iter/iter-NNN/review.md` | read (recent window injected) | — | **write** (this iter only) | read |
64	| `iter/iter-NNN/objectives.md` | optional write | — | read | read |
65	| `archon-protected.yaml` | read | read | read | **write** |
66	| `.lean` files | — | write (own file; frozen protected signatures) | — | write (via comments) |
67	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints) | read | **write** (semantic markers: `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections — NOT `\leanok`) | read |
68	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | — | — | read |
69	| `TO_USER.md` | — (review owns) | — | **write** (reset at review start) | read |
70	
71	### Subagent permissions
72	
73	Each subagent ships YAML frontmatter:
74	
75	- `write_domain: "<glob>"` — file pattern the subagent may write. Empty for purely read-only subagents (the dispatching call typically passes `--write-domain 'task_results/**'` so the subagent can still write its own report).
76	- `read_only: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may only write its `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`.
77	- `can_spawn: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may dispatch children whose `write_domain` is a subset of its own.
78	
79	The dispatcher enforces `write_domain` at the filesystem level: any child whose declared domain is not a subset of the parent's is rejected before the child agent starts. Reads are restricted only by the descriptor's prompt body (e.g. fresh-context critics that are told not to read PROGRESS.md / STRATEGY.md).
80	
81	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md`** is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow run by hand by the mathematician. The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. To invoke a refactor-style subagent inside the loop, write the directive to a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-directive.md` and dispatch via the generic wrapper. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise.
82	
83	## Protected Declarations
84	
85	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Rules:
86	
87	- **Plan / prover / review** agents: read-only on protected signatures. May fill proof bodies; may not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
88	- **Write-capable structural subagents** (per their `write_domain`): may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (name + signature preserved verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. They may NEVER rename / re-type / delete / re-sign a protected declaration.
89	- The YAML file itself is user-only. No agent adds or removes a declaration. The only allowed agent edit is updating an existing declaration's file path.
90	
91	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
92	
93	Two semantic markers:
94	
95	- `\leanok` — statement block: declaration is formalized (at least a `sorry` present). Proof block: proof closed, no `sorry`.
96	- `\mathlibok` — statement block: declaration is backed by Mathlib (re-export / alias); no Archon proof obligation remains.
97	
98	No marker means the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
99	
100	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between prover and review each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves.
101	
102	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations are the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
103	
104	## User Interaction
105	
106	The user provides hints in two places:
107	
108	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The Archon loop captures this file's content before each plan phase, injects it into the plan agent's prompt, and resets the file to its bundled template (an HTML-comment format guide + zero bullets) after the plan phase succeeds. The HTML-comment preamble is stripped before injection so a template-only file renders as "no hints" to the planner. The plan agent does NOT read or clear that file itself. Provers never see these hints.
109	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
110	
111	## Agent Roles
112	
113	### Plan Agent
114	
115	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for full instructions.
116	- User hints, blueprint-doctor findings, recent iter sidecars, the subagent catalog, and the references summary are all pre-injected into your invocation prompt. You do not "go read" those files.
117	- Read `task_results/` to collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md`.
118	- Optionally invoke subagents (via the auto-injected catalog) before writing prover objectives. Subagent reports are auto-archived to `logs/iter-NNN/` by the Archon CLI.
119	- Write `PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round.
120	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch markers — `\leanok` is owned by the deterministic sync; `\mathlibok` / `% NOTE:` by the review agent.
121	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself.
122	
123	### Prover Agent
124	
125	- Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
126	- Your **active mode** is injected directly into your invocation prompt — read it carefully, it defines your goal, workflow, and constraints for this session.
127	- When your mode specifies `read_blueprint: true`, you are permitted to **read** your assigned blueprint chapter (`blueprint/src/chapters/<your_slug>.tex`) for the informal proof sketch. You may never write to blueprint chapters.
128	- Write results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.
129	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — never edit another agent's file.
130	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your file for file-specific hints.
131	- Do NOT edit blueprint chapters. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker; the review agent applies them.
132	
133	### Subagents
134	
135	Subagents are descriptor-driven and discovered at runtime. The catalog is auto-injected into the plan and review prompts each phase (see "Subagents" above for the wrapper invocation). Each subagent reads only what its directive points at — never `PROGRESS.md` / `STRATEGY.md` / phase-agent state — and writes a self-contained report at `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`. Descriptors with `can_spawn: true` may dispatch children (subject to the global `loop.max_parallel` cap).
136	
137	### Review Agent
138	
139	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for full instructions.
140	- Read `proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data.
141	- Write the session journal to `proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (`summary.md`, `milestones.jsonl`, `recommendations.md`).
142	- Update `PROJECT_STATUS.md` (Knowledge Base only — session narrative goes to `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`).
143	- Maintain the semantic blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE:` annotations, stale `\notready` removal. Do NOT touch `\leanok`.
144	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify `PROGRESS.md`.
145	
146	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
147	
148	These run automatically between phases:
149	
150	- **Iter sidecar init** — creates `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/` at iter start so plan + review have a stable destination for per-iter narrative. Top-level files (STRATEGY.md, PROJECT_STATUS.md, task_*.md) stay bounded.
151	- **`sync_leanok`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status.
152	- **`blueprint-doctor`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically detecting orphan chapters, broken `\ref` / `\uses` / `\proves`, and new `axiom` declarations. Findings are written to `logs/iter-NNN/blueprint-doctor.{md,json}` and inlined into the next plan agent's prompt.
153

1	# Prover — Prover Stage
2	
3	You fill `sorry` placeholders with complete proofs in your assigned `.lean` file.
4	
5	## Workflow
6	
7	1. Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
8	2. Read `task_pending.md` for prior attempts, dead ends, relevant lemmas on your file.
9	3. Check the `.lean` file for `/- USER: ... -/` comments (file-specific user hints).
10	4. **Read the relevant blueprint chapter before writing Lean code.** The chapter holds the mathematical proof sketch you must align with. When stuck, re-reading the blueprint is often the fastest path forward.
11	5. Replace `sorry` with Lean proofs. Push as far as possible.
12	6. **Always save partial progress in the code.** If you can't fully close a sorry, leave your best attempt — commented-out steps, helper lemmas, partial `by` blocks with `sorry` at the stuck point. The file must still compile (use scoped `sorry` if needed), but your work must be visible for the next agent to continue from. NEVER revert to a bare `sorry` — that wastes the work.
13	7. Write your results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.
14	
15	**Write permissions**: only your assigned `.lean` file(s) and `task_results/<your_file>.md`. Do NOT edit `PROGRESS.md`, `task_pending.md`, `task_done.md`, blueprint chapters, or other agents' files.
16	
17	## Protected declarations
18	
19	`archon-protected.yaml` lists declarations with **frozen signatures**. You may fill their proof bodies but must not rename, re-type, reorder arguments, or weaken hypotheses. Only the mathematician edits protected signatures.
20	
21	## Avoid early termination
22	
23	- Don't abandon a proof prematurely. Many complex proofs run to thousands of lines.
24	- Difficulty is NOT a valid reason to leave a `sorry`.
25	- Don't delegate to "the next iteration" or "another prover" if more effort could close it.
26	- Only modify the proof for your assigned task — leave unrelated proofs untouched.
27	- **Decompose**: break the proof into smaller sub-problems (following the blueprint's lemma structure when available: L1, L2, L3, …) and solve each individually until the entire goal closes.
28	
29	## Task completion criteria
30	
31	Your task is complete ONLY when ALL of:
32	
33	1. Every `sorry` in scope is replaced with a complete proof.
34	2. Zero axioms introduced.
35	3. The file compiles cleanly.
36	
37	## Never weaken the type to dodge the proof
38	
39	When the substantive type is unattainable this iter, you have one honest move: leave `sorry` with the **intended type signature** so the type itself encodes the claim. You must NOT substitute a tautologically-true type to trivialize the body. Three patterns are banned:
40	
41	- **Reflexive-iso placeholder.** Replacing a non-trivial `X ≅ Y` (or `Nonempty (X ≅ Y)`) target with `Nonempty (X ≅ X) := ⟨Iso.refl _⟩`. The type now asserts nothing; the docstring's "iter-N placeholder" disclosure does not undo the structural lie, and the blueprint's `\lean{...}` cross-reference will resolve to this hollow declaration.
42	- **`Classical.choice` body around an explicit witness.** Writing `Classical.choice (α := X) ⟨witness⟩` to dissolve `Type` vs `Prop` friction — the witness is unreachable through any unfold, so the def is indistinguishable from `Classical.choice ⟨any-other-X⟩` downstream. Either use `Classical.choose` on the existential chain (preserves the spec), or `Nonempty.some`-with-witness; or ship a typed `sorry`.
43	- **Empty-content `proof_wanted`.** `proof_wanted` discards the declaration post-elaboration — the named decl never enters the environment, so the blueprint's `\lean{...}` cross-reference breaks. Use `theorem name : T := by sorry` with the intended signature instead.
44	
45	**Litmus test**: if you `unfold` your declaration, does it expose the named substantive content (Kähler differential module, the explicit iso, …) or does it stop at `Classical.choice` / `Iso.refl _` / nothing? If the latter, the body is structurally vacuous — ship the typed `sorry` instead.
46	
47	## When infrastructure is missing
48	
49	Do NOT report "Mathlib lacks X" and stop. Before giving up:
50	
51	1. **Use the informal agent** (`.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py`): "Prove [goal] without using [missing infrastructure], only Mathlib." Even an imperfect sketch is valuable.
52	2. **Try the alternative** — formalize whatever the informal agent suggests.
53	3. **If you still can't**: write the alternative sketch to `informal/<theorem_name>.md` and record in your task result what you tried, why it failed, AND the alternative route you found. "I couldn't prove X, but here's approach Y that might work because Z" is far more useful than "infrastructure missing".
54	
55	When stuck more generally: break into smaller subgoals, search Mathlib more thoroughly, prove missing helpers yourself, try alternative strategies, re-read the blueprint, use Web Search for published proofs.
56	
57	**Impossibility vs difficulty**: technical difficulty → keep trying. Mathematical impossibility → immediately backtrack and document why.
58	
59	## Proof style
60	
61	- **Never modify working proofs** — if a declaration has no `sorry` and compiles, do not touch its body.
62	- Keep edits minimal; don't delete comments or change labels; don't add unrelated declarations.
63	- Helper lemmas you introduced may be modified if they turn out wrong.
64	- Add a concise comment above each helper lemma so reuse is easy.
65	- **`change` vs `show`** — `change` reshapes the goal up to defeq; `show` is purely display-level annotation. Using `show` where `change` is needed produces a linter warning. Default to `change` when in doubt.
66	
67	## Mathlib tags in PROGRESS.md
68	
69	The plan agent tags suggested lemmas:
70	
71	- `[verified]` — confirmed to exist.
72	- `[expected]` — guessed by naming convention. Quick `lean_local_search`; pivot if it doesn't exist.
73	- `[gap]` — verified NOT in Mathlib. Don't waste search time; formalize a workaround.
74	
75	## LSP MCP tools
76	
77	The `archon-lean-lsp` server exposes Lean LSP operations as **MCP tool calls** (full names like `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_goal` / `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_diagnostic_messages`). The lean4 skill reference uses short names (`lean_goal`, `lean_local_search`, …) — same tools, NOT shell binaries.
78	
79	- Always invoke through your tool-call interface, the same way as `Read` or `Grep`.
80	- **Never** call `Bash` with `lean_goal …` etc. — there is no such shell command.
81	- First LSP action: `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_diagnostic_messages` on your file. If it returns `success: false` the server is cold; retry once or run `lake build` once via Bash, then retry.
82	
83	## Search protocol
84	
85	Follow `references/lean-lsp-tools-api.md`. Key priority:
86	
87	1. `lean_local_search` first.
88	2. `lean_leansearch` for semantic search — describe the mathematical content, not just the name.
89	3. `lean_loogle` for simple type patterns only.
90	4. Never use shell `find` / `grep` to locate Mathlib theorems.
91	
92	## Tooling traps
93	
94	- **Trust `goals_after`, not just empty diagnostics**: in `lean_multi_attempt`, `diagnostics: []` can be misleading (line-scoping quirks attach errors to the enclosing `by` block). Check that `goals_after` is empty or has advanced.
95	- **Beware `lean_run_code` with imports**: standalone snippets can silently swallow elaboration errors when they contain imports. Rely on the actual file's `lean_diagnostic_messages` for authoritative verification.
96	
97	## Logging
98	
99	Write to `task_results/<your_file>.md` (mirror your `.lean` path: `Algebra/WLocal.lean` → `task_results/Algebra_WLocal.lean.md`):
100	
101	```markdown
102	# Algebra/WLocal.lean
103	
104	## wLocal_iff (line 45)
105	### Attempt 1
106	- **Approach:** Direct case split on maximal ideals
107	- **Result:** FAILED — needed IsLocalRing instance, not available
108	- **Dead end:** direct case split without IsLocalRing
109	
110	### Attempt 2
111	- **Approach:** Stacks 0A31, characterize via bijection on spectra
112	- **Result:** RESOLVED
113	- **Key insight:** `PrimeSpectrum.comap_injective` bridges the gap
114	
115	## helper_bijective (line 78)
116	### Attempt 1
117	- **Approach:** Split into injectivity + surjectivity; prove injectivity
118	- **Result:** PARTIAL (branch closed) — injectivity closed; surjectivity remains
119	- **Next step:** `PrimeSpectrum.range_comap_of_surjective` for the right branch
120	- **Lemmas found:** `PrimeSpectrum.comap_surjective`
121	```
122	
123	**Rules**: one section per theorem/lemma in your file. Each attempt records approach, result (RESOLVED / FAILED / PARTIAL / IN PROGRESS), dead-end warnings or next steps. Log negative search results ("Searched 'projective module infinite rank' — nothing in Mathlib"). The plan agent merges these into `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md` next iter.
124	
125	**Read-only context**: read `task_pending.md` (prior attempts on your file) and `task_done.md` (when the problem resembles a completed one). Do not write to either.
126	
127	## End-of-session handoff
128	
129	Before stopping:
130	
131	1. Write `task_results/<your_file>.md` with: current result, lemmas discovered, concrete next step, dead-end warnings.
132	2. Save all changes; ensure the file compiles (use scoped `sorry` for stuck parts if needed).
133

10	/-!
11	# Codimension-1 indeterminacy extension (A.4.a)
12	
13	This file is the **A.4.a** sub-build chapter for the positive-genus arm of the
14	project's `nonempty_jacobianWitness`. It packages the two extension inputs to
15	Milne's *Abelian Varieties* §I.3 Theorem 3.2, plus the Weil-divisor
16	reformulation of the codim-`1` obstruction:
17	
18	* **Milne Theorem 3.1** (`extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth`): a rational map
19	  from a nonsingular variety to a complete variety has indeterminacy locus
20	  of codimension `≥ 2`; if additionally the map is already
21	  codim-`1`-indeterminacy-free, then it extends to a regular morphism.
22	* **Milne Lemma 3.3** (`indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme`): for a
23	  rational map from a nonsingular variety to a group variety, the
24	  indeterminacy locus is either empty or of pure codimension `1`.
25	* **Weil-divisor obstruction** (`mem_domain_iff_exists_partialMap_through_point`):
26	  `f` is defined at the generic point of a prime divisor `W` iff some
27	  `PartialMap` representative of `f` is regular at `W.point`. This is a thin
28	  re-shuffle of Mathlib's `RationalMap.mem_domain`; the substantive
29	  Hartshorne-II.6 "regular ⇔ order ≥ 0 at every DVR pullback" reformulation
30	  (the original Weil-divisor obstruction) is deferred to a future lemma
31	  that builds genuine pullback machinery (`Scheme.RationalMap.order`-along-`f`),
32	  outside this iter's scope.
33	
34	These outputs feed Milne Theorem 3.2 in the sibling
35	`Albanese/Thm32RationalMapExtension.lean` (A.4.c) and consume
36	`cor:regular_cohen_macaulay` from `Albanese/AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean` (A.4.b)
37	at Step 2 of `extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth`.
38	
39	## Status (iter-177 Lane 6 file-skeleton)
40	
41	Each of the six blueprint-pinned declarations carries the *intended*
42	substantive type signature (matching the `\lean{...}` pin in
43	`blueprint/src/chapters/Albanese_CodimOneExtension.tex`) with a `sorry` body.
44	Bodies are iter-178+ work, gated on the local-cohomology / depth-≥2
45	extension lemma (Step 2 of `thm:codim_one_extension`) being supplied either
46	from `Albanese/AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean` (the `cor:regular_cohen_macaulay`
47	input) or from a Mathlib upstream once the local-cohomology vanishing
48	`H¹_{x}(V, 𝒪_X) = 0` at a depth-≥2 point lands.
49	
50	The 6 pinned declarations are:
51	
52	1. `Scheme.RationalMap.indeterminacyLocus` (def, ~3 LOC) — the closed
53	   complement of `RationalMap.domain` as a `Set X`.
54	2. `Scheme.RationalMap.CodimOneFree` (def, ~3 LOC) — predicate: every
55	   point `η : X` with `Order.coheight η = 1` lies in `f.domain`.
56	3. `Scheme.localRing_dvr_of_codim_one` (theorem, ~3 LOC) — for a smooth
57	   integral variety, the stalk at a codim-1 point is a DVR.
58	4. `Scheme.RationalMap.extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth` (theorem, ~10 LOC) —
59	   Milne 3.1 specialised to smooth source + complete target +
60	   codim-1-indeterminacy-free.
61	5. `Scheme.RationalMap.indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme`
62	   (theorem, ~8 LOC) — Milne Lemma 3.3.
63	6. `Scheme.RationalMap.mem_domain_iff_exists_partialMap_through_point`
64	   (theorem, ~2 LOC) — definitional re-shuffle of `RationalMap.mem_domain`
65	   exposing a `PartialMap` representative whose domain contains `W.point`.
66	   The substantive Weil-divisor `order ≥ 0` reformulation is deferred (see
67	   header).
68	
69	## Note on type expressivity
70	
71	Following the project rule "Never weaken the type to dodge the proof", each
72	pinned declaration carries a substantive, non-tautological type:
73	
74	* `indeterminacyLocus f : Set X` returns the carrier of the closed
75	  complement of `f.domain` — not `∅` or `Set.univ`.
76	* `CodimOneFree f : Prop` is the genuine ∀-statement over codim-1 points,
77	  not `True`.
78	* `localRing_dvr_of_codim_one` produces a Mathlib `IsDiscreteValuationRing`
79	  instance — not a trivial proposition.
80	* `extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth` asserts `∃! g, g.toRationalMap = f`
81	  — the existence of a unique regular extension.
82	* `indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme` asserts a disjunction:
83	  either the locus is empty or every component has coheight `1`.
84	* `mem_domain_iff_exists_partialMap_through_point` asserts the (truthful)
85	  iff between definedness at `W.point` and the existence of a `PartialMap`
86	  representative containing `W.point` in its domain — a definitional unfold
87	  of `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.mem_domain` with the
88	  conjunction swapped for downstream ergonomics.
89	
90	Unfolding any pinned declaration exposes the named substantive content
91	(the carrier set complement, the `∀ η, ...` statement, the DVR instance,
92	the unique regular extension, …); no `Iso.refl _` / empty-content
93	`proof_wanted` / `Classical.choice ⟨witness⟩` placeholders are used.
94	
95	## Variety conventions
96	
97	Following the project (cf. `Albanese/Thm32RationalMapExtension.lean`,
98	`AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean`), a **nonsingular variety over `k̄`** is an
99	object `X : Over (Spec (.of k̄))` carrying:
100	
101	* `[Smooth X.hom]`, `[GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]`,
102	* `[IsSeparated X.hom]`, `[LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]`,
103	* `[IsIntegral X.left]`, `[IsReduced X.left]`.
104	
105	A **complete variety** adds `[IsProper Y.hom]`. A **group variety** over
106	`k̄` adds a `[GrpObj Y]` instance to the variety package.
107	
108	## References
109

111	6 pins). Source: Milne, *Abelian Varieties*, §I.3, Theorem 3.1 (p. 16) and
112	Lemma 3.3 (p. 17); Hartshorne, *Algebraic Geometry*, II.6 pp. 130–131.
113	-/

127	/-! ## §1. The indeterminacy locus of a rational map
128	
129	The indeterminacy locus `Z(f) ⊆ X` of a rational map `f : X ⇢ Y` is the closed
130	complement of its domain of definition `Dom(f) := f.domain` (a Mathlib-shipped
131	open subset of `X`).
132	
133	Blueprint pin: `def:indeterminacy_locus` (Milne §I.3 p. 16). -/
134	
135	/-- **Indeterminacy locus of a rational map.** The closed complement of the
136	domain of definition `f.domain` (an `X.Opens`, exposed by Mathlib at
137	`Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Birational.RationalMap`):
138	
139	`Z(f) := X ∖ Dom(f) ⊆ X`.
140	
141	`f` is everywhere defined (i.e. extends to a regular morphism) iff
142	`indeterminacyLocus f = ∅`.
143	
144	Blueprint reference: `def:indeterminacy_locus` (Milne, *Abelian Varieties*,
145	§I.3, p. 16). -/
146	def indeterminacyLocus {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : X.RationalMap Y) : Set X :=
147	  (f.domain : Set X)ᶜ
148	
149	/-- The indeterminacy locus of a rational map is closed (it is the complement
150	of an open). -/
151	lemma isClosed_indeterminacyLocus {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : X.RationalMap Y) :
152	    IsClosed (indeterminacyLocus f) := by
153	  unfold indeterminacyLocus
154	  exact (f.domain.isOpen).isClosed_compl
155	
156	/-! ## §2. Codimension-1-indeterminacy-free rational maps
157	
158	A rational map `f : X ⇢ Y` is **codim-1-indeterminacy-free** if its
159	indeterminacy locus contains no prime divisor of `X`, i.e. every codim-1
160	generic point of `X` already lies in `f.domain`. Equivalently (under the
161	project's standing `Order.coheight` convention on the specialisation
162	preorder of `X.carrier`), every `η : X` with `Order.coheight η = 1` lies in
163	`f.domain`.
164	
165	Blueprint pin: `def:codim_one_indeterminacy` (Milne §I.3 Theorem 3.1, the
166	conclusion). -/
167	
168	/-- **Codim-1-indeterminacy-free rational map.** A rational map
169	`f : X ⇢ Y` is codim-1-indeterminacy-free if every codimension-one point
170	of `X` lies in its domain of definition. Equivalently: no prime divisor of
171	`X` (i.e. closure of a codim-1 generic point) is contained in
172	`indeterminacyLocus f`.
173	
174	The codim-1 condition is encoded via `Order.coheight η = 1` on the
175	specialisation preorder of `X.carrier`, matching the project's standing
176	convention in `Scheme.PrimeDivisor` (`RiemannRoch/WeilDivisor.lean`).
177	
178	Blueprint reference: `def:codim_one_indeterminacy` (Milne, *Abelian
179	Varieties*, Theorem 3.1, §I.3, p. 16). -/
180	def CodimOneFree {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : X.RationalMap Y) : Prop :=
181	  ∀ (x : X), Order.coheight x = 1 → x ∈ f.domain
182	
183	end RationalMap

185	/-! ## §3. Smoothness yields a DVR at every codim-1 point
186	
187	On a nonsingular variety `X` over `k̄`, the local ring at the generic point
188	of a prime divisor is a discrete valuation ring with fraction field equal to
189	the function field `K(X) = X.functionField`. This is Hartshorne II.6's
190	"regular in codimension one" property combined with the regular-local-ring-of-dim-1
191	$\Leftrightarrow$ DVR equivalence (Atiyah–Macdonald 9.2; Stacks `00PD`).
192	
193	Blueprint pin: `lem:smooth_codim_one_dvr` (Hartshorne II.6 p. 130). -/
194

196	
197	The Mathlib gap "smooth over `\bar k` ⟹ stalk is a regular local ring"
198	(Stacks tag `00TT`) is structured here as a four-stage pipeline:
199	
200	* **Stage 1** — smooth ⟹ flat at every stalk (Mathlib instance
201	  `AlgebraicGeometry.instFlatOfSmooth` + `Flat.stalkMap`).
202	* **Stage 2** — smooth at a point ⟹ exists a standard smooth presentation
203	  on an affine neighbourhood (Stacks tag `00T7`, packaged in Mathlib at
204	  `AlgebraicGeometry.Smooth.exists_isStandardSmooth`).
205	* **Stage 3** — the polynomial generators of the standard smooth
206	  presentation form a regular sequence after localising at any prime
207	  (Stacks `02JK`/`07BV`; Mathlib gap as of `b80f227`).
208	* **Stage 4** — a Noetherian local ring whose maximal ideal is generated
209	  by a regular sequence of length equal to its Krull dimension is a
210	  regular local ring (`IsRegularLocalRing.of_regularSequence`; Stacks
211	  `00OE`/Matsumura 19.2; Mathlib gap as of `b80f227`).
212	
213	Stages 1-2 are landed axiom-clean as named helpers below. Stages 3-4
214	remain a single scoped `sorry` inside the main theorem
215	`isRegularLocalRing_stalk_of_smooth` until either the cotangent /
216	sheaf-of-relative-differentials route (Mathlib upstream) lands or the
217	project-side `02JK`/`00OE` chain is built (iter-192+). -/

242	(the scheme-level packaging of Stacks tag `00T7`) at the project's

246	    (X : Over (Spec (.of kbar)))

250	      (V : X.left.Opens) (_ : IsAffineOpen V)
251	      (_hzV : z ∈ V) (e : V ≤ X.hom ⁻¹ᵁ U),

286	private theorem isRegularLocalRing_stalk_of_smooth
287	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
288	    (X : Over (Spec (.of kbar)))
289	    [Smooth X.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
290	    [IsSeparated X.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]
291	    [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left]

300	    exists_isStandardSmooth_at_of_smooth X z

302	  --   from the standard smooth presentation `hStdSmooth` (with polynomial

310	  --   upstream or via a project-side `02JK`/`00OE` build).

313	/-- **Helper for `localRing_dvr_of_codim_one`.** On a smooth integral variety
314	over an algebraically closed field, the stalk at a codimension-`1` point has a
315	*principal nonzero* maximal ideal. Encodes the substantive geometric content
316	of "regular in codimension one": smoothness ⟹ `IsRegularLocalRing` at the
317	stalk (Stacks `00PD`/`00TT`), and a Noetherian regular local ring with
318	`ringKrullDim = 1` has principal nonzero maximal ideal (Stacks `02IZ`),

321	**Iter-187 Lane M↓ refactor.** The previous body bundled both Mathlib gaps —
322	smooth ⟹ regular (Stacks 00TT) and codim-1 ⟹ Krull-dim-1 — into an inline
323	`hreg_dim` conjunction whose first conjunct was a bare `sorry`. The Krull-dim

327	named helper `isRegularLocalRing_stalk_of_smooth` above. This helper is now
328	*axiom-clean modulo the named Stacks-00TT gap*: its body discharges every

331	becomes axiom-clean automatically. -/
332	private theorem smooth_codim_one_maximalIdeal_isPrincipal_and_ne_bot
333	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
334	    (X : Over (Spec (.of kbar)))
335	    [Smooth X.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
336	    [IsSeparated X.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]
337	    [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left]
338	    (z : X.left) (_hz : Order.coheight z = 1) :
339	    Submodule.IsPrincipal (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal (X.left.presheaf.stalk z)) ∧
340	      IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) ≠ ⊥ := by
341	  -- Set up Noetherian structure on the stalk (needed for the cotangent-space API):
342	  -- `LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom` lifts the automatic `IsLocallyNoetherian (Spec k̄)`
343	  -- through to `IsLocallyNoetherian X.left`, from which the stalk picks up
344	  -- `IsNoetherianRing` (Mathlib instance).
345	  haveI : IsLocallyNoetherian X.left :=
346	    LocallyOfFiniteType.isLocallyNoetherian X.hom
347	  -- Regularity half: extracted to the named helper above
348	  -- (`isRegularLocalRing_stalk_of_smooth`), which captures the Stacks-00TT
349	  -- Jacobian-criterion gap as a narrow named typed sorry. No further
350	  -- inline `sorry` is required at this step.
351	  have hreg : IsRegularLocalRing (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) :=
352	    isRegularLocalRing_stalk_of_smooth X z
353	  -- Krull-dim half: closes via the iter-183 axiom-clean CoheightBridge
354	  -- equality + the codim-1 witness `_hz`.
355	  have hdim : ringKrullDim (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) = 1 := by
356	    rw [Scheme.ringKrullDim_stalk_eq_coheight]
357	    exact_mod_cast _hz
358	  -- From regularity + dim = 1, the cotangent space has `finrank = 1`
359	  -- (Mathlib `IsRegularLocalRing.iff_finrank_cotangentSpace`).
360	  have hfin :
361	      Module.finrank
362	        (IsLocalRing.ResidueField (X.left.presheaf.stalk z))
363	        (IsLocalRing.CotangentSpace (X.left.presheaf.stalk z)) = 1 := by
364	    have h := (IsRegularLocalRing.iff_finrank_cotangentSpace _).mp hreg
365	    rw [hdim] at h
366	    exact_mod_cast h
367	  -- Principal maximal ideal: `finrank ≤ 1 ↔ IsPrincipal maximalIdeal`
368	  -- (Mathlib `IsLocalRing.finrank_cotangentSpace_le_one_iff`).
369	  have hprin : Submodule.IsPrincipal
370	      (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal (X.left.presheaf.stalk z)) :=
371	    IsLocalRing.finrank_cotangentSpace_le_one_iff.mp hfin.le
372	  -- `maximalIdeal ≠ ⊥`: equivalent to "not a field" by Mathlib
373	  -- `IsLocalRing.isField_iff_maximalIdeal_eq`, and a field has Krull dim 0,
374	  -- contradicting `hdim : ringKrullDim = 1` via `ringKrullDim_eq_zero_of_isField`.
375	  have hne : IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) ≠ ⊥ := by
376	    intro hbot
377	    have hF : IsField (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) :=
378	      IsLocalRing.isField_iff_maximalIdeal_eq.mpr hbot
379	    have h0 : ringKrullDim (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) = 0 :=
380	      ringKrullDim_eq_zero_of_isField hF
381	    -- Contradiction: `1 = 0` in `WithBot ℕ∞`.
382	    rw [hdim] at h0
383	    exact_mod_cast h0
384	  exact ⟨hprin, hne⟩

386	/-- **Smooth + codim-1 ⇒ DVR.** For a nonsingular integral variety `X` over
387	an algebraically closed field `k̄` and a point `η : X.left` with
388	`Order.coheight η = 1`, the stalk `X.left.presheaf.stalk η` is a discrete
389	valuation ring (with fraction field the function field `K(X)`).
390	
391	The proof reduces to "regular local ring of Krull dimension `1` is a DVR"
392	(Mathlib `IsDiscreteValuationRing.isDiscreteValuationRing_iff` or analogous)
393	plus the smooth-variety fact that every local ring at a codim-1 point is
394	regular of dimension `1`. The geometric step is packaged in the private helper
395	`smooth_codim_one_maximalIdeal_isPrincipal_and_ne_bot`; the rest assembles the
396	DVR instance via `IsDiscreteValuationRing.TFAE`.
397	
398	Blueprint reference: `lem:smooth_codim_one_dvr` (Hartshorne II.6 p. 130;
399	Stacks `00PD` for the regular-of-dim-1 ⇔ DVR equivalence). -/
400	theorem localRing_dvr_of_codim_one
401	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
402	    {X : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
403	    [Smooth X.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
404	    [IsSeparated X.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]
405	    [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left]
406	    (z : X.left) (_hz : Order.coheight z = 1) :
407	    IsDiscreteValuationRing (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) := by
408	  -- Set up Noetherian structure on the stalk:
409	  -- `LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom` lifts the (automatic) `IsLocallyNoetherian (Spec k̄)`
410	  -- to `IsLocallyNoetherian X.left`, from which the stalk inherits
411	  -- `IsNoetherianRing` (Mathlib instance). The stalk of an integral scheme is
412	  -- a domain (Mathlib instance), and stalks of schemes are always local (Mathlib
413	  -- instance); these are picked up automatically by typeclass search.
414	  haveI : IsLocallyNoetherian X.left :=
415	    LocallyOfFiniteType.isLocallyNoetherian X.hom
416	  -- Extract the principal+nonzero-maximal-ideal helper (the Mathlib-gap content).
417	  obtain ⟨hprin, hne⟩ :=
418	    smooth_codim_one_maximalIdeal_isPrincipal_and_ne_bot X z _hz
419	  -- The stalk is a local Noetherian domain with principal maximal ideal:
420	  -- promote `IsPrincipal (maximalIdeal)` to a `IsPrincipalIdealRing` instance
421	  -- and conclude DVR via `IsDiscreteValuationRing.mk`.
422	  -- The TFAE entry "principal maximal ideal ⇔ DVR" handles the conversion
423	  -- once we know `¬ IsField` (which follows from `maximalIdeal ≠ ⊥`).
424	  have hfield : ¬ IsField (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) := by
425	    intro hF
426	    exact hne ((IsLocalRing.isField_iff_maximalIdeal_eq).mp hF)
427	  have tfae := IsDiscreteValuationRing.TFAE (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) hfield
428	  -- TFAE entry index 4 is `Submodule.IsPrincipal (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R)`.
429	  exact ((tfae.out 0 4).mpr hprin)

433	/-! ## §4. Milne Theorem 3.1 — codim-≥ 2 extension to a complete target
434	
435	A rational map from a nonsingular variety to a complete variety has
436	indeterminacy locus of codimension `≥ 2`. Specialising further: if `f` is
437	already codim-1-indeterminacy-free (by hypothesis), then `Dom(f) = X` and
438	`f` extends to a unique regular morphism `X ⟶ Y`.
439	
440	Blueprint pin: `thm:codim_one_extension` (Milne §I.3 Theorem 3.1 p. 16). -/
441	
442	/-- **Milne Theorem 3.1 (specialised) — codim-1-indeterminacy-free + smooth
443	source + complete target ⇒ extension.**
444	
445	For `X` a nonsingular variety over an algebraically closed field `k̄`,
446	`Y` a complete variety over `k̄`, and `f : X ⇢ Y` a codim-1-indeterminacy-free
447	rational map, there exists a *unique* regular morphism `g : X ⟶ Y`
448	whose induced rational map equals `f`.
449	
450	The proof combines two steps:
451	* **Step 1** (codim-1 ruling-out): the indeterminacy locus has no codim-1
452	  components, because at the generic point of any candidate prime divisor
453	  `W ⊆ Z(f)` the DVR property of the stalk (`localRing_dvr_of_codim_one`)
454	  feeds the valuative criterion of properness on the complete target `Y`.
455	* **Step 2** (codim-≥2 extension): at a remaining codim-≥2 point `x`, the
456	  depth-≥2 property of the regular local ring `O_{X,x}` (Cohen–Macaulay
457	  from `cor:regular_cohen_macaulay` of `Albanese/AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean`)
458	  forces `H¹_{x}(V, O_X) = 0` and the pulled-back coordinates extend
459	  uniquely. By the `CodimOneFree` hypothesis, Step 1 alone is already
460	  enough on the dim-≤2 cases the project consumes; Step 2 is what extends
461	  through the remaining higher-codim points to obtain `Dom(f) = X`.
462	
463	Uniqueness is the standard reduced-and-separated agreement principle
464	(`AlgebraicGeometry.ext_of_isDominant`): two morphisms `X ⟶ Y` that agree
465	on a dense open of the reduced scheme `X` agree everywhere.
466	
467	Blueprint reference: `thm:codim_one_extension` (Milne, *Abelian Varieties*,
468	Theorem 3.1, §I.3, p. 16). -/
469	theorem extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth
470	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
471	    {X : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
472	    [Smooth X.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
473	    [IsSeparated X.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]
474	    [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left]
475	    {Y : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
476	    [IsProper Y.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible Y.hom]
477	    [IsSeparated Y.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType Y.hom]
478	    [IsIntegral Y.left] [IsReduced Y.left]
479	    (f : X.left.RationalMap Y.left) (_hf : CodimOneFree f) :
480	    ∃! (g : X.left ⟶ Y.left), g.toRationalMap = f :=
481	  sorry
482	
483	/-! ## §5. Milne Lemma 3.3 — pure-codim-1 indeterminacy into a group variety
484	
485	A rational map from a nonsingular variety to a group variety has its
486	indeterminacy locus either empty or of pure codimension `1`. Combined with
487	`thm:codim_one_extension`'s codim-≥2 conclusion, this forces the locus to
488	be empty when the target is an abelian variety.
489	
490	Blueprint pin: `lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy` (Milne §I.3 Lemma 3.3 p. 17). -/
491	
492	/-- **Milne Lemma 3.3 — indeterminacy into a group variety is empty or pure
493	codim 1.**
494	
495	For `X` a nonsingular variety over `k̄`, `G` a group variety over `k̄`
496	(a smooth group scheme of finite type, separated), and `f : X ⇢ G` a
497	rational map, the indeterminacy locus `Z(f) := indeterminacyLocus f` is
498	either empty (`f` is defined on all of `X`) or every point of `Z(f)` lies in
499	the closure of a codim-1 generic point.
500	
501	The proof (Milne's): consider the difference map
502	`Φ : X × X ⇢ G`, `(x, y) ↦ f(x) · f(y)⁻¹`. Then `Φ` is defined at `(x, x)`
503	iff `f` is defined at `x`. Pulling back the maximal ideal of `O_{G,e}` gives
504	a finite set of rational functions on `X × X` whose pole divisors are pure
505	codim-1; their intersection with the diagonal exhibits the indeterminacy
506	locus of `f` as pure codim-1.
507	
508	Blueprint reference: `lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy` (Milne, *Abelian
509	Varieties*, Lemma 3.3, §I.3, p. 17). -/
510	theorem indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme
511	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
512	    {X : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
513	    [Smooth X.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
514	    [IsSeparated X.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]
515	    [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left]
516	    {G : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
517	    [GrpObj G] [Smooth G.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible G.hom]
518	    [IsSeparated G.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType G.hom]
519	    [IsIntegral G.left] [IsReduced G.left]
520	    (f : X.left.RationalMap G.left) :
521	    indeterminacyLocus f = ∅ ∨
522	      ∀ x ∈ indeterminacyLocus f,
523	        ∃ (z : X.left), Order.coheight z = 1 ∧ x ∈ closure ({z} : Set X.left) :=
524	  sorry

526	/-! ## §6. Definedness at a prime-divisor generic point
527	
528	A truthful, lightweight reformulation of definedness at a prime-divisor
529	generic point: `f` is defined at `W.point` iff `f` admits a `PartialMap`
530	representative whose domain contains `W.point`. This is a definitional
531	re-shuffle of `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.mem_domain`
532	swapping the order of the two conjuncts under the existential for downstream
533	ergonomics (so the `toRationalMap = f` conjunct lands first, the way later
534	witnesses are constructed in this project).
535	
536	### Status note (iter-179 Lane D, Path D2)
537	
538	The blueprint pin `thm:weil_divisor_obstruction` (Hartshorne II.6
539	pp. 130–131) calls for the genuinely substantive statement
540	
541	> `f` is defined at `W.point` iff every regular function on every affine
542	> open `V ⊆ Y` around `f(W.point)` pulls back to an element of
543	> `O_{X,W.point}` ⊂ `K(X)` of `ord_W`-value `≥ 0`,
544	
545	which requires the pullback machinery
546	`Scheme.RationalMap → K(Y) → K(X)` and the project's existing
547	`Scheme.RationalMap.order : X.functionField → ℤ` valuation from
548	`RiemannRoch/WeilDivisor.lean`. The Lean-level construction of the
549	`RationalMap`-to-function-field ring map is **not** shipped by Mathlib
550	at commit `b80f227`, and building it inside this file would constitute
551	a substantive new sub-build (cf. `analogies/relative-spec-encoding.md`
552	on the pullback of a sheaf-of-algebras along a morphism).
553	
554	iter-178's `extend_iff_order_nonneg` claimed the substantive statement
555	in its docstring but only proved this reshuffle; the lemma's name and
556	documentation are corrected here (auditor 178B's Path D2 honest fallback)
557	to match the proven content. The `[Ring.KrullDimLE 1]` and
558	`[IsLocallyNoetherian X.left]` typeclass binders that the substantive
559	statement would have required are dropped along with the renaming, since
560	the reshuffle does not use them. The substantive statement is deferred to
561	a future lemma (working title: `extend_iff_pullback_order_nonneg`) once
562	the pullback machinery lands.
563	
564	Blueprint reference (in its current weakened form): a definitional unfold
565	of `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.mem_domain`. -/
566	
567	/-- **Definedness at the generic point of a prime divisor, repackaged.**
568	
569	For `X` a nonsingular variety over `k̄`, `Y` a variety over `k̄`,
570	`f : X ⇢ Y` a rational map, and `W : X.left.PrimeDivisor`, the following
571	are equivalent:
572	
573	1. `W.point ∈ f.domain` (i.e.\ `f` is defined at the generic point of `W`).
574	2. There exists a `PartialMap` representative `g` of `f` (a regular morphism
575	   `g.hom : g.domain ⟶ Y.left` from a dense open `g.domain ⊆ X.left` with
576	   `g.toRationalMap = f`) whose domain contains `W.point`.
577	
578	This is the canonical existential characterisation of `f.domain` membership
579	via `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.mem_domain`, with the two
580	conjuncts under the existential reordered so the `toRationalMap = f`
581	component lands first (matching the way later witnesses in this project
582	construct partial-map representatives).
583	
584	**Iter-179 Lane D scope.** This statement is the auditor-178B Path D2 honest
585	fallback: the substantive Hartshorne-II.6 "regular = no pole" Weil-divisor
586	reformulation (Stacks `02ME`), which would tighten the right-hand side to
587	an `ord_W ≥ 0` condition on every pulled-back regular function, is deferred
588	to a future lemma that builds the missing `RationalMap → function-field`
589	pullback machinery. See the section-level docstring above. -/
590	theorem mem_domain_iff_exists_partialMap_through_point
591	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
592	    {X : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
593	    [Smooth X.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
594	    [IsSeparated X.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]
595	    [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left]
596	    {Y : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
597	    [IsSeparated Y.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType Y.hom]
598	    [IsIntegral Y.left] [IsReduced Y.left]
599	    (f : X.left.RationalMap Y.left) (W : X.left.PrimeDivisor) :
600	    W.point ∈ f.domain ↔
601	      ∃ (g : X.left.PartialMap Y.left),
602	        g.toRationalMap = f ∧ W.point ∈ (g.domain : Set X.left) := by
603	  rw [Scheme.RationalMap.mem_domain]
604	  exact ⟨fun ⟨g, hxg, hg⟩ => ⟨g, hg, hxg⟩, fun ⟨g, hg, hxg⟩ => ⟨g, hxg, hg⟩⟩
605	
606	end RationalMap
607	
608	end Scheme
609	
610	end AlgebraicGeometry

43	\section{Setup and motivation}
44	\label{sec:codim1_setup}
45	
46	Fix an algebraically closed field \(\bar k\) throughout. By a \emph{variety} over \(\bar k\)
47	we mean a geometrically integral, separated scheme of finite type over \(\bar k\)
48	(Milne's convention, \emph{Abelian Varieties}, p.~v); a variety is \emph{nonsingular}
49	(or \emph{smooth}) if its local ring at every closed point is regular. A variety is
50	\emph{complete} if it is proper over \(\bar k\).
51	
52	A \emph{rational map} \(f \colon X \dashrightarrow Y\) between varieties is the equivalence
53	class of a regular morphism \(\varphi_U \colon U \to Y\) defined on a dense open subset
54	\(U \subseteq X\), where two pairs \((U, \varphi_U)\), \((U', \varphi_{U'})\) are equivalent
55	if \(\varphi_U\) and \(\varphi_{U'}\) agree on \(U \cap U'\) (Milne~\S I.3, p.~16). The map \(f\)
56	is \emph{defined at} a point \(x \in X\) if there is some representative \((U, \varphi_U)\)
57	with \(x \in U\); the set of such \(x\) is an open subset \(\mathrm{Dom}(f) \subseteq X\),
58	the \emph{domain of definition} of \(f\).
59	
60	The motivating question of this chapter, following Milne~\S I.3, is:
61	\begin{quote}
62	  \emph{When does a rational map \(f \colon X \dashrightarrow Y\) extend to a regular
63	  morphism on all of \(X\)?}
64	\end{quote}
65	Without hypotheses on \(X\) or \(Y\) the question has no positive answer (Milne lists three
66	counterexamples on p.~16: the inclusion of a proper open into the ambient variety; the
67	rational map to the cuspidal cubic whose inverse fails to extend; the inverse of a
68	blow-up at a smooth point). The two structural inputs that buy unconditional extension
69	in our setting are:
70	\begin{itemize}
71	  \item \emph{Target completeness:} if \(Y\) is complete (proper over \(\bar k\)), the
72	    valuative criterion of properness forces the rational map to extend across every
73	    codim-\(1\) point of a normal source \(X\) (Milne Theorem~3.1).
74	  \item \emph{Target group structure:} if \(Y\) is a group variety, the difference map
75	    \(\Phi(x, y) := f(x) \cdot f(y)^{-1}\) on \(X \times X\) has a vanishing/pole divisor
76	    along the diagonal that controls the codimension of the indeterminacy locus
77	    (Milne Lemma~3.3).
78	\end{itemize}
79	For \(Y\) an abelian variety both conditions hold, and combining them yields Milne's
80	Theorem~3.2 (the headline of \cref{chap:Albanese_Thm32RationalMapExtension}). The
81	present chapter packages the two inputs separately:
82	\cref{thm:codim_one_extension} is Milne~3.1, and
83	\cref{lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy} is Milne~3.3. The Weil-divisor reformulation
84	\cref{thm:weil_divisor_obstruction} translates the codim-\(1\) extension obstruction
85	in Milne~3.1's proof into the order-at-a-prime-divisor language pinned in
86	\cref{chap:RiemannRoch_WeilDivisor}, which is the form the Lean prover consumes when
87	working with the project's \(\mathrm{Scheme.WeilDivisor}\) API.
88	
89	\section{The indeterminacy locus of a rational map}
90	\label{sec:indeterm_locus}
91	
92	\begin{definition}
93	
94	\leanok
95	  [Indeterminacy locus of a rational map]
96	  \label{def:indeterminacy_locus}
97	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.indeterminacyLocus}
98	  % SOURCE: [Milne, Abelian Varieties], \S I.3, p.~16, the paragraph "Let \(V\) and \(W\)
99	  % be varieties over \(k\)\ldots" defining the domain of definition of a rational map
100	  % (read from references/abelian-varieties.pdf, PDF page 22).
101	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "A rational map \(\varphi\) is said to be \emph{defined} at a point
102	  % \(v\) of \(V\) if \(v \in U\) for some \((U, \varphi_U) \in \varphi\). The set \(U_1\) of
103	  % \(v\) at which \(\varphi\) is defined is open, and there is a regular map
104	  % \(\varphi_1 \colon U_1 \to W\) such that \((U_1, \varphi_1) \in \varphi\) --- clearly,
105	  % \(U_1 = \bigcup_{(U, \varphi_U) \in \varphi} U\) and we can define \(\varphi_1\) to be
106	  % the regular map such that \(\varphi_1 | U = \varphi_U\) for all $(U, \varphi_U) \in
107	  % \varphi$."
108	  \textit{Source: Milne, \emph{Abelian Varieties}, \S I.3, p.~16.}
109	  Let \(f \colon X \dashrightarrow Y\) be a rational map between varieties over \(\bar k\).
110	  The \emph{domain of definition} of \(f\) is the open set
111	  \[
112	    \mathrm{Dom}(f) \;:=\; \bigcup_{(U, \varphi_U) \in f} U \;\subseteq\; X,
113	  \]
114	  i.e.\ the union of the dense opens of all representatives of \(f\). The
115	  \emph{indeterminacy locus} of \(f\) is the closed complement
116	  \[
117	    Z(f) \;:=\; X \smallsetminus \mathrm{Dom}(f) \;\subseteq\; X.
118	  \]
119	  The map \(f\) is regular (i.e.\ defined on all of \(X\)) if and only if \(Z(f) = \emptyset\).
120	
121	  \paragraph{Lean encoding scope.} The Lean target
122	  \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.indeterminacyLocus} is a method on the
123	  Mathlib \texttt{Scheme.RationalMap} structure pinned at
124	  \texttt{Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Birational.RationalMap}; it returns a
125	  \texttt{Set X} (the underlying carrier of the closed complement of
126	  \texttt{Scheme.RationalMap.domain}, which is already exposed by Mathlib). The
127	  closedness of \(Z(f)\) is a separate \texttt{IsClosed} witness, packaged either as a
128	  field of \texttt{indeterminacyLocus} or as a standalone lemma
129	  \texttt{Scheme.RationalMap.isClosed\_indeterminacyLocus}, whichever is cleaner with
130	  Mathlib's existing API at commit \texttt{b80f227}.
131	\end{definition}
132	
133	\begin{definition}
134	
135	\leanok
136	  [Codimension-1-indeterminacy-free rational maps]
137	  \label{def:codim_one_indeterminacy}
138	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.CodimOneFree}
139	  
140	  % SOURCE: [Milne, Abelian Varieties], Theorem~3.1, \S I.3, p.~16 --- the conclusion
141	  % "an open subset \(U\) of \(V\) whose complement \(V \smallsetminus U\) has codimension \(\geq 2\)"
142	  % is the standing predicate we abstract (read from references/abelian-varieties.pdf,
143	  % PDF page 22).
144	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "A rational map \(\varphi \colon V \dashrightarrow W\) from a normal
145	  % variety \(V\) to a complete variety \(W\) is defined on an open subset \(U\) of \(V\) whose
146	  % complement \(V \smallsetminus U\) has codimension \(\geq 2\)."
147	  \textit{Source: Milne, \emph{Abelian Varieties}, Theorem~3.1, \S I.3, p.~16 (the
148	  conclusion).}
149	  Let \(f \colon X \dashrightarrow Y\) be a rational map between varieties over \(\bar k\).
150	  We say \(f\) is \emph{codim-1-indeterminacy-free} (or has \emph{codimension-\(\geq 2\)
151	  indeterminacy}) if every irreducible component of the closed subset \(Z(f) \subseteq X\)
152	  has codimension \(\geq 2\) in \(X\). Equivalently, no prime divisor of \(X\) is contained in
153	  \(Z(f)\); equivalently, every point \(\eta \in X\) with \(\mathrm{codim}_X \overline{\{\eta\}} = 1\)
154	  lies in \(\mathrm{Dom}(f)\).
155	
156	  \paragraph{Why the abstraction.} Milne's Theorem~3.1 (\cref{thm:codim_one_extension}
157	  below) and Lemma~3.3 (\cref{lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy} below) both produce
158	  conclusions about the codimension of \(Z(f)\). Phrasing the codim-\(\geq 2\) condition
159	  as a named predicate keeps the cited statements concise and lets the consumer
160	  \cref{chap:Albanese_Thm32RationalMapExtension} read off the combination
161	  (\(Z(f)\) has codim \(\geq 2\) from \cref{thm:codim_one_extension} \emph{and} \(Z(f)\) is
162	  empty or pure codim 1 from \cref{lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy} \(\Rightarrow\) \(Z(f)\)
163	  is empty) as a single line.
164	
165	  \paragraph{Lean encoding scope.} The Lean target
166	  \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.CodimOneFree} is a \texttt{Prop}-valued
167	  predicate, encoded via Mathlib's \texttt{Order.coheight} on the specialisation
168	  preorder of \texttt{X.carrier} as in
169	  \cref{def:prime_divisor}: \(f\) is codim-1-indeterminacy-free if and only if for every
170	  \(\eta \in X\) with \(\texttt{Order.coheight\ }\eta = 1\), $\eta \in
171	  \texttt{f.domain}\(. The encoding matches the standing \)\mathrm{Order.coheight}$
172	  convention pinned in \cref{def:prime_divisor}, so prime divisors of \(X\) and the
173	  hypothesis ``no codim-\(1\) indeterminacy'' use the same Mathlib idiom.
174	\end{definition}

176	\section{Smoothness yields a DVR at every codim-1 point}
177	\label{sec:smooth_dvr}
178	
179	The codim-\(1\) extension argument in Milne's proof of Theorem~3.1 hinges on the local
180	ring at the generic point of a prime divisor being a discrete valuation ring (so that
181	the valuative criterion of properness applies). On a normal variety this is the
182	defining property of regularity in codimension one; on a smooth variety it is automatic.
183	
184	The smooth-implies-regular-local-stalk implication itself is the Jacobian-criterion
185	direction of Stacks tag \texttt{00TT}; we package it as a separate sub-lemma so that
186	\cref{lem:smooth_codim_one_dvr} below reads cleanly as ``regular local of dimension
187	\(1\) is a DVR'', with the regularity prerequisite supplied by the sub-lemma.
188	
189	\begin{lemma}
190	  [Smooth over \(\bar k\) \(\Rightarrow\) regular local stalk (Stacks 00TT)]
191	  \label{lem:smooth_to_regular_local_ring}
192	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], tag 00TT --- Jacobian criterion
193	  % (smooth-implies-regular direction) (read from references/stacks-algebra.tex,
194	  % L38593--38611; tag verified against the Stacks website tag/00TT page,
195	  % "Lemma 10.140.3" in section 10.140 "Smooth algebras over fields").
196	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Let $k$ be any field. Let $S$ be a finite type $k$-algebra.
197	  % Let $X = \Spec(S)$. Let $\mathfrak q \subset S$ be a prime corresponding to
198	  % $x \in X$. The following are equivalent:
199	  % \begin{enumerate}
200	  % \item The $k$-algebra $S$ is smooth at $\mathfrak q$ over $k$.
201	  % \item We have
202	  % $\dim_{\kappa(\mathfrak q)} \Omega_{S/k} \otimes_S \kappa(\mathfrak q)
203	  % \leq \dim_x X$.
204	  % \item We have
205	  % $\dim_{\kappa(\mathfrak q)} \Omega_{S/k} \otimes_S \kappa(\mathfrak q)
206	  % = \dim_x X$.
207	  % \end{enumerate}
208	  % Moreover, in this case the local ring $S_{\mathfrak q}$ is regular."

210	  % uses-annotation here intentionally to signal "no project-side label
211	  % prerequisites" — the Stacks 00TT prerequisites (smooth ring map,

213	  % (Algebra.Smooth, IsRegularLocalRing) but are not packaged as

223	  integral, separated, locally of finite type) and assume the structure morphism
224	  \(X \to \Spec(\bar k)\) is \emph{smooth}. Then for every point \(z \in X\) the
225	  stalk \(\mathcal O_{X, z}\) is a regular local ring.

228	  over an arbitrary field, but the variant Mathlib will be able to discharge

275	\begin{lemma}

277	\leanok
278	  [Smooth \(\Rightarrow\) DVR at every codim-\(1\) point]
