280	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.localRing_dvr_of_codim_one}
281	  % SOURCE: [Hartshorne], II.6, p.~130 (the definition ``regular in codimension one'',
282	  % together with the immediately preceding remark that on a nonsingular variety every
283	  % local ring is regular) (read from references/hartshorne-algebraic-geometry.pdf,
284	  % PDF page 147).
285	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "\textbf{Definition.} We say a scheme \(X\) is \emph{regular in
286	  % codimension one} (or sometimes \emph{nonsingular in codimension one}) if every
287	  % local ring \(\mathcal O_x\) of \(X\) of dimension one is regular."
288	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "The most important examples of such schemes are nonsingular
289	  % varieties over a field (I, \S5) and noetherian normal schemes. On a nonsingular
290	  % variety the local ring of every closed point is regular (I, 5.1), hence all the
291	  % local rings are regular, since they are localizations of the local rings of
292	  % closed points."
293	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "If \(Y\) is a prime divisor on \(X\), let \(\eta \in Y\) be its generic

295	  % valuation ring with quotient field \(K\), the function field of \(X\)."
296	  \textit{Source: Hartshorne, II.6, p.~130 (definition of regular in codimension one;
297	  the DVR consequence at a codim-1 point).}
298	  Let \(X\) be a nonsingular variety over \(\bar k\) and let \(\eta \in X\) be a point with
299	  \(\mathrm{codim}_X \overline{\{\eta\}} = 1\). Then the local ring \(\mathcal O_{X, \eta}\) is a
300	  discrete valuation ring with fraction field the function field \(K(X)\) of \(X\).
301	\end{lemma}
302	
303	\begin{proof}
304	
305	\leanok
306	  % NOTE (iter-185 writer): the `\leanok` marker on this proof block reflects
307	  % the Krull-dim half (Tier-1 axiom-clean closure achieved iter-184 via
308	  % `Scheme.ringKrullDim_stalk_eq_coheight` from the iter-183
309	  % `Albanese/CoheightBridge.lean`, see
310	  % `\cref{chap:Albanese_CoheightBridge}`). The `IsRegularLocalRing` half is
311	  % tracked separately as the Stacks 00TT gap and is now packaged in
312	  % `\cref{lem:smooth_to_regular_local_ring}` above; that block carries no
313	  % `\leanok` because the Mathlib hooks (i)--(iv) in its proof sketch are not
314	  % all axiom-clean at commit `b80f227`. The composition (00TT regularity +
315	  % iter-183 Krull-dim bridge) inside the private Lean helper
316	  % `smooth_codim_one_maximalIdeal_isPrincipal_and_ne_bot`
317	  % (`AlgebraicJacobian/Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean:223`) keeps a single
318	  % typed `sorry` on the regularity half pending an iter-185+ prover round.
319	  By \cref{lem:smooth_to_regular_local_ring} (Stacks tag \texttt{00TT}, the
320	  Jacobian-criterion direction), the stalk \(\mathcal O_{X, x}\) is regular for
321	  every point \(x \in X\) --- in particular at every closed point (the case
322	  Hartshorne~I.5.1 states classically). Localising a regular local ring at a
323	  prime ideal yields a regular local ring; in particular, for \(\eta \in X\) with
324	  \(\mathrm{codim}_X \overline{\{\eta\}} = 1\), the local ring
325	  \(\mathcal O_{X, \eta}\) is regular of Krull dimension \(1\). A regular local ring of Krull dimension \(1\) is exactly a
326	  discrete valuation ring (Atiyah--Macdonald, Proposition 9.2; or Stacks tag
327	  \texttt{00PD}). Its fraction field is the function field \(K(X)\) of \(X\) by
328	  irreducibility of \(X\) (the unique generic point of \(X\) lies above \(\eta\), and the
329	  stalk at the generic point of an integral scheme is the function field).
330	
331	  \paragraph{Lean encoding scope.} The Lean target
332	  \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.localRing\_dvr\_of\_codim\_one} is the
333	  instance/lemma stating that \(\mathcal O_{X, \eta}\) is a Mathlib
334	  \texttt{IsDiscreteValuationRing} under the typeclass set
335	  \texttt{[Smooth\,(\dots)\,X.hom]\ [IsIntegral X.left]} and the hypothesis
336	  \texttt{Order.coheight\ \(\eta\) = 1}. The fraction-field identification with
337	  \texttt{X.functionField} is a downstream named instance
338	  \texttt{Scheme.fractionRing\_localRing\_eq\_functionField} (or whatever Mathlib name
339	  the prover chooses at commit \texttt{b80f227}). The regular-local-ring-of-dimension-1
340	  \(\Leftrightarrow\) DVR equivalence is shipped in Mathlib as
341	  \texttt{IsRegularLocalRing.isDiscreteValuationRing\_of\_dim\_one} (or analogous);
342	  the prover re-exports it rather than re-proving.
343	\end{proof}
344	
345	\section{Milne Theorem 3.1: codimension-\(\geq 2\) extension}
346	\label{sec:milne_thm31}
347	
348	The first input to Milne's Theorem~3.2 is the following: a rational map from a normal
349	variety to a \emph{complete} variety automatically extends across every codim-\(1\)
350	point.
351	
352	\begin{theorem}
353	
354	\leanok
355	  [Milne Theorem~3.1: codim-\(\geq 2\) extension to a complete target]
356	  \label{thm:codim_one_extension}
357	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth}
358	  
359	  % SOURCE: [Milne, Abelian Varieties], Theorem~3.1, \S I.3, p.~16
360	  % (read from references/abelian-varieties.pdf, PDF page 22).
361	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "\textsc{Theorem 3.1.} \emph{A rational map $\varphi \colon V
362	  % \dashrightarrow W\( from a normal variety \)V\( to a complete variety \)W$ is defined
363	  % on an open subset \(U\) of \(V\) whose complement \(V \smallsetminus U\) has codimension
364	  % \(\geq 2\).}"
365	  \textit{Source: Milne, \emph{Abelian Varieties}, Theorem~3.1, \S I.3, p.~16.}
366	  Let \(X\) be a nonsingular variety over \(\bar k\), let \(Y\) be a complete variety over
367	  \(\bar k\), and let \(f \colon X \dashrightarrow Y\) be a rational map. Then the
368	  indeterminacy locus \(Z(f)\) (\cref{def:indeterminacy_locus}) has codimension
369	  \(\geq 2\) in \(X\); equivalently, \(f\) is codim-\(1\)-indeterminacy-free
370	  (\cref{def:codim_one_indeterminacy}).
371	
372	  Specialising further: if in addition \(f\) \emph{is} codim-\(1\)-indeterminacy-free
373	  ``for free'' (i.e.\ no prime divisor of \(X\) is contained in \(Z(f)\)), then
374	  \(\mathrm{Dom}(f) = X\) and \(f\) extends to a regular morphism $\widetilde f \colon X
375	  \to Y\(. The extension is unique because \)X\( is reduced and \)\mathrm{Dom}(f)$ is dense
376	  in \(X\).
377	\end{theorem}
378	
379	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "\textsc{Proof.} Assume first that \(V\) is a curve. Thus we are
380	% given a nonsingular curve \(V\) and a regular map \(\varphi \colon U \to W\) from an
381	% open subset of \(V\) which we want to extend to \(V\). Consider the maps
382	% [diagram: \(U \hookrightarrow V\), $U \xrightarrow{u \mapsto (u, \varphi(u))} Z
383	% \subseteq V \times W\(, \)U \xrightarrow{\varphi} W$, with projections from
384	% \(V \times W\) to \(V\) and to \(W\)]
385	% Let \(U'\) be the image of \(U\) in \(V \times W\), and let \(Z\) be its closure. The image
386	% of \(Z\) in \(V\) is closed (because \(W\) is complete), and contains \(U\) (the composite
387	% \(U \to V\) is the given inclusion), and so \(Z\) maps onto \(V\). The maps $U \to U'
388	% \to U\( are isomorphisms. Therefore, \)Z \to V$ is a surjective map from a complete
389	% curve onto a nonsingular complete curve that is an isomorphism on open subsets.
390	% Such a map must be an isomorphism (complete nonsingular curves are determined by
391	% their function fields). The restriction of the projection map \(V \times W \to W\)
392	% to \(Z\ (\simeq V)\) is the extension of \(\varphi\) to \(V\) we are seeking.
393	%   The general case can be reduced to the one-dimensional case (using schemes). Let
394	% \(U\) be the largest subset on which \(\varphi\) is defined, and suppose that $V
395	% \smallsetminus U\( has codimension 1. Then there is a prime divisor \)Z\( in \)V
396	% \smallsetminus U\(. Because \)V$ is normal, its associated local ring is a discrete
397	% valuation ring \(\mathcal O_Z\) with field of fractions \(k(V)\). The map \(\varphi\)
398	% defines a morphism of schemes \(\operatorname{Spec}(k(V)) \to W\), which the
399	% valuative criterion of properness (Hartshorne 1977, II 4.7) shows extends to a
400	% morphism \(\operatorname{Spec}(\mathcal O_Z) \to W\). This implies that \(\varphi\)
401	% has a representative defined on an open subset that meets \(Z\) in a nonempty set,
402	% which is a contradiction."
403	\begin{proof}
404	  
405	  Following Milne's two-step proof.
406	
407	  \emph{Step 1: rule out codim-\(1\) components.} Suppose for contradiction that \(Z(f)\)
408	  contains a prime divisor \(W \subseteq X\) (i.e.\ a codim-\(1\) irreducible component).
409	  Let \(\eta \in W\) be its generic point, so \(\mathrm{codim}_X \overline{\{\eta\}} = 1\). By
410	  \cref{lem:smooth_codim_one_dvr}, the local ring \(\mathcal O_{X, \eta}\) is a discrete
411	  valuation ring with fraction field the function field \(K(X)\). Any representative
412	  \((U, \varphi_U) \in f\) has \(U \cap \mathrm{Dom}(f) \neq \emptyset\) and so determines
413	  a \(\bar k\)-algebra map from the function field \(K(Y)\) of \(Y\) to \(K(X)\): explicitly,
414	  \(\varphi_U\) defines a morphism of schemes \(\operatorname{Spec}(K(X)) \to Y\) (since
415	  \(\operatorname{Spec}(K(X))\) is the generic point of \(X\)). By the valuative criterion
416	  of properness applied to the complete variety \(Y\)
417	  (\emph{cf.}\ Hartshorne~II.4.7, cited verbatim in Milne~3.1's proof), this morphism
418	  extends to a morphism \(\operatorname{Spec}(\mathcal O_{X, \eta}) \to Y\). Standard
419	  algebraic-geometry spreading then exhibits an open neighbourhood \(V\) of \(\eta\) in
420	  \(X\) on which \(f\) has a regular representative; in particular $\eta \in
421	  \mathrm{Dom}(f)\(, contradicting \)\eta \in W \subseteq Z(f)\(. Hence no codim-\)1$
422	  component of \(X\) lies in \(Z(f)\), i.e.\ \(Z(f)\) has codimension \(\geq 2\).
423	
424	  \emph{Step 2: extend across codim-\(\geq 2\) points (the "codim-1-indeterminacy-free
425	  \(\Rightarrow\) extension" half).} Suppose now that \(Z(f)\) has codimension \(\geq 2\)
426	  but is non-empty (we want to deduce \(Z(f) = \emptyset\) under the additional input
427	  that ``\(f\) is already codim-1-indeterminacy-free, by hypothesis''). Pick a closed
428	  point \(x \in Z(f)\) with \(\mathrm{codim}_X \overline{\{x\}} \geq 2\). By
429	  \cref{cor:regular_cohen_macaulay} applied to the regular local ring
430	  \(\mathcal O_{X, x}\), we have
431	  \(\mathrm{depth}(\mathcal O_{X, x}) = \dim(\mathcal O_{X, x}) \geq 2\). The sheaf
432	  \(\mathcal O_X\) on the punctured neighbourhood \(V \smallsetminus \{x\}\)
433	  (any small affine neighbourhood \(V \ni x\)) has \(H^0_{\{x\}}(V, \mathcal O_X) = 0\)
434	  and \(H^1_{\{x\}}(V, \mathcal O_X) = 0\) (the depth-\(\geq 2\) characterisation of
435	  local-cohomology vanishing, Stacks tag \texttt{0AVF}; cf.\ Hartshorne~III.3.5 for
436	  the surface case). Pull back the coordinates of any affine chart of \(Y\) around the
437	  closure \(\overline{f(\mathrm{Dom}(f) \cap V)}\) along \(f\); the resulting sections of
438	  \(\mathcal O_X\) on \(V \cap \mathrm{Dom}(f)\) extend uniquely to sections on \(V\),
439	  yielding a regular representative of \(f\) on \(V\). Hence \(x \in \mathrm{Dom}(f)\),
440	  contradicting \(x \in Z(f)\). Therefore \(Z(f) = \emptyset\) and \(f\) extends to a
441	  regular morphism \(\widetilde f \colon X \to Y\).
442	
443	  Uniqueness of \(\widetilde f\) is the standard reduced-and-separated agreement
444	  principle: two morphisms \(X \to Y\) that agree on the dense open \(\mathrm{Dom}(f)\)
445	  agree on all of \(X\).
446	\end{proof}
447	
448	\begin{remark}
449	  \label{rmk:codim1_role_of_ab}
450	  \textbf{Where Auslander--Buchsbaum enters.} Step~1 of the proof uses only normality
451	  + the valuative criterion of properness (Hartshorne~II.4.7) and is characteristic-free.
452	  Step~2 uses the depth-\(\geq 2\) extension property at a codim-\(\geq 2\) regular
453	  point, which is exactly what \cref{cor:regular_cohen_macaulay} of
454	  \cref{chap:Albanese_AuslanderBuchsbaum} supplies (regular local of dimension \(d\)
455	  \(\Rightarrow\) Cohen--Macaulay of depth \(d\)). For our application the source \(X\) is a
456	  smooth variety, so the regular-local-ring hypothesis is automatic; the Cohen--Macaulay
457	  consequence is what feeds the local-cohomology vanishing.
458	
459	  Concretely, when \(X\) is a smooth surface (e.g.\ \(\mathbb P^1 \times \mathbb P^1\) or
460	  \(C \times C\) for a smooth projective curve \(C\)), the codim-\(\geq 2\) closed points of
461	  \(X\) are exactly the closed points of \(X\) (because \(\dim X = 2\)), and at each such
462	  point \(\mathcal O_{X, x}\) is regular of dimension \(2\), hence Cohen--Macaulay of
463	  depth \(2\). The smooth-surface case is the one A.4.c will invoke when extending the
464	  symmetric-product rational map \(C^{(g)} \dashrightarrow A\) along the Abel--Jacobi
465	  birational equivalence to \(J = \Pic^0_{C/k}\).
466	\end{remark}

468	\section{Milne Lemma 3.3: indeterminacy locus of a map to a group variety is codim 1}
469	\label{sec:milne_lem33}
470	
471	The second input to Milne's Theorem~3.2 is structural: when the target \(Y\) is a group
472	variety, the indeterminacy locus is either empty or of \emph{pure} codimension \(1\).
473	Combined with \cref{thm:codim_one_extension}'s ``codimension \(\geq 2\)'' conclusion,
474	this forces the locus to be empty.
475	
476	\begin{lemma}
477	
478	\leanok
479	  [Milne Lemma~3.3: pure-codim-\(1\) structure of the indeterminacy locus into a group
480	  variety]
481	  \label{lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy}
482	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme}
483	  
484	  % SOURCE: [Milne, Abelian Varieties], Lemma~3.3, \S I.3, p.~17
485	  % (read from references/abelian-varieties.pdf, PDF page 23).
486	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "\textsc{Lemma 3.3.} \emph{Let \(\varphi \colon V \dashrightarrow G\)
487	  % be a rational map from a nonsingular variety to a group variety. Then either
488	  % \(\varphi\) is defined on all of \(V\) or the points where it is not defined form a
489	  % closed subset of pure codimension 1 in \(V\) (i.e., a finite union of prime
490	  % divisors).}"
491	  \textit{Source: Milne, \emph{Abelian Varieties}, Lemma~3.3, \S I.3, p.~17.}
492	  Let \(X\) be a nonsingular variety over \(\bar k\), let \(G\) be a group variety over
493	  \(\bar k\) (a smooth group scheme of finite type), and let $f \colon X \dashrightarrow
494	  G\( be a rational map. Then either \)f\( is defined on all of \)X$, or the indeterminacy
495	  locus \(Z(f) \subseteq X\) is a closed subset of pure codimension \(1\) (i.e.\ a finite
496	  union of prime divisors).
497	
498	  Equivalently: it is \emph{never} the case that \(f\) has a non-empty indeterminacy
499	  locus all of whose irreducible components have codimension \(\geq 2\).
500	\end{lemma}
501	
502	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "\textsc{Proof.} Define a rational map
503	%   \(\Phi \colon V \times V \dashrightarrow G\), $\;(x, y) \mapsto \varphi(x) \cdot
504	%   \varphi(y)^{-1}$.
505	% More precisely, if \((U, \varphi_U)\) represents \(\varphi\), then \(\Phi\) is the rational
506	% map represented by
507	%   $U \times U \xrightarrow{\varphi_U \times \varphi_U} G \times G
508	%   \xrightarrow{\mathrm{id} \times \mathrm{inv}} G
509	%   \xrightarrow{m} G$.
510	% Clearly \(\Phi\) is defined at a diagonal point \((x, x)\) if \(\varphi\) is defined at
511	% \(x\), and then \(\Phi(x, x) = e\). Conversely, if \(\Phi\) is defined at \((x, x)\), then
512	% it is defined on an open neighbourhood of \((x, x)\); in particular, there will be an
513	% open subset \(U\) of \(V\) such that \(\Phi\) is defined on \(\{x\} \times U\). After
514	% possible replacing \(U\) by a smaller open subset (not necessarily containing \(x\)),
515	% \(\varphi\) will be defined on \(U\). For \(u \in U\), the formula
516	%   \(\varphi(x) = \Phi(x, u) \cdot \varphi(u)\)
517	% defines \(\varphi\) at \(x\). Thus \(\varphi\) is defined at \(x\) if and only if \(\Phi\) is
518	% defined at \((x, x)\). The rational map \(\Phi\) defines a map
519	%   \(\Phi^\ast \colon \mathcal O_{G, e} \to k(V \times V)\).
520	% Since \(\Phi\) sends \((x, x)\) to \(e\) if it is defined there, it follows that \(\Phi\)
521	% is defined at \((x, x)\) if and only if
522	%   \(\operatorname{Im}(\mathcal O_{G, e}) \subset \mathcal O_{V \times V, (x, x)}\).
523	% Now \(V \times V\) is nonsingular, and so we have a good theory of divisors (AG,
524	% Chapter 12). For a nonzero rational function \(f\) on \(V \times V\), write
525	%   \(\operatorname{div}(f) = \operatorname{div}(f)_0 - \operatorname{div}(f)_\infty\),
526	% with \(\operatorname{div}(f)_0\) and \(\operatorname{div}(f)_\infty\) effective divisors
527	% --- note that \(\operatorname{div}(f)_\infty = \operatorname{div}(f^{-1})_0\). Then
528	%   $\mathcal O_{V \times V, (x, x)} = \{f \in k(V \times V) \mid
529	%   \operatorname{div}(f)_\infty \text{ does not contain } (x, x)\} \cup \{0\}$.
530	% Suppose \(\phi\) is not defined at \(x\). Then for some $f \in \operatorname{Im}
531	% (\varphi^\ast)\(, \)(x, x) \in \operatorname{div}(f)_\infty\(, and clearly \)\Phi$ is
532	% not defined at the points \((y, y) \in \Delta \cap \operatorname{div}(f)_\infty\).
533	% This is a subset of pure codimension one in \(\Delta\) (AG 9.2), and when we identify
534	% it with a subset of \(V\), it is a subset of \(V\) of codimension one passing through
535	% \(x\) on which \(\varphi\) is not defined."
536	\begin{proof}
537	  
538	  Following Milne's proof of Lemma~3.3. The argument has four sub-steps. Throughout we
539	  fix a representative \((U, \varphi_U)\) of \(f\).
540	
541	  \emph{Sub-step 1: the difference rational map.} Define
542	  \[
543	    \Phi \colon X \times X \dashrightarrow G,
544	    \qquad
545	    \Phi(x, y) \;:=\; \varphi(x) \cdot \varphi(y)^{-1},
546	  \]
547	  via the composition
548	  \[
549	    U \times U
550	    \;\xrightarrow{\varphi_U \times \varphi_U}\; G \times G
551	    \;\xrightarrow{\mathrm{id} \times \mathrm{inv}}\; G \times G
552	    \;\xrightarrow{m}\; G,
553	  \]
554	  where \(\mathrm{inv} \colon G \to G\) is the group-variety inverse and
555	  \(m \colon G \times G \to G\) is the group law. The map \(\Phi\) is rational by
556	  composition: its domain is at least \(U \times U \subseteq X \times X\), which is dense
557	  because \(U\) is dense in \(X\).
558	
559	  \emph{Sub-step 2: \(\Phi\)-defined at \((x, x)\) \(\Leftrightarrow\) \(\varphi\)-defined at
560	  \(x\).} If \(\varphi\) is defined at \(x\), the explicit formula \(\Phi(x, x) = e\) (the
561	  identity of \(G\)) shows \(\Phi\) is defined at \((x, x)\). Conversely, if \(\Phi\) is
562	  defined at \((x, x)\), openness of \(\mathrm{Dom}(\Phi)\) in \(X \times X\) produces an
563	  open set \(W \ni (x, x)\) on which \(\Phi\) is defined; by projection (taking the slice
564	  \(\{x\} \times W' \subseteq W\) for some open \(W' \subseteq X\), possibly shrinking
565	  \(W'\) so \(\varphi\) is defined on \(W'\) as well --- \(W'\) need not contain \(x\)), the
566	  identity
567	  \[
568	    \varphi(x) \;=\; \Phi(x, u) \cdot \varphi(u),
569	    \qquad u \in W',
570	  \]
571	  exhibits a regular representative of \(\varphi\) at \(x\). Hence $x \in
572	  \mathrm{Dom}(\varphi)\( if and only if \)(x, x) \in \mathrm{Dom}(\Phi)$.
573	
574	  \emph{Sub-step 3: rephrase via pullback of local rings.} The rational map \(\Phi\)
575	  pulls \(\mathcal O_{G, e}\) back to a \(\bar k\)-subalgebra of the function field
576	  \(K(X \times X)\):
577	  \[
578	    \Phi^\ast \colon \mathcal O_{G, e} \;\longrightarrow\; K(X \times X).
579	  \]
580	  Because \(\Phi\) sends \((x, x)\) to \(e\) wherever it is defined, \(\Phi\) is defined at
581	  \((x, x)\) if and only if $\Phi^\ast(\mathcal O_{G, e}) \subseteq \mathcal O_{X
582	  \times X, (x, x)}\( (i.e.\ every local function on \)G\( near \)e$ has no pole at
583	  \((x, x)\) after pullback).
584	
585	  \emph{Sub-step 4: pole locus is pure codimension \(1\).} The variety \(X \times X\) is
586	  nonsingular (smoothness is stable under products over \(\bar k\)), so the Weil-divisor
587	  apparatus of \cref{def:codim1_cycles} and the order map of
588	  \cref{def:order_at_point} apply on \(X \times X\). For any nonzero rational function
589	  \(f \in K(X \times X)^\ast\) decompose its divisor as
590	  \[
591	    \mathrm{div}(f) \;=\; \mathrm{div}(f)_0 - \mathrm{div}(f)_\infty,
592	  \]
593	  with \(\mathrm{div}(f)_0\) and \(\mathrm{div}(f)_\infty\) effective and
594	  \(\mathrm{div}(f)_\infty = \mathrm{div}(f^{-1})_0\) (Milne's notation, matching the
595	  project's \(\div(f) = \sum \ord_Y(f) \cdot Y\) of
596	  \cref{def:principal_divisor}: \(\mathrm{div}(f)_0\) collects the prime divisors with
597	  \(\ord_Y(f) > 0\) and \(\mathrm{div}(f)_\infty\) those with \(\ord_Y(f) < 0\)). The local
598	  ring at \((x, x)\) can be characterised as
599	  \[
600	    \mathcal O_{X \times X, (x, x)}
601	    \;=\; \bigl\{\, f \in K(X \times X) \;\big|\;
602	    \mathrm{div}(f)_\infty \text{ does not contain } (x, x) \,\bigr\} \cup \{0\}.
603	  \]
604	  Suppose \(f \in K(X \times X)^\ast\) is not in \(\mathcal O_{X \times X, (x, x)}\);
605	  equivalently, \((x, x) \in \mathrm{supp}(\mathrm{div}(f)_\infty)\). The pole divisor
606	  \(\mathrm{div}(f)_\infty\) is a finite sum of prime divisors of \(X \times X\) (i.e.\ a
607	  closed subset of pure codimension \(1\) in \(X \times X\)). Its intersection with the
608	  diagonal \(\Delta_X \subseteq X \times X\) is a closed subset of pure codimension \(1\)
609	  in \(\Delta_X\) (this is Milne's reference to AG~9.2, the standard fact that a prime
610	  divisor of \(X \times X\) either contains \(\Delta_X\) or meets it in pure codimension
611	  \(1\); on a nonsingular variety this is an instance of Krull's principal-ideal theorem
612	  applied to the local equation of \(\Delta_X\) inside \(X \times X\)).
613	
614	  \emph{Conclusion.} Suppose \(f \colon X \dashrightarrow G\) is not defined on all of
615	  \(X\). Pick \(x \in Z(f)\). By Sub-steps~2--3, there is some $f_0 \in
616	  \Phi^\ast(\mathcal O_{G, e})\( with \)(x, x) \in \mathrm{supp}(\mathrm{div}(f_0)_\infty)$.
617	  By Sub-step~4 the intersection $\mathrm{supp}(\mathrm{div}(f_0)_\infty) \cap
618	  \Delta_X\( is pure codimension \)1\( in \)\Delta_X\(, passing through \)(x, x)$.
619	  Identifying \(\Delta_X\) with \(X\) via the diagonal isomorphism, the corresponding
620	  closed subset of \(X\) has pure codimension \(1\) and passes through \(x\). By Sub-step~2,
621	  every point of this codim-\(1\) subset is a point where \(\Phi\) is undefined on the
622	  diagonal, hence where \(\varphi\) is undefined. Thus \(Z(\varphi)\) contains a pure
623	  codim-\(1\) closed subset passing through \(x\); since \(x \in Z(\varphi)\) was arbitrary,
624	  every irreducible component of \(Z(\varphi)\) has codimension exactly \(1\), i.e.\
625	  \(Z(\varphi)\) is of pure codimension \(1\).
626	\end{proof}
627	
628	\begin{remark}
629	  \label{rmk:milne_lem33_charfree}
630	  \textbf{Characteristic-free.} The above proof uses only: (i) the group law on \(G\)
631	  and its inverse (formal smoothness of \(G\) as a group object); (ii) the Weil-divisor
632	  apparatus on the nonsingular variety \(X \times X\) (Hartshorne~II.6, or
633	  \cref{chap:RiemannRoch_WeilDivisor} in the project's notation); (iii) the
634	  decomposition of a divisor on a nonsingular variety into effective pieces, and
635	  Krull's principal-ideal theorem on \(X \times X\). None of these has a characteristic
636	  restriction, so \cref{lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy} holds over every
637	  algebraically closed base field, in arbitrary characteristic. (Milne states the
638	  lemma in this generality at \S I.3, p.~17.)
639	\end{remark}
640	
641	\section{Weil-divisor obstruction to codim-1 extension}
642	\label{sec:weil_obstruction}
643	
644	The proof of \cref{thm:codim_one_extension}'s Step~1 (codim-\(1\) ruling-out) goes
645	through the valuative criterion. In the project's Weil-divisor language
646	(\cref{chap:RiemannRoch_WeilDivisor}) the same obstruction has a cleaner phrasing:
647	the extension fails at a prime divisor \(W\) if and only if some affine coordinate of
648	\(f\) around the image \(\overline{f(\eta_W)}\) has a strictly negative order
649	\(\ord_W(\cdot) < 0\). This is the form the Lean prover needs when consuming
650	\cref{def:order_at_point} and \cref{def:principal_divisor}.
651	
652	\begin{theorem}
653	
654	  [Weil-divisor characterisation of the codim-1 extension obstruction]
655	  \label{thm:weil_divisor_obstruction}
656

674	  % SOURCE: [Hartshorne], II.6, pp.~130--131 (the valuation \(v_Y\) associated to a
675	  % prime divisor, and the characterisation of \(\mathcal O_{X, \eta_Y}\) as the
676	  % subring of \(K(X)\) on which \(v_Y \geq 0\)) (read from
677	  % references/hartshorne-algebraic-geometry.pdf, PDF pages 147--148).
678	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "If \(Y\) is a prime divisor on \(X\), let \(\eta \in Y\) be its generic
679	  % point. Then the local ring \(\mathcal O_{\eta, X}\) of \(X\) at \(\eta\) is a discrete
680	  % valuation ring with quotient field \(K\), the function field of \(X\). We call the
681	  % corresponding discrete valuation \(v_Y\) the \emph{valuation of \(Y\)}."
682	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Now let \(f \in K^\ast\) be any nonzero rational function on \(X\).
683	  % Then \(v_Y(f)\) is an integer. If it is positive, we say \(f\) has a \emph{zero} along
684	  % \(Y\), of that order; if it is negative, we say \(f\) has a \emph{pole} along \(Y\), of
685	  % order \(-v_Y(f)\)."
686	  \textit{Source: Hartshorne, II.6, pp.~130--131 (valuation \(v_Y\) and the order map).}
687	  Let \(X\) be a nonsingular variety over \(\bar k\), let \(Y\) be a variety over \(\bar k\),
688	  let \(f \colon X \dashrightarrow Y\) be a rational map, and let \(W \subseteq X\) be a
689	  prime divisor with generic point \(\eta_W \in W\) (so
690	  \(\mathrm{codim}_X \overline{\{\eta_W\}} = 1\)). Let \((U, \varphi_U)\) be any representative of
691	  \(f\) with \(U \cap (X \smallsetminus W) \neq \emptyset\). The following are equivalent:
692	  \begin{enumerate}
693	    \item \(f\) is defined at \(\eta_W\) (i.e.\ \(\eta_W \in \mathrm{Dom}(f)\), equivalently
694	      \(W \not\subseteq Z(f)\)).
695	    \item For some (equivalently, every) affine open neighbourhood \(V \subseteq Y\) of
696	      the generic image of \(f\) near \(W\), every regular function $g \in
697	      \mathcal O_Y(V)\( pulls back to a rational function \)\varphi_U^\ast(g) \in K(X)$
698	      satisfying
699	      \[
700	        \ord_W\bigl(\varphi_U^\ast(g)\bigr) \;\geq\; 0.
701	      \]
702	  \end{enumerate}
703	
704	  Consequently: \(f\) is codim-\(1\)-indeterminacy-free
705	  (\cref{def:codim_one_indeterminacy}) if and only if, for every prime divisor
706	  \(W \subseteq X\) and every affine open \(V \subseteq Y\) as above, the pullback
707	  \(\varphi_U^\ast(g)\) has \(\ord_W \geq 0\) for all \(g \in \mathcal O_Y(V)\).
708	\end{theorem}
709	
710	\begin{proof}
711	  
712	  By \cref{lem:smooth_codim_one_dvr}, the local ring \(\mathcal O_{X, \eta_W}\) is a
713	  DVR with fraction field \(K(X)\) and uniformiser determined by \(W\); by
714	  \cref{def:order_at_point} its valuation is exactly \(\ord_W\). The local ring at
715	  \(\eta_W\) is therefore characterised inside \(K(X)\) as the subring
716	  \[
717	    \mathcal O_{X, \eta_W} \;=\; \bigl\{\, h \in K(X) \;\big|\; \ord_W(h) \geq 0
718	    \,\bigr\} \cup \{0\}.
719	  \]
720	  The rational map \(f\) is defined at \(\eta_W\) if and only if, for any affine open
721	  \(V = \operatorname{Spec} A \subseteq Y\) around the image \(\overline{f(\eta_W)}\),
722	  the pullback \(\varphi_U^\ast \colon A \to K(X)\) lands in
723	  \(\mathcal O_{X, \eta_W}\): this is the standard local characterisation of where a
724	  rational map is regular at a codim-\(1\) point of a smooth variety (it is the
725	  ``regular = no pole'' criterion). By the displayed identity, the latter is
726	  equivalent to \(\ord_W(\varphi_U^\ast(g)) \geq 0\) for every \(g \in A\), which is
727	  condition (2). The equivalence (1)~\(\Leftrightarrow\)~(2) is therefore established.
728	
729	  For the consequence, \(f\) is codim-\(1\)-indeterminacy-free iff no prime divisor \(W\) is
730	  contained in \(Z(f)\), which by the equivalence above is iff the \(\ord_W \geq 0\)
731	  condition holds for every prime divisor \(W\).
732	\end{proof}
733	
734	\begin{remark}
735	  \label{rmk:weil_obstruction_application}

737	  \cref{chap:Albanese_Thm32RationalMapExtension} the Lean prover combines
738	  \cref{thm:codim_one_extension} with \cref{lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy}; in
739	  isolation, the abstract codim-\(\geq 2\) statement
740	  \cref{thm:codim_one_extension} suffices and \cref{thm:weil_divisor_obstruction}
741	  is not invoked. The Weil-divisor form is the bridge to the project's
742	  \(\mathrm{Scheme.WeilDivisor}\) API, which is the form A.4.a's own internal sub-proofs
743	  use when establishing \emph{specific} extensions in genus-\(0\) classifying-map

746	\end{remark}

748	\begin{lemma}
749	
750	\leanok
751	  [Definedness at a prime divisor, repackaged as a through-the-point partial map]
752	  \label{lem:mem_domain_partial_map_reshuffle}
753	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.mem_domain_iff_exists_partialMap_through_point}
754	  Let \(X\) and \(Y\) be varieties over \(\bar k\) and let
755	  \(f \colon X \dashrightarrow Y\) be a rational map. For a prime divisor
756	  \(W \subseteq X\) with generic point \(\eta_W = W.\mathrm{point}\), the
757	  following are equivalent:
758	  \begin{enumerate}
759	    \item \(\eta_W \in \mathrm{Dom}(f)\) (\(f\) is defined at the generic point of
760	      \(W\)).
761	    \item There exists a partial-map representative \((U, \varphi_U)\) of \(f\) ---
762	      i.e.\ a regular morphism \(\varphi_U \colon U \to Y\) on a dense open
763	      \(U \subseteq X\) whose induced rational map equals \(f\) --- whose domain
764	      \(U\) contains \(\eta_W\).
765	  \end{enumerate}
766	\end{lemma}
767	
768	\begin{proof}
769	
770	\leanok
771	  The lemma is a definitional reshuffle of the Mathlib membership predicate
772	  \texttt{Scheme.RationalMap.mem\_domain}, which already states that
773	  \(\eta_W \in \mathrm{Dom}(f)\) iff there is some partial-map representative of
774	  \(f\) defined at \(\eta_W\); the only content here is reordering the two
775	  conjuncts under the existential so the \(\varphi_U \mapsto f\) component lands
776	  first (matching the way later witnesses in the project construct partial-map
777	  representatives). The forward and backward implications are immediate from
778	  the unfolded definition.
779	\end{proof}
780	
781	\begin{remark}
782	  \label{rmk:mem_domain_partial_map_reshuffle_scope}
783	  \textbf{Relationship to \cref{thm:weil_divisor_obstruction}.}
784	  \cref{lem:mem_domain_partial_map_reshuffle} is the iter-179 Lane~D honest
785	  fallback for the substantive Hartshorne-II.6 ``regular = no pole''
786	  reformulation that \cref{thm:weil_divisor_obstruction} states in its full
787	  \(\ord_W \geq 0\) form. The reshuffle captures only the
788	  definitional half of the equivalence (existence of a partial-map
789	  representative through a prime-divisor generic point); upgrading it to the
790	  substantive \(\ord_W \geq 0\) form requires the
791	  \texttt{Scheme.RationalMap}-to-function-field pullback machinery, which is
792	  not shipped by Mathlib at commit \texttt{b80f227}. See the iter-179
793	  \texttt{\% NOTE} block on \cref{thm:weil_divisor_obstruction} for the
794	  history.
795	\end{remark}

909	\section{Out of scope}
910	\label{sec:codim1_out_of_scope}
911	
912	This chapter packages \emph{only} the two extension inputs and the Weil-divisor
913	reformulation needed for A.4.a. The following adjacent content is explicitly out of
914	scope:
915	\begin{itemize}
916	  \item \textbf{The Auslander--Buchsbaum formula and the regular \(\Rightarrow\)
917	    Cohen--Macaulay corollary.} Proved in \cref{chap:Albanese_AuslanderBuchsbaum}
918	    as \cref{thm:auslander_buchsbaum} and \cref{cor:regular_cohen_macaulay}; this
919	    chapter only consumes them (in Step~2 of \cref{thm:codim_one_extension}; see
920	    \cref{rmk:codim1_role_of_ab}).
921	  \item \textbf{Milne Theorem~3.2 itself.} The combination of
922	    \cref{thm:codim_one_extension} and \cref{lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy} into the
923	    statement ``a rational map from a nonsingular variety to an abelian variety
924	    extends'' lives in \cref{chap:Albanese_Thm32RationalMapExtension} as
925	    \cref{thm:rational_map_to_av_extends}; this chapter only supplies the two inputs.
926	  \item \textbf{Milne Theorem~3.4 / Corollary~3.7 / Theorem~3.8 / Proposition~3.9 /
927	    Proposition~3.10.} The remainder of Milne~\S I.3 (the ``map between AVs is
928	    translated homomorphism'' family of results, and the
929	    \(\mathbb A^1 \dashrightarrow A\) / unirational\(\dashrightarrow A\) constancy
930	    statements) is downstream of \cref{thm:rational_map_to_av_extends} but is not
931	    needed by A.4 in the project's current routing: the genus-\(0\) base case is
932	    handled via the proven Rigidity Lemma + \(\mathbb G_m\)-scaling shortcut
933	    (\cref{chap:RigidityKbar}), and the positive-genus arm consumes only the bare
934	    Theorem~3.2 statement.
935	  \item \textbf{Weil-divisor degree on \(X \times X\) for \(X\) a curve.} The proof of
936	    \cref{lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy} invokes the Weil-divisor apparatus on the
937	    smooth surface \(X \times X\) but does \emph{not} need the degree map of
938	    \cref{def:divisor_degree} (which is curve-specific); only the codim-\(1\)
939	    decomposition \(\div(f) = \div(f)_0 - \div(f)_\infty\) on a nonsingular surface is
940	    used. The bilinear degree formula on \(X \times X\) for \(X\) a curve (intersection
941	    numbers, Hartshorne~V.1) is out of scope.
942	  \item \textbf{Cartier divisors and the Cartier/Weil comparison.} On a smooth variety
943	    they coincide and the chapter uses Weil throughout; the Cartier formalism is not
944	    introduced.
945	  \item \textbf{Generalisation to non-algebraically-closed base fields.} The
946	    project's positive-genus arm runs over \(\bar k\) throughout; descent of the
947	    extension to a non-algebraically-closed base, if ever needed, would require
948	    Galois-descent machinery and is not in scope.
949	  \item \textbf{Resolution of indeterminacy by blow-ups} (Hironaka-style, for higher
950	    indeterminacy codimensions on a smooth variety with a non-group-variety target):
951	    out of scope. The combination of \cref{thm:codim_one_extension} and
952	    \cref{lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy} for \(G\) a group variety is sharper than any
953	    blow-up-based approach because it produces an extension on the original \(X\)
954	    rather than on a blow-up; no blow-up apparatus is needed.
955	\end{itemize}

6	module
7	
8	public import Mathlib.Algebra.Module.SpanRankOperations
9	public import Mathlib.RingTheory.DiscreteValuationRing.Basic
10	public import Mathlib.RingTheory.Ideal.KrullsHeightTheorem
11	public import Mathlib.RingTheory.KrullDimension.Field
12	public import Mathlib.RingTheory.KrullDimension.PID
13	
14	/-!
15	# Regular local rings
16	
17	For a Noetherian local ring `R`, we define `IsRegularLocalRing` as
18	`(maximalIdeal R).spanFinrank = ringKrullDim R`. This definition is equivalent to
19	the dimension of the cotangent space over the residue field being equal to `ringKrullDim R`,
20	(see `IsRegularLocalRing.iff_finrank_cotangentSpace`).
21	
22	# Main Definition and Results
23	
24	* `IsRegularLocalRing` : A Noetherian local ring is regular if
25	  `(maximalIdeal R).spanFinrank = ringKrullDim R`,
26	  i.e. its maximal ideal can be generated by `dim R` elements.
27	
28	* `IsRegularLocalRing.iff_finrank_cotangentSpace` : the equivalence of `IsRegularLocalRing` and
29	  `Module.finrank (ResidueField R) (CotangentSpace R) = ringKrullDim R`
30	
31	-/
32	
33	@[expose] public section
34	
35	open IsLocalRing
36	
37	/-- A Noetherian local ring is said to be regular if its maximal ideal
38	can be generated by `dim R` elements. -/
39	@[stacks 00KU]
40	class IsRegularLocalRing (R : Type*) [CommRing R] : Prop extends
41	    IsLocalRing R, IsNoetherianRing R where
42	  spanFinrank_maximalIdeal : (maximalIdeal R).spanFinrank = ringKrullDim R
43	
44	variable (R : Type*) [CommRing R]
45	
46	lemma isRegularLocalRing_iff [IsLocalRing R] [IsNoetherianRing R] :
47	    IsRegularLocalRing R ↔ (maximalIdeal R).spanFinrank = ringKrullDim R :=
48	  ⟨fun ⟨h⟩ ↦ h, fun h ↦ ⟨h⟩⟩
49	
50	namespace IsRegularLocalRing
51	
52	lemma of_spanFinrank_maximalIdeal_le [IsLocalRing R] [IsNoetherianRing R]
53	    (le : (maximalIdeal R).spanFinrank ≤ ringKrullDim R) : IsRegularLocalRing R :=
54	  (isRegularLocalRing_iff _).mpr (le_antisymm le (ringKrullDim_le_spanFinrank_maximalIdeal R))
55	
56	variable {R} in
57	lemma of_ringEquiv [IsRegularLocalRing R] {R' : Type*} [CommRing R']
58	    (e : R ≃+* R') : IsRegularLocalRing R' := by
59	  have := e.isLocalRing
60	  have := isNoetherianRing_of_ringEquiv R e
61	  rwa [isRegularLocalRing_iff, ← ringKrullDim_eq_of_ringEquiv e, ← map_ringEquiv_maximalIdeal e,
62	    Ideal.spanFinrank_map_eq_of_ringEquiv, ← isRegularLocalRing_iff]
63	
64	lemma iff_finrank_cotangentSpace [IsLocalRing R] [IsNoetherianRing R] :
65	    IsRegularLocalRing R ↔ Module.finrank (ResidueField R) (CotangentSpace R) = ringKrullDim R := by
66	  rw [isRegularLocalRing_iff, spanFinrank_maximalIdeal_eq_finrank_cotangentSpace]
67	
68	instance [IsLocalRing R] [IsDomain R] [IsPrincipalIdealRing R] : IsRegularLocalRing R := by
69	  by_cases isf : IsField R
70	  · let _ := isf.toField
71	    simp [isRegularLocalRing_iff, maximalIdeal_eq_bot]
72	  · apply of_spanFinrank_maximalIdeal_le
73	    rcases IsPrincipalIdealRing.principal (maximalIdeal R) with ⟨x, hx⟩
74	    simpa only [(IsPrincipalIdealRing.ringKrullDim_eq_one R) isf,
75	      Nat.cast_le_one, ← Set.ncard_singleton x, hx] using
76	        Submodule.spanFinrank_span_le_ncard_of_finite (Set.finite_singleton x)
77	
78	end IsRegularLocalRing

6	module
7	
8	public import Mathlib.RingTheory.Extension.Cotangent.Basis
9	public import Mathlib.RingTheory.Extension.Cotangent.Free
10	public import Mathlib.RingTheory.Smooth.Locus
11	
12	/-!
13	# Standard smooth of free Kaehler differentials
14	
15	In this file we show a presentation independent characterization of being
16	standard smooth: An `R`-algebra `S` of finite presentation is standard smooth if and only if
17	`H¹(S/R) = 0` and `Ω[S⁄R]` is free on `{d sᵢ}ᵢ` for some `sᵢ : S`.
18	
19	From this we deduce relations of standard smooth with other local properties.
20	
21	## Main results
22	
23	- `IsStandardSmooth.iff_exists_basis_kaehlerDifferential`: An `R`-algebra `S` of finite
24	  presentation is standard smooth if and only if `H¹(S/R) = 0` and `Ω[S⁄R]` is free on
25	  `{d sᵢ}ᵢ` for some `sᵢ : S`.
26	- `Etale.iff_isStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension_zero`: An `R`-algebra `S` is
27	  étale if and only if it is standard smooth of relative dimension zero.
28	- `IsSmoothAt.exists_notMem_isStandardSmooth`: If `S` is `R`-smooth at a prime `p`,
29	  it is standard smooth on a standard open containing `p`.
30	
31	## Notes
32	
33	For an example of an algebra with `H¹(S/R) = 0` and `Ω[S⁄R]` finite and free, but
34	`S` not standard smooth over `R`, consider `R = ℝ` and `S = R[x,y]/(x² + y² - 1)` the
35	coordinate ring of the circle. One can show that then `Ω[S⁄R]` is `S`-free on `ω = xdy - ydx`,
36	but there are no `f g : S` such that `ω = g df`.
37	-/
38	
39	public section
40	
41	namespace Algebra
42	
43	open KaehlerDifferential
44	
45	variable {R S : Type*} [CommRing R] [CommRing S] [Algebra R S]
46	
47	/-- If `H¹(S/R) = 0` and `Ω[S⁄R]` is free on `{d sᵢ}ᵢ` for some `sᵢ : S`, then `S`
48	is `R`-standard smooth. -/
49	theorem IsStandardSmooth.of_basis_kaehlerDifferential [FinitePresentation R S]
50	    [Subsingleton (H1Cotangent R S)]
51	    {I : Type*} (b : Module.Basis I S (Ω[S⁄R])) (hb : Set.range b ⊆ Set.range (D R S)) :
52	    IsStandardSmooth R S := by
53	  nontriviality S
54	  obtain ⟨n, ⟨P⟩⟩ := (FiniteType.iff_exists_generators (R := R) (S := S)).mp inferInstance
55	  choose f' hf' using hb
56	  let P := P.extend fun i ↦ f' ⟨i, rfl⟩
57	  have hb (i : I) : b i = D R S (P.val (Sum.inr i)) := by simp [P, hf']
58	  have : Function.Bijective (P.cotangentRestrict _) :=
59	    P.cotangentRestrict_bijective_of_basis_kaehlerDifferential Sum.inl_injective
60	      Set.isCompl_range_inl_range_inr.symm b hb
61	  let bcot' : Module.Basis (Fin n) S P.toExtension.Cotangent :=
62	    .ofRepr (.ofBijective (P.cotangentRestrict _) this)
63	  have : Finite I := Module.Finite.finite_basis b
64	  obtain ⟨Q, bcot, hcomp, hbcot⟩ := P.exists_presentation_of_basis_cotangent bcot'
65	  let P' : PreSubmersivePresentation R S (Unit ⊕ Fin n ⊕ I) (Unit ⊕ Fin n) :=
66	    { __ := Q
67	      map := Sum.map _root_.id Sum.inl
68	      map_inj := Sum.map_injective.mpr ⟨fun _ _ h ↦ h, Sum.inl_injective⟩ }
69	  have hcompl : IsCompl (Set.range (Sum.inr ∘ Sum.inr)) (Set.range P'.map) := by
70	    simp [P', ← eq_compl_iff_isCompl, Set.ext_iff, Set.mem_compl_iff]
71	  have hbij : Function.Bijective (P'.cotangentRestrict P'.map_inj) := by
72	    apply P'.cotangentRestrict_bijective_of_basis_kaehlerDifferential P'.map_inj hcompl b
73	    intro k
74	    simp only [hb, ← hcomp, P', Function.comp_def]
75	  let P'' : SubmersivePresentation R S _ _ :=
76	    ⟨P', P'.isUnit_jacobian_of_cotangentRestrict_bijective bcot hbcot hbij⟩
77	  exact P''.isStandardSmooth
78	
79	/-- An `R`-algebra `S` of finite presentation is standard smooth if and only if
80	`H¹(S/R) = 0` and `Ω[S⁄R]` is free on `{d sᵢ}ᵢ` for some `sᵢ : S`. -/
81	theorem IsStandardSmooth.iff_exists_basis_kaehlerDifferential [FinitePresentation R S] :
82	    IsStandardSmooth R S ↔ Subsingleton (H1Cotangent R S) ∧
83	      ∃ (I : Type) (b : Module.Basis I S (Ω[S⁄R])), Set.range b ⊆ Set.range (D R S) := by
84	  refine ⟨fun h ↦ ⟨inferInstance, ?_⟩, fun ⟨h, ⟨_, b, hb⟩⟩ ↦ .of_basis_kaehlerDifferential b hb⟩
85	  obtain ⟨ι, σ, _, _, ⟨P⟩⟩ := Algebra.IsStandardSmooth.out (R := R) (S := S)
86	  exact ⟨_, P.basisKaehler, by simp [Set.range_subset_iff]⟩
87	
88	/-- `S` is an étale `R`-algebra if and only if it is standard smooth of relative dimension `0`. -/
89	theorem Etale.iff_isStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension_zero :
90	    Etale R S ↔ IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension 0 R S := by
91	  refine ⟨fun h ↦ ?_, fun _ ↦ inferInstance⟩
92	  nontriviality S
93	  suffices h : IsStandardSmooth R S by
94	    simp [IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.iff_of_isStandardSmooth]
95	  rw [IsStandardSmooth.iff_exists_basis_kaehlerDifferential]
96	  refine ⟨inferInstance, ⟨Empty, Module.Basis.empty Ω[S⁄R], ?_⟩⟩
97	  simp [Set.range_subset_iff]
98	
99	variable (R) in
100	/-- If `S` is `R`-smooth at a prime `p`, then `S` is `R`-standard-smooth in a neighbourhood of `p`:
101	there exists a basic open `p ∈ D(f)` of `Spec S` such that `S[1/f]` is standard smooth. -/
102	theorem IsSmoothAt.exists_notMem_isStandardSmooth [FinitePresentation R S] (p : Ideal S) [p.IsPrime]
103	    [IsSmoothAt R p] :
104	    ∃ (f : S), f ∉ p ∧ IsStandardSmooth R (Localization.Away f) := by
105	  -- By replacing `S` by some `S[1/g]` we may assume `S` is globally smooth.
106	  wlog h : Smooth R S
107	  · obtain ⟨g, hg, hsm⟩ := IsSmoothAt.exists_notMem_smooth R p
108	    have _ : (Ideal.map (algebraMap S (Localization.Away g)) p).IsPrime := by
109	      apply IsLocalization.isPrime_of_isPrime_disjoint (.powers g) _ _ ‹_›
110	      rwa [Ideal.disjoint_powers_iff_notMem _ (Ideal.IsPrime.isRadical ‹_›)]
111	    obtain ⟨g', hg', hstd⟩ := this (R := R) (p.map (algebraMap S (Localization.Away g))) hsm
112	    have : IsLocalization.Away (g * (IsLocalization.Away.sec g g').1) (Localization.Away g') :=
113	      .mul_of_associated _ _ g' <| IsLocalization.Away.associated_sec_fst g g'
114	    let e : Localization.Away (g * (IsLocalization.Away.sec g g').1) ≃ₐ[S] Localization.Away g' :=
115	      Localization.algEquiv _ _
116	    refine ⟨g * (IsLocalization.Away.sec g g').1, ?_, .of_algEquiv (e.restrictScalars R).symm⟩
117	    refine Ideal.IsPrime.mul_notMem ‹_› hg fun hmem ↦ hg' ?_
118	    rw [Ideal.mem_iff_of_associated (IsLocalization.Away.associated_sec_fst g g').symm]
119	    exact Ideal.mem_map_of_mem (algebraMap S (Localization.Away g)) hmem
120	  -- `Ω[Sₚ⁄R]` is projective, so free over the local ring `Sₚ` and
121	  -- a basis extends to a neighbourhood `D(g)`.
122	  obtain ⟨κ, a, b, hb⟩ := Module.exists_basis_of_span_of_flat _
123	    (span_range_map_derivation_of_isLocalization R _ (Localization.AtPrime p) p.primeCompl)
124	  let e : (κ →₀ S) →ₗ[S] Ω[S⁄R] :=
125	    Finsupp.linearCombination S fun i : κ ↦ D R S (a i)
126	  let l₁ : (κ →₀ S) →ₗ[S] (κ →₀ Localization.AtPrime p) :=
127	    Finsupp.mapRange.linearMap (Algebra.linearMap S (Localization.AtPrime p))
128	  let l₂ : Ω[S⁄R] →ₗ[S] Ω[Localization.AtPrime p⁄R] := map R R S (Localization.AtPrime p)
129	  let eₚ : (κ →₀ Localization.AtPrime p) →ₗ[Localization.AtPrime p] Ω[Localization.AtPrime p⁄R] :=
130	    IsLocalizedModule.mapExtendScalars p.primeCompl l₁ l₂ (Localization.AtPrime p) e
131	  have : eₚ = b.repr.symm := by
132	    ext i
133	    trans IsLocalizedModule.map p.primeCompl l₁ l₂ e <| l₁ <| Finsupp.single i 1
134	    · simp [eₚ, -IsLocalizedModule.map_apply, l₁]
135	    · simp [l₂, e, hb]
136	  have heₚ : Function.Bijective eₚ := this ▸ b.repr.symm.bijective
137	  have : Finite κ := Module.Finite.finite_basis b
138	  obtain ⟨g, hg, h⟩ := Module.FinitePresentation.exists_notMem_bijective e p l₁ l₂ heₚ
139	  let l₁ₜ : (κ →₀ S) →ₗ[S] (κ →₀ Localization.Away g) :=
140	    Finsupp.mapRange.linearMap (Algebra.linearMap S _)
141	  let l₂ₜ : Ω[S⁄R] →ₗ[S] Ω[Localization.Away g⁄R] :=
142	    map R R S (Localization.Away g)
143	  rw [← IsLocalizedModule.map_bijective_iff_localizedModuleMap_bijective l₁ₜ l₂ₜ] at h
144	  let eₜ' : (κ →₀ Localization.Away g) →ₗ[Localization.Away g] Ω[Localization.Away g⁄R] :=
145	    IsLocalizedModule.mapExtendScalars (Submonoid.powers g) l₁ₜ l₂ₜ (Localization.Away g) e
146	  refine ⟨g, hg, .of_basis_kaehlerDifferential (.ofRepr (LinearEquiv.ofBijective eₜ' h).symm) ?_⟩
147	  rintro - ⟨i, rfl⟩
148	  exact ⟨algebraMap S _ (a i), by simp +zetaDelta [IsLocalizedModule.map_linearCombination]⟩
149	
150	variable (R S) in
151	/-- If `S` is `R`-smooth, there exists a cover by basic opens `D(sᵢ)` such that
152	`S[1/sᵢ]` is `R`-standard-smooth. -/
153	theorem Smooth.exists_span_eq_top_isStandardSmooth [Smooth R S] :
154	    ∃ (s : Set S), Ideal.span s = ⊤ ∧ ∀ x ∈ s, IsStandardSmooth R (Localization.Away x) := by
155	  choose f hf₁ hf₂ using IsSmoothAt.exists_notMem_isStandardSmooth R (S := S)
156	  /- #adaptation_note Before https://github.com/leanprover/lean4/pull/13166
157	  (replacing grind's canonicalizer with a type-directed normalizer), the original proof was:
158	  `refine ⟨Set.range (fun p : PrimeSpectrum S ↦ f p.asIdeal), ?_, by grind⟩`
159	  It is not yet clear whether this is due to defeq abuse in Mathlib or a problem in the new
160	  canonicalizer; a minimization would help. -/
161	  refine ⟨Set.range (fun p : PrimeSpectrum S ↦ f p.asIdeal), ?_, by
162	    simp only [Set.mem_range, forall_exists_index, forall_apply_eq_imp_iff]; intro; apply hf₂⟩
163	  simp [← PrimeSpectrum.iSup_basicOpen_eq_top_iff, TopologicalSpace.Opens.ext_iff, Set.ext_iff]
164	  grind
165	
166	end Algebra

78	
79	/--
80	A morphism of schemes is smooth if and only if for each `x : X` there exists an affine open
81	neighborhood `V` of `x` and an affine open neighborhood `U` of `f.base x` with `V ≤ f ⁻¹ᵁ U`
82	such that the induced map `Γ(Y, U) ⟶ Γ(X, V)` is standard smooth.
83	-/
84	lemma Smooth.iff_forall_exists_isStandardSmooth (f : X ⟶ Y) :
85	    Smooth f ↔
86	      ∀ (x : X), ∃ (U : Y.Opens) (_ : IsAffineOpen U) (V : X.Opens) (_ : IsAffineOpen V) (_ : x ∈ V)
87	        (e : V ≤ f ⁻¹ᵁ U), (f.appLE U V e).hom.IsStandardSmooth := by
88	  have : HasRingHomProperty @Smooth.{u} (Locally IsStandardSmooth) := by
89	    convert (inferInstance : HasRingHomProperty @Smooth.{u} RingHom.Smooth)
90	    ext f
91	    rw [RingHom.smooth_iff_locally_isStandardSmooth]
92	  rw [HasRingHomProperty.iff_exists_appLE_locally (P := @Smooth)]
93	  · congr!
94	    simp [Subtype.exists]
95	    grind [Scheme.affineOpens]
96	  · exact isStandardSmooth_stableUnderCompositionWithLocalizationAway.left
97	  · exact isStandardSmooth_respectsIso
98	
99	lemma Smooth.exists_isStandardSmooth (f : X ⟶ Y) [Smooth f] (x : X) :
100	    ∃ (U : Y.Opens) (_ : IsAffineOpen U) (V : X.Opens) (_ : IsAffineOpen V) (_ : x ∈ V)
101	        (e : V ≤ f ⁻¹ᵁ U), (f.appLE U V e).hom.IsStandardSmooth :=
102	  (iff_forall_exists_isStandardSmooth f).mp ‹_› x
103	
104	/-- Being smooth is stable under composition. -/
105	instance : MorphismProperty.IsStableUnderComposition @Smooth :=
106	  HasRingHomProperty.stableUnderComposition Smooth.stableUnderComposition
107	
108	/-- The composition of smooth morphisms is smooth. -/
109	instance smooth_comp {Z : Scheme.{u}} (g : Y ⟶ Z) [Smooth f] [Smooth g] :
110	    Smooth (f ≫ g) :=
111	  MorphismProperty.comp_mem _ f g ‹Smooth f› ‹Smooth g›
112	
113	instance (priority := low) [Smooth f] : Flat f where
114	  flat_appLE {_} hU {_} hV e := (f.smooth_appLE hU hV e).flat
115	
116	/-- Smooth is stable under base change. -/
117	instance smooth_isStableUnderBaseChange : MorphismProperty.IsStableUnderBaseChange @Smooth :=
118	  HasRingHomProperty.isStableUnderBaseChange Smooth.isStableUnderBaseChange
119	
120	instance : MorphismProperty.Respects @Smooth @IsOpenImmersion :=
121	  HasRingHomProperty.respects_isOpenImmersion
122	    (RingHom.Smooth.stableUnderComposition.stableUnderCompositionWithLocalizationAway
123	      RingHom.Smooth.holdsForLocalizationAway).1
124	
125	@[deprecated (since := "2026-02-09")]
126	alias isSmooth_isStableUnderBaseChange := smooth_isStableUnderBaseChange
127	
128	/--
129	A morphism of schemes `f : X ⟶ Y` is smooth of relative dimension `n` if for each `x : X` there
130	exists an affine open neighborhood `V` of `x` and an affine open neighborhood `U` of
131	`f.base x` with `V ≤ f ⁻¹ᵁ U` such that the induced map `Γ(Y, U) ⟶ Γ(X, V)` is
132	standard smooth of relative dimension `n`.
133	-/
134	@[mk_iff]
135	class SmoothOfRelativeDimension : Prop where
136	  exists_isStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension : ∀ (x : X), ∃ (U : Y.Opens) (_ : IsAffineOpen U)
137	    (V : X.Opens) (_ : IsAffineOpen V) (_ : x ∈ V) (e : V ≤ f ⁻¹ᵁ U),
138	    IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension n (f.appLE U V e).hom
139	
140	@[deprecated (since := "2026-02-09")] alias IsSmoothOfRelativeDimension := SmoothOfRelativeDimension
141	
142	/-- If `f` is smooth of any relative dimension, it is smooth. -/
143	lemma SmoothOfRelativeDimension.smooth [SmoothOfRelativeDimension n f] : Smooth f := by
144	  rw [Smooth.iff_forall_exists_isStandardSmooth]
145	  intro x
146	  obtain ⟨U, hU, V, hV, hx, e, hf⟩ := exists_isStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension (n := n) (f := f) x
147	  exact ⟨U, hU, V, hV, hx, e, hf.isStandardSmooth⟩
148	
149	@[deprecated (since := "2026-02-09")]
150	alias IsSmoothOfRelativeDimension.isSmooth := SmoothOfRelativeDimension.smooth
151	
152	/-- The property of scheme morphisms `SmoothOfRelativeDimension n` is associated with the ring
153	homomorphism property `Locally (IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension n)`. -/
154	instance : HasRingHomProperty (@SmoothOfRelativeDimension n)
155	    (Locally (IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension n)) := by
156	  apply HasRingHomProperty.locally_of_iff
157	  · exact (isStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension_localizationPreserves n).away
158	  · exact isStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension_stableUnderCompositionWithLocalizationAway n
159	  · intro X Y f
160	    rw [smoothOfRelativeDimension_iff]
161	    congr!
162	    simp [Subtype.exists]
163	    grind [Scheme.affineOpens]

166	lemma smoothOfRelativeDimension_isStableUnderBaseChange :

168	  HasRingHomProperty.isStableUnderBaseChange <| locally_isStableUnderBaseChange
169	    isStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension_respectsIso

201	/-- If `f` is smooth of relative dimension `n` and `g` is smooth of relative dimension
202	`m`, then `f ≫ g` is smooth of relative dimension `n + m`. -/
203	instance smoothOfRelativeDimension_comp {Z : Scheme.{u}} (g : Y ⟶ Z)
204	    [hf : SmoothOfRelativeDimension n f] [hg : SmoothOfRelativeDimension m g] :
205	    SmoothOfRelativeDimension (n + m) (f ≫ g) where
206	  exists_isStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension x := by
207	    obtain ⟨U₂, hU₂, V₂, hV₂, hfx₂, e₂, hf₂⟩ := hg.exists_isStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension (f x)
208	    obtain ⟨U₁', hU₁', V₁', hV₁', hx₁', e₁', hf₁'⟩ :=
209	      hf.exists_isStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension x
210	    obtain ⟨r, s, hx₁, e₁, hf₁⟩ := exists_basicOpen_le_appLE_of_appLE_of_isAffine
211	      (isStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension_stableUnderCompositionWithLocalizationAway n).right
212	      (isStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension_localizationPreserves n).away
213	      x ⟨V₂, hV₂⟩ ⟨U₁', hU₁'⟩ ⟨V₁', hV₁'⟩ ⟨V₁', hV₁'⟩ hx₁' hx₁' e₁' hf₁' hfx₂
214	    have e : X.basicOpen s ≤ (f ≫ g) ⁻¹ᵁ U₂ :=
215	      le_trans e₁ <| f.preimage_mono <| le_trans (Y.basicOpen_le r) e₂
216	    have heq : (f ≫ g).appLE U₂ (X.basicOpen s) e = g.appLE U₂ V₂ e₂ ≫
217	        CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap Γ(Y, V₂) Γ(Y, Y.basicOpen r)) ≫
218	          f.appLE (Y.basicOpen r) (X.basicOpen s) e₁ := by
219	      rw [RingHom.algebraMap_toAlgebra, CommRingCat.ofHom_hom,
220	        g.appLE_map_assoc, Scheme.Hom.appLE_comp_appLE]
221	    refine ⟨U₂, hU₂, X.basicOpen s, hV₁'.basicOpen s, hx₁, e, heq ▸ ?_⟩
222	    apply IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.comp ?_ hf₂
223	    haveI : IsLocalization.Away r Γ(Y, Y.basicOpen r) := hV₂.isLocalization_basicOpen r
224	    exact (isStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension_stableUnderCompositionWithLocalizationAway n).left
225	      _ r _ hf₁

243	lemma formallySmooth_stalkMap_iff {f : X ⟶ Y} {x : X} (U : Y.Opens)
244	      (hU : IsAffineOpen U) (V : X.Opens) (hV : IsAffineOpen V) (hVU : V ≤ f ⁻¹ᵁ U)
245	      (hx : x ∈ V) :
246	    letI := (f.appLE U V hVU).hom.toAlgebra
247	    (f.stalkMap x).hom.FormallySmooth ↔
248	      hV.primeIdealOf ⟨x, hx⟩ ∈ Algebra.smoothLocus Γ(Y, U) Γ(X, V) := by
249	  letI := (f.appLE U V hVU).hom.toAlgebra
250	  have : (hV.primeIdealOf ⟨x, hx⟩).asIdeal.LiesOver (hU.primeIdealOf ⟨f x, hVU hx⟩).asIdeal :=
251	    ⟨congr($(IsAffineOpen.comap_primeIdealOf_appLE U hU V hV hVU hx).1).symm⟩
252	  trans Algebra.FormallySmooth
253	    (Localization.AtPrime (hU.primeIdealOf ⟨f x, hVU hx⟩).asIdeal)
254	    (Localization.AtPrime (hV.primeIdealOf ⟨x, hx⟩).asIdeal)
255	  · rw [← formallySmooth_algebraMap]
256	    exact RingHom.FormallySmooth.respectsIso.arrow_mk_iso_iff
257	      (IsAffineOpen.arrowStalkMapIso f U hU V hV hVU hx)

261	lemma exists_smooth_of_formallySmooth_stalk
262	    (f : X ⟶ Y) [LocallyOfFinitePresentation f]
263	    (x : X) (H : (f.stalkMap x).hom.FormallySmooth) :
264	    ∃ (U : Y.Opens) (_ : IsAffineOpen U) (V : X.Opens) (_ : IsAffineOpen V) (hVU : V ≤ f ⁻¹ᵁ U),
265	      x ∈ V ∧ (f.appLE U V hVU).hom.Smooth := by
266	  obtain ⟨_, ⟨U, hU, rfl⟩, hxU, -⟩ :=
267	    Y.isBasis_affineOpens.exists_subset_of_mem_open (Set.mem_univ (f x)) isOpen_univ
268	  obtain ⟨_, ⟨V, hV, rfl⟩, hxV, hVU⟩ :=
269	    X.isBasis_affineOpens.exists_subset_of_mem_open hxU (U.2.preimage f.continuous)
270	  have := f.finitePresentation_appLE hU hV hVU
271	  algebraize [(f.appLE U V hVU).hom]
272	  have : Algebra.IsSmoothAt _ _ := (formallySmooth_stalkMap_iff U hU V hV hVU hxV).mp H
273	  obtain ⟨r, hrx, hr⟩ := Algebra.IsSmoothAt.exists_notMem_smooth Γ(Y, U)
274	    (hV.primeIdealOf ⟨x, hxV⟩).asIdeal
275	  refine ⟨_, hU, _, hV.basicOpen r, (X.basicOpen_le r).trans hVU, ?_, ?_⟩
276	  · rwa [← PrimeSpectrum.mem_basicOpen, IsAffineOpen.primeIdealOf,
277	      ← hV.fromSpec_preimage_basicOpen, Scheme.Hom.mem_preimage, ← Scheme.Hom.comp_apply,
278	      IsAffineOpen.isoSpec_hom, IsAffineOpen.toSpecΓ_fromSpec] at hrx
279	  · have := hV.isLocalization_basicOpen r
280	    rw [← RingHom.smooth_algebraMap] at hr
281	    convert RingHom.Smooth.propertyIsLocal.respectsIso.1 _
282	      (IsLocalization.algEquiv (.powers r) _ Γ(X, X.basicOpen r)).toRingEquiv hr
283	    ext
284	    dsimp
285	    simp only [IsScalarTower.algebraMap_apply Γ(Y, U) Γ(X, V) (Localization _),
286	      IsLocalization.map_eq]
287	    simp only [algebraMap_toAlgebra, RingHomCompTriple.comp_apply, ← ConcreteCategory.comp_apply,
288	      Scheme.Hom.appLE_map]

291	    IsOpen { x | (f.stalkMap x).hom.FormallySmooth } := by

294	  algebraize [(f.appLE U V hVU).hom]
295	  exact ⟨V, fun y hy ↦ (formallySmooth_stalkMap_iff U hU V hV hVU hy).mpr
296	    (inferInstanceAs (Algebra.IsSmoothAt _ _)), V.2, hxV⟩

841	    I ≤ J ↔ ∀ (x : X) (h : x ∈ U),

854	def arrowStalkMapIso (f : X ⟶ Y) {x : X} (U : Y.Opens)
855	      (hU : IsAffineOpen U) (V : X.Opens) (hV : IsAffineOpen V) (hVU : V ≤ f ⁻¹ᵁ U)
856	      (hx : x ∈ V) :
857	    Arrow.mk (f.stalkMap x) ≅ Arrow.mk (CommRingCat.ofHom <|
858	      Localization.localRingHom _ _ (f.appLE U V hVU).hom
859	        congr($(IsAffineOpen.comap_primeIdealOf_appLE U hU V hV hVU hx).1).symm) := by
860	  let := Y.presheaf.algebra_section_stalk ⟨f x, hVU hx⟩
861	  have := hU.isLocalization_stalk ⟨f x, hVU hx⟩
862	  let := X.presheaf.algebra_section_stalk ⟨x, hx⟩
863	  have := hV.isLocalization_stalk ⟨x, hx⟩
864	  refine Arrow.isoMk' _ _ ?_ ?_ ?_
865	  · exact ((IsLocalization.algEquiv (hU.primeIdealOf ⟨f x, hVU hx⟩).asIdeal.primeCompl
866	      (Y.presheaf.stalk (f x))
867	      (Localization.AtPrime (hU.primeIdealOf ⟨f x, hVU hx⟩).asIdeal)).toCommRingCatIso:)
868	  · exact ((IsLocalization.algEquiv (hV.primeIdealOf ⟨x, hx⟩).asIdeal.primeCompl
869	      (X.presheaf.stalk x)
870	      (Localization.AtPrime (hV.primeIdealOf ⟨x, hx⟩).asIdeal)).toCommRingCatIso:)

872	    ext1

875	    ext a

884	@[simps]

886	    (X : Scheme) {U : X.affineOpens} (f : Γ(X, U)) : X.affineOpens :=
887	  ⟨X.basicOpen f, U.prop.basicOpen f⟩
888

890	    (X : Scheme) {V : X.affineOpens} (f : Γ(X, V.1)) : X.affineBasicOpen f ≤ V :=
891	  X.basicOpen_le f

894	/--
895	In an affine open set `U`, a family of basic open covers `U` iff the sections span `Γ(X, U)`.
896	See `iSup_basicOpen_of_span_eq_top` for the inverse direction without the affine-ness assumption.
897	-/
898	theorem iSup_basicOpen_eq_self_iff {s : Set Γ(X, U)} :
899	    ⨆ f : s, X.basicOpen (f : Γ(X, U)) = U ↔ Ideal.span s = ⊤ := by
900	  trans ⋃ i : s, (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen i.1).1 = Set.univ
901	  · trans hU.fromSpec ⁻¹' (⨆ f : s, X.basicOpen (f : Γ(X, U))).1 = hU.fromSpec ⁻¹' U.1
902	    · refine ⟨fun h => by rw [h], ?_⟩
903	      intro h
904	      apply_fun Set.image hU.fromSpec at h
905	      rw [Set.image_preimage_eq_inter_range, Set.image_preimage_eq_inter_range, hU.range_fromSpec]
906	        at h
907	      simp only [Set.inter_self, Opens.carrier_eq_coe, Set.inter_eq_right] at h
908	      ext1
909	      refine Set.Subset.antisymm ?_ h
910	      simp only [Set.iUnion_subset_iff, SetCoe.forall, Opens.coe_iSup]
911	      intro x _
912	      exact X.basicOpen_le x
913	    · simp only [Opens.iSup_def, Set.preimage_iUnion]
914	      congr! 1
915	      · refine congr_arg (Set.iUnion ·) ?_
916	        ext1 x
917	        exact congr_arg Opens.carrier (hU.fromSpec_preimage_basicOpen _)
918	      · exact congr_arg Opens.carrier hU.fromSpec_preimage_self
919	  · simp only [Opens.carrier_eq_coe, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen_eq_zeroLocus_compl]
920	    rw [← Set.compl_iInter, Set.compl_univ_iff, ← PrimeSpectrum.zeroLocus_iUnion, ←
921	      PrimeSpectrum.zeroLocus_empty_iff_eq_top, PrimeSpectrum.zeroLocus_span]
922	    simp only [Set.iUnion_singleton_eq_range, Subtype.range_val_subtype, Set.setOf_mem_eq]
923	
924	include hU in
925	theorem self_le_iSup_basicOpen_iff {s : Set Γ(X, U)} :
926	    (U ≤ ⨆ f : s, X.basicOpen f.1) ↔ Ideal.span s = ⊤ := by
927	  rw [← hU.iSup_basicOpen_eq_self_iff, @comm _ Eq]
928	  refine ⟨fun h => le_antisymm h ?_, le_of_eq⟩
929	  simp only [iSup_le_iff, SetCoe.forall]
930	  intro x _
931	  exact X.basicOpen_le x

1	/-

3	Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.

5	-/
6	module
7	
8	public import Mathlib.RingTheory.Etale.Kaehler
9	public import Mathlib.RingTheory.Spectrum.Prime.FreeLocus
10	public import Mathlib.RingTheory.Support
11	
12	/-!
13	# Smooth locus of an algebra
14	
15	Most results in this file are proved for algebras of finite presentations.
16	Some of them are true for arbitrary algebras but the proof is substantially harder.
17	
18	## Main results
19	- `Algebra.smoothLocus` : The set of primes that are smooth over the base.
20	- `Algebra.basicOpen_subset_smoothLocus_iff` :
21	  `D(f)` is contained in the smooth locus if and only if `A_f` is smooth over `R`.
22	- `Algebra.smoothLocus_eq_univ_iff` :
23	  The smooth locus is the whole spectrum if and only if `A` is smooth over `R`.
24	- `Algebra.isOpen_smoothLocus` : The smooth locus is open.
25	-/
26	
27	@[expose] public section
28	
29	universe u
30	
31	variable (R A : Type*) [CommRing R] [CommRing A] [Algebra R A]
32	
33	namespace Algebra
34	
35	variable {A} in
36	/--
37	An `R`-algebra `A` is smooth at a prime `p` of `A` if `Aₚ` is formally smooth over `R`.
38	
39	This does not imply `Aₚ` is smooth over `R` under the mathlib definition
40	even if `A` is finitely presented,
41	but it can be shown that this is equivalent to the stacks project definition that `A` is smooth
42	at `p` if and only if there exists `f ∉ p` such that `A_f` is smooth over `R`.
43	See `Algebra.basicOpen_subset_smoothLocus_iff_smooth` and `Algebra.isOpen_smoothLocus`.
44	-/
45	@[stacks 00TB]
46	abbrev IsSmoothAt (p : Ideal A) [p.IsPrime] : Prop :=
47	  Algebra.FormallySmooth R (Localization.AtPrime p)
48	
49	/-- `Algebra.smoothLocus R A` is the set of primes `p` of `A`
50	such that `Aₚ` is formally smooth over `R`. -/
51	def smoothLocus : Set (PrimeSpectrum A) := { p | IsSmoothAt R p.asIdeal }
52	
53	variable {R A}
54	
55	attribute [local instance] Module.finitePresentation_of_projective in
56	lemma smoothLocus_eq_compl_support_inter [EssFiniteType R A] :
57	    smoothLocus R A = (Module.support A (H1Cotangent R A))ᶜ ∩ Module.freeLocus A Ω[A⁄R] := by
58	  ext p
59	  simp only [Set.mem_inter_iff, Set.mem_compl_iff, Module.notMem_support_iff,
60	    Module.mem_freeLocus]
61	  refine (Algebra.formallySmooth_iff _ _).trans (and_comm.trans ?_)
62	  congr! 1
63	  · have := IsLocalizedModule.iso p.asIdeal.primeCompl
64	      (H1Cotangent.map R R A (Localization.AtPrime p.asIdeal))
65	    exact this.subsingleton_congr.symm
66	  · trans Module.Free (Localization.AtPrime p.asIdeal) Ω[Localization.AtPrime p.asIdeal⁄R]
67	    · have : EssFiniteType A (Localization.AtPrime p.asIdeal) :=
68	        .of_isLocalization _ p.asIdeal.primeCompl
69	      have : EssFiniteType R (Localization.AtPrime p.asIdeal) := .comp _ A _
70	      exact ⟨fun _ ↦ Module.free_of_flat_of_isLocalRing, fun _ ↦ inferInstance⟩
71	    · have := IsLocalizedModule.iso p.asIdeal.primeCompl
72	        (KaehlerDifferential.map R R A (Localization.AtPrime p.asIdeal))
73	      have := this.extendScalarsOfIsLocalization
74	        p.asIdeal.primeCompl (Localization.AtPrime p.asIdeal)
75	      exact ⟨fun H ↦ H.of_equiv' this.symm, fun H ↦ H.of_equiv' this⟩
76	
77	lemma basicOpen_subset_smoothLocus_iff [FinitePresentation R A] {f : A} :
78	    ↑(PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f) ⊆ smoothLocus R A ↔
79	      Algebra.FormallySmooth R (Localization.Away f) := by
80	  rw [smoothLocus_eq_compl_support_inter, Set.subset_inter_iff, Set.subset_compl_comm,
81	    PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen_eq_zeroLocus_compl, compl_compl,
82	    ← LocalizedModule.subsingleton_iff_support_subset,
83	    Algebra.formallySmooth_iff, iff_comm, and_comm]
84	  congr! 1
85	  · have := IsLocalizedModule.iso (.powers f) (H1Cotangent.map R R A (Localization.Away f))
86	    rw [this.subsingleton_congr]
87	  · rw [← PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen_eq_zeroLocus_compl, Module.basicOpen_subset_freeLocus_iff]
88	    have := IsLocalizedModule.iso (.powers f)
89	        (KaehlerDifferential.map R R A (Localization.Away f))
90	    have := this.extendScalarsOfIsLocalization (.powers f) (Localization.Away f)
91	    exact ⟨fun _ ↦ .of_equiv this.symm, fun _ ↦ .of_equiv this⟩
92	
93	lemma basicOpen_subset_smoothLocus_iff_smooth [FinitePresentation R A] {f : A} :
94	    ↑(PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f) ⊆ smoothLocus R A ↔
95	      Algebra.Smooth R (Localization.Away f) := by
96	  have : FinitePresentation A (Localization.Away f) := IsLocalization.Away.finitePresentation f
97	  rw [basicOpen_subset_smoothLocus_iff]
98	  exact ⟨fun H ↦ ⟨H, .trans _ A _⟩, fun H ↦ H.1⟩
99	
100	lemma smoothLocus_eq_univ_iff [FinitePresentation R A] :
101	    smoothLocus R A = Set.univ ↔ Algebra.FormallySmooth R A := by
102	  have := IsLocalization.atUnits A (.powers 1) (S := Localization.Away (1 : A)) (by simp)
103	  rw [Algebra.FormallySmooth.iff_of_equiv (this.restrictScalars R),
104	    ← basicOpen_subset_smoothLocus_iff]
105	  simp
106	
107	lemma smoothLocus_eq_univ [Smooth R A] : smoothLocus R A = Set.univ := by
108	  rw [smoothLocus_eq_univ_iff]
109	  infer_instance
110	
111	lemma smoothLocus_comap_of_isLocalization {Af : Type*} [CommRing Af] [Algebra A Af] [Algebra R Af]
112	    [IsScalarTower R A Af] (f : A) [IsLocalization.Away f Af] :
113	    PrimeSpectrum.comap (algebraMap A Af) ⁻¹' smoothLocus R A = smoothLocus R Af := by
114	  ext p
115	  let q := PrimeSpectrum.comap (algebraMap A Af) p
116	  have : IsLocalization.AtPrime (Localization.AtPrime p.asIdeal) q.asIdeal :=
117	    IsLocalization.isLocalization_isLocalization_atPrime_isLocalization (.powers f) _ p.asIdeal
118	  refine Algebra.FormallySmooth.iff_of_equiv ?_
119	  exact (IsLocalization.algEquiv q.asIdeal.primeCompl _ _).restrictScalars R
120	
121	-- Note that this does not follow directly from `smoothLocus_eq_compl_support_inter` because
122	-- `H¹(L_{S/R})` is not necessarily finitely generated.
123	open PrimeSpectrum in
124	lemma isOpen_smoothLocus [FinitePresentation R A] : IsOpen (smoothLocus R A) := by
125	  rw [isOpen_iff_forall_mem_open]
126	  intro x hx
127	  obtain ⟨_, ⟨_, ⟨f, rfl⟩, rfl⟩, hxf, hf⟩ :=
128	    isBasis_basic_opens.exists_subset_of_mem_open
129	    (smoothLocus_eq_compl_support_inter.le hx).2 Module.isOpen_freeLocus
130	  rw [Module.basicOpen_subset_freeLocus_iff] at hf
131	  let Af := Localization.Away f
132	  have : Algebra.FinitePresentation A (Localization.Away f) :=
133	    IsLocalization.Away.finitePresentation f
134	  have : Algebra.FinitePresentation R (Localization.Away f) :=
135	    .trans _ A _
136	  have : IsOpen (smoothLocus R Af) := by
137	    have := IsLocalizedModule.iso (.powers f)
138	      (KaehlerDifferential.map R R A (Localization.Away f))
139	    have := this.extendScalarsOfIsLocalization (.powers f) (Localization.Away f)
140	    have := Module.Projective.of_equiv this
141	    rw [smoothLocus_eq_compl_support_inter, Module.support_eq_zeroLocus]
142	    exact (isClosed_zeroLocus _).isOpen_compl.inter Module.isOpen_freeLocus
143	  rw [← smoothLocus_comap_of_isLocalization f] at this
144	  replace this := (PrimeSpectrum.localization_away_isOpenEmbedding Af f).isOpenMap _ this
145	  rw [Set.image_preimage_eq_inter_range, localization_away_comap_range Af f] at this
146	  exact ⟨_, Set.inter_subset_left, this, hx, hxf⟩
147	
148	variable (R) in
149	open PrimeSpectrum in
150	lemma IsSmoothAt.exists_notMem_smooth [FinitePresentation R A] (p : Ideal A) [p.IsPrime]
151	    [IsSmoothAt R p] :
152	    ∃ f ∉ p, Smooth R (Localization.Away f) := by
153	  obtain ⟨_, ⟨_, ⟨f, rfl⟩, rfl⟩, hxf, hf⟩ :=
154	    isBasis_basic_opens.exists_subset_of_mem_open ‹⟨p, ‹_›⟩ ∈ smoothLocus R A› isOpen_smoothLocus
155	  refine ⟨f, by simpa using hxf, ⟨?_, inferInstance⟩⟩
156	  rwa [basicOpen_subset_smoothLocus_iff] at hf
157	
158	end Algebra

6	module

13	/-!
14	# Standard smooth ring homomorphisms
15	
16	In this file we define standard smooth ring homomorphisms and show their
17	meta properties.
18	
19	## Main definitions
20	
21	- `RingHom.IsStandardSmooth`: A ring homomorphism `R →+* S` is standard smooth if `S` is standard
22	  smooth as `R`-algebra.
23	- `RingHom.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension n`: A ring homomorphism `R →+* S` is standard
24	  smooth of relative dimension `n` if `S` is standard smooth of relative dimension `n` as
25	  `R`-algebra.
26	
27	## Notes
28	
29	This contribution was created as part of the AIM workshop "Formalizing algebraic geometry"
30	in June 2024.
31	
32	-/
33	
34	@[expose] public section
35	universe t t' w w' u v
36	
37	variable (n m : ℕ)
38	
39	open TensorProduct
40	
41	namespace RingHom
42	
43	variable {R : Type u} {S : Type v} [CommRing R] [CommRing S]
44	
45	/-- A ring homomorphism `R →+* S` is standard smooth if `S` is standard smooth as `R`-algebra. -/
46	@[algebraize RingHom.IsStandardSmooth.toAlgebra]
47	def IsStandardSmooth (f : R →+* S) : Prop :=
48	  @Algebra.IsStandardSmooth _ _ _ _ f.toAlgebra
49	
50	lemma isStandardSmooth_algebraMap [Algebra R S] :
51	    (algebraMap R S).IsStandardSmooth ↔ Algebra.IsStandardSmooth R S := by
52	  rw [RingHom.IsStandardSmooth, toAlgebra_algebraMap]
53	
54	/-- Helper lemma for the `algebraize` tactic -/
55	lemma IsStandardSmooth.toAlgebra {f : R →+* S} (hf : IsStandardSmooth f) :
56	    @Algebra.IsStandardSmooth R S _ _ f.toAlgebra := hf
57	
58	/-- A ring homomorphism `R →+* S` is standard smooth of relative dimension `n` if
59	`S` is standard smooth of relative dimension `n` as `R`-algebra. -/
60	@[algebraize RingHom.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.toAlgebra]
61	def IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension (f : R →+* S) : Prop :=
62	  @Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension n _ _ _ _ f.toAlgebra
63	
64	lemma isStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension_algebraMap [Algebra R S] :
65	    (algebraMap R S).IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension n ↔
66	      Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension n R S := by
67	  rw [RingHom.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension, toAlgebra_algebraMap]
68	
69	/-- Helper lemma for the `algebraize` tactic -/
70	lemma IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.toAlgebra {f : R →+* S}
71	    (hf : IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension n f) :
72	    @Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension n R S _ _ f.toAlgebra := hf
73	
74	lemma IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.isStandardSmooth (f : R →+* S)
75	    (hf : IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension n f) :
76	    IsStandardSmooth f := by
77	  algebraize [f]
78	  exact Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.isStandardSmooth n
79	
80	variable {n m}
81	
82	variable (R) in
83	lemma IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.id :
84	    IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension 0 (RingHom.id R) :=
85	  Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.id R
86	
87	lemma IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.equiv (e : R ≃+* S) :
88	    IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension 0 (e : R →+* S) := by
89	  algebraize [e.toRingHom]
90	  exact Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.of_algebraMap_bijective e.bijective
91	
92	variable {T : Type*} [CommRing T]
93	
94	lemma IsStandardSmooth.comp {g : S →+* T} {f : R →+* S}
95	    (hg : IsStandardSmooth g) (hf : IsStandardSmooth f) :
96	    IsStandardSmooth (g.comp f) := by
97	  rw [IsStandardSmooth]
98	  algebraize [f, g, (g.comp f)]
99	  exact Algebra.IsStandardSmooth.trans R S T

141	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
142	
143	namespace Scheme
144	
145	/-- **Coheight ↔ Krull dimension bridge for scheme points.** For any scheme
146	`X` and any point `z : X`, the Krull dimension of the stalk at `z` equals the
147	coheight of `z` in the underlying topological space (with the specialisation
148	preorder). -/
149	theorem ringKrullDim_stalk_eq_coheight
150	    (X : Scheme.{u}) (z : X) :
151	    ringKrullDim (X.presheaf.stalk z) = Order.coheight z := by
152	  -- Step 1: pick an affine open U ∋ z.
153	  obtain ⟨U, hU, hzU, _⟩ :=
154	    exists_isAffineOpen_mem_and_subset (X := X) (x := z) (U := ⊤)
155	      (by trivial)
156	  -- Step 2: name the prime corresponding to z inside U.
157	  set p : PrimeSpectrum Γ(X, U) := hU.primeIdealOf ⟨z, hzU⟩ with hp
158	  -- Bind the section-to-stalk algebra explicitly so the localisation theorem
159	  -- sees the expected `Algebra` instance.
160	  letI : Algebra Γ(X, U) (X.presheaf.stalk z) :=
161	    TopCat.Presheaf.algebra_section_stalk X.presheaf ⟨z, hzU⟩
162	  -- Step 3: stalk is the localisation of Γ(X,U) at p.
163	  haveI hloc : IsLocalization.AtPrime (X.presheaf.stalk z) p.asIdeal :=
164	    hU.isLocalization_stalk ⟨z, hzU⟩
165	  -- Step 4: ringKrullDim stalk = p.asIdeal.height (= primeHeight p).
166	  have h4 :
167	      ringKrullDim (X.presheaf.stalk z)
168	        = (Order.height (α := PrimeSpectrum Γ(X, U)) p : WithBot ℕ∞) := by
169	    rw [IsLocalization.AtPrime.ringKrullDim_eq_height
170	          (R := Γ(X, U)) p.asIdeal (X.presheaf.stalk z),
171	        Ideal.height_eq_primeHeight]
172	    rfl
173	  -- Step 5: relate height(p) to coheight(z) via Decls 1 + 2 and the
174	  -- homeomorphism `Spec Γ(X,U) ≃ U.toScheme` from `hU.isoSpec`.
175	  -- (5a) Decl 1: coheight z in X = coheight ⟨z, hzU⟩ in U.1 subspace.
176	  have h1' :
177	      @Order.coheight X (specializationPreorder X) z
178	        = @Order.coheight U.1 (specializationPreorder U.1) ⟨z, hzU⟩ :=
179	    Order.coheight_eq_of_isOpenEmbedding (X := X) (U := U.1) U.isOpen z hzU
180	  -- For a Scheme, the standard `Preorder` instance is definitionally
181	  -- `specializationPreorder`. Cast h1' to use the Scheme's preorder on the
182	  -- X side and U.toScheme's preorder on the U side (both defeq).
183	  have h1 :
184	      Order.coheight (α := X) z
185	        = Order.coheight (α := U.toScheme) ⟨z, hzU⟩ := h1'
186	  -- (5b) The scheme iso `hU.isoSpec : U.toScheme ≅ Spec Γ(X,U)` is a Scheme
187	  -- iso; the underlying carrier-level map is a homeomorphism, giving an
188	  -- order iso of spec preorders.
189	  let hHomeo : U.toScheme ≃ₜ Spec Γ(X, U) :=
190	    TopCat.homeoOfIso (Scheme.forgetToTop.mapIso hU.isoSpec)
191	  let eOrder : U.toScheme ≃o Spec Γ(X, U) :=
192	    { toEquiv := hHomeo.toEquiv
193	      map_rel_iff' := by
194	        intro a b
195	        constructor
196	        · intro h
197	          have hsp : hHomeo b ⤳ hHomeo a := h
198	          have hsp' := hsp.map hHomeo.symm.continuous
199	          rw [hHomeo.symm_apply_apply, hHomeo.symm_apply_apply] at hsp'
200	          exact (hsp' : a ≤ b)
201	        · intro h
202	          have hsp : b ⤳ a := h
203	          exact (hsp.map hHomeo.continuous : hHomeo a ≤ hHomeo b) }
204	  have h2 : Order.coheight (α := U.toScheme) ⟨z, hzU⟩
205	      = Order.coheight (α := Spec Γ(X, U)) (eOrder ⟨z, hzU⟩) :=
206	    (Order.coheight_orderIso eOrder ⟨z, hzU⟩).symm
207	  have h3 : eOrder ⟨z, hzU⟩ = p := rfl
208	  -- (5c) Decl 2: coheight (Spec R) p = height (PrimeSpectrum R) p.
209	  have h6 : Order.coheight (α := Spec Γ(X, U)) p
210	      = Order.height (α := PrimeSpectrum Γ(X, U))
211	          ⟨p.asIdeal, p.isPrime⟩ :=
212	    Order.coheight_spec_eq_height_primeSpectrum p
213	  have h7 : (⟨p.asIdeal, p.isPrime⟩ : PrimeSpectrum Γ(X, U)) = p := rfl
214	  -- Assemble.
215	  rw [h4, h1, h2, h3, h6, h7]
216	
217	end Scheme
218	
219	end AlgebraicGeometry
220	
221	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
222	
223	namespace Scheme
224	
225	/-- **Codim-1 wrapper: coheight = 1 ⟹ stalk Krull dim ≤ 1.** Consumer-facing
226	specialisation of `ringKrullDim_stalk_eq_coheight` for the codim-`1` case used
227	in `Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean` and `RiemannRoch/WeilDivisor.lean`. -/
228	lemma ringKrullDimLE_of_coheight_eq_one
229	    (X : Scheme.{u}) (z : X) (hz : Order.coheight z = 1) :
230	    Ring.KrullDimLE 1 (X.presheaf.stalk z) := by
231	  rw [Ring.krullDimLE_iff, ringKrullDim_stalk_eq_coheight, hz]
232	  -- Goal: ((1 : ℕ∞) : WithBot ℕ∞) ≤ ((1 : ℕ) : WithBot ℕ∞)
233	  norm_cast
234	
235	end Scheme
236	
237	end AlgebraicGeometry

6	module
