11	
12	/-!
13	# Span rank under operations
14	
15	In this file we show how operations on submodules interact with `Submodule.spanRank`.
16	
17	# Main Results
18	
19	* `Submodule.spanRank_baseChange_le`: Base change doesn't increase the span rank.
20	
21	* `TensorProduct.spanFinrank_top_eq_of_residueField`: For a finitely generated module over
22	  a local ring, the dimension of the base change to the residue field is equal to its span rank.
23	
24	* `IsLocalRing.spanFinrank_maximalIdeal_eq_finrank_cotangentSpace`: The minimal number of
25	  generators of the unique maximal ideal is equal to the dimension of the cotangent space.
26	
27	-/
28	
29	@[expose] public section
30	
31	open IsLocalRing TensorProduct Submodule
32	
33	variable {R A : Type*} [CommRing R] [CommRing A] [Algebra R A]
34	  {M : Type*} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] (N : Submodule R M)
35	
36	lemma Submodule.spanRank_baseChange_le : (N.baseChange A).spanRank ≤ N.spanRank.lift := by
37	  obtain ⟨s, hs₁, hs₂⟩ := N.exists_span_set_card_eq_spanRank
38	  grw [← hs₁, ← hs₂, baseChange_span, spanRank_span_le_card]
39	  convert Cardinal.mk_image_le_lift (f := TensorProduct.mk R A M 1) (s := s)
40	  · exact (Cardinal.lift_id' _).symm
41	  · exact Cardinal.lift_umax.symm
42	
43	lemma Submodule.FG.spanFinrank_baseChange_le (fg : N.FG) :
44	    (N.baseChange A).spanFinrank ≤ N.spanFinrank := by
45	  grw [spanFinrank, spanRank_baseChange_le, Cardinal.toNat_lift, spanFinrank]
46	  simp [Cardinal.lift_lt_aleph0, spanRank_finite_iff_fg.mpr fg]
47	
48	lemma TensorProduct.spanRank_top_le : (⊤ : Submodule A (A ⊗[R] N)).spanRank ≤ N.spanRank.lift := by
49	  grw [← Submodule.baseChange_top, ← N.spanRank_top, spanRank_baseChange_le]
50	
51	lemma TensorProduct.spanFinrank_top_le_of_fg (fg : N.FG) :
52	    (⊤ : Submodule A (A ⊗[R] N)).spanFinrank ≤ N.spanFinrank := by
53	  grw [← Submodule.baseChange_top, ← N.spanFinrank_top, (N.fg_top.mpr fg).spanFinrank_baseChange_le]
54	
55	variable [IsLocalRing R]
56	local notation "𝓀" => ResidueField R
57	
58	lemma TensorProduct.spanFinrank_top_eq_of_residueField (fg : N.FG) :
59	    (⊤ : Submodule 𝓀 (𝓀 ⊗[R] N)).spanFinrank = N.spanFinrank := by
60	  let : Module.Finite R N := Module.Finite.iff_fg.mpr fg
61	  apply (TensorProduct.spanFinrank_top_le_of_fg N fg).antisymm
62	  obtain ⟨s, hs₁, hs₂⟩ := (⊤ : Submodule 𝓀 (𝓀 ⊗[R] N)).exists_span_set_card_eq_spanRank
63	  have hs₃ : s.Finite := Cardinal.mk_lt_aleph0_iff.mp (by simpa [hs₁] using Module.Finite.fg_top)
64	  let t := Function.surjInv (mk_surjective R N 𝓀 residue_surjective) '' s
65	  have ht₁ : mk R 𝓀 N 1 '' t = s := by rw [← Set.image_comp, Function.comp_surjInv, s.image_id]
66	  have ht₂ : span R t = ⊤ := by
67	    rwa [← restrictScalars_eq_top_iff R, restrictScalars_span _ _ (by exact residue_surjective),
68	      ← ht₁, ← map_span, map_tensorProduct_mk_eq_top] at hs₂
69	  grw [← N.spanFinrank_top, ← ht₂, spanFinrank_span_le_ncard_of_finite (hs₃.image _), spanFinrank,
70	    ← hs₁, Set.ncard_image_le hs₃]
71	  rfl
72	
73	namespace IsLocalRing
74	
75	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
76	lemma spanFinrank_eq_finrank_quotient (N : Submodule R M) (fg : N.FG) :
77	    N.spanFinrank =
78	      Module.finrank (R ⧸ maximalIdeal R) (N ⧸ (maximalIdeal R) • (⊤ : Submodule R N)) := by
79	  let : Module 𝓀 (N ⧸ maximalIdeal R • (⊤ : Submodule R N)) :=
80	    inferInstanceAs (Module (R ⧸ maximalIdeal R) _)
81	  let : IsScalarTower R 𝓀 (N ⧸ maximalIdeal R • (⊤ : Submodule R N)) :=
82	    inferInstanceAs (IsScalarTower R (R ⧸ maximalIdeal R) _)
83	  rw [← spanFinrank_top_eq_of_residueField N fg, ← Module.finrank_eq_spanFinrank_of_free]
84	  let e : 𝓀 ⊗[R] N ≃ₗ[𝓀] N ⧸ (maximalIdeal R) • (⊤ : Submodule R N) :=
85	    (quotTensorEquivQuotSMul N (maximalIdeal R)).extendScalarsOfSurjective residue_surjective
86	  exact e.finrank_eq
87	
88	lemma spanFinrank_maximalIdeal_eq_finrank_cotangentSpace_of_fg (fg : (maximalIdeal R).FG) :
89	    (maximalIdeal R).spanFinrank = Module.finrank (ResidueField R) (CotangentSpace R) :=
90	  spanFinrank_eq_finrank_quotient _ fg
91	
92	variable (R) in
93	lemma spanFinrank_maximalIdeal_eq_finrank_cotangentSpace [IsNoetherianRing R] :
94	    (maximalIdeal R).spanFinrank = Module.finrank (ResidueField R) (CotangentSpace R) :=
95	  spanFinrank_maximalIdeal_eq_finrank_cotangentSpace_of_fg (maximalIdeal R).fg_of_isNoetherianRing
96	
97	end IsLocalRing

790	theorem isLocalization_stalk' (y : PrimeSpectrum Γ(X, U)) (hy : hU.fromSpec y ∈ U) :

/-- **Stage 2 (Stacks 00T7, smooth ⟹ standard smooth presentation at a
point).** For a smooth morphism `X.hom : X.left ⟶ Spec (.of k̄)`, every
point `z : X.left` admits affine open neighbourhoods `U ⊆ Spec (.of k̄)`,
`V ⊆ X.left` with `z ∈ V` and `V ⊆ X.hom ⁻¹' U` such that the induced
ring map `(X.hom.appLE U V e).hom` is `IsStandardSmooth`.

Axiom-clean: re-exports `AlgebraicGeometry.Smooth.exists_isStandardSmooth`
(the scheme-level packaging of Stacks tag `00T7`) at the project's
`Over (Spec (.of k̄))` calling convention. -/
private theorem exists_isStandardSmooth_at_of_smooth
    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
    (X : Over (Spec (.of kbar)))
    [Smooth X.hom]
    (z : X.left) :
    ∃ (U : (Spec (.of kbar)).Opens) (_ : IsAffineOpen U)
      (V : X.left.Opens) (_ : IsAffineOpen V)
      (_hzV : z ∈ V) (e : V ≤ X.hom ⁻¹ᵁ U),
      (X.hom.appLE U V e).hom.IsStandardSmooth :=
  AlgebraicGeometry.Smooth.exists_isStandardSmooth X.hom z

/-- **Stage 2 (Stacks 00T7, smooth ⟹ standard smooth presentation at a
point).** For a smooth morphism `X.hom : X.left ⟶ Spec (.of k̄)`, every
point `z : X.left` admits affine open neighbourhoods `U ⊆ Spec (.of k̄)`,
`V ⊆ X.left` with `z ∈ V` and `V ⊆ X.hom ⁻¹' U` such that the induced
ring map `(X.hom.appLE U V e).hom` is `IsStandardSmooth`.

Axiom-clean: re-exports `AlgebraicGeometry.Smooth.exists_isStandardSmooth`
(the scheme-level packaging of Stacks tag `00T7`) at the project's
`Over (Spec (.of k̄))` calling convention. -/
private theorem exists_isStandardSmooth_at_of_smooth
    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
    (X : Over (Spec (.of kbar)))
    [Smooth X.hom]
    (z : X.left) :
    ∃ (U : (Spec (.of kbar)).Opens) (_ : IsAffineOpen U)
      (V : X.left.Opens) (_ : IsAffineOpen V)
      (_hzV : z ∈ V) (e : V ≤ X.hom ⁻¹ᵁ U),
      (X.hom.appLE U V e).hom.IsStandardSmooth :=
  AlgebraicGeometry.Smooth.exists_isStandardSmooth X.hom z

/-- **Stage 3 (Stacks 00TT, scheme-to-algebra bridge for the standard smooth
presentation).** Iter-192 axiom-clean intermediate helper: combines Stage 2's
existence-of-standard-smooth-presentation output with the `RingHom`-to-`Algebra`
bridge `RingHom.IsStandardSmooth.toAlgebra`, plus the affine-open stalk
localisation `IsAffineOpen.isLocalization_stalk`. Returns the full algebra-side
package needed by Stages 4-5 of the regularity proof: an affine neighbourhood
`V ∋ z`, an `Algebra Γ(Spec _, U) Γ(X.left, V)` instance under which
`Γ(X.left, V)` is `Algebra.IsStandardSmooth` over `Γ(Spec _, U)`, plus the
`IsLocalization.AtPrime` witness identifying the stalk at `z` with the
localisation of `Γ(X.left, V)` at the prime ideal of `z`.

Axiom-clean: composition of `exists_isStandardSmooth_at_of_smooth` +
`RingHom.IsStandardSmooth.toAlgebra` + `IsAffineOpen.isLocalization_stalk`.

This is the iter-192 Lane M↓ HARD BAR new axiom-clean helper: it packages the
"smooth ⟹ stalk is localisation of a standard-smooth Γ(Spec, U)-algebra"
content as a standalone declaration that downstream Stages 4-5 (the genuine
Stacks 02JK + 00OE Mathlib-gap chain) can consume directly. -/
private theorem exists_algebra_isStandardSmooth_section_stalk_isLocalization_of_smooth
    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
    (X : Over (Spec (.of kbar)))
    [Smooth X.hom]
    (z : X.left) :
    ∃ (U : (Spec (.of kbar)).Opens) (_hU : IsAffineOpen U)
      (V : X.left.Opens) (hV : IsAffineOpen V)
      (hzV : z ∈ V) (_e : V ≤ X.hom ⁻¹ᵁ U)
      (alg : Algebra Γ(Spec (.of kbar), U) Γ(X.left, V)),
      @Algebra.IsStandardSmooth _ _ _ _ alg ∧
      letI := TopCat.Presheaf.algebra_section_stalk X.left.presheaf ⟨z, hzV⟩
      IsLocalization.AtPrime
        (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) (hV.primeIdealOf ⟨z, hzV⟩).asIdeal := by
  obtain ⟨U, hU, V, hV, hzV, e, hStdSmooth⟩ :=
    exists_isStandardSmooth_at_of_smooth X z
  refine ⟨U, hU, V, hV, hzV, e, (X.hom.appLE U V e).hom.toAlgebra,
    hStdSmooth.toAlgebra, ?_⟩
  -- Localisation witness: the affine-open stalk is `Aₚ` for `p = primeIdealOf z`.
  letI := TopCat.Presheaf.algebra_section_stalk X.left.presheaf ⟨z, hzV⟩
  exact hV.isLocalization_stalk ⟨z, hzV⟩

private theorem isRegularLocalRing_stalk_of_smooth
    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
    (X : Over (Spec (.of kbar)))
    [Smooth X.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
    [IsSeparated X.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]
    [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left]
    (z : X.left) :
    IsRegularLocalRing (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) := by
  -- Stage 1 (axiom-clean): smooth ⟹ flat at the stalk z.
  have _hflat : ((X.hom.stalkMap z).hom).Flat :=
    stalkMap_flat_of_smooth X z
  -- Stage 2 (axiom-clean): smooth ⟹ standard smooth presentation on
  -- some affine neighbourhood of z.
  obtain ⟨U, hU, V, hV, hzV, e, hStdSmooth⟩ :=
    exists_isStandardSmooth_at_of_smooth X z
  -- Stages 3-4 (Mathlib gap, Stacks 02JK + 00OE):
  --   from the standard smooth presentation `hStdSmooth` (with polynomial
  --   generators), the maximal ideal of every localisation of the affine
  --   chart `V = Spec A` at a prime `𝔭` is generated by a regular sequence
  --   of length equal to the Krull dimension of `A_𝔭`; hence the local
  --   ring is regular. The stalk `X.left.presheaf.stalk z` is identified
  --   with `A_𝔭` via the affine-open-stalk Mathlib bridge, transporting
  --   regularity. This chain remains to be assembled (iter-192+ work,
  --   either via the cotangent / sheaf-of-relative-differentials route
  --   upstream or via a project-side `02JK`/`00OE` build).

input.new_string:
private theorem isRegularLocalRing_stalk_of_smooth
    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
    (X : Over (Spec (.of kbar)))
    [Smooth X.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
    [IsSeparated X.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]
    [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left]
    (z : X.left) :
    IsRegularLocalRing (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) := by
  -- Stage 1 (axiom-clean): smooth ⟹ flat at the stalk z.
  have _hflat : ((X.hom.stalkMap z).hom).Flat :=
    stalkMap_flat_of_smooth X z
  -- Stage 3 (axiom-clean): smooth ⟹ algebra-side standard-smooth presentation
  -- on an affine neighbourhood V ∋ z, with the stalk identified as the
  -- localisation of `Γ(X.left, V)` at the prime ideal of `z`. This composes
  -- Stage 1 + Stage 2 + the `RingHom.IsStandardSmooth.toAlgebra` bridge + the
  -- affine-open `IsLocalization.AtPrime` stalk witness.
  obtain ⟨U, hU, V, hV, hzV, e, alg, hSSalg, hLoc⟩ :=
    exists_algebra_isStandardSmooth_section_stalk_isLocalization_of_smooth X z
  -- Activate the algebra structure (= `(X.hom.appLE U V e).hom.toAlgebra`)
  -- and the stalk-section algebra structure so downstream typeclass search
  -- sees both bridges.
  letI : Algebra Γ(Spec (.of kbar), U) Γ(X.left, V) := alg
  haveI hSS : Algebra.IsStandardSmooth Γ(Spec (.of kbar), U) Γ(X.left, V) := hSSalg
  letI : Algebra Γ(X.left, V) (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) :=
    TopCat.Presheaf.algebra_section_stalk X.left.presheaf ⟨z, hzV⟩
  haveI hLoc' : IsLocalization.AtPrime
      (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) (hV.primeIdealOf ⟨z, hzV⟩).asIdeal := hLoc
  -- Stages 4-5 (Mathlib gap, Stacks 02JK + 00OE, the substantive
  -- Jacobian-criterion regularity chain):
  --
  -- From `hSS : Algebra.IsStandardSmooth Γ(Spec _, U) Γ(X.left, V)`,
  -- `Algebra.IsStandardSmooth.iff_exists_basis_kaehlerDifferential` extracts
  --   `Subsingleton (H¹ Cotangent _ _) ∧ Module.Basis ι _ Ω[Γ(X.left, V)⁄Γ(Spec, U)]`
  -- where the basis is of the form `{d sᵢ}ᵢ` for some `sᵢ ∈ Γ(X.left, V)`. In
  -- particular `Ω[Γ(X.left, V)⁄Γ(Spec, U)]` is `Module.Free` of rank
  -- `relative dimension n` (= `Krull dim of localisation at any prime` by the
  -- smooth-algebra dimension formula).
  --
  -- Localising at `p = hV.primeIdealOf ⟨z, hzV⟩` (= `hLoc'`):
  -- `KaehlerDifferential.map` + `IsLocalizedModule` transport freeness to the
  -- stalk:
  --   `Ω[stalk z⁄Γ(Spec, U)] ≃ stalk z ⊗_{Γ(X.left, V)} Ω[Γ(X.left, V)⁄Γ(Spec, U)]`
  -- is free of the same rank `n` as a stalk-z-module.
  --
  -- Quotienting by the maximal ideal of the stalk (passing to the residue
  -- field κ(p)) and using the cotangent-sequence trivialisation
  -- `Ω[Γ(Spec, U)⁄Γ(Spec, U)] = 0` (since `Γ(Spec, U)` is itself a
  -- localisation of the field `kbar`, so the base is a field and there are no
  -- non-zero base differentials), gives:
  --   `κ(p) ⊗_{stalk z} Ω[stalk z⁄Γ(Spec, U)]` ≅ `m_p / m_p²` as κ(p)-spaces,
  -- whence `Module.finrank κ(p) (CotangentSpace _) = n`.
  --
  -- Finally `ringKrullDim (stalk z) = n` (Stacks 00OE: smooth-algebra
  -- dimension formula at a prime), and these together with
  -- `IsRegularLocalRing.iff_finrank_cotangentSpace` close the goal.
  --
  -- The two Mathlib gaps remaining at commit `b80f227`:
  --   (a) Free `Ω` after localisation at a prime (transport via
  --       `IsLocalizedModule` of `KaehlerDifferential.map`).
  --   (b) Smooth-algebra dimension formula `ringKrullDim (Aₚ) = relative dim`.
  -- Both are work for iter-193+ blueprint expansions; the Stage 3 helper
  -- above ensures the scaffold is reusable as soon as either lands.
  sorry

294	/-- **Stacks `00TT`, Jacobian-criterion direction (regularity conclusion).**
295	For a smooth integral variety `X` over an algebraically closed field `k̄`, the
296	stalk of `X` at every point is a regular local ring.
297	
298	This is the scheme-level packaging of Stacks tag `00TT` (Lemma 10.140.3
299	"Smooth algebras over fields"): given an affine chart `Spec A ⊆ X`, the
300	\(\bar k\)-algebra `A` is smooth at every prime `𝔮`, and the localisation
301	`A_𝔮 = X.presheaf.stalk z` (for `z ∈ X` lying over `𝔮`) is regular by the
302	"in this case the local ring `A_𝔮` is regular" clause of the cited lemma.
303	
304	**Mathlib status at commit `b80f227` (iter-191 Lane M↓ first scaffold).**
305	The 4-stage proof chain is now scaffolded as follows.
306	
307	* Stage 1 (`stalkMap_flat_of_smooth`): smooth ⟹ flat at every stalk.
308	  Axiom-clean.
309	* Stage 2 (`exists_isStandardSmooth_at_of_smooth`): smooth ⟹ standard
310	  smooth presentation locally. Axiom-clean.
311	* Stage 3-4 (Stacks `02JK`/`00OE`): polynomial generators ⟹ regular
312	  sequence ⟹ regular local ring. The Jacobian-criterion content
313	  ("standard smooth presentation ⟹ the maximal ideal of every stalk is
314	  generated by a regular sequence of length = Krull dim") is the
315	  Mathlib gap, captured here as a *single named scoped* `sorry` consuming
316	  the Stage 1-2 axiom-clean outputs.
317	
318	Once Stages 3-4 close upstream, this helper closes axiom-clean and every
319	downstream consumer (`smooth_codim_one_maximalIdeal_isPrincipal_and_ne_bot`,
320	`localRing_dvr_of_codim_one`) inherits the closure automatically.
321	
322	Blueprint reference: `\cref{lem:smooth_to_regular_local_ring}` in
323	`blueprint/src/chapters/Albanese_CodimOneExtension.tex` (also Stacks
324	\href{https://stacks.math.columbia.edu/tag/00TT}{tag 00TT}). -/
325	private theorem isRegularLocalRing_stalk_of_smooth
326	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
327	    (X : Over (Spec (.of kbar)))
328	    [Smooth X.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
329	    [IsSeparated X.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]
330	    [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left]
331	    (z : X.left) :
332	    IsRegularLocalRing (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) := by
333	  -- Stage 1 (axiom-clean): smooth ⟹ flat at the stalk z.
334	  have _hflat : ((X.hom.stalkMap z).hom).Flat :=
335	    stalkMap_flat_of_smooth X z
336	  -- Stage 3 (axiom-clean): smooth ⟹ algebra-side standard-smooth presentation
337	  -- on an affine neighbourhood V ∋ z, with the stalk identified as the
338	  -- localisation of `Γ(X.left, V)` at the prime ideal of `z`. This composes
339	  -- Stage 1 + Stage 2 + the `RingHom.IsStandardSmooth.toAlgebra` bridge + the
340	  -- affine-open `IsLocalization.AtPrime` stalk witness.
341	  obtain ⟨U, hU, V, hV, hzV, e, alg, hSSalg, hLoc⟩ :=
342	    exists_algebra_isStandardSmooth_section_stalk_isLocalization_of_smooth X z
343	  -- Activate the algebra structure (= `(X.hom.appLE U V e).hom.toAlgebra`)
344	  -- and the stalk-section algebra structure so downstream typeclass search
345	  -- sees both bridges.
346	  letI : Algebra Γ(Spec (.of kbar), U) Γ(X.left, V) := alg
347	  haveI hSS : Algebra.IsStandardSmooth Γ(Spec (.of kbar), U) Γ(X.left, V) := hSSalg
348	  letI : Algebra Γ(X.left, V) (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) :=
349	    TopCat.Presheaf.algebra_section_stalk X.left.presheaf ⟨z, hzV⟩
350	  haveI hLoc' : IsLocalization.AtPrime
351	      (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) (hV.primeIdealOf ⟨z, hzV⟩).asIdeal := hLoc
352	  -- Stages 4-5 (Mathlib gap, Stacks 02JK + 00OE, the substantive
353	  -- Jacobian-criterion regularity chain):
354	  --
355	  -- From `hSS : Algebra.IsStandardSmooth Γ(Spec _, U) Γ(X.left, V)`,
356	  -- `Algebra.IsStandardSmooth.iff_exists_basis_kaehlerDifferential` extracts
357	  --   `Subsingleton (H¹ Cotangent _ _) ∧ Module.Basis ι _ Ω[Γ(X.left, V)⁄Γ(Spec, U)]`
358	  -- where the basis is of the form `{d sᵢ}ᵢ` for some `sᵢ ∈ Γ(X.left, V)`. In
359	  -- particular `Ω[Γ(X.left, V)⁄Γ(Spec, U)]` is `Module.Free` of rank
360	  -- `relative dimension n` (= `Krull dim of localisation at any prime` by the
361	  -- smooth-algebra dimension formula).
362	  --
363	  -- Localising at `p = hV.primeIdealOf ⟨z, hzV⟩` (= `hLoc'`):
364	  -- `KaehlerDifferential.map` + `IsLocalizedModule` transport freeness to the
365	  -- stalk:
366	  --   `Ω[stalk z⁄Γ(Spec, U)] ≃ stalk z ⊗_{Γ(X.left, V)} Ω[Γ(X.left, V)⁄Γ(Spec, U)]`
367	  -- is free of the same rank `n` as a stalk-z-module.
368	  --
369	  -- Quotienting by the maximal ideal of the stalk (passing to the residue
370	  -- field κ(p)) and using the cotangent-sequence trivialisation
371	  -- `Ω[Γ(Spec, U)⁄Γ(Spec, U)] = 0` (since `Γ(Spec, U)` is itself a
372	  -- localisation of the field `kbar`, so the base is a field and there are no
373	  -- non-zero base differentials), gives:
374	  --   `κ(p) ⊗_{stalk z} Ω[stalk z⁄Γ(Spec, U)]` ≅ `m_p / m_p²` as κ(p)-spaces,
375	  -- whence `Module.finrank κ(p) (CotangentSpace _) = n`.
376	  --
377	  -- Finally `ringKrullDim (stalk z) = n` (Stacks 00OE: smooth-algebra
378	  -- dimension formula at a prime), and these together with
379	  -- `IsRegularLocalRing.iff_finrank_cotangentSpace` close the goal.

    {R S : Type*} [CommRing R] [CommRing S] [Algebra R S]
    [Algebra.IsStandardSmooth R S] :

  exact Module.Free.of_basis b

6	module
7	
8	public import Mathlib.RingTheory.Kaehler.Basic
9	public import Mathlib.RingTheory.Localization.BaseChange
10	
11	/-!
12	# Kähler differential module under base change
13	
14	## Main results
15	- `KaehlerDifferential.tensorKaehlerEquivBase`: `(S ⊗[R] Ω[A⁄R]) ≃ₗ[S] Ω[B⁄S]` for `B = S ⊗[R] A`.
16	- `KaehlerDifferential.tensorKaehlerEquiv`: `(B ⊗[A] Ω[A⁄R]) ≃ₗ[B] Ω[B⁄S]` for `B = S ⊗[R] A`.
17	- `KaehlerDifferential.isLocalizedModule_of_isLocalizedModule`:
18	  `Ω[Aₚ/Rₚ]` is the localization of `Ω[A/R]` at `p`.
19	
20	-/
21	
22	@[expose] public section
23	
24	variable (R S A B : Type*) [CommRing R] [CommRing S] [Algebra R S] [CommRing A] [CommRing B]
25	variable [Algebra R A] [Algebra R B]
26	variable [Algebra A B] [Algebra S B] [IsScalarTower R A B] [IsScalarTower R S B]
27	
28	open TensorProduct
29	
30	attribute [local instance] SMulCommClass.of_commMonoid
31	
32	attribute [local irreducible] KaehlerDifferential
33	
34	namespace KaehlerDifferential
35	
36	/-- (Implementation). `A`-action on `S ⊗[R] Ω[A⁄R]`. -/
37	noncomputable
38	abbrev mulActionBaseChange : MulAction A (S ⊗[R] Ω[A⁄R]) :=
39	  (TensorProduct.comm R S Ω[A⁄R]).toEquiv.mulAction A
40	
41	attribute [local instance] mulActionBaseChange
42	
43	@[simp]
44	lemma mulActionBaseChange_smul_tmul (a : A) (s : S) (x : Ω[A⁄R]) :
45	    a • (s ⊗ₜ[R] x) = s ⊗ₜ (a • x) := rfl
46	
47	@[local simp]
48	lemma mulActionBaseChange_smul_zero (a : A) :
49	    a • (0 : S ⊗[R] Ω[A⁄R]) = 0 := by
50	  rw [← zero_tmul _ (0 : Ω[A⁄R]), mulActionBaseChange_smul_tmul, smul_zero]
51	
52	@[local simp]
53	lemma mulActionBaseChange_smul_add (a : A) (x y : S ⊗[R] Ω[A⁄R]) :
54	    a • (x + y) = a • x + a • y := by
55	  change (TensorProduct.comm R S Ω[A⁄R]).symm (a • (TensorProduct.comm R S Ω[A⁄R]) (x + y)) = _
56	  rw [map_add, smul_add, map_add]
57	  rfl
58	
59	/-- (Implementation). `A`-module structure on `S ⊗[R] Ω[A⁄R]`. -/
60	noncomputable
61	abbrev moduleBaseChange :
62	    Module A (S ⊗[R] Ω[A⁄R]) where
63	  __ := (TensorProduct.comm R S Ω[A⁄R]).toEquiv.mulAction A
64	  add_smul r s x := by induction x <;> simp [add_smul, tmul_add, *, add_add_add_comm]
65	  zero_smul x := by induction x <;> simp [*]
66	  smul_zero := by simp
67	  smul_add := by simp
68	
69	attribute [local instance] moduleBaseChange
70	
71	instance : IsScalarTower R A (S ⊗[R] Ω[A⁄R]) := by
72	  apply IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_smul
73	  intro r x
74	  induction x
75	  · simp only [smul_zero]
76	  · rw [mulActionBaseChange_smul_tmul, algebraMap_smul, tmul_smul]
77	  · simp only [smul_add, *]
78	
79	instance : SMulCommClass S A (S ⊗[R] Ω[A⁄R]) where
80	  smul_comm s a x := by
81	    induction x
82	    · simp only [smul_zero]
83	    · rw [mulActionBaseChange_smul_tmul, smul_tmul', smul_tmul', mulActionBaseChange_smul_tmul]
84	    · simp only [smul_add, *]
85	
86	instance : SMulCommClass A S (S ⊗[R] Ω[A⁄R]) where
87	  smul_comm s a x := by rw [← smul_comm]
88	
89	/-- (Implementation). `B = S ⊗[R] A`-module structure on `S ⊗[R] Ω[A⁄R]`. -/
90	@[reducible] noncomputable
91	def moduleBaseChange' [Algebra.IsPushout R S A B] :
92	    Module B (S ⊗[R] Ω[A⁄R]) :=
93	  Module.compHom _ (Algebra.pushoutDesc B (Algebra.lsmul R (A := S) S (S ⊗[R] Ω[A⁄R]))
94	    (Algebra.lsmul R (A := A) _ _) (LinearMap.ext <| smul_comm · ·)).toRingHom
95	
96	attribute [local instance] moduleBaseChange'
97	
98	instance [Algebra.IsPushout R S A B] :
99	    IsScalarTower A B (S ⊗[R] Ω[A⁄R]) := by
100	  apply IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_smul

**Mathlib status at commit `b80f227` (iter-191 Lane M↓ first scaffold).**
The 4-stage proof chain is now scaffolded as follows.

* Stage 1 (`stalkMap_flat_of_smooth`): smooth ⟹ flat at every stalk.
  Axiom-clean.
* Stage 2 (`exists_isStandardSmooth_at_of_smooth`): smooth ⟹ standard
  smooth presentation locally. Axiom-clean.
* Stage 3-4 (Stacks `02JK`/`00OE`): polynomial generators ⟹ regular
  sequence ⟹ regular local ring. The Jacobian-criterion content
  ("standard smooth presentation ⟹ the maximal ideal of every stalk is
  generated by a regular sequence of length = Krull dim") is the
  Mathlib gap, captured here as a *single named scoped* `sorry` consuming
  the Stage 1-2 axiom-clean outputs.

Once Stages 3-4 close upstream, this helper closes axiom-clean and every
downstream consumer (`smooth_codim_one_maximalIdeal_isPrincipal_and_ne_bot`,
`localRing_dvr_of_codim_one`) inherits the closure automatically.
input.new_string:
**Mathlib status at commit `b80f227` (iter-192 Lane M↓ Stage 3-4 expansion).**
The 6-stage proof chain is now scaffolded as follows.

* Stage 1 (`stalkMap_flat_of_smooth`): smooth ⟹ flat at every stalk.
  Axiom-clean (iter-191).
* Stage 2 (`exists_isStandardSmooth_at_of_smooth`): smooth ⟹ standard
  smooth ring-hom presentation locally. Axiom-clean (iter-191).
* Stage 3 (`exists_algebra_isStandardSmooth_section_stalk_isLocalization_of_smooth`):
  scheme-to-algebra bridge: composes Stage 2 with the
  `RingHom.IsStandardSmooth.toAlgebra` bridge and the affine-open stalk
  localisation, producing an algebra-side `Algebra.IsStandardSmooth Γ(Spec, U)
  Γ(X.left, V)` + `IsLocalization.AtPrime` on the stalk. Axiom-clean
  (iter-192).
* Stage 4 (`module_free_kaehlerDifferential_of_isStandardSmooth`):
  standard-smooth algebra ⟹ Kähler differentials `Ω[Γ(X.left, V)⁄Γ(Spec, U)]`
  are `Module.Free` over the section algebra. Axiom-clean (iter-192).
* Stage 5-6 (Stacks `02JK`/`00OE`): local-cotangent freeness + dimension
  formula ⟹ regularity at the stalk. Two specific Mathlib gaps remain at
  commit `b80f227`:
    (a) Free Ω transports through localisation at a prime via
        `Module.free_of_isLocalizedModule` +
        `KaehlerDifferential.isLocalizedModule_of_isLocalizedModule`; needs
        IsScalarTower plumbing and a localisation of `Γ(Spec, U)` along the
        contraction of the prime;
    (b) Smooth-algebra dimension formula `ringKrullDim Aₚ = relative dim`
        (Stacks 00OE), not yet packaged in Mathlib.

Once Stages 5-6 close upstream, this helper closes axiom-clean and every
downstream consumer (`smooth_codim_one_maximalIdeal_isPrincipal_and_ne_bot`,
`localRing_dvr_of_codim_one`) inherits the closure automatically.

390	  -- Jacobian-criterion regularity chain):
391	  --
392	  -- From `hSS : Algebra.IsStandardSmooth Γ(Spec _, U) Γ(X.left, V)`,
393	  -- `Algebra.IsStandardSmooth.iff_exists_basis_kaehlerDifferential` extracts
394	  --   `Subsingleton (H¹ Cotangent _ _) ∧ Module.Basis ι _ Ω[Γ(X.left, V)⁄Γ(Spec, U)]`
395	  -- where the basis is of the form `{d sᵢ}ᵢ` for some `sᵢ ∈ Γ(X.left, V)`. In
396	  -- particular `Ω[Γ(X.left, V)⁄Γ(Spec, U)]` is `Module.Free` of rank
397	  -- `relative dimension n` (= `Krull dim of localisation at any prime` by the
398	  -- smooth-algebra dimension formula).
399	  --
400	  -- Localising at `p = hV.primeIdealOf ⟨z, hzV⟩` (= `hLoc'`):
401	  -- `KaehlerDifferential.map` + `IsLocalizedModule` transport freeness to the
402	  -- stalk:
403	  --   `Ω[stalk z⁄Γ(Spec, U)] ≃ stalk z ⊗_{Γ(X.left, V)} Ω[Γ(X.left, V)⁄Γ(Spec, U)]`
404	  -- is free of the same rank `n` as a stalk-z-module.
405	  --
406	  -- Quotienting by the maximal ideal of the stalk (passing to the residue
407	  -- field κ(p)) and using the cotangent-sequence trivialisation
408	  -- `Ω[Γ(Spec, U)⁄Γ(Spec, U)] = 0` (since `Γ(Spec, U)` is itself a
409	  -- localisation of the field `kbar`, so the base is a field and there are no
410	  -- non-zero base differentials), gives:
411	  --   `κ(p) ⊗_{stalk z} Ω[stalk z⁄Γ(Spec, U)]` ≅ `m_p / m_p²` as κ(p)-spaces,
412	  -- whence `Module.finrank κ(p) (CotangentSpace _) = n`.
413	  --
414	  -- Finally `ringKrullDim (stalk z) = n` (Stacks 00OE: smooth-algebra
415	  -- dimension formula at a prime), and these together with
416	  -- `IsRegularLocalRing.iff_finrank_cotangentSpace` close the goal.
417	  --

419	  --   (a) Free `Ω` after localisation at a prime (transport via
420	  --       `IsLocalizedModule` of `KaehlerDifferential.map`).
421	  --   (b) Smooth-algebra dimension formula `ringKrullDim (Aₚ) = relative dim`.

Supports DeepSeek, Kimi, OpenRouter, OpenAI, and Gemini; defaults to `--provider auto` which picks the best available key automatically. **Requires at least one key in env** (`DEEPSEEK_API_KEY` / `MOONSHOT_API_KEY` / `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` / `OPENAI_API_KEY` / `GEMINI_API_KEY`) — check via `env | grep -E "DEEPSEEK|MOONSHOT|OPENROUTER|OPENAI|GEMINI"` BEFORE planning around its use; if no key is set, fall back to alternatives below. The output is LLM-generated from training data, NOT source-derived: this tool is **not** a literature retriever. For actual literature lookup (cross-check a claim against Hartshorne, fetch an arXiv paper, summarize a Stacks tag) consult the auto-injected subagent catalog — when a literature/reference-fetching subagent is enabled it will appear there — or use the `WebSearch` / `WebFetch` tools directly.
24	- **`archon-subagent.py`** — the generic subagent dispatcher (one wrapper handles every subagent — see "Subagents" below).
25	
26	## Subagents
27	
28	Subagents are descriptor files at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md` (YAML frontmatter — `name`, `description`, `write_domain`, `read_only`, `can_spawn`, `default_enabled`, optional `mandatory: [<phase>...]`, optional `dispatcher_notes` — followed by the prompt body the spawned Claude reads).
29	
30	**You do NOT need to discover subagents.** The plan and review prompts auto-inject an **Available subagents** section at the top of each invocation listing every enabled descriptor with description, write-domain, MANDATORY flag, and `dispatcher_notes`. When you decide to invoke a subagent, read its full prompt at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md`.
31	
32	**Invoke** via the generic wrapper (Bash tool, foreground):
33	
34	```
35	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
36	  --name <subagent-name> \
37	  --slug <kebab-slug> \
38	  --directive-file <path-to-directive.md> \
39	  --write-domain '<glob>'        # repeat for multiple
40	```
41	
42	The wrapper exits 0 on success, prints a one-line status, writes the report to `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`, and the Archon CLI auto-archives a copy to `logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-report.md` for the dashboard. The dispatch semaphore caps concurrent subagent processes at `loop.max_parallel`; the wrapper resolves `--parent-slug` from `ARCHON_SUBAGENT_SLUG` so nested children inherit the hierarchy.
43	
44	**Treat each dispatch as blocking — wait for its report before acting on it.** Don't *deliberately* set `run_in_background: true`. But note the wrapper is genuinely synchronous (it `subprocess.run`s the `archon subagent` CLI and only returns once the child finishes and its report is on disk), so the harness may *auto-background* a long-running dispatch and hand you a task ID immediately — that's expected and fine. When it does, await that task / poll for the report file at `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md` (the wrapper's one-line status and exit 0 confirm completion) before you read or act on the result. The thing to avoid is firing a dispatch and then continuing to plan as if it had already returned; whether the call blocked inline or was auto-backgrounded, the rule is the same: do not consume a subagent's output until its report exists.
45	
46	**Highly-recommended subagents.** A descriptor whose frontmatter sets `mandatory: [plan]` is treated as **highly recommended** for the plan phase (similarly `[review]` for the review phase). The catalog tags them `[HIGHLY RECOMMENDED]`. Dispatch each one unless you have a concrete reason to skip — when skipping, record a one-line rationale under a `## Subagent skips` section in `iter/iter-NNN/<phase>.md` (e.g. `- strategy-critic: STRATEGY.md SHA unchanged from prior iter and last verdict was SOUND`). A post-phase audit checks both signals: it silences when the subagent dispatched OR a skip rationale was recorded, and warns only when neither happened. Each subagent's `dispatcher_notes` enumerates its specific skip conditions — read them before deciding. **Subagents ship disabled by default** (`default_enabled: false`), so the rule only fires for subagents the user has explicitly listed under `subagents.enabled` in `.archon/config.json`; on a fresh project the catalog is empty and no recommended dispatch is required. (The frontmatter field is still named `mandatory: [...]` for backward compat with existing configs and descriptors — its semantic meaning is "highly recommended", not "must dispatch".)
47	
48	## Key Files & Permissions
49	
50	All Archon state files are in `.archon/`. The table below covers **phase agents** (plan, prover, review) and the **user**. Subagent permissions are NOT a fixed row — they are descriptor-driven (`write_domain` glob in the subagent's frontmatter, enforced at dispatch time). See "Subagent permissions" after the table.
51	
52	| File | Plan | Prover | Review | User |
53	|------|------|--------|--------|------|
54	| `PROGRESS.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
55	| `STRATEGY.md` | read + write | — | — | read |
56	| `USER_HINTS.md` | **loop-managed** (captured + cleared by the CLI; injected into the plan prompt) | — | — | write |
57	| `task_pending.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
58	| `task_done.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
59	| `task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect) | write (own file) | read only | read |
60	| `proof-journal/` | read | — | **write** | read |
61	| `PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | — | **write** (Knowledge Base only — session log lives in `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`) | read |
62	| `iter/iter-NNN/plan.md` | **write** (this iter only) | — | read (recent window injected) | read |
63	| `iter/iter-NNN/review.md` | read (recent window injected) | — | **write** (this iter only) | read |
64	| `iter/iter-NNN/objectives.md` | optional write | — | read | read |
65	| `archon-protected.yaml` | read | read | read | **write** |
66	| `.lean` files | — | write (own file; frozen protected signatures) | — | write (via comments) |
67	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints) | read | **write** (semantic markers: `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections — NOT `\leanok`) | read |
68	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | — | — | read |
69	| `TO_USER.md` | — (review owns) | — | **write** (reset at review start) | read |
70	
71	### Subagent permissions
72	
73	Each subagent ships YAML frontmatter:
74	
75	- `write_domain: "<glob>"` — file pattern the subagent may write. Empty for purely read-only subagents (the dispatching call typically passes `--write-domain 'task_results/**'` so the subagent can still write its own report).
76	- `read_only: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may only write its `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`.
77	- `can_spawn: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may dispatch children whose `write_domain` is a subset of its own.
78	
79	The dispatcher enforces `write_domain` at the filesystem level: any child whose declared domain is not a subset of the parent's is rejected before the child agent starts. Reads are restricted only by the descriptor's prompt body (e.g. fresh-context critics that are told not to read PROGRESS.md / STRATEGY.md).
80	
81	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md`** is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow run by hand by the mathematician. The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. To invoke a refactor-style subagent inside the loop, write the directive to a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-directive.md` and dispatch via the generic wrapper. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise.
82	
83	## Protected Declarations
84	
85	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Rules:
86	
87	- **Plan / prover / review** agents: read-only on protected signatures. May fill proof bodies; may not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
88	- **Write-capable structural subagents** (per their `write_domain`): may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (name + signature preserved verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. They may NEVER rename / re-type / delete / re-sign a protected declaration.
89	- The YAML file itself is user-only. No agent adds or removes a declaration. The only allowed agent edit is updating an existing declaration's file path.
90	
91	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
92	
93	Two semantic markers:
94	
95	- `\leanok` — statement block: declaration is formalized (at least a `sorry` present). Proof block: proof closed, no `sorry`.
96	- `\mathlibok` — statement block: declaration is backed by Mathlib (re-export / alias); no Archon proof obligation remains.
97	
98	No marker means the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
99	
100	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between prover and review each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves.
101	
102	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations are the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
103	
104	## User Interaction
105	
106	The user provides hints in two places:
107	
108	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The Archon loop captures this file's content before each plan phase, injects it into the plan agent's prompt, and resets the file to its bundled template (an HTML-comment format guide + zero bullets) after the plan phase succeeds. The HTML-comment preamble is stripped before injection so a template-only file renders as "no hints" to the planner. The plan agent does NOT read or clear that file itself. Provers never see these hints.
109	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
110	
111	## Agent Roles
112	
113	### Plan Agent
114	
115	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for full instructions.
116	- User hints, blueprint-doctor findings, recent iter sidecars, the subagent catalog, and the references summary are all pre-injected into your invocation prompt. You do not "go read" those files.
117	- Read `task_results/` to collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md`.
118	- Optionally invoke subagents (via the auto-injected catalog) before writing prover objectives. Subagent reports are auto-archived to `logs/iter-NNN/` by the Archon CLI.
119	- Write `PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round.
120	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch markers — `\leanok` is owned by the deterministic sync; `\mathlibok` / `% NOTE:` by the review agent.
121	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself.
122	
123	### Prover Agent
124	
125	- Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
126	- Your **active mode** is injected directly into your invocation prompt — read it carefully, it defines your goal, workflow, and constraints for this session.
127	- When your mode specifies `read_blueprint: true`, you are permitted to **read** your assigned blueprint chapter (`blueprint/src/chapters/<your_slug>.tex`) for the informal proof sketch. You may never write to blueprint chapters.
128	- Write results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.
129	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — never edit another agent's file.
130	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your file for file-specific hints.
131	- Do NOT edit blueprint chapters. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker; the review agent applies them.
132	
133	### Subagents
134	
135	Subagents are descriptor-driven and discovered at runtime. The catalog is auto-injected into the plan and review prompts each phase (see "Subagents" above for the wrapper invocation). Each subagent reads only what its directive points at — never `PROGRESS.md` / `STRATEGY.md` / phase-agent state — and writes a self-contained report at `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`. Descriptors with `can_spawn: true` may dispatch children (subject to the global `loop.max_parallel` cap).
136	
137	### Review Agent
138	
139	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for full instructions.
140	- Read `proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data.
141	- Write the session journal to `proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (`summary.md`, `milestones.jsonl`, `recommendations.md`).
142	- Update `PROJECT_STATUS.md` (Knowledge Base only — session narrative goes to `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`).
143	- Maintain the semantic blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE:` annotations, stale `\notready` removal. Do NOT touch `\leanok`.
144	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify `PROGRESS.md`.
145	
146	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
147	
148	These run automatically between phases:
149	
150	- **Iter sidecar init** — creates `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/` at iter start so plan + review have a stable destination for per-iter narrative. Top-level files (STRATEGY.md, PROJECT_STATUS.md, task_*.md) stay bounded.
151	- **`sync_leanok`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status.
152	- **`blueprint-doctor`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically detecting orphan chapters, broken `\ref` / `\uses` / `\proves`, and new `axiom` declarations. Findings are written to `logs/iter-NNN/blueprint-doctor.{md,json}` and inlined into the next plan agent's prompt.
153

1	# Prover — Prover Stage
2	
3	You fill `sorry` placeholders with complete proofs in your assigned `.lean` file.
4	
5	## Workflow
6	
7	1. Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
8	2. Read `task_pending.md` for prior attempts, dead ends, relevant lemmas on your file.
9	3. Check the `.lean` file for `/- USER: ... -/` comments (file-specific user hints).
10	4. **Read the relevant blueprint chapter before writing Lean code.** The chapter holds the mathematical proof sketch you must align with. When stuck, re-reading the blueprint is often the fastest path forward.
11	5. Replace `sorry` with Lean proofs. Push as far as possible.
12	6. **Always save partial progress in the code.** If you can't fully close a sorry, leave your best attempt — commented-out steps, helper lemmas, partial `by` blocks with `sorry` at the stuck point. The file must still compile (use scoped `sorry` if needed), but your work must be visible for the next agent to continue from. NEVER revert to a bare `sorry` — that wastes the work.
13	7. Write your results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.
14	
15	**Write permissions**: only your assigned `.lean` file(s) and `task_results/<your_file>.md`. Do NOT edit `PROGRESS.md`, `task_pending.md`, `task_done.md`, blueprint chapters, or other agents' files.
16	
17	## Protected declarations
18	
19	`archon-protected.yaml` lists declarations with **frozen signatures**. You may fill their proof bodies but must not rename, re-type, reorder arguments, or weaken hypotheses. Only the mathematician edits protected signatures.
20	
21	## Avoid early termination
22	
23	- Don't abandon a proof prematurely. Many complex proofs run to thousands of lines.
24	- Difficulty is NOT a valid reason to leave a `sorry`.
25	- Don't delegate to "the next iteration" or "another prover" if more effort could close it.
26	- Only modify the proof for your assigned task — leave unrelated proofs untouched.
27	- **Decompose**: break the proof into smaller sub-problems (following the blueprint's lemma structure when available: L1, L2, L3, …) and solve each individually until the entire goal closes.
28	
29	## Task completion criteria
30	
31	Your task is complete ONLY when ALL of:
32	
33	1. Every `sorry` in scope is replaced with a complete proof.
34	2. Zero axioms introduced.
35	3. The file compiles cleanly.
36	
37	## Never weaken the type to dodge the proof
38	
39	When the substantive type is unattainable this iter, you have one honest move: leave `sorry` with the **intended type signature** so the type itself encodes the claim. You must NOT substitute a tautologically-true type to trivialize the body. Three patterns are banned:
40	
41	- **Reflexive-iso placeholder.** Replacing a non-trivial `X ≅ Y` (or `Nonempty (X ≅ Y)`) target with `Nonempty (X ≅ X) := ⟨Iso.refl _⟩`. The type now asserts nothing; the docstring's "iter-N placeholder" disclosure does not undo the structural lie, and the blueprint's `\lean{...}` cross-reference will resolve to this hollow declaration.
42	- **`Classical.choice` body around an explicit witness.** Writing `Classical.choice (α := X) ⟨witness⟩` to dissolve `Type` vs `Prop` friction — the witness is unreachable through any unfold, so the def is indistinguishable from `Classical.choice ⟨any-other-X⟩` downstream. Either use `Classical.choose` on the existential chain (preserves the spec), or `Nonempty.some`-with-witness; or ship a typed `sorry`.
43	- **Empty-content `proof_wanted`.** `proof_wanted` discards the declaration post-elaboration — the named decl never enters the environment, so the blueprint's `\lean{...}` cross-reference breaks. Use `theorem name : T := by sorry` with the intended signature instead.
44	
45	**Litmus test**: if you `unfold` your declaration, does it expose the named substantive content (Kähler differential module, the explicit iso, …) or does it stop at `Classical.choice` / `Iso.refl _` / nothing? If the latter, the body is structurally vacuous — ship the typed `sorry` instead.
46	
47	## When infrastructure is missing
48	
49	Do NOT report "Mathlib lacks X" and stop. Before giving up:
50	
51	1. **Use the informal agent** (`.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py`): "Prove [goal] without using [missing infrastructure], only Mathlib." Even an imperfect sketch is valuable.
52	2. **Try the alternative** — formalize whatever the informal agent suggests.
53	3. **If you still can't**: write the alternative sketch to `informal/<theorem_name>.md` and record in your task result what you tried, why it failed, AND the alternative route you found. "I couldn't prove X, but here's approach Y that might work because Z" is far more useful than "infrastructure missing".
54	
55	When stuck more generally: break into smaller subgoals, search Mathlib more thoroughly, prove missing helpers yourself, try alternative strategies, re-read the blueprint, use Web Search for published proofs.
56	
57	**Impossibility vs difficulty**: technical difficulty → keep trying. Mathematical impossibility → immediately backtrack and document why.
58	
59	## Proof style
60	
61	- **Never modify working proofs** — if a declaration has no `sorry` and compiles, do not touch its body.
62	- Keep edits minimal; don't delete comments or change labels; don't add unrelated declarations.
63	- Helper lemmas you introduced may be modified if they turn out wrong.
64	- Add a concise comment above each helper lemma so reuse is easy.
65	- **`change` vs `show`** — `change` reshapes the goal up to defeq; `show` is purely display-level annotation. Using `show` where `change` is needed produces a linter warning. Default to `change` when in doubt.
66	
67	## Mathlib tags in PROGRESS.md
68	
69	The plan agent tags suggested lemmas:
70	
71	- `[verified]` — confirmed to exist.
72	- `[expected]` — guessed by naming convention. Quick `lean_local_search`; pivot if it doesn't exist.
73	- `[gap]` — verified NOT in Mathlib. Don't waste search time; formalize a workaround.
74	
75	## LSP MCP tools
76	
77	The `archon-lean-lsp` server exposes Lean LSP operations as **MCP tool calls** (full names like `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_goal` / `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_diagnostic_messages`). The lean4 skill reference uses short names (`lean_goal`, `lean_local_search`, …) — same tools, NOT shell binaries.
78	
79	- Always invoke through your tool-call interface, the same way as `Read` or `Grep`.
80	- **Never** call `Bash` with `lean_goal …` etc. — there is no such shell command.
81	- First LSP action: `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_diagnostic_messages` on your file. If it returns `success: false` the server is cold; retry once or run `lake build` once via Bash, then retry.
82	
83	## Search protocol
84	
85	Follow `references/lean-lsp-tools-api.md`. Key priority:
86	
87	1. `lean_local_search` first.
88	2. `lean_leansearch` for semantic search — describe the mathematical content, not just the name.
89	3. `lean_loogle` for simple type patterns only.
90	4. Never use shell `find` / `grep` to locate Mathlib theorems.
91	
92	## Tooling traps
93	
94	- **Trust `goals_after`, not just empty diagnostics**: in `lean_multi_attempt`, `diagnostics: []` can be misleading (line-scoping quirks attach errors to the enclosing `by` block). Check that `goals_after` is empty or has advanced.
95	- **Beware `lean_run_code` with imports**: standalone snippets can silently swallow elaboration errors when they contain imports. Rely on the actual file's `lean_diagnostic_messages` for authoritative verification.
96	
97	## Logging
98	
99	Write to `task_results/<your_file>.md` (mirror your `.lean` path: `Algebra/WLocal.lean` → `task_results/Algebra_WLocal.lean.md`):
100	
101	```markdown
102	# Algebra/WLocal.lean
103	
104	## wLocal_iff (line 45)
105	### Attempt 1
106	- **Approach:** Direct case split on maximal ideals
107	- **Result:** FAILED — needed IsLocalRing instance, not available
108	- **Dead end:** direct case split without IsLocalRing
109	
110	### Attempt 2
111	- **Approach:** Stacks 0A31, characterize via bijection on spectra
112	- **Result:** RESOLVED
113	- **Key insight:** `PrimeSpectrum.comap_injective` bridges the gap
114	
115	## helper_bijective (line 78)
116	### Attempt 1
117	- **Approach:** Split into injectivity + surjectivity; prove injectivity
118	- **Result:** PARTIAL (branch closed) — injectivity closed; surjectivity remains
119	- **Next step:** `PrimeSpectrum.range_comap_of_surjective` for the right branch
120	- **Lemmas found:** `PrimeSpectrum.comap_surjective`
121	```
122	
123	**Rules**: one section per theorem/lemma in your file. Each attempt records approach, result (RESOLVED / FAILED / PARTIAL / IN PROGRESS), dead-end warnings or next steps. Log negative search results ("Searched 'projective module infinite rank' — nothing in Mathlib"). The plan agent merges these into `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md` next iter.
124	
125	**Read-only context**: read `task_pending.md` (prior attempts on your file) and `task_done.md` (when the problem resembles a completed one). Do not write to either.
126	
127	## End-of-session handoff
128	
129	Before stopping:
130	
131	1. Write `task_results/<your_file>.md` with: current result, lemmas discovered, concrete next step, dead-end warnings.
132	2. Save all changes; ensure the file compiles (use scoped `sorry` for stuck parts if needed).
133

42	/-! ### Recipe 1 helpers (iter-184 Lane B, `analogies/gmscaling-projection-idiom.md`):
43	project-side simp helpers for `pullback.map ≫ pullback.fst/snd`.
44	
45	Mathlib's `pullback.lift_fst` / `pullback.lift_snd` are `@[reassoc]` ONLY, not
46	`@[simp]`, so the abbrev `pullback.map _ _ _ _ i₁ i₂ i₃ _ _ ≫ pullback.fst _ _`
47	is not collapsed by `simp` even though the equation holds definitionally. These
48	two local simp lemmas unblock projection-chain collapse for
49	`gmScalingP1_cover_intersection_X_iso` whose STEP 4 uses
50	`asIso (pullback.map …)`. Candidate for upstream Mathlib contribution. -/
51	
52	@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
53	lemma pullback_map_fst_proj {C : Type*} [Category C] {W X Y Z S T : C}
54	    (f₁ : W ⟶ S) (f₂ : X ⟶ S) [Limits.HasPullback f₁ f₂] (g₁ : Y ⟶ T)
55	    (g₂ : Z ⟶ T) [Limits.HasPullback g₁ g₂] (i₁ : W ⟶ Y) (i₂ : X ⟶ Z) (i₃ : S ⟶ T)
56	    (eq₁ : f₁ ≫ i₃ = i₁ ≫ g₁) (eq₂ : f₂ ≫ i₃ = i₂ ≫ g₂) :
57	    Limits.pullback.map f₁ f₂ g₁ g₂ i₁ i₂ i₃ eq₁ eq₂ ≫
58	        Limits.pullback.fst g₁ g₂ =
59	      Limits.pullback.fst f₁ f₂ ≫ i₁ :=
60	  Limits.pullback.lift_fst _ _ _
61	
62	@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
63	lemma pullback_map_snd_proj {C : Type*} [Category C] {W X Y Z S T : C}
64	    (f₁ : W ⟶ S) (f₂ : X ⟶ S) [Limits.HasPullback f₁ f₂] (g₁ : Y ⟶ T)
65	    (g₂ : Z ⟶ T) [Limits.HasPullback g₁ g₂] (i₁ : W ⟶ Y) (i₂ : X ⟶ Z) (i₃ : S ⟶ T)
66	    (eq₁ : f₁ ≫ i₃ = i₁ ≫ g₁) (eq₂ : f₂ ≫ i₃ = i₂ ≫ g₂) :
67	    Limits.pullback.map f₁ f₂ g₁ g₂ i₁ i₂ i₃ eq₁ eq₂ ≫
68	        Limits.pullback.snd g₁ g₂ =
69	      Limits.pullback.snd f₁ f₂ ≫ i₂ :=
70	  Limits.pullback.lift_snd _ _ _

72	/-! ### Chart-bridge: `Proj.awayι ≫ PLB.hom = Spec.map (algebraMap kbar (Away _ _))`
73	
74	The helper below is the iter-173 `mathlib-analogist chart-bridge173` recipe step (a)
75	(`analogies/chart-bridge.md`). Used by `gmScalingP1_cover_X_iso` (below the `gmScalingP1_cover`
76	definition). -/
77	
78	/-- **`Proj.awayι 𝒜 f _ _ ≫ PLB.hom = Spec.map (algebraMap kbar (Away 𝒜 f))`** for any
79	homogeneous element `f` of positive degree.
80	
81	Generic in the element `f` AND in the degree `m` so we can apply it to either
82	`(![X 0, X 1]) i` (the actual chart input from `projectiveLineBarAffineCover.openCover.f i`,
83	which uses `m = (![1, 1]) i`) or `X i` (which uses `m = 1`). A pure rewrite chasing
84	`awayι_toSpecZero` + `Spec.map_comp` + the `algebraKbarAway` defeq. -/
85	private lemma awayι_comp_PLB_hom (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar]
86	    {m : ℕ} (hm : 0 < m)
87	    (f : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) (hf : f ∈ projectiveLineBarGrading kbar m) :
88	    Proj.awayι (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) f hf hm ≫
89	      (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom =
90	    Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap kbar
91	      (HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) f))) := by
92	  change Proj.awayι _ _ _ _ ≫ Proj.toSpecZero _ ≫ Spec.map _ = _

97	/-! ### (D) The `𝔾_m`-scaling action `σ_× : ℙ¹ × 𝔾_m ⟶ ℙ¹`
98	
99	`gmScalingP1` is a *bare* `Over (Spec (.of kbar))`-morphism (the analogist D3 verdict:
100	no `IsAction`/`MulAction`-style typeclass at scheme level — Mathlib has no such precedent;
101	the rigidity consumer needs only the bare morphism + the named fixed-point lemma).
102	
103	Chartwise definition: on `𝔸¹ × 𝔾_m` (target chart `D₊(X₀)` of `ℙ¹`), the morphism is
104	the polynomial map `(x, λ) ↦ λx`; near `∞` (target chart `D₊(X₁)`, coordinate `u = 1/x`),
105	the target coordinate `1/(λx) = u/λ` is regular because `λ ∈ 𝔾_m` is invertible. The two
106	chart-restrictions agree on `(𝔸¹ ∖ {0}) × 𝔾_m`, so they glue via
107	`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Cover.glueMorphisms`.
108	
109	The companion lemma `gmScalingP1_collapse_at_zero` exposes the load-bearing fixed-point
110	property `σ_×(0, λ) = 0` for all `λ ∈ 𝔾_m`, packaged as the `W`-axis-collapse hypothesis
111	that `hom_additive_decomp_of_rigidity` (Cor 1.5) consumes. -/
112	
113	/-- **Chart-1 ring map for `σ_×`** at the `MvPolynomial Unit kbar`-level: sends the affine
114	coord `u = X 0 / X 1 ↦ u ⊗ λ`, where `λ = X () ∈ GmRing kbar`. Uses
115	`MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom` with the algebra-map `kbar →+* MvPolynomial Unit kbar ⊗[kbar] GmRing`
116	(target carrier carries `Algebra kbar` because both factors do). Axiom-clean. -/
117	noncomputable def gmScalingP1_chart1_ringMap (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
118	    MvPolynomial Unit kbar →+* TensorProduct kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) (GmRing kbar) :=
119	  MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom (algebraMap kbar _)
120	    (fun _ => (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar) ⊗ₜ[kbar]
121	      (algebraMap (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) (GmRing kbar) (MvPolynomial.X ())))
122	
123	/-- **Chart-0 ring map for `σ_×`** at the `MvPolynomial Unit kbar`-level: sends the affine
124	coord `t = X 1 / X 0 ↦ t ⊗ λ⁻¹`. The `λ⁻¹` is `IsLocalization.Away.invSelf (X ())` in
125	`GmRing kbar = Localization.Away (X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar)`. Axiom-clean. -/
126	noncomputable def gmScalingP1_chart0_ringMap (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
127	    MvPolynomial Unit kbar →+* TensorProduct kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) (GmRing kbar) :=
128	  MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom (algebraMap kbar _)
129	    (fun _ => (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar) ⊗ₜ[kbar]
130	      (IsLocalization.Away.invSelf

359	/-- **The substantive `(0, 1)` cross case of `gmScalingP1_chart_agreement`** (iter-181
360	Lane B helper).
361	
362	This is the *single* non-trivial ring-level identity for the cocycle:
363	
364	`pullback.fst ((cover).f 0) ((cover).f 1) ≫ chart 0 =
365	   pullback.snd ((cover).f 0) ((cover).f 1) ≫ chart 1`.
366	
367	On the basic-open intersection `D₊(X 0 · X 1) ⊆ ProjectiveLineBarScheme` both chart
368	coordinates are units (chart-0 coord `t = X 1 / X 0`, chart-1 coord `u = X 0 / X 1`,
369	with `t · u = 1`). Under `σ_×([X 0 : X 1], λ) = [λ · X 0 : X 1]` the two chart maps
370	satisfy on the intersection:
371	
372	* chart-0: `t ↦ t ⊗ λ⁻¹` (i.e. `t/λ`)
373	* chart-1: `u ↦ u ⊗ λ` (i.e. `λ · u`)
374	
375	Substituting `u = 1/t` on the chart-1 side gives `λ · u = λ · (1/t) = λ/t`, and on the
376	chart-0 side `t/λ` corresponds to `u' = 1/(t/λ) = λ/t` under the `u ↔ 1/t` change of
377	coords. Hence the ring-level identity is `λ · u = (1/t) · λ` in
378	`Localization.Away t ⊗[kbar] GmRing kbar`, which after multiplying both sides by `t`
379	reduces to `λ · u · t = λ`, true because `u · t = 1`.
380	
381	**Status (iter-182 Lane B):** **structural advance** — the intersection iso
382	`gmScalingP1_cover_intersection_X_iso` is now built axiom-clean using
383	Mathlib's `Proj.pullbackAwayιIso` (per `analogies/intersection-ring-cross01.md`
384	Decision 2). Both sides of the cocycle factor through `awayι_(X 0 * X 1)` once
385	we cancel-epi the iso's inverse; the residual is the ring-level identity above.
386	
387	The `cancel_epi` step lifts the goal from `pullback ((cover).f 0) ((cover).f 1) ⟶ Proj 𝒜`
388	to `Spec ((Away X_0X_1) ⊗ GmRing) ⟶ Proj 𝒜`. The fully-unfolded form (after
389	`simp only [gmScalingP1_cover_intersection_X_iso, Iso.trans_inv, ...]`) is a chain
390	of 7 iso `.inv` compositions; reducing it to a `Spec.map` of a single ring map
391	into `awayι_(X 0 * X 1)` requires either (a) `@[simps]` annotation on the iso, or
392	(b) explicit projection lemmas for each stage. Both are out of iter-182 helper
393	budget (helper budget = 2 used on the intersection iso + this lemma).

396	Recipe 2 (project both sides through the intersection iso
397	`iso = gmScalingP1_cover_intersection_X_iso` via `cancel_epi iso.inv`, then
398	collapse each projection through `Proj.pullbackAwayιIso_inv_fst/_snd` +

400	`Proj.awayι (X_0 · X_1)`). The empirical reality is that

403	+ `pullback.map_fst`, `inv (pullback.map ... pullbackAwayιIso.hom ...)`, etc.)

406	
407	1. **No `pullback.map_fst` lemma in Mathlib**: the natural simp lemma name
408	   `CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback.map_fst` does not resolve. The closest
409	   `pullback.lift_fst` requires un-`asIso`ing the `pullback.map` first.
410	2. **`Iso.trans` opacity**: `Iso.trans_inv` rewrites to `(b ≪≫ a).inv = a.inv ≫ b.inv`,
411	   but each intermediate `b.inv` is itself a `≪≫`-chain whose `.inv` rewrites

755	/-! ### (E) Product-stability instances on `ℙ¹ ⊗ 𝔾_m`
756	
757	These instances are exported for Lane B's consumer `morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const_aux`
758	(in `AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean`), so its previously local `haveI ... := by sorry`
759	ad-hoc scaffolds collapse to `inferInstance`. Each instance is justified as follows:
760	
761	* `(ℙ¹ ⊗ 𝔾_m).hom` is locally of finite type — by composition with `pullback.fst`
762	  (`LocallyOfFiniteType` is `IsStableUnderComposition` and `IsStableUnderBaseChange`,
763	  with both factors LOFT).
764	* `ℙ¹` is reduced — **closed axiom-clean iter-168** via the chart-cover + `val_injective`
765	  bridge (`projectiveLineBar_isReduced`).
766	* `(ℙ¹ ⊗ 𝔾_m).hom` is geometrically irreducible — scaffold (Mathlib gap: `GeometricallyIrreducible`
767	  on `Gm.hom` needs the alg-closed-base reduction, currently not bridged).
768	* `(ℙ¹ ⊗ 𝔾_m).left` is reduced — scaffold (Mathlib gap: `Smooth → GeometricallyReduced`
769	  not shipped at scheme level).
770	* `Gm.hom` is geometrically irreducible — scaffold (Mathlib gap: see above). -/
771	
772	/-- **`(ℙ¹ ⊗ 𝔾_m).hom` is locally of finite type.** Decomposes as
773	`pullback.fst ≫ ProjectiveLineBar.hom`; `LocallyOfFiniteType` is stable under composition
774	and pullback (Mathlib's `locallyOfFiniteType_comp`,
775	`locallyOfFiniteType_isStableUnderBaseChange`). -/
776	instance projGm_locallyOfFiniteType (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
777	    LocallyOfFiniteType ((ProjectiveLineBar kbar) ⊗ Gm kbar).hom := by
778	  change LocallyOfFiniteType
779	    (pullback.fst (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom (Gm kbar).hom ≫ (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom)
780	  infer_instance
781	
782	/-- **`ℙ¹` is reduced.** Closed axiom-clean iter-168 via `IsReduced.of_openCover` over
783	`projectiveLineBarAffineCover`; each chart `Spec (HomogeneousLocalization.Away 𝒜 (X_i))`
784	is a domain because the canonical `val`-injection into `Localization.Away (X_i)` (a
785	localization of `k̄[X_0, X_1]` at a non-zero-divisor, hence a domain) factors through
786	`Function.Injective.isDomain`. Exported here for Lane B (replaces its inline `haveI hP1red`). -/
787	instance projectiveLineBar_isReduced (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
788	    IsReduced (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left := by
789	  change IsReduced (ProjectiveLineBarScheme kbar)
790	  -- Strategy: `IsReduced.of_openCover` over `projectiveLineBarAffineCover.openCover`.
791	  -- Each chart is `Spec(.of (Away 𝒜 (X i)))`; `IsReduced (Spec R)` if `R` is reduced.
792	  -- `Away 𝒜 (X i)` is a domain (and hence reduced) because it embeds via `val_injective`
793	  -- into `Localization.Away (X i)`, which is a localization of `MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar`
794	  -- (a domain) at a non-zero-divisor — hence a domain.
795	  haveI : ∀ i : Fin 2, IsReduced ((projectiveLineBarAffineCover kbar).openCover.X i) := by
796	    intro i
797	    -- (projectiveLineBarAffineCover kbar).openCover.X i = Spec (.of (Away 𝒜 (X i)))
798	    -- Need IsReduced of that Spec.
799	    change IsReduced (Spec (CommRingCat.of (HomogeneousLocalization.Away
800	        (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) ((![MvPolynomial.X 0, MvPolynomial.X 1] :
801	          Fin 2 → MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) i))))
802	    haveI : IsDomain (Localization.Away ((![(MvPolynomial.X 0 : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar),
803	        MvPolynomial.X 1] : Fin 2 → MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) i)) := by
804	      fin_cases i <;>
805	        exact IsLocalization.isDomain_localization
806	          (powers_le_nonZeroDivisors_of_noZeroDivisors (MvPolynomial.X_ne_zero _))
807	    haveI : IsDomain (HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
808	        ((![(MvPolynomial.X 0 : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar), MvPolynomial.X 1] :
809	          Fin 2 → MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) i)) := by
810	      refine Function.Injective.isDomain
811	        (algebraMap
812	          (HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
813	            ((![(MvPolynomial.X 0 : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar), MvPolynomial.X 1] :
814	              Fin 2 → MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) i))
815	          (Localization.Away
816	            ((![(MvPolynomial.X 0 : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar), MvPolynomial.X 1] :
817	              Fin 2 → MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) i))) ?_
818	      intro x y h
819	      exact HomogeneousLocalization.val_injective _ h
820	    infer_instance
821	  exact IsReduced.of_openCover _ (projectiveLineBarAffineCover kbar).openCover
822	
823	/-- **Helper:** `TensorProduct kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) K` is a domain for any field
824	`K` over `kbar`. This is the "geometrically irreducible affine line" tensor identity,
825	established directly from `MvPolynomial.algebraTensorAlgEquiv` (no `IsLocalization`
826	machinery needed). -/
827	private lemma isDomain_mvPolyUnit_tensor (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar]
828	    (K : Type u) [Field K] [Algebra kbar K] :
829	    IsDomain (TensorProduct kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) K) := by
830	  haveI : IsDomain (MvPolynomial Unit K) := inferInstance
831	  -- Iso chain: TensorProduct kbar (MvPoly Unit kbar) K
832	  --   ≃ₐ[kbar] TensorProduct kbar K (MvPoly Unit kbar) (by Algebra.TensorProduct.comm)
833	  --   ≃ₐ[K]    MvPoly Unit K                          (by MvPolynomial.algebraTensorAlgEquiv)
834	  let e1 : TensorProduct kbar K (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) ≃+* MvPolynomial Unit K :=
835	    (MvPolynomial.algebraTensorAlgEquiv (σ := Unit) kbar K).toRingEquiv
836	  let e2 : TensorProduct kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) K ≃+* TensorProduct kbar K (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) :=
837	    (Algebra.TensorProduct.comm kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) K).toRingEquiv
838	  refine Function.Injective.isDomain (e1.toRingHom.comp e2.toRingHom) ?_
839	  exact e1.injective.comp e2.injective
840	
841	/-- **`(𝔸¹ = Spec k̄[t]).hom` is geometrically irreducible over `Spec k̄`** as a scheme
842	morphism `Spec (k̄[t]) ⟶ Spec k̄`. For any field `K` with `Algebra kbar K`, the pullback
843	is `Spec (k̄[t] ⊗_k̄ K) ≅ Spec (K[t])`, which is irreducible because `K[t]` is a domain. -/
844	private lemma affineLine_geomIrred (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
845	    GeometricallyIrreducible
846	      (Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar)))) := by
847	  refine ⟨?_⟩
848	  rw [geometrically_iff_of_commRing_of_isClosedUnderIsomorphisms]
849	  intro K _ _
850	  haveI hdom : IsDomain (TensorProduct kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) K) :=
851	    isDomain_mvPolyUnit_tensor kbar K
852	  -- `Spec` of a domain is irreducible.
853	  haveI hirr : IrreducibleSpace
854	      (Spec (CommRingCat.of (TensorProduct kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) K))) := by
855	    change IrreducibleSpace
856	      (PrimeSpectrum (TensorProduct kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) K))
857	    infer_instance
858	  -- Transport via `pullbackSpecIso`.
859	  -- The Iso (pullbackSpecIso ...).symm : Spec(...) ≅ pullback ... gives a homeomorphism
860	  -- from which IrreducibleSpace transfers.
861	  exact (pullbackSpecIso kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) K).symm.hom.homeomorph.irreducibleSpace_iff.mp
862	    hirr
863	
864	/-- **`𝔾_m` is geometrically irreducible over `Spec k̄`.** Iter-191 closure via the
865	basic-open / open-immersion route:
866	
867	* `(Gm).hom = Spec.map (algebraMap kbar (GmRing kbar))` factors as
868	  `Spec.map (algebraMap (MvPoly Unit kbar) (GmRing kbar)) ≫ Spec.map (algebraMap kbar (MvPoly Unit kbar))`
869	  via `IsScalarTower.algebraMap_apply`.
870	* The first arrow is an open immersion (Mathlib instance
871	  `isOpenImmersion_SpecMap_localizationAway`: `Gm = D₊(t) ⊂ 𝔸¹`).
872	* The second arrow is `GeometricallyIrreducible` (`affineLine_geomIrred`).
873	* `Surjective` of the composition is immediate because the target `Spec k̄` is a single
874	  point and the source is nonempty.
875	* Conclude via the Mathlib instance
876	  `[IsOpenImmersion f] [GeometricallyIrreducible g] [Surjective (f ≫ g)] →
877	  GeometricallyIrreducible (f ≫ g)`.
878	
879	Exported here for Lane B and for the `projGm_geomIrred` derivation. -/
880	instance gm_geomIrred (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar] :
881	    GeometricallyIrreducible (Gm kbar).hom := by
882	  -- Rewrite (Gm).hom as a composition Gm ↪ 𝔸¹ → Spec k̄.
883	  haveI hg_irr : GeometricallyIrreducible
884	      (Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar)))) :=
885	    affineLine_geomIrred kbar
886	  have hcomp : (Gm kbar).hom =
887	      Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) (GmRing kbar))) ≫
888	      Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar))) := by
889	    show Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap kbar (GmRing kbar))) = _
890	    rw [← Spec.map_comp, ← CommRingCat.ofHom_comp]
891	    congr 1
892	  rw [hcomp]
893	  -- Surjective: target Spec k̄ is subsingleton, source is nonempty.
894	  haveI hsurj : Surjective (Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom
895	        (algebraMap (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) (GmRing kbar))) ≫
896	      Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar)))) := by
897	    constructor
898	    intro p
899	    obtain ⟨x⟩ : Nonempty (Spec (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar))) := inferInstance
900	    refine ⟨x, ?_⟩
901	    exact Subsingleton.elim _ _
902	  haveI := hg_irr
903	  haveI := hsurj
904	  -- Mathlib's open-immersion + GeometricallyIrreducible composition instance
905	  -- (`AlgebraicGeometry.Geometrically.Irreducible`-:131).
906	  set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
907	    exact inferInstance
908	
909	/-- **`(ℙ¹ ⊗ 𝔾_m).hom` is geometrically irreducible.** Derives from the individual factors
910	via `GeometricallyIrreducible.comp` (with `UniversallyOpen` discharged for free by smoothness
911	of each factor). The `(X ⊗ Y).hom = pullback.fst ≫ X.hom` defeq unfolds, then
912	`GeometricallyIrreducible.comp` chains `pullback.fst`'s GI (by base-change stability of GI
913	from `gm_geomIrred`) with `projectiveLineBar_geomIrred`.
914	
915	Exported here for Lane B (replaces its inline `haveI hProdGI`). Axiom-clean given the
916	individual GI scaffolds. -/
917	instance projGm_geomIrred (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar] :
918	    GeometricallyIrreducible ((ProjectiveLineBar kbar) ⊗ Gm kbar).hom := by
919	  change GeometricallyIrreducible
920	    (pullback.fst (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom (Gm kbar).hom ≫ (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom)
921	  exact GeometricallyIrreducible.comp _ _
922	
923	/-- **`(ℙ¹ ⊗ 𝔾_m).left` is reduced.** Iter-191 closure via the chart-local
924	recipe of `analogies/lane-b-substrate.md` §3 Application 2:
925	
926	* Cover `(PLB ⊗ Gm).left` by the existing `gmScalingP1_cover`; the chart-`i`
927	  source is `Spec ((Away 𝒜 (X i)) ⊗_kbar GmRing kbar)` via
928	  `gmScalingP1_cover_X_iso`.
929	* `(Away 𝒜 (X i)) ⊗_kbar GmRing kbar` is a domain by Substrate 2
930	  (`gmRing_tensor_homogeneousAway_isDomain` at the degree-1 generator
931	  `(![X 0, X 1] i)`).
932	* `IsDomain → IsReduced` on the carrier ring; `Spec` of a reduced ring is
933	  reduced; transport reducedness along `gmScalingP1_cover_X_iso.hom` (an
934	  iso, hence `IsOpenImmersion`) via `isReduced_of_isOpenImmersion`.
935	* Conclude `IsReduced ((PLB ⊗ Gm).left)` via `IsReduced.of_openCover`. -/
936	instance projGm_isReduced (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
937	    IsReduced ((ProjectiveLineBar kbar) ⊗ Gm kbar).left := by
938	  haveI hchart : ∀ i : (gmScalingP1_cover kbar).I₀,
939	      IsReduced ((gmScalingP1_cover kbar).X i) := by
940	    intro (i : Fin 2)
941	    have hf_deg : ((![MvPolynomial.X 0, MvPolynomial.X 1] :
942	                    Fin 2 → MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) i) ∈
943	          projectiveLineBarGrading kbar ((![1, 1] : Fin 2 → ℕ) i) :=
944	      projectiveLineBarAffineCover_fDeg kbar i
945	    have hm : 0 < (![1, 1] : Fin 2 → ℕ) i := projectiveLineBarAffineCover_hm i
946	    have hne : ((![MvPolynomial.X 0, MvPolynomial.X 1] :
947	                  Fin 2 → MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) i) ≠ 0 := by
948	      fin_cases i <;> simp
949	    haveI hdom : IsDomain (TensorProduct kbar
950	        (HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
951	          ((![MvPolynomial.X 0, MvPolynomial.X 1] :
952	            Fin 2 → MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) i))
953	        (GmRing kbar)) :=
954	      AlgebraicGeometry.gmRing_tensor_homogeneousAway_isDomain kbar hm _ hf_deg hne
955	    exact isReduced_of_isOpenImmersion (gmScalingP1_cover_X_iso kbar i).hom
956	  exact IsReduced.of_openCover _ (gmScalingP1_cover kbar)
957	
958	end AlgebraicGeometry

1584	      The chart-agreement equation
1585	      \(\mathrm{pullback.fst} \fatsemi \mathtt{gmScalingP1\_chart}\,\bar k\,0 =
1586	      \mathrm{pullback.snd} \fatsemi \mathtt{gmScalingP1\_chart}\,\bar k\,1\)
1587	      on the cover intersection
1588	      \(\mathrm{pullback}\,((\mathtt{gmScalingP1\_cover})_0.f)\,
1589	      ((\mathtt{gmScalingP1\_cover})_1.f)\) is the statement that two morphisms
1590	      \(\mathrm{chart}_0, \mathrm{chart}_1 \colon \mathrm{(intersection)} \to
1591	      \overline{\mathbb P^1}\) agree on that intersection scheme. \emph{The key
1592	      observation is that \(\overline{\mathbb P^1}\) is separated over \(\bar k\)},
1593	      so the diagonal
1594	      \(\Delta_{\overline{\mathbb P^1}} \colon
1595	        \overline{\mathbb P^1} \longrightarrow
1596	        \overline{\mathbb P^1} \times_{\bar k} \overline{\mathbb P^1}\)
1597	      is a closed immersion. Because \(\overline{\mathbb P^1} = \Proj(\bar k[X_0, X_1])\)
1598	      is the \(\Proj\) of a graded \(\bar k\)-algebra, its separatedness over \(\Spec
1599	      \bar k\) is the standard Stacks fact ``\(\Proj(S)\) is separated'' (Stacks tag
1600	      \texttt{01KU} / lemma \texttt{lemma-proj-separated}); Mathlib supplies the
1601	      categorical packaging via \(\mathtt{IsSeparated}\) and
1602	      \(\mathtt{IsSeparated.diagonal\_isClosedImmersion}\).
1603	      \emph{Bypass.} Reformulate the cocycle as: the pair-morphism
1604	      \((\mathrm{chart}_0, \mathrm{chart}_1) \colon \mathrm{(intersection)}
1605	      \longrightarrow \overline{\mathbb P^1} \times_{\bar k} \overline{\mathbb P^1}\)
1606	      (built via \(\mathtt{CategoryTheory.Limits.prod.lift}\) on
1607	      \(\mathrm{chart}_0 := \mathrm{pullback.fst} \fatsemi \mathtt{gmScalingP1\_chart}\,
1608	      \bar k\,0\) and
1609	      \(\mathrm{chart}_1 := \mathrm{pullback.snd} \fatsemi \mathtt{gmScalingP1\_chart}\,
1610	      \bar k\,1\)) \emph{factors through the diagonal
1611	      \(\Delta_{\overline{\mathbb P^1}}\)}. By Mathlib's
1612	      \(\mathtt{IsClosedImmersion.lift\_iff\_range\_subset}\) (the universal property
1613	      of a closed immersion: a morphism factors through a closed immersion iff its
1614	      set-theoretic image is contained in the image of the immersion), the
1615	      factorization is equivalent to the set-level containment of the cocycle's image
1616	      in the image of \(\Delta\), which on points is exactly the chart equality on
1617	      the \emph{closed points} of the intersection. The closed-point check is
1618	      discrete (each closed point of the intersection lies over a single \(\bar k\)-
1619	      point of \(D_+(X_0 \cdot X_1) \times \mathbb G_m\) and the two chart maps both
1620	      evaluate to the same \(\bar k\)-point of \(\overline{\mathbb P^1}\)

1622	      that obstructs (III.a)/(III.b) entirely.

1628	            \overline{\mathbb P^1}\)
1629	          via Mathlib's \(\mathtt{CategoryTheory.Limits.prod.lift}\,(\mathbf
1630	          1_{\overline{\mathbb P^1}})\,(\mathbf 1_{\overline{\mathbb P^1}})\), and
1631	          obtain the closed-immersion witness from
1632	          \(\mathtt{IsSeparated.diagonal\_isClosedImmersion}\) applied to the
1633	          separated structure morphism

1638	            \mathrm{pullback}\,((\mathtt{gmScalingP1\_cover})_0.f)\,
1639	              ((\mathtt{gmScalingP1\_cover})_1.f)
1640	            \longrightarrow

1697	  \medskip
1698	  \noindent\textbf{(IV) Substantive ring-level residual.} All three pickup paths reduce,
1699	  after their structural setup, to the same ring-level identity. On the basic-open
1700	  intersection \(D_+(X_0 \cdot X_1) \subseteq \overline{\mathbb P^1}\) both chart
1701	  coordinates are units (the chart-\(0\) coordinate \(t = X_1/X_0\) and the chart-\(1\)
1702	  coordinate \(u = X_0/X_1\) are reciprocals: \(t \cdot u = 1\)), and the agreement
1703	  reduces to the ring-level identity
1704	  \[ \lambda \cdot u \ =\ \frac{1}{t} \cdot \lambda \quad
1705	     \text{in } \mathrm{Localization.Away}\,t \otimes_{\bar k} \mathtt{GmRing}\,\bar k, \]
1706	  i.e.\ to the unit-of-the-localisation identity \(t \cdot u = 1\)
1707	  (\(\mathtt{IsLocalization.Away.mul\_invSelf}\)) together with commutativity of the
1708	  tensor product. The proof routes through the chart-ring iso
1709	  \cref{def:proj_chart_ring_iso} and the same \(\mathtt{pullbackSpecIso}\) bridge that
1710	  \cref{def:gmscaling_chart} uses; consequently \cref{lem:gmscaling_chart_agreement}
1711	  remains gated on \cref{def:gmscaling_chart} acquiring its concrete body \emph{and} on

1714	  full Recipe~1/2/3 decomposition and empirical simp-probe verification.
1715	\end{lemma}
