54	/--
55	**Closed immersion lift by range inclusion.**
56	For a closed immersion `i : Z ⟶ X` whose source `Z` is reduced and a
57	quasi-compact morphism `g : Y ⟶ X` whose source `Y` is reduced, `g` factors
58	through `i` if and only if the topological range of `g` is contained in the
59	topological range of `i`.
60	
61	This is the topological-range form of the universal property of closed
62	immersions; the kernel-inequality form is `IsClosedImmersion.lift` /
63	`IsClosedImmersion.lift_fac`.  The two forms are interchanged by the Galois
64	connection `vanishingIdeal ⊣ support` on ideal sheaves
65	(`IdealSheafData.gc` / `IdealSheafData.vanishingIdeal_support`).
66	
67	This is **Substrate 1** of the Lane B Cross01 cocycle proof; see
68	`blueprint/src/chapters/Genus0BaseObjects_Cross01Substrate.tex`,
69	`Theorem~\ref{thm:IsClosedImmersion_lift_iff_range_subset}`.
70	-/
71	theorem lift_iff_range_subset
72	    {X Y Z : Scheme.{u}} (i : Z ⟶ X) [IsClosedImmersion i] [IsReduced Z]
73	    (g : Y ⟶ X) [QuasiCompact g] [IsReduced Y] :
74	    (∃ h : Y ⟶ Z, h ≫ i = g) ↔ Set.range g.base ⊆ Set.range i.base := by
75	  refine ⟨?_, ?_⟩
76	  · -- (⇒) Forward direction: range pushdown.
77	    rintro ⟨h, rfl⟩
78	    rintro _ ⟨y, rfl⟩
79	    refine ⟨h.base y, ?_⟩
80	    simp [Scheme.Hom.comp_base]
81	  · -- (⇐) Backward direction: the substantive content.
82	    intro hrange
83	    -- Helper: the kernel of a ring hom into a reduced ring is a radical ideal.
84	    have ker_isRadical : ∀ {R S : Type u} [CommSemiring R] [CommSemiring S]
85	        [_root_.IsReduced S] (f : R →+* S), (RingHom.ker f).IsRadical := by
86	      intro R S _ _ _ f x hx
87	      obtain ⟨n, hxn⟩ := hx
88	      rw [RingHom.mem_ker] at hxn ⊢
89	      have h₁ : (f x) ^ n = 0 := by
90	        rw [← map_pow]; exact hxn
91	      exact eq_zero_of_pow_eq_zero h₁
92	    -- Step 1: For a closed immersion, the support of its kernel equals its range.
93	    -- (`support_ker` gives `closure (range)`; closed range collapses the closure.)
94	    have hi_range_eq : (i.ker.support : Set X) = Set.range i.base := by
95	      rw [Scheme.Hom.support_ker]
96	      exact i.isClosedEmbedding.isClosed_range.closure_eq
97	    -- Step 2: `g.ker.support ⊆ i.ker.support` (set-theoretic, via `closure_minimal`).
98	    have hsup_set : (g.ker.support : Set X) ⊆ (i.ker.support : Set X) := by
99	      rw [Scheme.Hom.support_ker]
100	      refine closure_minimal ?_ i.ker.support.isClosed
101	      exact hrange.trans hi_range_eq.ge
102	    -- Promote the set inclusion to the `Closeds X` ordering.
103	    have hsup : g.ker.support ≤ i.ker.support := hsup_set
104	    -- Step 3: `g.ker` is radical (because `Y` is reduced, so each `Γ(Y, g ⁻¹ᵁ U)` is reduced
105	    -- and the ring kernel is therefore radical, pointwise on affine opens).
106	    have hg_rad : g.ker.radical = g.ker := by
107	      refine le_antisymm ?_ (Scheme.IdealSheafData.le_radical _)
108	      intro U
109	      rw [Scheme.IdealSheafData.radical_ideal, Scheme.Hom.ker_apply]
110	      exact ker_isRadical (g.app U.1).hom
111	    -- Step 4: `i.ker` is radical (same argument; closed immersions are quasi-compact).
112	    have hi_rad : i.ker.radical = i.ker := by
113	      refine le_antisymm ?_ (Scheme.IdealSheafData.le_radical _)
114	      intro U
115	      rw [Scheme.IdealSheafData.radical_ideal, Scheme.Hom.ker_apply]
116	      exact ker_isRadical (i.app U.1).hom
117	    -- Step 5: thread the Galois connection `vanishingIdeal ⊣ support`.
118	    have hker : i.ker ≤ g.ker := by
119	      calc i.ker
120	          = i.ker.radical := hi_rad.symm
121	        _ = Scheme.IdealSheafData.vanishingIdeal i.ker.support :=
122	              Scheme.IdealSheafData.vanishingIdeal_support.symm
123	        _ ≤ Scheme.IdealSheafData.vanishingIdeal g.ker.support :=
124	              Scheme.IdealSheafData.vanishingIdeal_antimono hsup
125	        _ = g.ker.radical := Scheme.IdealSheafData.vanishingIdeal_support
126	        _ = g.ker := hg_rad
127	    -- Step 6: feed the kernel inequality to Mathlib's `IsClosedImmersion.lift`.
128	    exact ⟨IsClosedImmersion.lift i g hker, IsClosedImmersion.lift_fac i g hker⟩
129	
130	end IsClosedImmersion
131	
132	/--
133	**Substrate 2 — `Away f ⊗_{k̄} GmRing k̄` is a domain.**
134	
135	For a non-zero homogeneous element `f` of positive degree `d` in the
136	`projectiveLineBar` grading on `MvPolynomial (Fin 2) k̄`, the tensor
137	`HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading k̄) f ⊗_{k̄} GmRing k̄`
138	is an integral domain.
139	
140	The proof identifies the tensor with the localization
141	`Localization.Away (X () : MvPolynomial Unit (Away f))` via the
142	chain of natural isomorphisms in `analogies/lane-b-substrate.md` §3:
143	* `Away f ⊗_{k̄} 𝕜[t] ≃ 𝕜[t][Away f]` (`MvPolynomial.algebraTensorAlgEquiv`),
144	* tensor base change with the localization
145	  `𝕜[t] → GmRing k̄` produces `Localization.Away (X ())`
146	  (`IsLocalization.Away.tensorRight`).
147	
148	`Localization.Away (X () : MvPolynomial Unit (Away f))` is a domain because
149	it is the localization of `MvPolynomial Unit (Away f)` (a polynomial ring
150	over the domain `Away f`) at the powers of the non-zero generator `X ()`.
151	
152	This is **Substrate 2** of the Lane B Cross01 cocycle proof; see
153	`blueprint/src/chapters/Genus0BaseObjects_Cross01Substrate.tex`,
154	`Theorem~\ref{thm:gmRing_tensor_homogeneousAway_isDomain}`.
155	-/
156	@[nolint unusedArguments]
157	theorem gmRing_tensor_homogeneousAway_isDomain
158	    (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] {d : ℕ} (_hd : 0 < d)
159	    (f : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)
160	    (_hf : f ∈ projectiveLineBarGrading kbar d)
161	    (_hf_ne : f ≠ 0) :
162	    IsDomain (TensorProduct kbar
163	      (HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) f)
164	      (GmRing kbar)) := by
165	  classical
166	  -- Abbreviation for the homogeneous localization (a `kbar`-algebra and a domain).
167	  let A : Type u := HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) f
168	  -- =====================================================================
169	  -- Step 1: `A` is a domain.
170	  -- =====================================================================
171	  -- The bridge `val : A → Localization.Away f` is injective, and we transport
172	  -- domain-ness via `Function.Injective.isDomain`.  This pattern is the same
173	  -- one used inside `projectiveLineBar_isReduced` (`GmScaling.lean` L734-738).
174	  haveI hLocAway_dom :
175	      IsDomain (Localization.Away (f : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)) := by
176	    have hPoly_dom : IsDomain (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) := inferInstance
177	    by_cases hf_zero : f = 0
178	    · -- if f = 0, Localization.Away f is Subsingleton, but we still need IsDomain.
179	      -- Instead, contradict with hf_ne.
180	      exact absurd hf_zero _hf_ne
181	    · exact IsLocalization.isDomain_localization
182	        (powers_le_nonZeroDivisors_of_noZeroDivisors hf_zero)
183	  haveI hA_dom : IsDomain A :=
184	    Function.Injective.isDomain (algebraMap A (Localization.Away f))
185	      (fun x y h => HomogeneousLocalization.val_injective _ h)
186	  -- =====================================================================
187	  -- Step 2: `MvPolynomial Unit A` is a domain (polynomial ring over a domain).
188	  -- =====================================================================
189	  haveI hT_dom : IsDomain (MvPolynomial Unit A) := inferInstance
190	  -- =====================================================================
191	  -- Step 3: Build the natural iso
192	  --   `A ⊗[kbar] (GmRing kbar) ≃ₐ[A] Localization.Away (X () : MvPolynomial Unit A)`.
193	  -- =====================================================================
194	  -- Step 3a:  `A ⊗[kbar] (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) ≃ₐ[A] MvPolynomial Unit A`.
195	  -- This is `MvPolynomial.algebraTensorAlgEquiv` directly.
196	  let e1 : TensorProduct kbar A (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) ≃ₐ[A] MvPolynomial Unit A :=
197	    MvPolynomial.algebraTensorAlgEquiv kbar A
198	  -- Step 3b: Establish IsLocalization of the target tensor.
199	  -- We use the `Algebra.TensorProduct.rightAlgebra` to view the tensor as a
200	  -- localization. The plan is to set up the algebra structure correctly

34	### Attempt 2 (RESOLVED)
35	
36	- **Approach**: completely **different route** — exploit the open immersion `Gm ↪ 𝔸¹` rather than the tensor IsDomain.
37	  1. **Helper `isDomain_mvPolyUnit_tensor`** (~10 LOC, axiom-clean): `TensorProduct kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) K` is a domain via the iso chain `Algebra.TensorProduct.comm` + `MvPolynomial.algebraTensorAlgEquiv` + `Function.Injective.isDomain`. **No `IsLocalization.Away`** in sight; just polynomial-ring base change.
38	  2. **Helper `affineLine_geomIrred`** (~15 LOC, axiom-clean): `GeometricallyIrreducible (Spec.map (algebraMap kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar)))` via `geometrically_iff_of_commRing_of_isClosedUnderIsomorphisms`, then `pullbackSpecIso` + the iso's `.hom.homeomorph.irreducibleSpace_iff` to transport `IrreducibleSpace (PrimeSpectrum (MvPoly Unit kbar ⊗ K))` (a domain) to the pullback.
39	  3. **`gm_geomIrred`** (~25 LOC, axiom-clean): factor `(Gm).hom = Spec.map (algebraMap (MvPoly Unit kbar) (GmRing kbar)) ≫ Spec.map (algebraMap kbar (MvPoly Unit kbar))`; first arrow is open immersion (Mathlib `isOpenImmersion_SpecMap_localizationAway`), second arrow is GI (from `affineLine_geomIrred`), `Surjective (composition)` via `Subsingleton (Spec K)` for K a field. Apply Mathlib's `[IsOpenImmersion f] [GeometricallyIrreducible g] [Surjective (f ≫ g)] → GeometricallyIrreducible (f ≫ g)` instance (requires `set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in`).
40	- **Result**: **RESOLVED axiom-clean**.
41	- **Key insight**: `Gm` is the basic open `D₊(t) ⊂ 𝔸¹`, so its `GeometricallyIrreducible` follows from `𝔸¹.hom`'s `GeometricallyIrreducible` via the open-immersion + surjectivity instance. **This bypasses the heavy `K ⊗_kbar (GmRing kbar)` tensor IsDomain entirely**, replacing it with the much lighter `K ⊗_kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) ≃ MvPolynomial Unit K`.
42	- **Helper budget used**: 2 (over budget of 1). Both new helpers (`isDomain_mvPolyUnit_tensor`, `affineLine_geomIrred`) are reusable for Lane M↓ + iter-192+ `projectiveLineBar_geomIrred` (currently a `sorry` in `BareScheme.lean` could be closed via similar `Proj.affineOpenCoverOfIrrelevantLESpan`-based route, but that's not this iter's scope).

49	  1. **`hred_inter : IsReduced intersection`** — Substrate 2 at degree-2 generator `(X 0) * (X 1)` + transport via `gmScalingP1_cover_intersection_X_iso.hom` (an iso, hence `IsOpenImmersion`). Axiom-clean.

51	- **Result**: PARTIAL — structural setup deepened. The sorry now stands at the **topological range containment** `Set.range s_pair.base ⊆ Set.range (pullback.diagonal PLB.hom).base`, which is the substantive math (closed-point + density check).
52	- **Substantive content of the residual** (documented in the lemma body): at kbar-rational closed points of intersection, the (x, λ) point maps under chart 0 to `(1 : x⁻¹λ⁻¹)` and under chart 1 to `(xλ : 1)`, both representing the same point `[xλ : 1] = [1 : 1/(xλ)] = [1 : x⁻¹λ⁻¹]` of ℙ¹. Closed-point density (kbar alg-closed, intersection LOFT) + closure-of-range argument upgrades this to all points. **The lemma's current signature lacks `[IsAlgClosed kbar]`** — this is the only blocker, and adding it propagates trivially to consumers.

57	  4. apply `IsClosedImmersion.lift_iff_range_subset` (Substrate 1) to extract `s : intersection → PLB`;
58	  5. derive cocycle via `pullback.diagonal_fst` / `_snd`.

9:# Genus-`0` base objects (Stratum 1): the bare scheme `ProjectiveLineBar` and its 2-chart cover
16:* the projective line `ProjectiveLineBarScheme` / `ProjectiveLineBar` as an object of
18:* properness of `ProjectiveLineBar.hom` (`projectiveLineBar_isProper`);
45:Used to define `ProjectiveLineBar = Proj` of this graded ring. -/
87:def ProjectiveLineBar (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Over (Spec (.of kbar)) :=
107:    IsProper (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom := by
155:    GeometricallyIrreducible (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom :=
162:    SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom :=

75	
76	/-- The natural `Over (Spec (.of kbar))` structure on `ProjectiveLineBarScheme` via
77	`Proj.toSpecZero` and the identification `k̄ ≃ ↥(𝒜 0)`. -/
78	instance projectiveLineBarScheme_canOver (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
79	    (ProjectiveLineBarScheme kbar).Over (Spec (.of kbar)) where
80	  hom := Proj.toSpecZero (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) ≫
81	    Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom
82	      (algebraMap kbar ↥((projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) 0)))
83	
84	/-- **The projective line `ℙ¹_{k̄}` as an object of `Over (Spec (.of kbar))`.** This is the
85	concrete scheme used by `morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const` (iter-166) to formalise the
86	`𝔾_m`-scaling shortcut. -/
87	def ProjectiveLineBar (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Over (Spec (.of kbar)) :=
88	  (ProjectiveLineBarScheme kbar).asOver (Spec (.of kbar))
89	
90	/-- **`ℙ¹_{k̄}` is proper over `Spec k̄`.** FREE from
91	`AlgebraicGeometry.Proj.instIsProperToSpecZero…` — the algebra `k̄[X₀, X₁]` is finite type
92	over its degree-`0` piece `↥(𝒜 0) ≃ k̄` — chained via the standard properness-of-composition
93	lemma with the `Spec.map` of the algebra map `k̄ → ↥(𝒜 0)` (which is bijective hence
94	gives an iso of `Spec`s).
95	
96	The chain:
97	* `Proj.toSpecZero 𝒜` is proper (Mathlib's
98	  `Proj.instIsProperToSpecZeroOfFiniteTypeSubtypeMemOfNatNat`, given
99	  `Algebra.FiniteType ↥(𝒜 0) (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) k̄)` — supplied by the
100	  `IsScalarTower kbar ↥(𝒜 0) (MvPolynomial _ _)` + `Algebra.FiniteType k̄ (MvPolynomial _ _)`
101	  chain via `Algebra.FiniteType.of_restrictScalars_finiteType`);
102	* `Spec.map (algebraMap k̄ ↥(𝒜 0))` is an iso because `algebraMap k̄ ↥(𝒜 0)` is bijective
103	  in this standard ℕ-grading case (`𝒜 0 = MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule (Fin 2) k̄ 0`
104	  is the constants subalgebra, naturally ≅ k̄);
105	* composition of proper + iso is proper. -/
106	instance projectiveLineBar_isProper (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
107	    IsProper (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom := by
108	  -- Unfold to expose the Proj.toSpecZero ≫ Spec.map chain.
109	  change IsProper (Proj.toSpecZero (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) ≫
110	    Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom
111	      (algebraMap kbar ↥((projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) 0))))
112	  -- The IsScalarTower kbar ↥(𝒜 0) (MvPolynomial _ kbar) needed for the FiniteType derivation.
113	  haveI : IsScalarTower kbar
114	      ↥(MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule (Fin 2) kbar 0)
115	      (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) :=
116	    IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_eq fun _ => rfl
117	  -- MvPolynomial.{Fin 2} k̄ is finite type over ↥(𝒜 0) (chained from finite-type over k̄).
118	  haveI : Algebra.FiniteType
119	      ↥(MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule (Fin 2) kbar 0)
120	      (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) :=
121	    Algebra.FiniteType.of_restrictScalars_finiteType kbar _ _
122	  -- The algebra map `k̄ → ↥(𝒜 0)` is bijective: the degree-`0` piece of the homogeneous
123	  -- decomposition is exactly the constants `C(k̄) ⊆ MvPolynomial (Fin 2) k̄`. Injectivity
124	  -- follows from `MvPolynomial.C_injective`. Surjectivity uses
125	  -- `MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent_of_mem` (which says the degree-`0` component of a
126	  -- homogeneous-degree-`0` polynomial is itself) plus `homogeneousComponent_zero`
127	  -- (which expresses the degree-`0` component as `C` of the constant coefficient).
128	  have hbij : Function.Bijective (algebraMap kbar
129	      ↥(MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule (Fin 2) kbar 0)) := by
130	    refine ⟨?_, ?_⟩
131	    · intro x y h
132	      apply MvPolynomial.C_injective (Fin 2) kbar
133	      exact congrArg Subtype.val h
134	    · intro ⟨v, hv⟩
135	      refine ⟨MvPolynomial.coeff 0 v, ?_⟩
136	      apply Subtype.ext
137	      rw [SetLike.GradeZero.coe_algebraMap]
138	      have key := MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent_of_mem hv (m := 0)
139	      -- `key : (homogeneousComponent 0) v = if 0 = 0 then v else 0` which simp resolves
140	      -- and combines with `homogeneousComponent_zero` to give `C (coeff 0 v) = v`.
141	      simp only [MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent_zero, if_true] at key
142	      exact key
143	  -- Therefore `Spec.map (algebraMap k̄ ↥(𝒜 0))` is iso, and composition of proper + iso is
144	  -- proper.
145	  haveI : IsIso (Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom
146	      (algebraMap kbar ↥(MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule (Fin 2) kbar 0)))) := by
147	    rw [isIso_SpecMap_iff]
148	    exact hbij
149	  infer_instance
150	
151	/-- **`ℙ¹_{k̄}` is geometrically irreducible over `Spec k̄`.** Project-side scaffold sorry
152	(Mathlib does not ship `GeometricallyIrreducible` for `Proj` of a polynomial ring;
153	plan-marked acceptable for iter-165). -/
154	instance projectiveLineBar_geomIrred (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
155	    GeometricallyIrreducible (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom :=
156	  sorry

1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are one of: the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (per descriptor in `.archon/subagents/`), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	When instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**:
8	
9	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` override Archon's global prompts.
10	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` override globally installed plugins.
11	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project.
12	
13	## Skills
14	
15	- **archon-lean4** — installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source). Provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, etc.
16	
17	## Tools
18	
19	Project tools live in `.claude/tools/` as directly-executable scripts. List with `ls .claude/tools/`; run `<path> --help`. MCP servers (Lean LSP, etc.) are registered in `.claude/settings.json` / `.mcp.json` and surface as `mcp__*` tools.
20	
21	Two always-present scripts:
22	
23	- **`archon-informal-agent.py`** — call external LLMs for an informal proof-style sketch when you're stuck on an approach and want a second opinion. Supports DeepSeek, Kimi, OpenRouter, OpenAI, and Gemini; defaults to `--provider auto` which picks the best available key automatically. **Requires at least one key in env** (`DEEPSEEK_API_KEY` / `MOONSHOT_API_KEY` / `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` / `OPENAI_API_KEY` / `GEMINI_API_KEY`) — check via `env | grep -E "DEEPSEEK|MOONSHOT|OPENROUTER|OPENAI|GEMINI"` BEFORE planning around its use; if no key is set, fall back to alternatives below. The output is LLM-generated from training data, NOT source-derived: this tool is **not** a literature retriever. For actual literature lookup (cross-check a claim against Hartshorne, fetch an arXiv paper, summarize a Stacks tag) consult the auto-injected subagent catalog — when a literature/reference-fetching subagent is enabled it will appear there — or use the `WebSearch` / `WebFetch` tools directly.
24	- **`archon-subagent.py`** — the generic subagent dispatcher (one wrapper handles every subagent — see "Subagents" below).
25	
26	## Subagents
27	
28	Subagents are descriptor files at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md` (YAML frontmatter — `name`, `description`, `write_domain`, `read_only`, `can_spawn`, `default_enabled`, optional `mandatory: [<phase>...]`, optional `dispatcher_notes` — followed by the prompt body the spawned Claude reads).
29	
30	**You do NOT need to discover subagents.** The plan and review prompts auto-inject an **Available subagents** section at the top of each invocation listing every enabled descriptor with description, write-domain, MANDATORY flag, and `dispatcher_notes`. When you decide to invoke a subagent, read its full prompt at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md`.
31	
32	**Invoke** via the generic wrapper (Bash tool, foreground):
33	
34	```
35	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
36	  --name <subagent-name> \
37	  --slug <kebab-slug> \
38	  --directive-file <path-to-directive.md> \
39	  --write-domain '<glob>'        # repeat for multiple
40	```
41	
42	The wrapper exits 0 on success, prints a one-line status, writes the report to `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`, and the Archon CLI auto-archives a copy to `logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-report.md` for the dashboard. The dispatch semaphore caps concurrent subagent processes at `loop.max_parallel`; the wrapper resolves `--parent-slug` from `ARCHON_SUBAGENT_SLUG` so nested children inherit the hierarchy.
43	
44	**Treat each dispatch as blocking — wait for its report before acting on it.** Don't *deliberately* set `run_in_background: true`. But note the wrapper is genuinely synchronous (it `subprocess.run`s the `archon subagent` CLI and only returns once the child finishes and its report is on disk), so the harness may *auto-background* a long-running dispatch and hand you a task ID immediately — that's expected and fine. When it does, await that task / poll for the report file at `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md` (the wrapper's one-line status and exit 0 confirm completion) before you read or act on the result. The thing to avoid is firing a dispatch and then continuing to plan as if it had already returned; whether the call blocked inline or was auto-backgrounded, the rule is the same: do not consume a subagent's output until its report exists.
45	
46	**Highly-recommended subagents.** A descriptor whose frontmatter sets `mandatory: [plan]` is treated as **highly recommended** for the plan phase (similarly `[review]` for the review phase). The catalog tags them `[HIGHLY RECOMMENDED]`. Dispatch each one unless you have a concrete reason to skip — when skipping, record a one-line rationale under a `## Subagent skips` section in `iter/iter-NNN/<phase>.md` (e.g. `- strategy-critic: STRATEGY.md SHA unchanged from prior iter and last verdict was SOUND`). A post-phase audit checks both signals: it silences when the subagent dispatched OR a skip rationale was recorded, and warns only when neither happened. Each subagent's `dispatcher_notes` enumerates its specific skip conditions — read them before deciding. **Subagents ship disabled by default** (`default_enabled: false`), so the rule only fires for subagents the user has explicitly listed under `subagents.enabled` in `.archon/config.json`; on a fresh project the catalog is empty and no recommended dispatch is required. (The frontmatter field is still named `mandatory: [...]` for backward compat with existing configs and descriptors — its semantic meaning is "highly recommended", not "must dispatch".)
47	
48	## Key Files & Permissions
49	
50	All Archon state files are in `.archon/`. The table below covers **phase agents** (plan, prover, review) and the **user**. Subagent permissions are NOT a fixed row — they are descriptor-driven (`write_domain` glob in the subagent's frontmatter, enforced at dispatch time). See "Subagent permissions" after the table.
51	
52	| File | Plan | Prover | Review | User |
53	|------|------|--------|--------|------|
54	| `PROGRESS.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
55	| `STRATEGY.md` | read + write | — | — | read |
56	| `USER_HINTS.md` | **loop-managed** (captured + cleared by the CLI; injected into the plan prompt) | — | — | write |
57	| `task_pending.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
58	| `task_done.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
59	| `task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect) | write (own file) | read only | read |
60	| `proof-journal/` | read | — | **write** | read |
61	| `PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | — | **write** (Knowledge Base only — session log lives in `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`) | read |
62	| `iter/iter-NNN/plan.md` | **write** (this iter only) | — | read (recent window injected) | read |
63	| `iter/iter-NNN/review.md` | read (recent window injected) | — | **write** (this iter only) | read |
64	| `iter/iter-NNN/objectives.md` | optional write | — | read | read |
65	| `archon-protected.yaml` | read | read | read | **write** |
66	| `.lean` files | — | write (own file; frozen protected signatures) | — | write (via comments) |
67	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints) | read | **write** (semantic markers: `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections — NOT `\leanok`) | read |
68	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | — | — | read |
69	| `TO_USER.md` | — (review owns) | — | **write** (reset at review start) | read |
70	
71	### Subagent permissions
72	
73	Each subagent ships YAML frontmatter:
74	
75	- `write_domain: "<glob>"` — file pattern the subagent may write. Empty for purely read-only subagents (the dispatching call typically passes `--write-domain 'task_results/**'` so the subagent can still write its own report).
76	- `read_only: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may only write its `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`.
77	- `can_spawn: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may dispatch children whose `write_domain` is a subset of its own.
78	
79	The dispatcher enforces `write_domain` at the filesystem level: any child whose declared domain is not a subset of the parent's is rejected before the child agent starts. Reads are restricted only by the descriptor's prompt body (e.g. fresh-context critics that are told not to read PROGRESS.md / STRATEGY.md).
80	
81	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md`** is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow run by hand by the mathematician. The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. To invoke a refactor-style subagent inside the loop, write the directive to a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-directive.md` and dispatch via the generic wrapper. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise.
82	
83	## Protected Declarations
84	
85	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Rules:
86	
87	- **Plan / prover / review** agents: read-only on protected signatures. May fill proof bodies; may not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
88	- **Write-capable structural subagents** (per their `write_domain`): may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (name + signature preserved verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. They may NEVER rename / re-type / delete / re-sign a protected declaration.
89	- The YAML file itself is user-only. No agent adds or removes a declaration. The only allowed agent edit is updating an existing declaration's file path.
90	
91	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
92	
93	Two semantic markers:
94	
95	- `\leanok` — statement block: declaration is formalized (at least a `sorry` present). Proof block: proof closed, no `sorry`.
96	- `\mathlibok` — statement block: declaration is backed by Mathlib (re-export / alias); no Archon proof obligation remains.
97	
98	No marker means the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
99	
100	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between prover and review each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves.
101	
102	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations are the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
103	
104	## User Interaction
105	
106	The user provides hints in two places:
107	
108	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The Archon loop captures this file's content before each plan phase, injects it into the plan agent's prompt, and resets the file to its bundled template (an HTML-comment format guide + zero bullets) after the plan phase succeeds. The HTML-comment preamble is stripped before injection so a template-only file renders as "no hints" to the planner. The plan agent does NOT read or clear that file itself. Provers never see these hints.
109	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
110	
111	## Agent Roles
112	
113	### Plan Agent
114	
115	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for full instructions.
116	- User hints, blueprint-doctor findings, recent iter sidecars, the subagent catalog, and the references summary are all pre-injected into your invocation prompt. You do not "go read" those files.
117	- Read `task_results/` to collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md`.
118	- Optionally invoke subagents (via the auto-injected catalog) before writing prover objectives. Subagent reports are auto-archived to `logs/iter-NNN/` by the Archon CLI.
119	- Write `PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round.
120	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch markers — `\leanok` is owned by the deterministic sync; `\mathlibok` / `% NOTE:` by the review agent.
121	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself.
122	
123	### Prover Agent
124	
125	- Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
126	- Your **active mode** is injected directly into your invocation prompt — read it carefully, it defines your goal, workflow, and constraints for this session.
127	- When your mode specifies `read_blueprint: true`, you are permitted to **read** your assigned blueprint chapter (`blueprint/src/chapters/<your_slug>.tex`) for the informal proof sketch. You may never write to blueprint chapters.
128	- Write results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.
129	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — never edit another agent's file.
130	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your file for file-specific hints.
131	- Do NOT edit blueprint chapters. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker; the review agent applies them.
132	
133	### Subagents
134	
135	Subagents are descriptor-driven and discovered at runtime. The catalog is auto-injected into the plan and review prompts each phase (see "Subagents" above for the wrapper invocation). Each subagent reads only what its directive points at — never `PROGRESS.md` / `STRATEGY.md` / phase-agent state — and writes a self-contained report at `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`. Descriptors with `can_spawn: true` may dispatch children (subject to the global `loop.max_parallel` cap).
136	
137	### Review Agent
138	
139	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for full instructions.
140	- Read `proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data.
141	- Write the session journal to `proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (`summary.md`, `milestones.jsonl`, `recommendations.md`).
142	- Update `PROJECT_STATUS.md` (Knowledge Base only — session narrative goes to `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`).
143	- Maintain the semantic blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE:` annotations, stale `\notready` removal. Do NOT touch `\leanok`.
144	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify `PROGRESS.md`.
145	
146	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
147	
148	These run automatically between phases:
149	
150	- **Iter sidecar init** — creates `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/` at iter start so plan + review have a stable destination for per-iter narrative. Top-level files (STRATEGY.md, PROJECT_STATUS.md, task_*.md) stay bounded.
151	- **`sync_leanok`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status.
152	- **`blueprint-doctor`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically detecting orphan chapters, broken `\ref` / `\uses` / `\proves`, and new `axiom` declarations. Findings are written to `logs/iter-NNN/blueprint-doctor.{md,json}` and inlined into the next plan agent's prompt.

1	# Prover — Prover Stage
2	
3	You fill `sorry` placeholders with complete proofs in your assigned `.lean` file.
4	
5	## Workflow
6	
7	1. Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
8	2. Read `task_pending.md` for prior attempts, dead ends, relevant lemmas on your file.
9	3. Check the `.lean` file for `/- USER: ... -/` comments (file-specific user hints).
10	4. **Read the relevant blueprint chapter before writing Lean code.** The chapter holds the mathematical proof sketch you must align with. When stuck, re-reading the blueprint is often the fastest path forward.
11	5. Replace `sorry` with Lean proofs. Push as far as possible.
12	6. **Always save partial progress in the code.** If you can't fully close a sorry, leave your best attempt — commented-out steps, helper lemmas, partial `by` blocks with `sorry` at the stuck point. The file must still compile (use scoped `sorry` if needed), but your work must be visible for the next agent to continue from. NEVER revert to a bare `sorry` — that wastes the work.
13	7. Write your results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.
14	
15	**Write permissions**: only your assigned `.lean` file(s) and `task_results/<your_file>.md`. Do NOT edit `PROGRESS.md`, `task_pending.md`, `task_done.md`, blueprint chapters, or other agents' files.
16	
17	## Protected declarations
18	
19	`archon-protected.yaml` lists declarations with **frozen signatures**. You may fill their proof bodies but must not rename, re-type, reorder arguments, or weaken hypotheses. Only the mathematician edits protected signatures.
20	
21	## Avoid early termination
22	
23	- Don't abandon a proof prematurely. Many complex proofs run to thousands of lines.
24	- Difficulty is NOT a valid reason to leave a `sorry`.
25	- Don't delegate to "the next iteration" or "another prover" if more effort could close it.
26	- Only modify the proof for your assigned task — leave unrelated proofs untouched.
27	- **Decompose**: break the proof into smaller sub-problems (following the blueprint's lemma structure when available: L1, L2, L3, …) and solve each individually until the entire goal closes.
28	
29	## Task completion criteria
30	
31	Your task is complete ONLY when ALL of:
32	
33	1. Every `sorry` in scope is replaced with a complete proof.
34	2. Zero axioms introduced.
35	3. The file compiles cleanly.
36	
37	## Never weaken the type to dodge the proof
38	
39	When the substantive type is unattainable this iter, you have one honest move: leave `sorry` with the **intended type signature** so the type itself encodes the claim. You must NOT substitute a tautologically-true type to trivialize the body. Three patterns are banned:
40	
41	- **Reflexive-iso placeholder.** Replacing a non-trivial `X ≅ Y` (or `Nonempty (X ≅ Y)`) target with `Nonempty (X ≅ X) := ⟨Iso.refl _⟩`. The type now asserts nothing; the docstring's "iter-N placeholder" disclosure does not undo the structural lie, and the blueprint's `\lean{...}` cross-reference will resolve to this hollow declaration.
42	- **`Classical.choice` body around an explicit witness.** Writing `Classical.choice (α := X) ⟨witness⟩` to dissolve `Type` vs `Prop` friction — the witness is unreachable through any unfold, so the def is indistinguishable from `Classical.choice ⟨any-other-X⟩` downstream. Either use `Classical.choose` on the existential chain (preserves the spec), or `Nonempty.some`-with-witness; or ship a typed `sorry`.
43	- **Empty-content `proof_wanted`.** `proof_wanted` discards the declaration post-elaboration — the named decl never enters the environment, so the blueprint's `\lean{...}` cross-reference breaks. Use `theorem name : T := by sorry` with the intended signature instead.
44	
45	**Litmus test**: if you `unfold` your declaration, does it expose the named substantive content (Kähler differential module, the explicit iso, …) or does it stop at `Classical.choice` / `Iso.refl _` / nothing? If the latter, the body is structurally vacuous — ship the typed `sorry` instead.
46	
47	## When infrastructure is missing
48	
49	Do NOT report "Mathlib lacks X" and stop. Before giving up:
50	
51	1. **Use the informal agent** (`.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py`): "Prove [goal] without using [missing infrastructure], only Mathlib." Even an imperfect sketch is valuable.
52	2. **Try the alternative** — formalize whatever the informal agent suggests.
53	3. **If you still can't**: write the alternative sketch to `informal/<theorem_name>.md` and record in your task result what you tried, why it failed, AND the alternative route you found. "I couldn't prove X, but here's approach Y that might work because Z" is far more useful than "infrastructure missing".
54	
55	When stuck more generally: break into smaller subgoals, search Mathlib more thoroughly, prove missing helpers yourself, try alternative strategies, re-read the blueprint, use Web Search for published proofs.
56	
57	**Impossibility vs difficulty**: technical difficulty → keep trying. Mathematical impossibility → immediately backtrack and document why.
58	
59	## Proof style
60	
61	- **Never modify working proofs** — if a declaration has no `sorry` and compiles, do not touch its body.
62	- Keep edits minimal; don't delete comments or change labels; don't add unrelated declarations.
63	- Helper lemmas you introduced may be modified if they turn out wrong.
64	- Add a concise comment above each helper lemma so reuse is easy.
65	- **`change` vs `show`** — `change` reshapes the goal up to defeq; `show` is purely display-level annotation. Using `show` where `change` is needed produces a linter warning. Default to `change` when in doubt.
66	
67	## Mathlib tags in PROGRESS.md
68	
69	The plan agent tags suggested lemmas:
70	
71	- `[verified]` — confirmed to exist.
72	- `[expected]` — guessed by naming convention. Quick `lean_local_search`; pivot if it doesn't exist.
73	- `[gap]` — verified NOT in Mathlib. Don't waste search time; formalize a workaround.
74	
75	## LSP MCP tools
76	
77	The `archon-lean-lsp` server exposes Lean LSP operations as **MCP tool calls** (full names like `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_goal` / `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_diagnostic_messages`). The lean4 skill reference uses short names (`lean_goal`, `lean_local_search`, …) — same tools, NOT shell binaries.
78	
79	- Always invoke through your tool-call interface, the same way as `Read` or `Grep`.
80	- **Never** call `Bash` with `lean_goal …` etc. — there is no such shell command.
81	- First LSP action: `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_diagnostic_messages` on your file. If it returns `success: false` the server is cold; retry once or run `lake build` once via Bash, then retry.
82	
83	## Search protocol
84	
85	Follow `references/lean-lsp-tools-api.md`. Key priority:
86	
87	1. `lean_local_search` first.
88	2. `lean_leansearch` for semantic search — describe the mathematical content, not just the name.
89	3. `lean_loogle` for simple type patterns only.
90	4. Never use shell `find` / `grep` to locate Mathlib theorems.
91	
92	## Tooling traps
93	
94	- **Trust `goals_after`, not just empty diagnostics**: in `lean_multi_attempt`, `diagnostics: []` can be misleading (line-scoping quirks attach errors to the enclosing `by` block). Check that `goals_after` is empty or has advanced.
95	- **Beware `lean_run_code` with imports**: standalone snippets can silently swallow elaboration errors when they contain imports. Rely on the actual file's `lean_diagnostic_messages` for authoritative verification.
96	
97	## Logging
98	
99	Write to `task_results/<your_file>.md` (mirror your `.lean` path: `Algebra/WLocal.lean` → `task_results/Algebra_WLocal.lean.md`):
100	
101	```markdown
102	# Algebra/WLocal.lean
103	
104	## wLocal_iff (line 45)
105	### Attempt 1
106	- **Approach:** Direct case split on maximal ideals
107	- **Result:** FAILED — needed IsLocalRing instance, not available
108	- **Dead end:** direct case split without IsLocalRing
109	
110	### Attempt 2
111	- **Approach:** Stacks 0A31, characterize via bijection on spectra
112	- **Result:** RESOLVED
113	- **Key insight:** `PrimeSpectrum.comap_injective` bridges the gap
114	
115	## helper_bijective (line 78)
116	### Attempt 1
117	- **Approach:** Split into injectivity + surjectivity; prove injectivity
118	- **Result:** PARTIAL (branch closed) — injectivity closed; surjectivity remains
119	- **Next step:** `PrimeSpectrum.range_comap_of_surjective` for the right branch
120	- **Lemmas found:** `PrimeSpectrum.comap_surjective`
121	```
122	
123	**Rules**: one section per theorem/lemma in your file. Each attempt records approach, result (RESOLVED / FAILED / PARTIAL / IN PROGRESS), dead-end warnings or next steps. Log negative search results ("Searched 'projective module infinite rank' — nothing in Mathlib"). The plan agent merges these into `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md` next iter.
124	
125	**Read-only context**: read `task_pending.md` (prior attempts on your file) and `task_done.md` (when the problem resembles a completed one). Do not write to either.
126	
127	## End-of-session handoff
128	
129	Before stopping:
130	
131	1. Write `task_results/<your_file>.md` with: current result, lemmas discovered, concrete next step, dead-end warnings.
132	2. Save all changes; ensure the file compiles (use scoped `sorry` for stuck parts if needed).
133

10	/-!
11	# The identity component of the Picard scheme (A.3)
12	
13	This file is the **A.3** sub-build chapter for the project's positive-genus arm
14	of `nonempty_jacobianWitness`. It scaffolds the abstract identity-component
15	substrate for a `k`-group scheme locally of finite type and specialises it to
16	`G = Pic_{C/k}`, packaging:
17	
18	1. the open-and-closed subgroup-scheme structure of the identity component,
19	2. the degree map `Pic_{C/k}(k) → ℤ`,
20	3. the abelian-variety identification of `Pic⁰_{C/k}` (the Jacobian variety
21	   of `C` when `C/k` is a smooth proper geometrically integral curve of
22	   positive genus).
23	
24	## Status (iter-185 NEW file-skeleton)
25	
26	This file is the **iter-185 IdentityComponent** file-skeleton (NEW lane,
27	blueprint-reviewer HARD GATE cleared for the iter-184 plan-phase chapter
28	`Picard_IdentityComponent.tex`). Each of the five blueprint-pinned
29	declarations carries the *intended* substantive type signature (matching the
30	`\lean{...}` pin in `Picard_IdentityComponent.tex`) with a `sorry` body. The
31	bodies are iter-186+ work; iter-185's mandate is the mechanical scaffold only.
32	
33	The 5 pinned declarations are:
34	
35	1. `AlgebraicGeometry.GroupScheme.IdentityComponent` (def, ~5 LOC) — the
36	   **identity component** `G^0` of a `k`-group scheme `G` locally of finite
37	   type, as a `k`-scheme (an `Over (Spec k)`-object). Abstract substrate
38	   reusable outside the Picard context; not yet in Mathlib.
39	2. `AlgebraicGeometry.GroupScheme.IdentityComponent.isOpenSubgroupScheme`
40	   (theorem, ~10 LOC) — the bundled statement that `G^0` is an open and
41	   closed subscheme of `G` via an open immersion `G^0 ↪ G`.
42	3. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Pic0Scheme` (def, ~5 LOC) — the **identity
43	   component of the Picard scheme** `Pic⁰_{C/k}`, obtained by applying
44	   `IdentityComponent` to `G = PicScheme C`.
45	4. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicScheme.degree` (def, ~5 LOC) — the **degree
46	   map** `Pic_{C/k}(k) → ℤ`, extracting the leading coefficient of the
47	   Hilbert polynomial of a representing invertible sheaf relative to a fixed
48	   degree-one polarisation `O_C(1)`.
49	5. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Pic0Scheme.isAbelianVariety` (theorem, ~10 LOC)
50	   — the **abelian-variety identification** of `Pic⁰_{C/k}`: smooth, proper,
51	   geometrically irreducible `k`-group scheme of dimension `g(C)` --- the
52	   Jacobian variety of `C`.
53	
54	## Note on type expressivity
55	
56	Following the project rule "Never weaken the type to dodge the proof", each
57	pinned declaration carries a substantive, non-tautological type:
58	
59	- `IdentityComponent G : Over (Spec (.of k))` — a `k`-scheme, not the
60	  tautological `G` itself.
61	- `IdentityComponent.isOpenSubgroupScheme G` — asserts the *existence* of an
62	  open immersion morphism `IdentityComponent G ⟶ G` whose underlying map of
63	  schemes is both an open and a closed immersion (clopen subscheme).
64	- `Pic0Scheme C : Over (Spec (.of k))` — a `k`-scheme.
65	- `PicScheme.degree C : (Spec (.of k) ⟶ (PicScheme C).left) → ℤ` —
66	  a genuine function from `k`-points to `ℤ`, not a constant.
67	- `Pic0Scheme.isAbelianVariety C` — asserts the conjunction of the four
68	  abelian-variety properties (proper, smooth, geometrically irreducible,
69	  group-object structure); not vacuous because each conjunct is a genuine
70	  property/structure on the (typed-sorry) `Pic0Scheme C`.
71	
72	## References
73	
74	Blueprint: `blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_IdentityComponent.tex` (560 LOC,
75	5 pins). Sources:
76	- Kleiman, "The Picard scheme", §5, Lem.~`lem:agps` (identity component
77	  substrate) + Prp.~`prp:pic0` (specialisation to `Pic_{C/k}`) +
78	  Thm.~`th:qpp&p` (quasi-projectivity/projectivity) + Cor.~`cor:sm`
79	  (smoothness/dimension) + Ex.~`ex:jac` + Rmk.~`rmk:Jac`
80	  (arXiv:math/0504020 pp. 36, 38, 47, 50–51);
81	- Milne, "Abelian Varieties" (course notes, 2008), §III.1
82	  (def. of abelian variety, p. 8; dimension equals genus, Rmk. III.1.4(e),
83	  p. 86).
84	-/

94	/-! ## §1. The identity component of a group scheme — abstract substrate
95	
96	The identity component is an abstract feature of `k`-group schemes locally of
97	finite type. We package the definition + open-and-closed-subgroup-scheme
98	structure in the `GroupScheme` namespace so they are reusable outside the
99	Picard context.
100	
101	In this project, a "`k`-group scheme" is encoded as an
102	`Over (Spec (.of k))`-object `G` carrying a `[GrpObj G]` instance (the
103	group-object structure in the over-category). The locally-of-finite-type
104	hypothesis is the `[LocallyOfFiniteType G.hom]` instance on the structural
105	morphism to the base.
106	
107	Blueprint references: `def:identity_component_group_scheme` +
108	`thm:identity_component_open_subgroup` (Kleiman §5 Lem.~`lem:agps`). -/
109	
110	namespace GroupScheme
111	
112	/-- Helper (iter-188): in a **Noetherian** topological space `α`, the set of
113	connected components is finite.
114	
115	Proof: the map `irreducibleComponents α → ConnectedComponents α` sending each
116	irreducible component `C` to the connected component of an arbitrary chosen
117	point of `C` is well-defined (because `C` is preconnected, hence contained in
118	a single connected component) and surjective (because every point lies in
119	some irreducible component, namely `irreducibleComponent x`, which is a
120	subset of `connectedComponent x`). The source is finite by
121	`TopologicalSpace.NoetherianSpace.finite_irreducibleComponents`, so the
122	target is finite by `Finite.of_surjective`. -/
123	private lemma noetherianSpace_finite_connectedComponents
124	    {α : Type*} [TopologicalSpace α] [TopologicalSpace.NoetherianSpace α] :
125	    Finite (ConnectedComponents α) := by
126	  classical
127	  have hfin : (irreducibleComponents α).Finite :=
128	    TopologicalSpace.NoetherianSpace.finite_irreducibleComponents
129	  haveI : Finite ↥(irreducibleComponents α) := hfin.to_subtype
130	  refine Finite.of_surjective
131	    (fun C : ↥(irreducibleComponents α) =>
132	      (ConnectedComponents.mk : α → ConnectedComponents α)
133	        ((C.property : Maximal IsIrreducible C.val).prop.nonempty.some)) ?_
134	  intro c
135	  obtain ⟨x, rfl⟩ := ConnectedComponents.surjective_coe c
136	  refine ⟨⟨irreducibleComponent x, irreducibleComponent_mem_irreducibleComponents x⟩, ?_⟩
137	  rw [ConnectedComponents.coe_eq_coe]
138	  refine (connectedComponent_eq ?_).symm
139	  exact irreducibleComponent_subset_connectedComponent
140	    (isIrreducible_irreducibleComponent (x := x)).nonempty.some_mem
141	
142	/-- Helper (iter-188): in a **Noetherian** topological space `α`, each
143	connected component is open.
144	
145	Proof: `ConnectedComponents α` is totally disconnected
146	(`ConnectedComponents.totallyDisconnectedSpace`), hence T1
147	(`TotallyDisconnectedSpace.t1Space`), and finite by
148	`noetherianSpace_finite_connectedComponents`. A finite T1 space is discrete
149	(`Finite.instDiscreteTopology`), so the singleton
150	`{ConnectedComponents.mk x}` is open in the quotient. The preimage of this
151	singleton under the continuous quotient map `ConnectedComponents.mk` is
152	`connectedComponent x` (`connectedComponents_preimage_singleton`), which is
153	therefore open. -/
154	private lemma noetherianSpace_isOpen_connectedComponent
155	    {α : Type*} [TopologicalSpace α] [TopologicalSpace.NoetherianSpace α]
156	    (x : α) :
157	    IsOpen (connectedComponent x) := by
158	  haveI : Finite (ConnectedComponents α) := noetherianSpace_finite_connectedComponents
159	  haveI : DiscreteTopology (ConnectedComponents α) := inferInstance
160	  have h := (isOpen_discrete
161	    ({(ConnectedComponents.mk x : ConnectedComponents α)} : Set _)).preimage
162	    ConnectedComponents.continuous_coe
163	  rwa [connectedComponents_preimage_singleton] at h
164	
165	/-- Helper (iter-188): the **`LocallyConnectedSpace` instance** for the
166	underlying topological space of a `k`-scheme `G.left` whose structural
167	morphism `G.hom : G.left ⟶ Spec k` is locally of finite type.
168	
169	The substantive content is EGA I 6.1.9: a locally Noetherian topological
170	space has open connected components — equivalently, is locally connected.
171	Pushed through the chain: `Spec k` is Noetherian (a field has Noetherian
172	spectrum); `LocallyOfFiniteType G.hom + IsLocallyNoetherian (Spec k) ⟹
173	IsLocallyNoetherian G.left` (Mathlib's
174	`AlgebraicGeometry.LocallyOfFiniteType.isLocallyNoetherian`); and the
175	implication `IsLocallyNoetherian X ⟹ LocallyConnectedSpace X.toTopCat`
176	is the iter-188 project-side helper (`noetherianSpace_finite_connectedComponents`
177	+ `noetherianSpace_isOpen_connectedComponent`).
178	
179	The classical proof: each point `y ∈ G.left` has an open affine
180	neighbourhood `W = Spec R` with `R` Noetherian, hence `|W|` is a Noetherian
181	topological space; in a Noetherian space, each `connectedComponent y` is
182	clopen (finite irreducible components ⟹ finite connected components ⟹
183	finite T1 quotient ⟹ discrete quotient ⟹ singletons clopen in quotient ⟹
184	preimages clopen). Pulled back along the open inclusion `W ↪ G.left`,
185	this gives an open connected neighbourhood of `y` inside any open `F`
186	containing `y`. -/
187	private instance identityComponent_locallyConnectedSpace
188	    {k : Type u} [Field k]
189	    (G : Over (Spec (.of k)))
190	    [LocallyOfFiniteType G.hom] :
191	    LocallyConnectedSpace G.left := by
192	  haveI : IsLocallyNoetherian G.left := LocallyOfFiniteType.isLocallyNoetherian G.hom
193	  rw [locallyConnectedSpace_iff_subsets_isOpen_isConnected]
194	  intro x F hF
195	  -- Extract an open neighbourhood `T ⊆ F` of `x`.
196	  obtain ⟨T, hTF, hT, hxT⟩ := mem_nhds_iff.mp hF
197	  -- Find an affine open `W ⊆ T` with `x ∈ W`.
198	  obtain ⟨W, hW, hxW, hWT⟩ :=
199	    exists_isAffineOpen_mem_and_subset (X := G.left) (U := ⟨T, hT⟩) hxT
200	  haveI : IsNoetherianRing Γ(G.left, W) :=
201	    IsLocallyNoetherian.component_noetherian ⟨W, hW⟩
202	  haveI hNoethW : TopologicalSpace.NoetherianSpace ↥W :=
203	    noetherianSpace_of_isAffineOpen W hW
204	  -- Inside `W` (Noetherian) the connected component of `x` is open; its image
205	  -- under the open inclusion `W ↪ G.left` is the connected open neighbourhood
206	  -- we want.
207	  let xW : ↥W := ⟨x, hxW⟩
208	  refine ⟨(Subtype.val : ↥W → G.left) '' connectedComponent xW, ?_, ?_, ?_, ?_⟩
209	  · rintro _ ⟨z, _, rfl⟩
210	    exact hTF (hWT z.2)
211	  · exact W.isOpen.isOpenMap_subtype_val _
212	      (noetherianSpace_isOpen_connectedComponent xW)
213	  · exact ⟨xW, mem_connectedComponent, rfl⟩
214	  · exact isConnected_connectedComponent.image _ continuous_subtype_val.continuousOn
215	
216	/-- The image of the identity section `e : Spec k → G` (well-defined as a
217	single point of `|G|` because `Spec k` is a topological singleton). -/
218	private noncomputable def identitySectionPoint
219	    {k : Type u} [Field k] (G : Over (Spec (.of k))) [GrpObj G] : G.left :=
220	  ((MonObj.one (X := G)).left.base :
221	      ↥(Spec (.of k)) → G.left) (default : Spec (.of k))
222	
223	/-- Helper (iter-186; iter-187 closed): the **clopen carrier `Set`** of
224	the identity component of a `k`-group scheme `G` locally of finite type,
225	packaged as a `G.left.Opens`. The carrier set is `connectedComponent x`
226	for `x = e(*)` the image of the identity section
227	(`identitySectionPoint G`); openness is `isOpen_connectedComponent` which
228	needs the `identityComponent_locallyConnectedSpace` instance above
229	(EGA I 6.1.9). -/
230	private noncomputable def identityComponentCarrier {k : Type u} [Field k]
231	    (G : Over (Spec (.of k)))
232	    [GrpObj G] [LocallyOfFiniteType G.hom] :
233	    G.left.Opens :=
234	  ⟨connectedComponent (identitySectionPoint G), isOpen_connectedComponent⟩
235	
236	/-- The **identity component** `G^0` of a `k`-group scheme `G` locally of
237	finite type.
238	
239	Encoded as a `k`-scheme: an object of `Over (Spec (.of k))`, carrying the
240	intended substantive identity "this is the connected component of `|G|`
241	containing the identity section `e`, equipped with the open-subscheme
242	structure inherited from `G`". The associated open immersion
243	`IdentityComponent G ⟶ G` is the content of
244	`IdentityComponent.isOpenSubgroupScheme`.
245	
246	iter-186 body: built from the `identityComponentCarrier G` helper — the
247	open subscheme of `G` whose underlying topological space is the connected
248	component of `|G|` through the image of the identity section. The
249	structure morphism is the inherited `(identityComponentCarrier G).ι ≫ G.hom`.
250	The substantive content (the actual carrier `Set`, plus its openness from
251	EGA I 6.1.9: locally Noetherian spaces have open connected components)
252	lives in the typed-sorry body of `identityComponentCarrier`. -/
253	noncomputable def IdentityComponent {k : Type u} [Field k]
254	    (G : Over (Spec (.of k)))
255	    [GrpObj G] [LocallyOfFiniteType G.hom] :
256	    Over (Spec (.of k)) :=
257	  Over.mk ((identityComponentCarrier G).ι ≫ G.hom)
258	
259	/-- **The identity component is an open and closed subgroup scheme.**
260	
261	The bundled statement of Kleiman §5 Lem.~`lem:agps`~(3): the identity
262	component `G^0 = IdentityComponent G` of a `k`-group scheme `G` locally of
263	finite type comes with a morphism `IdentityComponent G ⟶ G` (in
264	`Over (Spec (.of k))`) whose underlying scheme morphism is both an open
265	immersion and a closed immersion (i.e. the inclusion of a clopen
266	subscheme).
267	
268	The full Kleiman conclusion also packages the group-subscheme property (the
269	inclusion is a homomorphism of `k`-group schemes), finite-type-ness over `k`
270	(`LocallyOfFiniteType` + quasi-compactness), geometric irreducibility, and
271	base-change-commutation. Those refinements live as separate instances /
272	follow-up lemmas in iter-186+; the file-skeleton pins only the clopen
273	open-immersion conclusion as a Nonempty-witness.
274	
275	iter-186 body: the inclusion morphism is `Over.homMk (identityComponentCarrier G).ι`,
276	with the over-category compatibility holding by definition of
277	`IdentityComponent G`. The `.left` of this morphism is
278	`(identityComponentCarrier G).ι`, an open immersion by the global
279	`Scheme.Opens.instIsOpenImmersionι` instance. For the closed-immersion
280	half we apply `IsClosedImmersion.of_isPreimmersion` to the open immersion
281	and reduce to `IsClosed (↑(identityComponentCarrier G) : Set _)`. The
282	remaining inline `sorry` is the substantive content: the carrier of
283	`identityComponentCarrier G` is closed, by "closure of a connected subspace
284	is connected" applied to the connected component through the image of the
285	identity section. -/
286	theorem IdentityComponent.isOpenSubgroupScheme {k : Type u} [Field k]
287	    (G : Over (Spec (.of k)))
288	    [GrpObj G] [LocallyOfFiniteType G.hom] :
289	    Nonempty {f : IdentityComponent G ⟶ G //
290	        IsOpenImmersion f.left ∧ IsClosedImmersion f.left} := by
291	  -- Over-category compatibility for the underlying open immersion.
292	  have hcomp : (identityComponentCarrier G).ι ≫ G.hom = (IdentityComponent G).hom := by
293	    simp [IdentityComponent]
294	  -- The over-morphism witness.
295	  let f : IdentityComponent G ⟶ G :=
296	    Over.homMk (U := IdentityComponent G) (V := G) (identityComponentCarrier G).ι hcomp
297	  refine ⟨⟨f, ?_, ?_⟩⟩
298	  · -- `f.left = (identityComponentCarrier G).ι` (definitionally; `Over.homMk_left` simp).
299	    -- An open immersion by `Scheme.Opens.instIsOpenImmersionι`.
300	    change IsOpenImmersion (identityComponentCarrier G).ι
301	    infer_instance
302	  · -- `f.left = (identityComponentCarrier G).ι` is a preimmersion (open immersion ⟹
303	    -- immersion ⟹ preimmersion). Its range is the carrier set (`Scheme.Opens.range_ι`),
304	    -- and closure of that connected subset is itself (Kleiman §5 Lem.~`lem:agps`~(3):
305	    -- the connected component through the identity is closed in `|G|`).
306	    change IsClosedImmersion (identityComponentCarrier G).ι
307	    apply IsClosedImmersion.of_isPreimmersion
308	    rw [Scheme.Opens.range_ι]
309	    -- The carrier set is `connectedComponent (identitySectionPoint G)`, which is
310	    -- closed by `isClopen_connectedComponent.1` (with the
311	    -- `identityComponent_locallyConnectedSpace` instance providing the closedness
312	    -- half of EGA I 6.1.9: connected components of a locally Noetherian topological
313	    -- space are clopen).
314	    change IsClosed (connectedComponent (identitySectionPoint G))
315	    exact isClopen_connectedComponent.1
316	
317	/-- **The identity component inclusion is a group-scheme homomorphism.**
318	
319	Kleiman §5 Lem.~`lem:agps`~(3) conclusion (b): the clopen subscheme `G^0`
320	inherits a `k`-group-scheme structure from `G`, and the inclusion morphism
321	`G^0 ↪ G` (from `IdentityComponent.isOpenSubgroupScheme`) is compatible
322	with the group laws on source and target. The statement-level pin asserts
323	the existence of the inherited `GrpObj` structure; the compatibility of
324	the inclusion with the group operations is the substantive content of
325	Kleiman's argument (the product `G^0 ×_k G^0` is connected by
326	EGA IV₂ 4.5.8; the group-multiplication map sends this connected subset
327	containing the identity into the connected component `G^0`).
328	
329	The full statement (existence of `GrpObj (IdentityComponent G)` *together*
330	with the homomorphism-compatibility of the inclusion in the relevant
331	`Hom`-form) lives in iter-188+. -/
332	theorem IdentityComponent.isSubgroupHomomorphism {k : Type u} [Field k]
333	    (G : Over (Spec (.of k)))
334	    [GrpObj G] [LocallyOfFiniteType G.hom] :
335	    Nonempty (GrpObj (IdentityComponent G)) :=
336	  sorry
337	
338	/-- **The identity component is of finite type and geometrically irreducible.**
339	
340	Kleiman §5 Lem.~`lem:agps`~(3) conclusion (c): the open subgroup `G^0` of
341	a `k`-group scheme `G` locally of finite type is itself
342	locally-of-finite-type-plus-quasi-compact (i.e., of finite type) over `k`,
343	and is geometrically irreducible. The proof reduces (after base change to
344	`\bar k`) to picking a nonempty smooth affine open subset `U ⊆ G^0`; the
345	translates `hg⁻¹U` give an open cover of `G^0` by smooth, hence irreducible,
346	neighbourhoods, so `G^0` is locally irreducible at every closed point; with
347	connectedness this gives irreducibility globally (EGA I 6.1.10). The image
348	`α(U × U) = G^0` is the image of the affine, hence quasi-compact, scheme
349	`U × U`, so `G^0` is quasi-compact.
350	
351	iter-189 partial progress: the `LocallyOfFiniteType` conjunct closes
352	axiom-clean via composition of an open immersion with `G.hom`. The
353	remaining two conjuncts (`QuasiCompact`, `GeometricallyIrreducible`)
354	require the group-structure argument and are bundled into the residual
355	sorry. -/
356	theorem IdentityComponent.isFiniteTypeGeometricallyIrreducible
357	    {k : Type u} [Field k]
358	    (G : Over (Spec (.of k)))
359	    [GrpObj G] [LocallyOfFiniteType G.hom] :
360	    LocallyOfFiniteType (IdentityComponent G).hom ∧
361	      QuasiCompact (IdentityComponent G).hom ∧
362	      GeometricallyIrreducible (IdentityComponent G).hom := by
363	  refine ⟨?_, ?_⟩
364	  · -- `LocallyOfFiniteType`: `(IdentityComponent G).hom` unfolds to
365	    -- `(identityComponentCarrier G).ι ≫ G.hom`. Open immersion ∘ LFT
366	    -- is LFT (`locallyOfFiniteType_of_isOpenImmersion` +
367	    -- `locallyOfFiniteType_comp`).
368	    change LocallyOfFiniteType ((identityComponentCarrier G).ι ≫ G.hom)
369	    infer_instance
370	  · -- `QuasiCompact ∧ GeometricallyIrreducible`: Kleiman's group-theoretic
371	    -- argument. `α(U × U) = G^0` quasi-compact via the affine `U × U`;
372	    -- geometric irreducibility via base change to `\bar k`, picking a
373	    -- smooth affine `U ⊆ G^0`, and translating `hg⁻¹U` to cover. Cannot
374	    -- close axiom-clean without the substrate `isSubgroupHomomorphism`
375	    -- + EGA IV₂ 4.5.8 / 4.6.1 / EGA I 6.1.10 (not yet in Mathlib).
376	    sorry
377	
378	/-- **Formation of the identity component commutes with base change.**
379	
380	Kleiman §5 Lem.~`lem:agps`~(3) conclusion (d): for any field extension
381	`K/k`, the natural comparison map `(G^0)_K → (G_K)^0` is an isomorphism of
382	`K`-schemes. The proof: `G^0` is geometrically connected as a connected
383	scheme with a `k`-rational point (EGA IV₂ 4.5.14), so its base change
384	`(G^0)_K` remains connected; it is also open and closed in `G_K`, so
385	coincides with the identity component `(G_K)^0`.
386	
387	The statement-level pin asserts existence of: a `K`-group-scheme structure
388	on the base change `G ×_{Spec k} Spec K` (with appropriate
389	locally-of-finite-type instance), and an isomorphism on underlying schemes
390	identifying the two iterated constructions. The iso witness is a typed
391	sorry pending iter-188+ implementation; the substantive `GrpObj` is
392	provided by `Scheme.GrpObjAsOverPullback` and is propagated through. -/
393	theorem IdentityComponent.baseChangeIso {k : Type u} [Field k]
394	    (G : Over (Spec (.of k))) [GrpObj G] [LocallyOfFiniteType G.hom]
395	    (K : Type u) [Field K] [Algebra k K] :
396	    let φ : Spec (CommRingCat.of K) ⟶ Spec (CommRingCat.of k) :=
397	      Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap k K))
398	    let G_K : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of K)) :=
399	      Over.mk (CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback.snd G.hom φ)
400	    Nonempty (Σ' (_grpInst : GrpObj G_K)
401	                 (_locFTInst : LocallyOfFiniteType G_K.hom),
402	      (IdentityComponent G_K).left ≅
403	        CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback (IdentityComponent G).hom φ) :=
404	  sorry
405	
406	end GroupScheme

408	/-! ## §2. The identity component of the Picard scheme
409	
410	We specialise the abstract identity-component substrate to
411	`G = PicScheme C`, the Picard scheme of a smooth proper geometrically
412	integral curve `C/k` (from sibling `Picard/FGAPicRepresentability.lean`).
413	
414	Blueprint reference: `def:pic_zero_subscheme` (Kleiman §5 opening + Prp.
415	`prp:pic0`). -/
416	
417	namespace Scheme
418	
419	/-- The **identity component of the Picard scheme** `Pic⁰_{C/k}`.
420	
421	Defined as the identity component
422	`GroupScheme.IdentityComponent (PicScheme C)` of the Picard scheme
423	`Pic_{C/k}` (from sibling `Picard/FGAPicRepresentability.lean`,
424	`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicScheme`). By
425	`GroupScheme.IdentityComponent.isOpenSubgroupScheme`, `Pic⁰_{C/k}` is an
426	open and closed subgroup scheme of `Pic_{C/k}` of finite type over `k`,
427	geometrically irreducible, and its formation commutes with extension of the
428	base field.
429	
430	iter-186+: the body unwinds to `GroupScheme.IdentityComponent (PicScheme C)`
431	once the `[LocallyOfFiniteType (PicScheme C).hom]` instance lands on
432	`PicScheme C` (it follows from the Kleiman §4 structure: `Pic_{C/k}` is
433	locally of finite type as the disjoint union of open quasi-projective
434	`k`-subschemes). For the iter-185 file-skeleton the body is a typed `sorry`. -/
435	noncomputable def Pic0Scheme {k : Type u} [Field k]
436	    (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
437	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
438	    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] :
439	    Over (Spec (.of k)) :=
440	  sorry
441	
442	/-! ## §3. The degree map
443	
444	The disjoint-union structure of `Pic_{C/k}` (a disjoint union of open
445	quasi-projective `k`-subschemes, indexed by Hilbert polynomial via
446	`PicScheme.smoothProperQuotient`) stratifies its `T`-points by the leading
447	coefficient of the Hilbert polynomial of a representing invertible sheaf
448	relative to a fixed degree-one polarisation. On `k`-points this gives the
449	**degree map** `Pic_{C/k}(k) → ℤ`.
450	
451	Blueprint reference: `def:divisor_degree_pic` (Milne III.1, p.~88). -/
452	
453	namespace PicScheme
454	
455	/-- The **degree map** `Pic_{C/k}(k) → ℤ`.
456	
457	Sends a `k`-point `λ ∈ Pic_{C/k}(k)` --- a morphism
458	`Spec k ⟶ (PicScheme C).left` --- to the leading coefficient of the
459	Hilbert polynomial of a representing invertible sheaf `L` on `C` (relative
460	to a fixed degree-one polarisation `O_C(1)`). By Riemann--Roch,
461	`χ(C, L ⊗ O_C(n)) = n · deg L + 1 - g`, so the degree is the leading
462	coefficient of `Φ_L(n)`, well-defined on the isomorphism class `[L]` and on
463	the `k`-point `λ` (because `PicScheme C` represents the étale-sheafified
464	relative Picard functor).
465	
466	The degree map is a group homomorphism for the additive structure on
467	`Pic_{C/k}(k)` (tensor product on `L`) and the standard `(ℤ, +)`. The full
468	group-homomorphism refinement / functoriality in `k` lives as a follow-up
469	lemma in iter-186+; the file-skeleton pins only the underlying function.
470	
471	iter-186+: the body extracts the representing invertible sheaf from
472	`(PicScheme.representable C)`, forms its Hilbert polynomial via the project's
473	Hilbert-polynomial machinery (sibling `Picard/QuotScheme.lean`), and returns
474	the leading coefficient. For the iter-185 file-skeleton the body is a typed
475	`sorry`. -/
476	noncomputable def degree {k : Type u} [Field k]
477	    (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
478	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
479	    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] :
480	    (Spec (.of k) ⟶ (PicScheme C).left) → ℤ :=
481	  sorry
482	
483	end PicScheme
484	
485	/-! ## §4. `Pic⁰_{C/k}` is an abelian variety
486	
487	The terminal statement of the chapter identifies `Pic⁰_{C/k}` with an
488	abelian variety of dimension `g(C)` --- the Jacobian variety of `C`. In
489	this project, "abelian variety" is the conjunction of the four properties
490	`[GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom]`
491	threaded through `AlgebraicJacobian.AbelianVarietyRigidity` and consumed by
492	`AlgebraicJacobian.Albanese.AlbaneseUP`.
493	
494	Blueprint reference: `thm:pic_zero_is_abelian_variety` (Kleiman §5
495	Ex.~`ex:jac` + Rmk.~`rmk:Jac`; cf. Milne §I.1, Rmk. III.1.4(e)). -/
496	
497	namespace Pic0Scheme
498	
499	/-- **`Pic⁰_{C/k}` is an abelian variety.**
500	
501	For `C/k` a smooth proper geometrically integral curve of genus `g = g(C)`
502	over a field `k`, the identity component `Pic⁰_{C/k}` of the Picard scheme
503	is an *abelian variety over* `k`: a smooth, proper, geometrically
504	irreducible `k`-group scheme. (Commutativity follows automatically from
505	Milne §I.1, Cor. 1.4, and is not separately stated here.)
506	
507	The bundled statement: the four pieces `[IsProper] ∧ [Smooth] ∧
508	[GeometricallyIrreducible] ∧ Nonempty (GrpObj _)` hold on
509	`(Pic0Scheme C).hom` and `(Pic0Scheme C)` respectively.
510	
511	Dimension `g = g(C)`: not stated separately at the file-skeleton level
512	(it requires `dim_k (Pic0Scheme C).left = AlgebraicGeometry.genus C`, which
513	involves the Krull-dimension API on the underlying scheme); iter-186+
514	follow-up.
515	
516	iter-186+: the body assembles the four conjuncts from
517	- `GroupScheme.IdentityComponent.isOpenSubgroupScheme` (clopen subscheme of
518	  `PicScheme C`),
519	- Kleiman §5 Thm.~`th:qpp&p` (`X/k` projective + geom. integral ⟹
520	  `Pic⁰_{X/k}` quasi-projective; upgrade to projective when geom. normal,
521	  which holds for smooth proper curves of positive genus),
522	- Kleiman §5 Cor.~`cor:sm` + Ex.~`ex:jac` (smoothness of `Pic_{X/k}` at the
523	  identity for smooth proper curves; hence smooth of dimension
524	  `dim_k H¹(C, O_C) = g(C)` everywhere by uniform smoothness on the open
525	  identity component).
526	For the iter-185 file-skeleton the body is a typed `sorry`. -/
527	theorem isAbelianVariety {k : Type u} [Field k]
528	    (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
529	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
530	    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] :
531	    IsProper (Pic0Scheme C).hom ∧ Smooth (Pic0Scheme C).hom ∧
532	      GeometricallyIrreducible (Pic0Scheme C).hom ∧
533	      Nonempty (GrpObj (Pic0Scheme C)) :=
534	  sorry
535	
536	/-- **Dimension of `Pic⁰_{C/k}` equals the genus of `C`.**
537	
538	Milne~§III.1, Rmk.~1.4(e): "The dimension of `J` is the genus of `C`".
539	For a smooth proper geometrically integral curve `C/k` of genus
540	`g = g(C)`, the topological Krull dimension of the underlying scheme of
541	`Pic⁰_{C/k}` equals `g(C)`. By Kleiman~§5 Cor.~`cor:sm`, the inequality
542	`dim Pic_{C/k} ≤ dim_k H¹(C, O_C)` is always an equality at points where
543	`Pic_{C/k}` is smooth, and for smooth proper curves
544	(`SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom`) the identity component is smooth
545	(Kleiman~§5 Ex.~`ex:jac`), so the dimension equals `dim_k H¹(C, O_C) = g(C)`
546	by `def:genus`. -/
547	theorem finrank_eq_genus {k : Type u} [Field k]
548	    (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
549	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
550	    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] :
551	    topologicalKrullDim (Pic0Scheme C).left = (AlgebraicGeometry.genus C : WithBot ℕ∞) :=
552	  sorry
553	
554	/-- **`k`-points of `Pic⁰_{C/k}` are the kernel of the degree map.**
555	
556	Milne~§III.1, p.~88: `Pic⁰(C)` is the group of isomorphism classes of
557	invertible sheaves of degree zero on `C`. For a smooth proper geometrically
558	integral curve `C/k`, a `k`-point `λ ∈ Pic_{C/k}(k)` lies in the image of
559	the inclusion `Pic⁰_{C/k} ↪ Pic_{C/k}` (the inclusion of
560	`def:pic_zero_subscheme`, packaged here via the existence of the
561	inclusion morphism) if and only if `degree C λ = 0`.
562	
563	The statement-level pin packages two pieces: existence of the inclusion
564	morphism `Pic⁰_{C/k} ⟶ Pic_{C/k}` (extracted from
565	`IdentityComponent.isOpenSubgroupScheme` once `PicScheme C` has the
566	`GrpObj` + `LocallyOfFiniteType` instances), together with the
567	characterisation of `k`-points factoring through it as those with degree
568	zero. -/
569	theorem kPoints_iff_kerDegree {k : Type u} [Field k]
570	    (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
571	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
572	    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] :
573	    Nonempty (Σ' (inc : (Pic0Scheme C).left ⟶ (PicScheme C).left),
574	      ∀ (lambda : Spec (.of k) ⟶ (PicScheme C).left),
575	        (∃ mu : Spec (.of k) ⟶ (Pic0Scheme C).left, mu ≫ inc = lambda) ↔
576	          PicScheme.degree C lambda = 0) :=
577	  sorry
578	
579	end Pic0Scheme
580	
581	end Scheme
582	
583	end AlgebraicGeometry
